### `PO/Core/Variable.lean` вҖ” PO variables and regimed variables

Abstracts the `S.hXbool.measurable.comp ((measurable_pi_apply _).comp (P.measurable_eval _))`
chain used throughout IV/LATE-style proofs.  A `POVar P Оұ` bundles a node
`v : P.V` with a measurable equivalence `P.X v вүғбөҗ Оұ`, so factual and
counterfactual realisations land in a fixed measurable type.  `RegimedVar`
further attaches a regime and exposes a uniform `value : P.О© вҶ’ Оұ`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POVar P Оұ` | `structure { v : P.V, equiv : P.X v вүғбөҗ Оұ }` | PO system variable identified with a fixed measurable value type `Оұ` | **Defined** |
| `POVar.cf a r` | `P.О© вҶ’ Оұ` | Counterfactual value `a` under regime `r`: `Пү вҶҰ a.equiv (P.eval r Пү a.v)` | **Defined** |
| `POVar.factual a` | `P.О© вҶ’ Оұ` | Factual value `a.cf Regime.empty` | **Defined** |
| `POVar.measurable_cf` | `Measurable (a.cf r)` | One-liner measurability of the counterfactual map | **Proved** |
| `POVar.measurable_factual` | `Measurable a.factual` | Specialisation of `measurable_cf` to the empty regime | **Proved** |
| `POVar.event a x` | `Set P.О©` | Preimage `a.factual вҒ»В№' {x}` вҖ” the event `{a = x}` | **Defined** |
| `POVar.measurableSet_event` | `MeasurableSet (a.event x)` | Measurability of the factual event (needs `MeasurableSingletonClass Оұ`) | **Proved** |
| `POVar.cfUnder a w y` | `P.О© вҶ’ Оұ` | Counterfactual `a` under the single-node intervention `{w вҶҗ y}`, i.e. `a.cf (Regime.single w.v (w.equiv.symm y))`; replaces hand-rolled `DofZ z`, `YofD d` patterns | **Defined** |
| `POVar.measurable_cfUnder` | `Measurable (a.cfUnder w y)` | Measurability of the single-intervention counterfactual | **Proved** |
| `RegimedVar P Оұ` | `structure { var : POVar P Оұ, regime : Regime P.V P.X }` | PO variable bundled with an intervention regime | **Defined** |
| `RegimedVar.value` | `P.О© вҶ’ Оұ` | `rv.var.cf rv.regime` вҖ” uniform evaluator used by `IndepCF` and `POCFBundle.jointValue` | **Defined** |
| `RegimedVar.measurable_value` | `Measurable rv.value` | Measurability of the uniform evaluator | **Proved** |
| `RegimedVar.ofFactual a` | `RegimedVar P Оұ` | Factual bundling `вҹЁa, Regime.emptyвҹ©` вҖ” the standard shape for "factual Z is independent of the cf bundle" | **Defined** |
| `RegimedVar.ofSingle a w x` | `RegimedVar P Оұ` | Bundling under the single-node intervention `{w вҶҗ x}` вҖ” shortcut for `dUnderZ`/`yUnderD` style variables | **Defined** |
| `POVar.indicator a x` | `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Real-valued indicator `1_{a = x}` (defined as `(a.event x).indicator 1`); subsumes per-system `indD`/`indA`/`indM` boilerplate | **Defined** |
| `POVar.indicator_eq_event_indicator` | `a.indicator x = (a.event x).indicator (fun _ => 1)` | Set-indicator form (definitional `rfl`) | **Proved** |
| `POVar.indicator_apply_eq_one` | `a.factual Пү = x вҶ’ a.indicator x Пү = 1` | Pointwise value on `{a = x}` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.indicator_apply_eq_zero` | `a.factual Пү вү  x вҶ’ a.indicator x Пү = 0` | Pointwise value off `{a = x}` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.measurable_indicator` | `Measurable (a.indicator x)` | Measurability of the indicator | **Proved** |
| `POVar.stronglyMeasurable_indicator_comap` | `StronglyMeasurable[comap a.factual _] (a.indicator x)` | Strong measurability w.r.t. the `a.factual` Пғ-algebra | **Proved** |
| `POVar.integrable_indicator` | `[IsFiniteMeasure P.Ој] вҶ’ Integrable (a.indicator x) P.Ој` | Indicator is bounded by 1, hence integrable | **Proved** |
| `POVar.indicator_eq_one_or_zero` | `a.indicator x Пү = 1 вҲЁ a.indicator x Пү = 0` | Binary value | **Proved** |
| `POVar.indicator_add_indicator_not` | `a.indicator true Пү + a.indicator false Пү = 1` | Binary-treatment complementarity (`POVar P Bool`) | **Proved** |

### `PO/Assumptions/ConsistencyLemmas.lean` вҖ” Pointwise consistency for `POVar`

Generalises the two pointwise consistency specialisations of `LATE.lean`
(`DofZ_eq_factualD_on_zEvent`, `factualY_eq_YofD_factualD`) to arbitrary
`POVar` pairs on distinct nodes.  Each lemma discharges the `Consistency.factual`
clause by reducing it to a singleton-regime agreement check.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POSystem.factualAgrees_empty` | `вҲҖ Пү, P.FactualAgrees Regime.empty Пү` | Trivial base case: every `Пү` factually agrees with the empty regime (target is `вҲ…`) | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.factualAgrees_sqcup` | `{rвӮҒ rвӮӮ : Regime}`, `h : rвӮҒ.Disjoint rвӮӮ`, `P.FactualAgrees rвӮҒ Пү` вҶ’ `P.FactualAgrees rвӮӮ Пү` вҶ’ `P.FactualAgrees (rвӮҒ.sqcup rвӮӮ h) Пү` | Combinator: `FactualAgrees` for a disjoint union reduces componentwise. Lets theorem files assemble multi-target consistency hypotheses from per-variable pieces without hand-rolling a `Finset.mem_union` case split | **Proved** |
| `POVar.factualAgrees_single` | `(a : POVar P Оұ) (x : Оұ)`, `a.factual Пү = x` вҶ’ `P.FactualAgrees (Regime.single a.v (a.equiv.symm x)) Пү` | Combinator: turns a factual equality `a.factual Пү = x` into `FactualAgrees` for the singleton regime `{a.v вҶҗ a.equiv.symm x}`. Base case for building compound `FactualAgrees` via `factualAgrees_sqcup` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.cf_eq_factual_on_event` | `hC : P.Consistency`, `a w : POVar`, `a.v вү  w.v`, `Пү вҲҲ w.event y` вҶ’ `a.cfUnder w y Пү = a.factual Пү` | Consistency of `a` under `{w вҶҗ y}`: on the event `{w = y}`, the `w`-intervened counterfactual of `a` equals the factual | **Proved** |
| `POVar.factual_eq_cfUnder_self_selected` | `hC : P.Consistency`, `a w : POVar`, `a.v вү  w.v`, `вҲҖ Пү` вҶ’ `a.factual Пү = a.cfUnder w (w.factual Пү) Пү` | Pointwise: the factual equals the counterfactual under `{w вҶҗ factual w(Пү)}` вҖ” the identity used to rewrite factual outcomes in terms of cf outcomes on the natural treatment choice | **Proved** |
| `POVar.factual_mul_indicator_eq_cfUnder_mul_indicator` | `hC : P.Consistency`, `a : POVar P в„қ`, `w : POVar P ОІ`, `a.v вү  w.v` вҶ’ `(fun Пү вҶҰ a.factual Пү * 1_{w=y} Пү) = (fun Пү вҶҰ a.cfUnder w y Пү * 1_{w=y} Пү)` | Integrated form of consistency: multiplying by the indicator `1_{w=y}` lets factual and cf values be freely interchanged; workhorse of backdoor-style Step-1 rewrites | **Proved** |
| `POVar.factual_mul_indicator_eq_cfUnder_mul_indicator_fn` | `hC`, `y : POVar P в„қ`, `w : POVar P ОІ`, `[MeasurableSingletonClass ОІ]`, `y.v вү  w.v` вҶ’ `(fun Пү вҶҰ y.factual Пү * w.indicator x Пү) = (fun Пү вҶҰ y.cfUnder w x Пү * w.indicator x Пү)` | Pointwise-function variant matching the new `POVar.indicator` shape; one-line reduction to the `Set.indicator` form above. Used by backdoor/Manski/frontdoor/DTR Step-1 rewrites | **Proved** |
| `POVar.cf_eq_factual_of_factualAgrees` | `hC : P.Consistency`, `a : POVar P Оұ`, `r : Regime`, `a.v вҲү r.target`, `P.FactualAgrees r Пү` вҶ’ `a.cf r Пү = a.factual Пү` | **Multi-target** consistency: pointwise, `a`'s counterfactual under any regime `r` (not naming `a.v`) coincides with its factual at every `Пү` that factually agrees with `r`. Generalises `cf_eq_factual_on_event` from singleton to arbitrary regimes | **Proved** |
| `POVar.factual_mul_indicator_eq_cf_mul_indicator` | `hC : P.Consistency`, `a : POVar P в„қ`, `r : Regime`, `a.v вҲү r.target`, `E : Set P.О©`, `(вҲҖ Пү вҲҲ E, P.FactualAgrees r Пү)` вҶ’ `(fun Пү вҶҰ a.factual Пү * 1_E Пү) = (fun Пү вҶҰ a.cf r Пү * 1_E Пү)` | **Multi-target** integrated consistency: on any event `E` of full factual agreement with `r`, the factual outcome and the multi-target counterfactual outcome are interchangeable inside `a В· 1_E`. Generalises `factual_mul_indicator_eq_cfUnder_mul_indicator` to arbitrary regimes; consumed directly by `cdtr_backdoor` Step 1 | **Proved** |
| `POVar.eventCondExp_cfUnder_eq_factual_on_event` | `hC : P.Consistency`, `y : POVar P в„қ`, `a : POVar P ОІ`, `y.v вү  a.v` вҶ’ `eventCondExp Ој (a.event aвӮҖ) (y.cfUnder a aвӮҖ) = eventCondExp Ој (a.event aвӮҖ) y.factual` | Consistency-on-event for `eventCondExp`: on the stratum `{a = aвӮҖ}`, the `{a вҶҗ aвӮҖ}`-counterfactual and the factual have the same event-level conditional mean. Shared rewrite in `Manski/{MTR,MTS}` and (via the `Fintype` total law below) the integrated MIV bounds; lives in `EventCondExp.lean` alongside the other `eventCondExp` tooling | **Proved** |

### `PO/Assumptions/IndepCF.lean` вҖ” Independence of PO counterfactual bundles

Named shapes for `IndepFun` / `CondIndepFun` over a heterogeneous bundle of
regimed PO variables.  Replaces bespoke product-tuple encodings such as the
`cfTuple` pattern in `PO/ID/Exact/LATE.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POCFBundle P` | `structure { n : в„•, type : Fin n вҶ’ Type*, inst : вҲҖ i, MeasurableSpace (type i), vars : вҲҖ i, RegimedVar P (type i) }` | Heterogeneous finite-length bundle of regimed PO variables over a fixed PO system | **Defined** |
| `POCFBundle.instMeasurableSpaceType` | `MeasurableSpace (B.type i)` | Per-component measurable-space instance derived from the `inst` field (for typeclass inference) | **Instance** |
| `POCFBundle.jointValue B` | `P.О© вҶ’ (вҲҖ i : Fin B.n, B.type i)` | Joint evaluator returning the dependent tuple of cf values | **Defined** |
| `POCFBundle.measurable_jointValue` | `Measurable B.jointValue` | Measurability of the joint evaluator (via `measurable_pi_lambda`) | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.nil P` | `POCFBundle P` | Empty bundle (length 0) | **Defined** |
| `POCFBundle.cons a B` | `POCFBundle P` | Prepend a `RegimedVar` to a bundle (length `B.n + 1`); used to build the `(D(1), D(0), Y(1), Y(0))` bundle in LATE | **Defined** |
| `POSystem.IndepCF` | `RegimedVar P Оұ вҶ’ POCFBundle P вҶ’ Measure P.О© вҶ’ Prop` | `IndepFun a.value B.jointValue Ој` вҖ” uniform shape for "regimed variable вҹӮ bundle" | **Defined** |
| `POSystem.CondIndepCF` | `RegimedVar P Оұ вҶ’ POCFBundle P вҶ’ RegimedVar P Оі вҶ’ Measure P.О© вҶ’ Prop` | Conditional independence of `a` from `B` given Пғ(`c.value`); requires `StandardBorelSpace P.О©` | **Defined** |
| `IndepCF.toIndepFun` / `IndepCF.ofIndepFun` | `P.IndepCF a B Ој вҶ” IndepFun a.value B.jointValue Ој` | Trivial bridges between `IndepCF` and the underlying `IndepFun` | **Proved** (`id`) |
| `CondIndepCF.toCondIndepFun` | `P.CondIndepCF a B c Ој вҶ’ CondIndepFun (comap c.value _) _ a.value B.jointValue Ој` | Unfolding bridge to Mathlib's `CondIndepFun` with the comap Пғ-algebra | **Proved** (`id`) |
| `POSystem.condIndepCF_congr_cond` | `comap c.value = comap c'.value вҶ’ P.CondIndepCF a B c Ој вҶ’ P.CondIndepCF a B c' Ој` | Transports conditional independence across conditioning variables that generate the same Пғ-algebra | **Proved** |
| `IndepCF.project` | `P.IndepCF a B Ој вҶ’ Measurable ПҲ вҶ’ IndepFun a.value (ПҲ вҲҳ B.jointValue) Ој` | Push `IndepCF` through an arbitrary measurable projection `ПҲ : (вҲҖ i, B.type i) вҶ’ ОІ` вҖ” replaces open-coded `.comp measurable_id` dances in ATE/LATE | **Proved** |
| `IndepCF.component` | `P.IndepCF a B Ој вҶ’ (i : Fin B.n) вҶ’ IndepFun a.value (fun Пү вҶҰ B.jointValue Пү i) Ој` | Specialisation of `project` to single-coordinate extraction (e.g. pick out `Y(d)` from a joint cf bundle) | **Proved** |
| `CondIndepCF.project` | `P.CondIndepCF a B c Ој вҶ’ Measurable ПҲ вҶ’ CondIndepFun (comap c.value _) _ a.value (ПҲ вҲҳ B.jointValue) Ој` | Analogue of `IndepCF.project` at the conditional-independence level | **Proved** |
| `CondIndepCF.component` | `P.CondIndepCF a B c Ој вҶ’ (i : Fin B.n) вҶ’ CondIndepFun (comap c.value _) _ a.value (fun Пү вҶҰ B.jointValue Пү i) Ој` | Analogue of `IndepCF.component` at the conditional-independence level | **Proved** |

### `PO/Conditioning/Bundle.lean` вҖ” Bundle-conditioned conditional expectation

Lifts the single-`POVar` conditioning machinery in `CondExpTooling.lean` to a
`POCFBundle`, i.e. conditioning on the *joint* cf-value map of a tuple of
regimed variables.  The conditioning Пғ-algebra `B.sigma` is
`MeasurableSpace.comap B.jointValue inferInstance`.  Used by the multi-stage
DTR / sequential-backdoor identification arguments (`PO/ID/Exact/DTR/`)
where the conditioning Пғ-algebra is generated by a *bundle* of covariates rather
than a single one; mirrors all the lemmas of `POVar.condExpGiven` /
`POVar.condExpRatio` at the bundle level.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POCFBundle.sigma B` | `MeasurableSpace P.О©` | `MeasurableSpace.comap B.jointValue inferInstance` вҖ” the Пғ-algebra generated by the bundle's joint cf-value map | **Defined** |
| `POCFBundle.sigma_le` | `B.sigma вүӨ inferInstance` | `B.sigma` is a sub-Пғ-algebra of the ambient Пғ-algebra on `P.О©` | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven B g Ој` | `POCFBundle P вҶ’ (P.О© вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Measure P.О© вҶ’ (P.О© вҶ’ в„қ)` | `Ој[g \| B.sigma]` вҖ” Mathlib `condExp` on the bundle's comap Пғ-algebra (bundle analogue of `POVar.condExpGiven`) | **Defined** |
| `POCFBundle.stronglyMeasurable_condExpGiven_comap` | `StronglyMeasurable[B.sigma] (B.condExpGiven g Ој)` | `condExpGiven g` is strongly measurable w.r.t. the sub-Пғ-algebra `B.sigma` | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.stronglyMeasurable_condExpGiven` | `StronglyMeasurable (B.condExpGiven g Ој)` | `condExpGiven g` is strongly measurable w.r.t. the ambient Пғ-algebra | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.integrable_condExpGiven` | `Integrable (B.condExpGiven g Ој) Ој` | `condExpGiven g` is integrable | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven_tower_of_le` | `m вүӨ B.sigma вҶ’ [SigmaFinite (Ој.trim _)] вҶ’ Ој[B.condExpGiven g Ој \| m] =бөҗ[Ој] Ој[g \| m]` | Tower property against an arbitrary smaller sub-Пғ-algebra `m вүӨ B.sigma`; bundle analogue of `POVar.condExpGiven_tower_of_le` | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left` | `StronglyMeasurable[B.sigma] f вҶ’ Integrable (f*g) Ој вҶ’ Integrable g Ој вҶ’ B.condExpGiven (f*g) Ој =бөҗ[Ој] f * B.condExpGiven g Ој` | Pull-out-left: a `B.sigma`-measurable factor pulls outside `Ој[В·\|B.sigma]` | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_right` | Symmetric right-pull-out | Pull-out-right form of the same identity | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven_indicator_mul` | `MeasurableSet[B.sigma] s вҶ’ вҖҰ вҶ’ B.condExpGiven (1_s * g) Ој =бөҗ[Ој] 1_s * B.condExpGiven g Ој` | Specialisation of pull-out-left to indicators of `B.sigma`-measurable sets | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven_congr_ae` | `f =бөҗ[Ој] g вҶ’ B.condExpGiven f Ој =бөҗ[Ој] B.condExpGiven g Ој` | a.e. congruence: equal-a.e. integrands give equal-a.e. bundle conditional expectations | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpRatio B g h Ој` | `POCFBundle P вҶ’ (P.О© вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (P.О© вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Measure P.О© вҶ’ (P.О© вҶ’ в„қ)` | Pointwise ratio `B.condExpGiven g Ој / B.condExpGiven h Ој` вҖ” bundle analogue of `POVar.condExpRatio`, used to package the inner/outer adjusted regressions in DTR | **Defined** |
| `POCFBundle.measurable_condExpRatio` | `Measurable (B.condExpRatio g h Ој)` | Pointwise-division measurability of `condExpRatio` | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.stronglyMeasurable_condExpRatio` | `StronglyMeasurable (B.condExpRatio g h Ој)` | Strong measurability of `condExpRatio` | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpRatio_eq_of_mul` | `B.condExpGiven g Ој =бөҗ B.condExpGiven h Ој * target вҶ’ (вҲҖбөҗ Пү, B.condExpGiven h Ој Пү вү  0) вҶ’ B.condExpRatio g h Ој =бөҗ[Ој] target` | ProductвҶ’ratio characterisation; the exact shape consumed by `cdtr_backdoor` | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.CondIndepCFBundle` | `RegimedVar P Оұ вҶ’ POCFBundle P вҶ’ POCFBundle P вҶ’ Measure P.О© вҶ’ Prop` | Bundle-conditioned conditional independence: `CondIndepFun C.sigma C.sigma_le a.value B.jointValue Ој` вҖ” bundle analogue of `POSystem.CondIndepCF`; requires `StandardBorelSpace P.О©` | **Defined** |
| `CondIndepCFBundle.toCondIndepFun` | `P.CondIndepCFBundle a B C Ој вҶ’ CondIndepFun C.sigma C.sigma_le a.value B.jointValue Ој` | Trivial unfolding bridge to Mathlib's `CondIndepFun` against `C.sigma` | **Proved** (`id`) |
| `CondIndepCFBundle.project` | `P.CondIndepCFBundle a B C Ој вҶ’ Measurable ПҲ вҶ’ CondIndepFun C.sigma C.sigma_le a.value (ПҲ вҲҳ B.jointValue) Ој` | Push `CondIndepCFBundle` through an arbitrary measurable projection of the bundle joint value вҖ” analogue of `CondIndepCF.project` for bundle-conditioned CI | **Proved** |

### `PO/Conditioning/EventCondExp.lean` вҖ” Event-level conditional expectation and drop-of-conditioning

Thin wrapper around `вҲ«_A g вҲӮОј / (Ој A).toReal` paired with a **drop-of-conditioning**
lemma: when the event-defining variable is `IndepCF` of a bundle `B`,
conditioning on a `value = x` event has no effect on `вҲ« h вҲҳ B.jointValue`.
Both multiplied and quotient forms are provided.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `eventCondExp Ој A g` | `в„қ`, defined as `(вҲ« Пү in A, g Пү вҲӮОј) / (Ој A).toReal` | Event-level conditional expectation, packaging the hand-rolled `condExpDZ` / `condExpYZ` idiom as a standalone operator | **Defined** |
| `eventCondExp_mul_measure_toReal` | `[IsFiniteMeasure Ој]`, `A : Set О©`, `f : О© вҶ’ в„қ` вҶ’ `eventCondExp Ој A f * (Ој A).toReal = вҲ« Пү in A, f Пү вҲӮОј` | Quotient-to-set-integral conversion (including the zero-measure case, where both sides collapse to `0`). Domain-agnostic; consumed by the Fintype total law below and by the Manski MTR/MTS set-integral manipulations | **Proved** |
| `integral_eq_sum_measure_mul_eventCondExp` | `[Fintype О№]`, `[IsFiniteMeasure Ој]`, `A : О№ вҶ’ Set О©` measurable / pairwise disjoint / covering `univ`, `f` integrable вҶ’ `вҲ« f = вҲ‘ i, (Ој (A i)).toReal * eventCondExp Ој (A i) f` | **Finite-partition total law.** Instantiates at `О№ := Bool` for `Manski/{MTR,MTS}` binary decompositions and at `О№ := Оұ` (discrete IV value space) for the integrated MIV bounds. Domain-agnostic вҖ” an upstream-ready identity | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_eq_sum_condProb_mul_eventCondExp` | `[Fintype О№]`, `[IsFiniteMeasure Ој]`, `A` measurable, `C : О№ вҶ’ Set О©` measurable / pairwise disjoint / covering `univ`, `f` integrable вҶ’ `eventCondExp Ој A f = вҲ‘ i, (Ој (A вҲ© C i)).toReal / (Ој A).toReal * eventCondExp Ој (A вҲ© C i) f` | **Conditional finite-partition total law (law of iterated expectations).** `E[fвҲЈA] = вҲ‘ i P(C iвҲЈA)В·E[fвҲЈA вҲ© C i]`. The measure-theoretic core of the finiteвҶ’population bridge for cell-indexed estimands: a finite covariate-weighted average of within-cell means equals the population conditional expectation `E[fвҲЈG=g]` when the weights are the conditional cell probabilities. Domain-agnostic | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_congr_ae` | `f =бөҗ[Ој] g вҶ’ eventCondExp Ој A f = eventCondExp Ој A g` | a.e.-equal integrands give equal `eventCondExp`; shared congruence used across DID / Manski consistency rewrites | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_congr_on` | `MeasurableSet A вҶ’ (вҲҖ Пү вҲҲ A, f Пү = g Пү) вҶ’ eventCondExp Ој A f = eventCondExp Ој A g` | Equal-on-`A` integrands give equal `eventCondExp`; specialisation of the a.e. version to a pointwise identity on a measurable set | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_mono_ae` | `IntegrableOn f A Ој вҶ’ IntegrableOn g A Ој вҶ’ f вүӨбөҗ[Ој] g вҶ’ eventCondExp Ој A f вүӨ eventCondExp Ој A g` | Monotonicity of `eventCondExp` under an a.e. inequality of integrable functions; workhorse of the Manski stratum-sandwich bounds | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_add` | `IntegrableOn gвӮҒ A Ој`, `IntegrableOn gвӮӮ A Ој` вҶ’ `eventCondExp Ој A (gвӮҒ + gвӮӮ) = eventCondExp Ој A gвӮҒ + eventCondExp Ој A gвӮӮ` | Additivity | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_sub` | Analogous subtraction identity | Subtraction | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_smul` | `eventCondExp Ој A (c вҖў g) = c * eventCondExp Ој A g` | Scalar linearity | **Proved** |
| `eventCondExp_of_ae_eq_IndepFun` | `IndepFun z B Ој`, measurable `z`, `B`, `h`, `factualF =бөҗ[Ој.restrict (z вҒ»В№' {x})] h вҲҳ B`, `0 < (Ој (z вҒ»В№' {x})).toReal` вҶ’ `eventCondExp Ој (z вҒ»В№' {x}) factualF = вҲ« h (B Пү) вҲӮОј` | Plain-`IndepFun` consistency-on-cell + drop-of-conditioning helper, reusable when the event variable is not packaged as a `POVar`; used by Angrist-Imbens factual Wald reductions | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.integral_restrict_value_eq_mul_of_IndepCF` | `P.IndepCF rv B Ој`, `Measurable h` вҶ’ `вҲ« Пү in rv.value вҒ»В№' {x}, h (B.jointValue Пү) вҲӮОј = (Ој (rv.value вҒ»В№' {x})).toReal * вҲ« Пү, h (B.jointValue Пү) вҲӮОј` | Drop-of-conditioning (multiplied form): the core identity, direct application of `IndepFun.integral_restrict_preimage_eq_mul` | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.integral_event_eq_mul_of_IndepCF` | Same identity with `A = a.event x` for factual `POVar a` | Specialisation to the factual event `{a = x}` | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.eventCondExp_eq_integral_of_IndepCF` | Under `IndepCF` and `Ој A вҲү {0, вҠӨ}`, `eventCondExp Ој (rv.value вҒ»В№' {x}) (h вҲҳ B.jointValue) = вҲ« h вҲҳ B.jointValue вҲӮОј` | Quotient form of drop-of-conditioning | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.eventCondExp_event_eq_integral_of_IndepCF` | Quotient form specialised to `a.event x` | Specialisation used directly in `late_wald`-style derivations | **Proved** |
| `POSystem.eventCondExp_of_consistency_IndepCF` | `P.IndepCF (ofFactual a) B Ој`, `Measurable h`, `(вҲҖ Пү вҲҲ a.event x, factualF Пү = h (B.jointValue Пү))`, `Ој (a.event x) вҲү {0, вҠӨ}` вҶ’ `eventCondExp Ој (a.event x) factualF = вҲ« h (B.jointValue Пү) вҲӮОј` | Workhorse combo: consistency-on-event rewrite + drop-of-conditioning in one call; collapses the repeated `hCE` blocks of `first_stage_identity` / `reduced_form_identity` | **Proved** |

### `PO/Conditioning/EventCondExpBundle.lean` вҖ” bundle analogue of `eventCondExp_of_consistency_IndepCF`

Lifts the single-period `EventCondExp` workhorse to the bundle-conditional setting (`P.CondIndepCFBundle a B C`) used by the Dynamic LATE bridges.  Per-stage workhorse: under bundle-conditional independence + consistency-on-event, the bundle conditional expectation of `factualF В· 1_{a=x}` factors into the conditional expectation of `h вҲҳ B.jointValue` times the conditional expectation of `1_{a=x}`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POCFBundle.condExpGiven_mul_of_consistency_CondIndepCFBundle` | `P.CondIndepCFBundle (ofFactual a) B C Ој`, `Measurable h`, `(factualF В· 1_{a=x}) =бөҗ (h вҲҳ B.jointValue) В· 1_{a=x}` вҶ’ `C.condExpGiven (factualF В· 1_{a=x}) =бөҗ C.condExpGiven (h вҲҳ B.jointValue) В· C.condExpGiven (1_{a=x})` | Product form of the bundle workhorse.  Proof reduces to `Causalean.condExp_mul_of_condIndep` with `f := a.factual`, `g := B.jointValue`, `u y := ({x} : Set Оұ).indicator 1`, `v := h`, exploiting `(RegimedVar.ofFactual a).value = a.factual` (rfl). | **Proved** |
| `POCFBundle.condExpRatio_of_consistency_CondIndepCFBundle` | Same hypotheses + `вҲҖбөҗ Пү, C.condExpGiven (1_{a=x}) Пү вү  0` вҶ’ `C.condExpRatio (factualF В· 1_{a=x}) (1_{a=x}) =бөҗ C.condExpGiven (h вҲҳ B.jointValue)` | Ratio form, derived from the product form via `condExpRatio_eq_of_mul`. | **Proved** |

### `PO/Conditioning/CondExpTooling.lean` вҖ” Пғ-algebra-based conditional expectation

Thin wrapper around `MeasureTheory.condExp` keyed by the factual map of a
`POVar`.  Continuous-`X` companion to the event-based `eventCondExp` in
`EventCondExp.lean`; used by the backdoor ATE argument under the PO
framework, where the conditioning covariate may be continuous and the
integrand has the shape `Y(d)В·1{D=d}` or `1{D=d}`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POVar.condExpGiven c g Ој` | `POVar P Оі вҶ’ (P.О© вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Measure P.О© вҶ’ (P.О© вҶ’ в„қ)` | `Ој[g \| Пғ(c.factual)]` вҖ” Mathlib `condExp` on the comap Пғ-algebra generated by `c.factual` | **Defined** |
| `POVar.comap_factual_le` | `MeasurableSpace.comap c.factual inferInstance вүӨ mО©` | The conditioning Пғ-algebra `Пғ(c.factual)` is a sub-Пғ-algebra of the ambient one on `P.О©` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_add` | `Integrable f Ој вҶ’ Integrable g Ој вҶ’ c.condExpGiven (f+g) Ој =бөҗ[Ој] c.condExpGiven f Ој + c.condExpGiven g Ој` | Additivity of `condExpGiven` (a.e.) | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_sub` | Analogous subtraction identity | Subtraction linearity of `condExpGiven` (a.e.) | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_smul` | `c.condExpGiven (k вҖў g) Ој =бөҗ[Ој] k вҖў c.condExpGiven g Ој` | Scalar homogeneity of `condExpGiven` (a.e.) | **Proved** |
| `POVar.stronglyMeasurable_condExpGiven_comap` | `StronglyMeasurable[Пғ(c.factual)] (c.condExpGiven g Ој)` | `condExpGiven g` is strongly measurable w.r.t. the sub-Пғ-algebra `Пғ(c.factual)` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.stronglyMeasurable_condExpGiven` | `StronglyMeasurable (c.condExpGiven g Ој)` | `condExpGiven g` is strongly measurable w.r.t. the ambient Пғ-algebra | **Proved** |
| `POVar.integrable_condExpGiven` | `Integrable (c.condExpGiven g Ој) Ој` | `condExpGiven g` is integrable | **Proved** |
| `POVar.integrable_mul_indicator` | `Integrable f P.Ој вҶ’ Measurable f вҶ’ Integrable (fun Пү => f Пү * a.indicator x Пү) P.Ој` | Multiplication by a factual `{0,1}`-valued `POVar` indicator preserves integrability; removes repeated bounded-indicator proofs in ATE, DTR, and Manski arguments | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left` | `StronglyMeasurable[Пғ(c.factual)] f вҶ’ Integrable (f*g) Ој вҶ’ Integrable g Ој вҶ’ c.condExpGiven (f*g) Ој =бөҗ[Ој] f * c.condExpGiven g Ој` | Pull-out-left: a `Пғ(c.factual)`-measurable factor pulls outside `Ој[В·\|Пғ(c.factual)]` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_right` | Symmetric right-pull-out | Pull-out-right form of the same identity | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_indicator_mul` | `MeasurableSet[Пғ(c.factual)] s вҶ’ вҖҰ вҶ’ c.condExpGiven (1_s * g) Ој =бөҗ[Ој] 1_s * c.condExpGiven g Ој` | Specialisation of pull-out-left to indicators of `Пғ(c.factual)`-measurable sets | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_tower_of_le` | `m вүӨ Пғ(c.factual) вҶ’ [SigmaFinite (Ој.trim вҖҰ)] вҶ’ Ој[c.condExpGiven g Ој \| m] =бөҗ[Ој] Ој[g \| m]` | Tower property against an arbitrary smaller sub-Пғ-algebra `m вүӨ Пғ(c.factual)` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpGiven_tower` | Between two POVars with `Пғ(cвӮҒ.factual) вүӨ Пғ(cвӮӮ.factual)`: `cвӮҒ.condExpGiven (cвӮӮ.condExpGiven g Ој) Ој =бөҗ[Ој] cвӮҒ.condExpGiven g Ој` | Two-POVar tower property | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpRatio c g h Ој` | `POVar P Оі вҶ’ (P.О© вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (P.О© вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Measure P.О© вҶ’ (P.О© вҶ’ в„қ)` | Pointwise ratio `c.condExpGiven g Ој / c.condExpGiven h Ој` вҖ” the adjusted-regression functional used in backdoor ATE | **Defined** |
| `POVar.measurable_condExpRatio` | `Measurable (c.condExpRatio g h Ој)` | Pointwise-division measurability of `condExpRatio` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.stronglyMeasurable_condExpRatio` | `StronglyMeasurable (c.condExpRatio g h Ој)` | Strong measurability of `condExpRatio` | **Proved** |
| `POVar.condExpRatio_eq_of_mul` | `c.condExpGiven g Ој =бөҗ c.condExpGiven h Ој * target вҶ’ (вҲҖбөҗ Пү, c.condExpGiven h Ој Пү вү  0) вҶ’ c.condExpRatio g h Ој =бөҗ[Ој] target` | ProductвҶ’ratio characterisation; the exact shape consumed by `cate_backdoor` Step 4 | **Proved** |
| `POVar.integral_sub_eq_integral_sub_of_condExpGiven_ae_eq` | `Integrable f Ој`, `Integrable g Ој`, `c.condExpGiven f Ој =бөҗ f'`, `c.condExpGiven g Ој =бөҗ g'` вҶ’ `вҲ« (f - g) вҲӮОј = вҲ« (f' - g') вҲӮОј` | CATE-to-ATE integrator: lifts an a.e. identity of conditional expectations to an identity of unconditional integrals of differences; closes `ate_backdoor` in one line | **Proved** |

---

### `PO/Analysis/Quantile.lean` вҖ” Distribution, cdf, and quantile of a real potential outcome

Distributional reading of a real-valued potential outcome: for a real `POVar a`
and regime `r`, the counterfactual `a.cf r : P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` has a law, cdf, and
quantile. Gives meaning to "the `П„`-quantile of `Y(d)`"; input to the QTE
(`PO/ID/Exact/QTE/QuantileEffect.lean`). Builds on `Stat/Quantile/Quantile.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POVar.cfLaw a r Ој` | `Measure в„қ` | Law of the potential outcome `a(r)`: `Ој.map (a.cf r)`. | **Defined** |
| `POVar.instIsProbabilityMeasureCfLaw` | instance | `cfLaw` is a probability measure when `Ој` is. | **Proved** |
| `POVar.cfCDF a r Ој` | `StieltjesFunction в„қ` | Distributional potential outcome `F_{a(r)} = cdf (a.cfLaw r Ој)`. | **Defined** |
| `POVar.cfQuantile a r Ој П„` | `в„қ` | `П„`-quantile of `a(r)`: `Stat.quantile (a.cfLaw r Ој) П„`. | **Defined** |
| `POVar.cfCDF_eq_measureReal` | `a.cfCDF r Ој y = (a.cfLaw r Ој).real (Iic y)` | The cdf at `y` is `P(a(r) вүӨ y)`. | **Proved** |
| `POVar.cfUnderLaw a w y Ој` | `Measure в„қ` | Law of `a` under single intervention `{w вҶҗ y}` (the `Y(d)` shape). | **Defined** |
| `POVar.cfUnderQuantile a w y Ој П„` | `в„қ` | `П„`-quantile of `a` under `{w вҶҗ y}` вҖ” the `П„`-quantile of `Y(d)`. | **Defined** |
| `POVar.cfUnderQuantile_eq` | `= Stat.quantile (a.cfUnderLaw w y Ој) П„` | Unfolds `cfUnderQuantile` to the law-quantile. | `rfl` |

## 9. `SCM/ID/Identifiable.lean` вҖ” Identifiability

Namespace: `Causalean.SCM.ID`.

### Identifiability definitions

Identifiability is phrased in terms of **measure-theoretic observational
equivalence** against the SCM observational kernel: `obsEquiv MвӮҒ MвӮӮ` is
`HEq` of `Causalean.SCM.obsKernel MвӮҒ` and `Causalean.SCM.obsKernel MвӮӮ` (the `HEq`
is necessary because `obsKernel` has dependent domain/codomain via
`FixedValues M` and `ObservedValues M`).

| Definition | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `CausalQuery` | `SCM N О© вҶ’ Оұ` | Functional of a gSCM (ATE, LATE, interventional law, etc.) |
| `obsEquiv` | `SCM N О© вҶ’ SCM N О© вҶ’ Prop` | `HEq (SCM.obsKernel MвӮҒ) (SCM.obsKernel MвӮӮ)` |
| `Identifiable` | `SWIGGraph N вҶ’ CausalQuery N О© Оұ вҶ’ Prop` | Same SWIG graph (`M.toSWIGGraph = G`, pinning observed/latent/fixed + edges) + `obsEquiv` вҹ№ same query value. Faithful to `def:scm-identifiability` (model class shares a SWIG graph, not merely a DAG) |
| `NonIdentifiable` | `SWIGGraph N вҶ’ CausalQuery N О© Оұ вҶ’ Prop` | Negation of `Identifiable` |
| `IdentifiableUnder` | `SWIGGraph N вҶ’ Af вҶ’ As вҶ’ CausalQuery N О© Оұ вҶ’ Prop` | Identifiable given functional (`Af`) and structural (`As`) assumption predicates over `SCM N О©`, within the model class sharing SWIG graph `G` |

### Theorems

| Theorem | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `nonIdentifiable_iff` | Non-identifiability iff there exist two models with the same SWIG graph and `obsEquiv` but different query values | Equivalence characterization of non-identifiability |
| `identifiable_eq_identifiableUnder_true` | Identifiability without assumptions is the special case `Af = As = fun _ => True` | Links unconditional identifiability to `IdentifiableUnder` with trivial assumptions |
| `identifiableUnder_mono` | Stronger assumptions `(AfвӮӮ, AsвӮӮ)` that imply `(AfвӮҒ, AsвӮҒ)` preserve identifiability | Monotonicity: identifiability under weaker assumptions follows from identifiability under stronger ones |

---

## 9a. `SCM/ID/Overlap.lean` вҖ” Rule 2 / backdoor overlap predicate

The kernel-level absolute-continuity overlap predicate consumed by the
kernel-native do-calculus Rule 2 and the backdoor / frontdoor identification
theorems.  (The quantitative small-value/weak-overlap rate analysis вҖ” polynomial
lower-tail inverse moments вҖ” is developed separately under `Stat/PolynomialTail/`.)

| Definition | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Rule2JointOverlap M' Z hZ_obs hZ_fixed W hZrW s'` | `Prop` | **Canonical Rule 2 overlap.** Absolute continuity `((M'.fixSet Z).obsKernel s').map ПҖ_{ZrвҲӘW} вүӘ (M'.obsKernel вҲҳ fixSetProj s').map ПҖ_{ZrвҲӘW}` вҖ” a kernel-level AC predicate with no pointwise singleton positivity requirement. Holds trivially in the discrete case; holds in the continuous case whenever the SCM's structural functions are measurable and latents match. Hypothesis of `obsCondKernel_fixSet_eq_ae_witness` (canonical posterior witness-kernel Rule 2) and through it of `do_rule2_kernel` and backdoor / frontdoor identification. | Complete |

Reference: Basic Concepts.tex, Definition 2.12 (Overlap assumption).

---

## 9b. Structural Assumption Ownership

Causalean no longer carries a standalone IV/DID/RDD/proxy assumption catalogue.
Structural assumptions are owned by the theorem module that consumes them
(`PO/ID/Exact/*`, `PO/ID/Partial/*`, or downstream CausalSmith artifacts), while
`SCM/ID/Identifiable.lean` still provides generic `Prop`-valued assumption slots
for abstract identifiability statements.

---

## 9c. `SCM/ID/GraphicalThms/` вҖ” Graphical Identification Theorems

Formalizes Basic Concepts.tex В§4.1.  Several live files:

* `InducedSubgraph.lean` вҖ” graph-only utilities (ancestral closure,
  proper-descendant / non-descendant sets in `G_R`).
* `CComponentFactor.lean` вҖ” c-factor definition (`qFactor`),
  c-component parent helpers, and the per-node chain-rule factorization
  theorem `M.obsKernel s = M.qFactorProduct s` (Tian full-history
  convention).
* `QFactorIdentity.lean` вҖ” Tian's Q-factor identity (Prop 2.19),
  intervention-target simplification (`fact4`), and the district-id
  corollary.  The Q-factor identity is currently formalized as the
  real marginal fixing identity obtained from Rule 3 and
  `induce_marginal_compat`; the final conditional-`qFactor` reindexing
  remains future work.

The old kernel-era files (`Fixable.lean`, `FixingStep.lean`,
`CFactor.lean`) are **archived** under
`archive/SCM/ID/GraphicalThms/`.  The ShpitserвҖ“Pearl c-component
recursion (the chosen design path for the Session-5 ID algorithm) does
not consume the fixing-step machinery, so it is no longer in the build.

### `InducedSubgraph.lean`

| Definition | Description |
|---|---|
| `SWIGGraph.isAncestrallyClosed` | Every observed parent of `v вҲҲ G.observed` is itself in `G.observed`; `Decidable` |
| `InducedFrom G R` | `G.induce R` вҖ” the `G_R` notation from the tex |
| `SWIGGraph.properDescIn G R vвӮҖ` | `D := (G.induce R).dag.descendants vвӮҖ` вҖ” proper descendants of `vвӮҖ` in `G_R`; `DAG.descendants` is already irreflexive |
| `SWIGGraph.nonDescIn G R vвӮҖ` | `T := (R.erase vвӮҖ) \ (G.induce R).dag.descendants vвӮҖ` вҖ” non-descendants of `vвӮҖ` in `G_R`, matching the tex convention that `De(vвӮҖ)` includes `vвӮҖ` |
| `vвӮҖ_not_mem_properDescIn` | `vвӮҖ вҲү G.properDescIn R vвӮҖ` вҖ” irreflexivity |
| `vвӮҖ_not_mem_nonDescIn` | `vвӮҖ вҲү G.nonDescIn R vвӮҖ` вҖ” by the `erase` in the definition |
| `properDescIn_subset_erase` | `G.properDescIn R vвӮҖ вҠҶ R.erase vвӮҖ` вҖ” **sorry** (needs `descendants_induce_subset`) |
| `nonDescIn_subset_erase` | `G.nonDescIn R vвӮҖ вҠҶ R.erase vвӮҖ` вҖ” proven via `Finset.sdiff_subset` |
| `nonDescIn_subset` | `G.nonDescIn R vвӮҖ вҠҶ R` |
| `properDescIn_disjoint_nonDescIn` | `Disjoint (properDescIn R vвӮҖ) (nonDescIn R vвӮҖ)` |
| `properDescIn_union_nonDescIn_eq_erase` | `D вҲӘ T = R.erase vвӮҖ` (for `vвӮҖ вҲҲ R`) вҖ” **sorry** (needs the subset helper) |

### `CComponentFactor.lean` вҖ” c-component factorization (Tian, 2002)

Formalizes `thm:scm-c-factor` (Basic Concepts.tex:662вҖ“672) against
`obsKernel`.  The PaвҒә semantics is the standard Tian (2002) convention:
*topological predecessors*, i.e. observed nodes preceding `v` in a fixed
topological order вҖ” not the direct-parent set.

| Definition / Statement | Description |
|---|---|
| `SWIGGraph.observedPredecessors v` | `PaвҒә_G(v) := {w вҲҲ G.observed | topoOrder w < topoOrder v}` |
| `SWIGGraph.qFactorParents C` | `(вӢғ_{v вҲҲ C} PaвҒә_G(v)) \ C` вҖ” conditioning set of `Q[C]` |
| `observedPredecessors_subset_observed` / `qFactorParents_subset_observed` | sanity lemmas (proven) |
| `SCM.qFactor M C hC s` | Tian's c-component factor `Q[C]` as a slice of `obsCondKernel M C (qFactorParents C)` at `s` (via `Kernel.comap`); requires `StandardBorelSpace`, `Nonempty`, `IsFiniteMeasure`, `CountableOrCountablyGenerated` instances |
| `SCM.c_component_factorization` | `M.obsKernel s = M.qFactorProduct s`; per-node chain-rule factorization over observed nodes, now sorry-free via `obsKernel_eq_qFactorProduct`; c-component grouping is deferred to a later theorem |

### `QFactorIdentity.lean`

Hypothesis-quantified frames for Tian's identity, fact4, and the
district-id corollary.  The statements are non-vacuous: Tian's identity is the
conditional q-factor equality (a.e. under the induced parent marginal), the
marginal fixing identity is retained as a proved helper, and fact4 is an
`SCM.Equiv` statement.

| Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `QFactorMarginalFixingConclusion M R T Wn` | Under ancestral closure, `T вҠҶ (M.induce R).observed`, `Wn.image .random = R \ T`, and the Rule-3 non-descendant condition on `T`, the `T`-marginal of `(M.fixSet Wn).obsKernel` equals the `T`-marginal of `(M.induce R).obsKernel` at the projected fixed slice | **Proved** marginal fixing identity |
| `q_factor_marginal_fixing` | Proves the marginal fixing form by composing `condDistrib_intervention_ancestral_eq` with `induce_marginal_compat` and projection composition | **Proved** |
| `QFactorIdentityConclusion M R T Wn` | Conditional Tian identity: the induced `qFactor (M.induce R) T` equals a.e. the `T | qFactorParents T` conditional extracted from `(M.fixSet Wn).obsKernel`, with the non-descendant condition strengthened to `T вҲӘ qFactorParents T` for the needed joint identity | Real conditional statement |
| `q_factor_identity` | Proves the conditional Q-factor identity by first deriving the joint law identity on `T вҲӘ qFactorParents T`, reindexing it to the `(qFactorParents T, T)` pair law, then applying `Kernel.condKernel` uniqueness/disintegration under the common parent marginal | **Proved** |
| `InterventionTargetSimpConclusion M Dn Yn T` | `SCM.Equiv ((M.fixSet Dn).fixSet Yn) (M.fixSet (Dn вҲӘ Yn))` under observed/fixed/disjointness well-formedness hypotheses | Real `SCM.Equiv` statement |
| `intervention_target_simp` | Proves `fact4` by Finset induction over `Yn`, using the singleton-insert composition helper | Proven modulo `swigInterventionSet_insert_equiv` |
| `SCM.swigInterventionSet_insert_equiv` | Structural equivalence between intervening on `X` then fresh `{y}` and intervening on `insert y X` in one shot | **Proved** |
| `DistrictIdConclusion M T Wn` | Specialization of `QFactorIdentityConclusion` at `R := M.observed` | `:= QFactorIdentityConclusion M M.observed T Wn` |
| `district_id` | Specializes `q_factor_identity` at `R := M.observed` | Direct specialization of the conditional Q-factor identity; **proved** |

### `Density/ReferenceMeasure.lean` вҖ” dominated-model density foundation

Namespace `Causalean.SCM`. Foundation for the **density-assisted** c-component
factorization: Tian's assembly `P(v) = вҲҸ_C Q[C]` is a commutative regrouping of
scalar density factors with no kernel-composition analogue, so the
`doKernelY` identification (obligation 2) is routed through the joint density.
Unifies discrete (counting reference) and continuous (Lebesgue reference) models.

| Definition / Theorem | Signature / Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ReferenceMeasures О©` | structure: `Ој : вҲҖ v, Measure (swigО© О© v)` + per-node `SigmaFinite` | A Пғ-finite reference measure per SWIG-node value space (counting for discrete, Lebesgue for continuous) |
| `ReferenceFaithful ref` | `вҲҖ v x, ref.Ој v {x} вү  0` | Full-support condition for finite references; counting reference measures satisfy it |
| `jointRef ref I` | `Measure (ValuesOn I (swigО© О©))` | Finite product `Measure.pi` of the per-node references over a node set `I` |
| `DominatedObs M ref` | `вҲҖ s, M.obsKernel s вүӘ jointRef ref M.observed` | The gSCM's observational law is absolutely continuous w.r.t. the joint reference (admits a density) |
| `obsDensity M ref s` | `ValuesOn M.observed (swigО© О©) вҶ’ в„қвүҘ0вҲһ` | Joint observational density `(obsKernel s).rnDeriv (jointRef вҖҰ)` |
| `withDensity_obsDensity_eq` | `DominatedObs M ref вҶ’ (jointRef вҖҰ).withDensity (obsDensity вҖҰ) = obsKernel s` | Density determines the law (Proved) |
| `obsKernel_eq_of_obsDensity_ae_eq` | a.e.-equal `obsDensity` slices вҹ№ equal `obsKernel` slices (single model) | Density injectivity used downstream for the soundness transport (Proved) |

### `Density/FiniteReference.lean` вҖ” finite discrete reference infrastructure

Namespace `Causalean.SCM`. Instances and helpers for the finite/discrete density
path: when every base-node value space is finite and has measurable singletons,
the SWIG value spaces and finite product references inherit the corresponding
finite/measurable structure needed by the finite-reference RN chain rule.

| Definition / Theorem | Signature / Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `instFintypeSwigО©` / `instMeasurableSingletonClassSwigО©` | `[вҲҖ n, Fintype (О© n)]` / `[вҲҖ n, MeasurableSingletonClass (О© n)]` вҹ№ the same instances for `swigО© О© sn` | Transfers finite and singleton-measurable structure from base nodes to random/fixed SWIG nodes |
| `isFiniteMeasure_of_finite_measurableSingleton` | a Пғ-finite measure on a finite measurable-singleton type is finite | Generic finite-space measure helper, proved by covering the space with finitely many measurable singletons |
| `instIsFiniteMeasure_refMu` / `instIsFiniteMeasure_jointRef` | finite node spaces вҹ№ `IsFiniteMeasure (ref.Ој v)` and `IsFiniteMeasure (jointRef ref I)` | Supplies the finite fibre and finite product-reference instances used by discrete density proofs |
| `jointRef_singleton_ne_zero` / `absolutelyContinuous_jointRef_of_faithful` | `ReferenceFaithful ref` вҹ№ every product singleton has nonzero `jointRef` mass, hence every measure on the finite coordinate product is `вүӘ jointRef ref I` | Full-support infrastructure used to prove do-law ancestral marginal domination |
| `aemeasurable_fiber_rnDeriv_of_finite` | any raw fibre RN selector `(Оә p.1).rnDeriv ПҒ p.2` is a.e.-measurable on a finite measurable-singleton product | Discharges the explicit product a.e.-measurability hypothesis of the finite-reference composition-product RN lemma |

### `Density/PiUnion.lean` вҖ” `Measure.pi` over a disjoint union

Namespace `Causalean.SCM`. The reference-splitting foundation for the density chain
rule: `Measure.pi` over a disjoint union of coordinates factors as a product.

| Definition / Theorem | Signature / Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `unionSumEquiv hDisj` | `({a // a вҲҲ A} вҠ• {b // b вҲҲ B}) вүғ {i // i вҲҲ A вҲӘ B}` | Index equivalence for disjoint `A`, `B` |
| `measurePreserving_valuesUnionEquiv` | `MeasurePreserving (valuesUnionEquiv hDisj) (Measure.pi Ој_{AвҲӘB}) ((Measure.pi Ој_A).prod (Measure.pi Ој_B))` | **`Measure.pi` over a disjoint union = the product measure.** Fully proved, axiom-clean; routes the project's union equiv through Mathlib `sumPiEquivProdPi` + `piCongrLeft` |

### `Density/ChainRuleDensity.lean` вҖ” observational chain-rule density

Namespace `Causalean.SCM`. Density analogue of the proven kernel chain rule:
the observational density is reduced to the scalar product of the
Radon--Nikodym derivatives of the one-node conditional kernels
`obsStepCondKernel`, evaluated along the observed topological order.

| Definition / Theorem | Signature / Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `obsStepCondDensity M ref s i` | `ValuesOn M.observed (swigО© О©) вҶ’ в„қвүҘ0вҲһ` | One observed-coordinate conditional density: the RN derivative of `obsStepCondKernel i.isLt` against the node reference measure, evaluated at the prefix and coordinate read from a full observed assignment |
| `qFactorDensityProduct M ref s` | `ValuesOn M.observed (swigО© О©) вҶ’ в„қвүҘ0вҲһ` | Product over `Fin M.observed.card` of the per-node conditional density factors in observed topological order |
| `qFactorProduct_rnDeriv_eq_qFactorDensityProduct` | with finite measurable-singleton node spaces, `DominatedObs M ref вҶ’ (M.qFactorProduct s).rnDeriv (jointRef ref M.observed) =бөҗ вҖҰ qFactorDensityProduct вҖҰ` | Analytic RN-derivative chain rule. **The `DominatedObs` hypothesis is essential** вҖ” false without it (singular part вҹ№ vanishing rnDeriv vs nonzero conditional-density product). Fully proved in the finite-reference setting |
| `obsChainKernel_rnDeriv_eq_prefixDensityProduct` | with finite measurable-singleton node spaces, general-`k` prefix induction: `(obsChainKernel k s).rnDeriv (jointRef prefix k) =бөҗ prefixDensityProduct k` | **Proved.** Base on `ValuesOn вҲ…`; successor peels `extendObsPrefix` via `MeasurableEmbedding.rnDeriv_map`, splits via `jointRef_extendObsPrefix`, derives joint product domination from base and fibre domination, and uses finite-space measurability for the raw fibre derivative |
| `obsChainKernel_absolutelyContinuous_jointRef_prefix` | `M.obsChainKernel k hk s вүӘ jointRef ref (M.prefixNodes k)` | **Proved (no Mathlib gap).** Chain = prefix marginal of the dominated `obsKernel`; the reference marginal is a scalar multiple of the prefix reference via the disjoint-union split (`measurePreserving_valuesUnionEquiv`) + `Measure.map_fst_prod` |
| `rnDeriv_compProd_prod_sigmaFinite` | `(Ој вҠ—вӮҳ Оә).rnDeriv (ОҪ.prod ПҒ) =бөҗ fun p => f p.1 * (Оә p.1).rnDeriv ПҒ p.2`, for finite `Ој`/`ПҒ`/`Оә`, Пғ-finite `ОҪ`, `Ој вүӘ ОҪ`, **joint domination** `Ој вҠ—вӮҳ Оә вүӘ ОҪ.prod ПҒ`, product a.e.-measurability of the raw fibre derivative, and `Ој.rnDeriv ОҪ =бөҗ f` | Finite-reference composition-product RN identity in `Causalean/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/RnDerivCompProdSigmaFinite.lean`. Finiteness of `ПҒ` makes `Kernel.const _ ПҒ` a finite kernel, so Mathlib extracts Ој-a.e. fibre domination from joint domination. The raw `Measure.rnDeriv` fibre representative still carries an explicit product-measurability hypothesis; Mathlib's measurable representative is `Kernel.rnDeriv Оә (Kernel.const _ ПҒ)` |
| `map_pi_valuesEquivOfEq`, `jointRef_prefix_card_map`, `singletonValues_map_ref_eq_jointRef`, `jointRef_extendObsPrefix`, `rnDeriv_compProd_same_left`, `absolutelyContinuous_of_map_measurableEmbedding`, `qFactorProduct_rnDeriv_eq_obsChainKernel_card_pullback_of_jointRef`, `obsChainKernel_card_rnDeriv_pullback_eq_qFactorDensityProduct_of_prefix` | reference reindex / singleton split / per-step `extendObsPrefix` reference split / embedding-pullback of `вүӘ` / `rnDeriv_map` peels / a.e. transport | The transport + reference-split sub-lemmas of the chain rule вҖ” **all proved, axiom-clean** |
| `obsDensity_eq_qFactorDensityProduct` | with finite measurable-singleton node spaces, `M.obsDensity ref s =бөҗ[jointRef ref M.observed] M.qFactorDensityProduct ref s` | Dominated observational density factors as the product of one-node conditional densities; D1 is now sorry-free in the finite-reference setting |

### `Density/CComponentDensity.lean` вҖ” c-component regrouping of the density

Namespace `Causalean.SCM`. The commutative regrouping with **no kernel analogue**:
the scalar density product is collected by c-component. (Composed kernels can't
be permuted to gather a non-contiguous c-component's factors; scalar densities
can.)

| Definition / Theorem | Signature / Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cComponentDensityFactor M ref s C` | `ValuesOn M.observed (swigО© О©) вҶ’ в„қвүҘ0вҲһ` | Product of the one-node conditional density factors over the observed indices whose node lies in c-component `C` (density analogue of Tian's `Q[C]`) |
| `qFactorDensityProduct_eq_prod_cComponentFactor` | `M.qFactorDensityProduct ref s x = вҲҸ C вҲҲ cComponentSet, cComponentDensityFactor вҖҰ C x` | The chain-rule density product regroups as a product over c-components. **Fully proved, axiom-clean** (pure `Finset.prod_fiberwise` regrouping; the kernel-impossible step) |

### Archived (under `archive/SCM/ID/GraphicalThms/`)

* `Fixable.lean` вҖ” `SWIGGraph.isFixable`, `isFixableSeq` (kernel-era; no
  longer used by the chosen ShpitserвҖ“Pearl recursion).
* `FixingStep.lean` вҖ” Lemma 1 skeleton.
* `CFactor.lean` вҖ” kernel-era c-factor (replaced by `CComponentFactor.lean`).

---

## 10. `SCM/Examples/IV.lean` вҖ” IV DAG Example

Demonstrates the full API on the standard instrumental variable DAG:

```
    U
   вҶҷ вҶҳ
  Z вҶ’ D вҶ’ Y
```

**What it tests:**

| Module | Tests |
|---|---|
| `Graph/DAG.lean` | `parents`, `children`, `isRoot`, `isAncestor`, `isAncestor_irrefl/trans` |
| `Graph/DSep.lean` | `dSep` with various conditioning sets; collider bias; instrument independence |
| `Graph/SWIG.lean` | `SWIGGraph` construction; initial SWIG; SWIG edge tests |
| `Graph/CComponents.lean` | `directlyConfounded` on `SWIGGraph`; `cComponentOf` |
| `SCM/Model/EdgeType.lean` | `EdgeTypeAssignment`; monotonic vs nonparametric; `refines` |
| `SCM/Model/SCM.lean` | Trivial `Causalean.SCM` instance `ivSCM` with `О©IV := fun _ => Unit`; `structFun` constant; `latentDist := Measure.dirac ()`; `iota_valueSpace` discharged via `Finset.notMem_empty`. Verifies `ivSCM.isStandard` |

---

## 10b. `SCM/Examples/BackDoor.lean` вҖ” Backdoor Adjustment Example

Demonstrates the backdoor criterion DAG:

```
    Z
   вҶҷ вҶҳ
  D вҶ’ Y
```

Observed: D (treatment), Y (outcome), Z (observed confounder). Latent roots UвӮҒ, UвӮӮ, UвӮғ added to satisfy `unobs_are_roots`.

**What it tests:**

| Module | Tests |
|---|---|
| `Graph/DAG.lean` | `parents`, `isRoot`, `isAncestor` (irreflexivity via `isAncestor_topoOrder_lt`) |
| `Graph/DSep.lean` | `dSep` for the observational graph; no d-separation by {Z} (direct edge D вҶ’ Y remains open) |
| `Graph/SWIG.lean` | `SWIGGraph` construction with three latent roots; `swig_random_root_of_root` for each |
| `Graph/SWIGSplitMono.lean` | Backdoor condition (ii): `splitMonoDAG {bdD}` d-separates Y from D given Z, verified by `native_decide` on the computable `splitMonoDAG` |
| `Graph/CComponents.lean` | Each observed node is its own singleton C-component (no confounding) |
| `SCM/Model/EdgeType.lean` | `allNonparametric` edge type assignment |

The file now additionally demonstrates the full `bdSWIG.backdoorCriterion` on this concrete 3-node DAG.  Condition (i) (no `z вҲҲ Z` is a descendant of any `random D вҲҲ X`) is discharged by `native_decide` + `simp`.  Condition (ii) is routed through `splitMonoDAG` (since `splitMono` itself is `noncomputable`) and also closed by `native_decide`.  No full SCM is built вҖ” only the graphical criterion is exercised.

---

## 10c. `SCM/Examples/ContinuousBackdoor.lean` вҖ” Linear-Gaussian backdoor (continuous treatment)

Validation of the posterior witness-kernel backdoor pipeline: a 3-node SCM with continuous value spaces (`в„қ`) on every observed node, exercising `backdoor_completeness_ae` end-to-end on a treatment variable that is *not* discrete.

DAG: `Z вҶ’ X`, `Z вҶ’ Y`, `X вҶ’ Y` (so `Z` is a backdoor-admissible confounder for the effect of `X` on `Y`).  All three observed nodes have value space `в„қ` (via `CBО© : Fin 3 вҶ’ Type := fun _ => в„қ`).  The latent set is empty, so the SCM is fully deterministic вҖ” the kernel-native pipeline does not need probabilistic latents to demonstrate the continuous-treatment regime; what matters is the continuity of the value spaces.

The example uses a *computable* SWIGGraph (`cbSWIGGraph`) so the backdoor criterion can be discharged via `native_decide` even though the parent `SCM` is `noncomputable` (carries `Measure`s).  `continuousBackdoorSCM.toSWIGGraph := cbSWIGGraph` definitionally.

| Declaration | Description |
|---|---|
| `CBNode`, `CBО©` | Node set `Fin 3` (`Zidx = 0`, `Xidx = 1`, `Yidx = 2`) with continuous value spaces `в„қ` |
| `cbEdgeBool`, `cbEdge`, `cbDAG` | Edge relation (`Bool`-lifted for `decide`-friendly `Decidable`) and underlying DAG |
| `cbSWIGGraph` | Computable SWIGGraph (empty latents, `observed = {Z, X, Y}` as `.random` nodes), used for graphical criterion proofs |
| `continuousBackdoorSCM` | The full SCM: extends `cbSWIGGraph`, constant-`0` structural functions, empty `latentDist`.  Marked `noncomputable` only because of the `Measure` API in the SCM structure |
| `cb_Xrand_obs`, `cb_Xfixed` | Witnesses that `random X` is observed and `fixed X` is not in `fixed`; supplied to `backdoor_completeness_ae`'s subset-hypotheses |
| `cb_backdoor_criterion` | Discharges `cbSWIGGraph.backdoorCriterion {X} _ _ {Y} {Z}`.  Condition (i) (`Z` not descendant of `X`) by `native_decide`; condition (ii) (d-separation in splitMono) by `native_decide` on the computable `splitMonoDAG` |

**What this validates.**  Continuous value spaces (`в„қ`) flow through the kernel-native pipeline (`obsKernel`, `obsCondKernel`, `Rule2JointOverlap`, `backdoor_completeness_ae`), and `SWIGGraph.backdoorCriterion` discharges by `native_decide` on the continuous SCM.  This example is sorry-free.

---

## 9d. `SCM/ID/Adjustment.lean` вҖ” Adjustment Functionals

Graph-level adjustment functionals that turn an SCM's observational
kernel into a post-intervention `Y`-marginal kernel under the backdoor
or frontdoor criterion.  The split into a *single-SCM completeness*
lemma plus a *cross-SCM congruence* lemma factors the cross-SCM HEq
plumbing out of the do-calculus assembly in `SCM/ID/Backdoor.lean` /
`PO/ID/Exact/Frontdoor.lean`.

| Definition / Statement | Description |
|---|---|
| `SCM.backdoorAdjustment M X hX_obs hX_fixed Y Z hY hZ` | Kernel `Kernel (M.fixSet X).FixedValues (ValuesOn Y _)` implementing `вҲ«_z P(Y \| X, Z=z) dP(z)` as a kernel-native `((zMarginalPost вҠ—вӮ– M.obsCondKernel Y (X.image .random вҲӘ Z)).comap ...).map Prod.snd` chain.  The conditional's `(X.image .random вҲӘ Z)`-argument is assembled via `M.fillZrW X hX_obs hX_fixed Z s_post z`, which fills the `X.image .random` slice from `s_post` (reindexed from `X.image .fixed` through `zFixedAsRandom`) and pairs it with the outer `Z`-value `z` вҖ” routing through `fillZrW` (rather than an inline `valuesUnionMk вҲҳ zFixedAsRandom вҲҳ valuesProjection`) removes a reindexing-bridge gap in the downstream backdoor completeness proof.  Signature carries `[StandardBorelSpace (ValuesOn Y (swigО© О©))]`, `[Nonempty (ValuesOn Y (swigО© О©))]`, `[вҲҖ s : M.FixedValues, IsFiniteMeasure (M.obsKernel s)]`, and a `CountableOrCountablyGenerated` instance for the conditional kernel |
| `SCM.frontdoorAdjustment M X hX_obs hX_fixed Y Z hY hZ` | **Proved** (sorry-free).  Kernel `Kernel (M.fixSet X).FixedValues (ValuesOn Y _)` implementing the frontdoor integral as a chain of `вҠ—вӮ–` legs: outer X-marginal `(M.obsKernel.map projXr).comap fixSetProj`, middle `M.obsCondKernel Z (X.image .random)` (Rule 2 leg, `P(Z|X)`), inner `M.obsCondKernel Y (X.image .random вҲӘ Z)` (Rule 2 leg on inner `do(Z)`, with `X` as adjustment set).  The three kernels compose to `((xMarginal вҠ—вӮ– zCondX) вҠ—вӮ– yCondXZ).map Prod.snd`.  Signature requires `[StandardBorelSpace (ValuesOn Y _)]`, `[StandardBorelSpace (ValuesOn Z _)]`, `[Nonempty (ValuesOn Y _)]`, `[Nonempty (ValuesOn Z _)]`, `[StandardBorelSpace (ValuesOn (X.image .random) _)]`, `[StandardBorelSpace (ValuesOn (X.image .random вҲӘ Z) _)]`, `[вҲҖ s, IsFiniteMeasure (M.obsKernel s)]`, and two `CountableOrCountablyGenerated` instances for the conditional kernels |
| `SCM.backdoorAdjustment_invariant` | **Proved** (sorry closed).  HEq cross-SCM invariance: two SCMs sharing `toSWIGGraph` and `obsKernel` produce the same `backdoorAdjustment` kernel.  Proof destructs both SCMs via `obtain`, `cases h_swig` unifies the SWIGGraph fields definitionally, then `apply heq_of_eq` reduces HEq to Eq. `eq_of_heq _h_obs` strips the obsKernel HEq; `unfold SCM.backdoorAdjustment` + nested `congr 1` peels off `.map Prod.snd` and `вҠ—вӮ–`, leaving the `zMarginalPost` branch (closed by `rw [h_ok]`) and the `condPost` branch (closed by unfolding `obsCondKernel`/`obsCondPairKernel` and `rw [h_ok]`). Does not require `heq_obsCondKernel` вҖ” `cases h_swig` already aligns the kernel arguments |
| `SCM.frontdoorAdjustment_invariant` | **Proved** (sorry-free).  Same shape as `backdoorAdjustment_invariant`.  After destructure + `cases h_swig`, `unfold SCM.frontdoorAdjustment` exposes the `вҠ—вӮ–` chain; unfolding `obsCondKernel`/`obsCondPairKernel` exposes the `condKernel`-of-`obsKernel.map`-joint pattern; `simp_rw [h_ok]` rewrites every `obsKernel` mention to the MвӮӮ-side, then `rfl` closes the remaining proof-irrelevance noise on `fixSetProj` (only SWIGGraph fields are used in `fixSetProj`; all unified after `cases h_swig`) |

---

## 9e. `SCM/ID/BackdoorCriterion.lean` вҖ” Backdoor criterion + Rule-3 leg

The graphical backdoor criterion (Basic Concepts.tex:636вҖ“645, Pearl 2009
Theorem 3.3.2) and the single regime-independent do-calculus leg the backdoor
identification proof consumes.  The identification *theorems* live in
`SCM/ID/Backdoor.lean` (В§9e').

**Design note on condition (ii).**  `Graph/Mutilation.lean` is not yet
implemented, so the lower-bar mutilation `G_{XМІ}` is encoded via
`SWIGGraph.splitMono X hX_obs hX_fix`: splitting `X` reroutes outgoing
edges of `random D` (for `D вҲҲ X`) to root nodes `fixed D`, which is
exactly the effect of lower-bar mutilation for d-separation purposes.

| Declaration | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `SWIGGraph.backdoorCriterion G X hX_obs hX_fix Y Z` | Two-condition criterion: (i) no `z вҲҲ Z` is a descendant of any `random D` with `D вҲҲ X` in `G`; (ii) `Z вҲӘ X.image .fixed` d-separates `Y` from `X.image .random` in `G.splitMono X hX_obs hX_fix` (encodes `G_{XМІ}` d-sep; fixed_X conditioning is vacuous in splitMono but carried to match `do_rule2`'s shape) | Defined; type-checks without sorry |
| `SCM.backdoor_rule3_Z_marginal` | **Rule-3 leg**: under criterion (i), `((M.fixSet X).obsKernel s_post).map proj_Z = (M.obsKernel (fixSetProj s_post)).map proj_Z`. Applies `do_rule3` with `Y_param := вҲ…`, `W_param := Z` and bridges `hNoDesc` via `fixSet_isAncestor_fixed_forward`. Consumed by `backdoor_completeness_ae`. | **Proved** |

---

## 9e'. `SCM/ID/Backdoor.lean` вҖ” Backdoor Identification (continuous, a.e.)

The sound **continuous-treatment** backdoor results, stated a.e. in the
treatment value (so they never read `obsCondKernel`/`backdoorAdjustment` on a
`{X = x}` null slice).  Built on the posterior witness-kernel Rule 2
(`condDistrib_fixSet_cross_SCM_bridge`).  Headline theorems
`backdoor_completeness_ae` and `backdoor_identifiable_ae`.

| Declaration | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `SCM.treatmentMarginal M X hXr s0` | The observational treatment marginal `ОҪX = (M.obsKernel s0).map ПҖ_{X.random}` вҖ” the supported measure the a.e. statements are anchored at | Defined |
| `SCM.BackdoorPositivityAE M X Z вҖҰ s0` | Product positivity / overlap `Prop`: `ОҪX вҠ— ОјZ вүӘ Ој_{X.rand вҲӘ Z}` (the standard continuous-treatment positivity; vacuous-free, covers both atomic and non-atomic regimes) | Defined |
| `SCM.doKernelY M X вҖҰ Y hY s0` | Post-intervention `Y`-marginal as a kernel in the treatment value `t`: `t вҶҰ ((M.fixSet X).obsKernel (extend s0 t)).map ПҖ_Y`. Type `Kernel (ValuesOn (X.image .random)) (ValuesOn Y)` вҖ” independent of `M`'s fixed values | Defined |
| `SCM.adjustmentKernelY M X вҖҰ Y Z hY hZ s0` | Backdoor-adjustment `Y`-marginal as a kernel in the treatment value: `(M.backdoorAdjustment вҖҰ).comap (fixSetExtend s0)` | Defined |
| `SCM.backdoor_completeness_ae_compProd M X вҖҰ s0 hOverlap hPositivity` | `ОҪX вҠ—вӮҳ doKernelY = ОҪX вҠ—вӮҳ adjustmentKernelY` at base `s0`, under backdoor criterion + joint overlap + `BackdoorPositivityAE`. The compProd (joint) primary form вҖ” well-posed for continuous treatment (never reads `obsCondKernel` on a null slice) | **Proved** (sorry-free) |
| `SCM.backdoor_completeness_ae M X вҖҰ s0 hOverlap hPositivity` | **Continuous backdoor completeness (a.e.).** For `ОҪX`-a.e. treatment value `t`, `doKernelY вҖҰ t = adjustmentKernelY вҖҰ t`. Derived from the compProd form via `Kernel.ae_eq_of_compProd_eq`. **Headline.** | **Proved** (sorry-free) |
| `SCM.backdoor_identifiable_ae MвӮҒ MвӮӮ h_swig X Y Z вҖҰ h_obs h_s0` | **Continuous backdoor identifiability (a.e., cross-SCM).** Two SCMs sharing the SWIG graph (`h_swig`) and observational kernel (`h_obs : HEq вҖҰ`) with aligned base points (`h_s0 : HEq s0вӮҒ s0вӮӮ`) produce `doKernelY MвӮҒ s0вӮҒ =бөҗ[treatmentMarginal MвӮҒ s0вӮҒ] doKernelY MвӮӮ s0вӮӮ`. A plain `=бөҗ` (not HEq) since `doKernelY` is `M`-fixed-value-independent. Proof: `backdoor_completeness_ae` on each side + cross-SCM `adjustmentKernelY` invariance (`backdoorAdjustment_invariant` after destructure + `cases h_swig`/`h_s0`). **Headline.** | **Proved** (sorry-free) |

---

## 9f. `PO/ID/Exact/LATE.lean` вҖ” IV/LATE Identification Theorem

Formalizes the Wald identification of LATE in the PO framework (Basic Concepts.tex: def:po-iv-system, def:po-iv-assumptions, def:po-late, prop:po-late, rem:po-late).

**Implementation note (2026-04-23 refactor).** The file now consumes the PO
abstraction layer introduced in `PO/`: counterfactual maps are built
as `POVar` (`zVar`, `dVar`, `yVar`), single-node interventions as
`RegimedVar.ofSingle` / `RegimedVar` (`dUnderZ`, `yUnderD`), joint bundles as
`POCFBundle` (`cfBundle`), and the IV independence premise as `P.IndepCF`.
A subsequent pass (2026-04-23) further collapsed the two `hCE` blocks in
`first_stage_identity` / `reduced_form_identity` via
`POSystem.eventCondExp_of_consistency_IndepCF`, deleting the product-form
`cfTuple` / `measurable_cfTuple` / `indepFun_factualZ_cfTuple` /
`integral_zEvent_of_cfTuple` scaffolding in favour of direct
`POCFBundle.jointValue` usage.
Net reduction: 429 вҶ’ 388 lines.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POIVSystem P` | `structure { Z, D, Y, hZbool, hDbool, hYreal, distinct }` | IV subsystem over a PO system | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.zVar`, `dVar`, `yVar` | `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P в„қ` | `POVar` wrappers around `Z`, `D`, `Y` using the `hZbool`/`hDbool`/`hYreal` equivalences; abstract away the hand-rolled measurability chains | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.instrumentRegime S z` | `Regime P.V P.X` | Regime `r_z := ({Z}, z)` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.treatmentRegime S d` | `Regime P.V P.X` | Regime `r_d := ({D}, d)` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.DofZ S z`, `YofD S d` | `P.О© вҶ’ Bool` / `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Counterfactual treatment `D(z)` and outcome `Y(d)` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.factualZ`, `factualD`, `factualY` | `P.О© вҶ’ Bool` / `Bool` / `в„қ` | Factual instrument / treatment / outcome | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.complierEvent` | `Set P.О©` | `{Пү | D(1)(Пү)=1 вҲ§ D(0)(Пү)=0}` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.zEvent S z` | `Set P.О©` | `{Пү | Z(Пү) = z}`, defined as `S.zVar.event z` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.measurable_{DofZ,factualZ,factualD,factualY,YofD}` | `Measurable вҖҰ` | Measurability of the counterfactual/factual variables (now one-line delegations to `POVar.measurable_cf` / `POVar.measurable_factual`) | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.measurableSet_complierEvent` / `measurableSet_zEvent` | `MeasurableSet вҖҰ` | Measurability of the two event sets | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.dUnderZ S z` | `RegimedVar P Bool` | `вҹЁS.dVar, Regime.single S.Z (S.hZbool.symm z)вҹ©` вҖ” regimed form of `D(z)` used to populate `cfBundle` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.yUnderD S d` | `RegimedVar P в„қ` | `вҹЁS.yVar, Regime.single S.D (S.hDbool.symm d)вҹ©` вҖ” regimed form of `Y(d)` used to populate `cfBundle` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.cfBundle` | `POCFBundle P` | The bundle `(D(1), D(0), Y(1), Y(0))` built via iterated `POCFBundle.cons`; target of the IV independence hypothesis | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.YofDofZ S z` | `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | `Y(D(z)) := 1_{D(z)=1} Y(1) + 1_{D(z)=0} Y(0)` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.condExpDZ S z`, `condExpYZ S z` | `в„қ` | Event-level `E[D | Z=z]`, `E[Y | Z=z]` | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, instrumentIndep, monotonicity, relevance }` where `instrumentIndep : P.IndepCF (RegimedVar.ofFactual S.zVar) S.cfBundle P.Ој` | def:po-iv-assumptions; the instrument-independence field is now phrased against the bundle-based `IndepCF` shape | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.LATE` | `в„қ` | `(вҲ«_C (Y(1)-Y(0)) вҲӮОј) / (Ој C).toReal` вҖ” def:po-late | **Defined** |
| `POIVSystem.DofZ_eq_factualD_on_zEvent` | `Пү вҲҲ zEvent z вҶ’ DofZ z Пү = factualD Пү` | Pointwise D-consistency on `{Z = z}` | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.factualY_eq_YofD_factualD` | `factualY Пү = YofD (factualD Пү) Пү` | Pointwise Y-consistency everywhere | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.first_stage_identity` | `condExpDZ 1 - condExpDZ 0 = (Ој C).toReal` | Step 1 of rem:po-late | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.reduced_form_identity` | `condExpYZ 1 - condExpYZ 0 = вҲ« (YofDofZ 1 - YofDofZ 0) вҲӮОј` | Step 2 of rem:po-late | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.pointwise_monotonicity` | `вҲҖбөҗ Пү, Y(D(1))-Y(D(0)) = (Y(1)-Y(0))В·1_C` | Step 3 of rem:po-late | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.event_conditioning_identity` | `вҲ« (Y(1)-Y(0))В·1_C вҲӮОј = (Ој C).toReal В· LATE` | Step 4 of rem:po-late | **Proved** |
| `POIVSystem.late_wald` | `(condExpYZ 1 - condExpYZ 0) / (condExpDZ 1 - condExpDZ 0) = LATE` | Wald identification of LATE, prop:po-late | **Proved** |

Drop-of-conditioning and consistency-on-event rewrites are delegated to
`POSystem.eventCondExp_of_consistency_IndepCF` from `PO/Conditioning/EventCondExp.lean`;
no private helper remains in this file.

---

## 9f'. `PO/ID/Exact/HeckmanRoy/{Setup,Wald}.lean` вҖ” HeckmanвҖ“Vytlacil IV / Generalized Roy Selection Model (PO framework)

Formalises the HeckmanвҖ“Vytlacil IV / generalized Roy selection model from `Basic Concepts.tex` (def:po-iv-heckman-roy-system, def:po-iv-heckman-roy-assumptions, def:po-iv-heckman-roy-late, prop:po-iv-heckman-roy-wald, rem:po-iv-heckman-roy-lean). Generalises `LATE.lean` to:

* an arbitrary measurable instrument value space `Оұ` (with `MeasurableSingletonClass`), instead of `Bool` вҖ” same parametrization pattern as `Manski/Setup.lean`;
* a latent uniform rank `U : О© вҶ’ в„қ` (`U ~ Unif[0,1]`) plus a propensity map `p : Оұ вҶ’ в„қ`, replacing the binary `D` potentials and monotonicity by threshold crossing `D(z) = 1_{U вүӨ p(z)}`;
* a **pairwise** Wald identity: for any `zвӮҖ, zвӮҒ : Оұ` with `p zвӮҖ < p zвӮҒ` and positive measure on `{Z = zвӮҖ}, {Z = zвӮҒ}`,
  `(E[Y|Z=zвӮҒ] вҲ’ E[Y|Z=zвӮҖ]) / (E[D|Z=zвӮҒ] вҲ’ E[D|Z=zвӮҖ]) = LATE(zвӮҖ, zвӮҒ)`.

**Out of scope (follow-ups):** the marginal treatment effect `MTE` (def:po-iv-heckman-roy-mte) and the continuous-Z / regression-representative formulation (rem:po-iv-heckman-roy-local-iv); both need the `IsRegressionFunction` infrastructure of `PO/Analysis/Regression.lean` to extend to non-singleton instruments.

### `HeckmanRoy/Setup.lean` вҖ” data layer

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POHeckmanRoySystem P Оұ` | `structure { Z, D, Y, U, hZ, hDbool, hYreal, hUreal, distinctness pairs, p, hp_mem }` | HeckmanвҖ“Roy IV subsystem over `P`, parametrised by instrument value space `Оұ` with `MeasurableSingletonClass` | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.{zVar, dVar, yVar, uVar}` | `POVar P Оұ / Bool / в„қ / в„қ` | `POVar` wrappers around `Z, D, Y, U` | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.instrumentRegime z` | `Regime P.V P.X` | Single-node regime `{Z вҶҗ z}` | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.treatmentRegime d` | `Regime P.V P.X` | Single-node regime `{D вҶҗ d}` | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.DofZ z` | `P.О© вҶ’ Bool` | Counterfactual treatment `D(z)` | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.YofD d` | `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Counterfactual outcome `Y(d)` | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.factualZ / D / Y / U` | `P.О© вҶ’ Оұ / Bool / в„қ / в„қ` | Factual realisations | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.zEvent z` | `Set P.О©` | `{Z = z}` as a `POVar.event` | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.intervalComplierEvent zвӮҖ zвӮҒ` | `Set P.О©` | `{Пү | p zвӮҖ < U Пү вүӨ p zвӮҒ}` (def:po-iv-heckman-roy-late) | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.measurable_*` | `Measurable вҖҰ` / `MeasurableSet вҖҰ` | One-line delegations to `POVar.*` for `DofZ, YofD, factualZ, factualD, factualY, factualU, YofDofZ` and the events `zEvent`, `intervalComplierEvent` | **Proved** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.YofDofZ z` | `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | `Y` composed with `D(z)`: `if D(z) Пү then Y(1) Пү else Y(0) Пү` | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.condExpDZ z`, `condExpYZ z` | `в„қ` | Event-level `E[D | Z=z]`, `E[Y | Z=z]` | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.yUnderD d` | `RegimedVar P в„қ` | Regimed outcome under `{D вҶҗ d}`, used for the bundle | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.cfBundle` | `POCFBundle P` | The bundle `(U, Y(1), Y(0))`; target of the instrument-independence assumption.  `D(z)` is omitted вҖ” it is determined by `U` via threshold crossing | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, instrumentIndep, thresholdCrossing, uniformU }` | def:po-iv-heckman-roy-assumptions; `instrumentIndep : P.IndepCF (RegimedVar.ofFactual S.zVar) S.cfBundle P.Ој`, `thresholdCrossing : вҲҖ z, вҲҖбөҗ Пү, (D(z) Пү = true вҶ” U Пү вүӨ p z)`, `uniformU : вҲҖ q вҲҲ [0,1], Ој {U вүӨ q} = ENNReal.ofReal q` | **Defined** |
| `POHeckmanRoySystem.LATE zвӮҖ zвӮҒ` | `в„қ` | Latent interval ATE: `(вҲ«_C (Y(1)-Y(0)) вҲӮОј) / (Ој C).toReal` (def:po-iv-heckman-roy-late) | **Defined** |

### `HeckmanRoy/Wald.lean` вҖ” proof layer

Mirrors the five-step decomposition of rem:po-iv-heckman-roy-lean.  All `theorem`/`lemma` proofs below are filled (Codex-assisted).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `DofZ_eq_factualD_on_zEvent` | `Пү вҲҲ zEvent z вҶ’ DofZ z Пү = factualD Пү` | Step 1: pointwise D-consistency on `{Z = z}`; one-line via `POVar.cf_eq_factual_on_event` | **Proved** |
| `factualY_eq_YofD_factualD` | `factualY Пү = YofD (factualD Пү) Пү` | Step 1: pointwise Y-consistency; one-line via `POVar.factual_eq_cfUnder_self_selected` | **Proved** |
| `interval_indicator_sub` | `1_{U вүӨ p zвӮҒ} Пү - 1_{U вүӨ p zвӮҖ} Пү = (intervalComplierEvent zвӮҖ zвӮҒ).indicator 1 Пү` (for `p zвӮҖ вүӨ p zвӮҒ`) | Step 5 (algebra): pointwise interval-subtraction identity | **Proved** |
| `complier_measure` | `(Ој (intervalComplierEvent zвӮҖ zвӮҒ)).toReal = p zвӮҒ - p zвӮҖ` (for `p zвӮҖ < p zвӮҒ`) | Step 4: uniform-threshold lemma applied at `p zвӮҖ, p zвӮҒ` plus measure subtraction | **Proved** |
| `first_stage_identity` | `condExpDZ zвӮҒ - condExpDZ zвӮҖ = p zвӮҒ - p zвӮҖ` | Step 1+3 (analogue of LATE `first_stage_identity`); uses `POSystem.eventCondExp_of_consistency_IndepCF` | **Proved** |
| `reduced_form_identity` | `condExpYZ zвӮҒ - condExpYZ zвӮҖ = вҲ« (YofDofZ zвӮҒ - YofDofZ zвӮҖ) вҲӮОј` | Step 1+3 (analogue of LATE `reduced_form_identity`); same drop-of-conditioning lemma | **Proved** |
| `pointwise_threshold_identity` | `вҲҖбөҗ Пү, YofDofZ zвӮҒ Пү - YofDofZ zвӮҖ Пү = (Y(1)-Y(0))(Пү) В· 1_{intervalComplierEvent zвӮҖ zвӮҒ}(Пү)` | Step 2: replaces LATE `pointwise_monotonicity`; case-split on `U Пү вүӨ p zвұј` using `thresholdCrossing` | **Proved** |
| `event_conditioning_identity` | `вҲ« (Y(1)-Y(0)) В· 1_C вҲӮОј = (Ој C).toReal В· LATE zвӮҖ zвӮҒ` | Direct copy of LATE `event_conditioning_identity` with `intervalComplierEvent` | **Proved** |
| `wald_pairwise` | `(condExpYZ zвӮҒ - condExpYZ zвӮҖ) / (condExpDZ zвӮҒ - condExpDZ zвӮҖ) = LATE zвӮҖ zвӮҒ` | Main theorem (prop:po-iv-heckman-roy-wald); stitches the helpers via `field_simp` | **Proved** |

---

## 9f''. `PO/ID/Exact/DynamicLATE/{Setup,Consistency,Bridges,WhenToTreat}.lean` вҖ” Two-Period Dynamic LATE (PO framework)

Formalises the two-period dynamic IV/LATE identification of Sojitra (2025) in the bare potential-outcome framework: `subsec:po-dynamic-late`, `def:po-dynamic-late-system`, `def:po-dynamic-late-assumptions`, `def:po-dynamic-late`, `def:po-dynamic-late-observable-functionals`, `prop:po-dynamic-late-when-to-treat`, `rem:po-dynamic-late-bridge`, `rem:po-dynamic-late-lean`. Generalises `LATE.lean` to two periods with sequential encouragements `ZвӮҒ,ZвӮӮ`, noncompliant treatments `DвӮҒ,DвӮӮ`, baseline state `SвӮҖ : POVar P ОівӮҖ`, intermediate state `SвӮҒ : POVar P ОівӮҒ`, and real outcome `Y`.

**Phase A scope.** Definitions, observable functionals, the assumption bundle, the four bridge identities of `rem:po-dynamic-late-bridge`, and the algebraic ratio identifications `whenToTreat_wald`, `mixtureLATE_wald`, plus the heterogeneous (`SвӮҖ`-conditional) versions. The bridge proofs and the structural composition-consistency rewrites are filled.

**Out of scope (Phase B):** `prop:po-dynamic-late-always-treat` and `cor:po-dynamic-late-staggered` (always-treat LATE under cross-period mean-independence / staggered-compliance). Will live in a future `AlwaysTreat.lean`.

### `DynamicLATE/Setup.lean` вҖ” data layer

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `PODynLATESystem P ОівӮҖ ОівӮҒ` | `structure { S0, S1, Z1, D1, Z2, D2, Y, hZ1bool, hD1bool, hZ2bool, hD2bool, hYreal, vars_inj }` | Two-period dynamic IV subsystem over a PO system (def:po-dynamic-late-system). Pairwise distinctness packaged as `Function.Injective` on a `Fin 7 вҶ’ P.V` accessor. | **Defined** |

| `PODynLATESystem.{Z1_ne_Z2, D1_ne_D2, D1_ne_Y, D2_ne_Y, Z1_ne_Y, Z2_ne_Y, Z1_ne_D1, Z1_ne_D2, Z2_ne_D1, Z2_ne_D2}` | `S.Z1 вү  S.Z2` etc. | Pairwise distinctness extracted from `vars_inj` (the most-used pairs) | **Proved** |

| `PODynLATESystem.YofD d` | `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Counterfactual outcome `Y(d)` under treatment regime fixing both `D`'s | **Defined** |

| `PODynLATESystem.YofZ2 zвӮӮ` | `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | `Y(DвӮҒ, DвӮӮ(ZвӮҒ, zвӮӮ))` realised as `Y` under the regime fixing only `ZвӮӮ = zвӮӮ` | **Defined** |

| `PODynLATESystem.cLATE z d` | `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Heterogeneous (in `SвӮҖ`) dynamic LATE via `historyBundle1.condExpRatio` | **Defined** |
