| `ResponseTypeStats.PopulationBridge.ObservedBridge.observedBeta2SLS_eq_responseTypeWeightedSum` | `observedFirstStageMoment Ој Z D I вү  0 вҶ’ observedBeta2SLS = ОЈ_g Пү_g О”_g` | End-to-end observed-level corollary taking the observable denominator condition directly | **Proved** |
| `ResponseTypeStats.ComponentwiseMonotoneRestriction` | `structure { allowed, step_nonneg_of_allowed }` | UNUSED PLACEHOLDER: does NOT encode MTW partial/componentwise monotonicity and is connected to no theorem | **Defined** |
| `ResponseTypeStats.unnormTypeWeight_nonneg_of_signAligned` | `SignAligned вҶ’ 0 вүӨ unnormTypeWeight I R g` | Sign alignment and nonnegative type masses imply nonnegative unnormalized response-type weights | **Proved** |
| `ResponseTypeStats.normalizedTypeWeight_nonneg_of_signAligned` | `SignAligned вҶ’ 0 < typeWeightDenom I R вҶ’ 0 вүӨ normalizedTypeWeight I R g` | Positive denominator preserves nonnegativity after normalization | **Proved** |
| `ResponseTypeStats.normalizedTypeWeight_sum_eq_one_of_pos` | `0 < typeWeightDenom I R вҶ’ вҲ‘ g, normalizedTypeWeight I R g = 1` | Normalized response-type weights sum to one under positive first-stage denominator | **Proved** |
| `ResponseTypeStats.beta2SLSFiniteAlgebra_eq_responseTypeWeightedSum` | `typeWeightDenom I R вү  0 вҶ’ beta2SLSFiniteAlgebra I R = responseTypeEstimand I R` | Response-type form of the finite MTW ratio (`prop:po-estimand-mtw-response-type-form`, finite algebra layer) | **Proved** |
| `ResponseTypeStats.beta2SLSFiniteAlgebra_eq_positiveResponseTypeAverage` | `SignAligned вҶ’ 0 < typeWeightDenom I R вҶ’ beta2SLSFiniteAlgebra = responseTypeEstimand вҲ§ weights nonnegative вҲ§ weights sum to one` | Positive-weight characterization under MTW sign alignment (`prop:po-estimand-mtw-positive-weights`, finite algebra layer) | **Proved** |
| `ResponseTypeStats.signedAdjacentNumerator` / `signedAdjacentDenominator` | `в„қ` / `в„қ` | Public facade names for the finite response-type numerator `ОЈ_g О»_g О”_g` and denominator `ОЈ_g О»_g` | **Defined** |
| `ResponseTypeStats.beta2SLSFiniteAlgebra_eq_signedAdjacentRatio` | `signedAdjacentDenominator I R вү  0 вҶ’ beta2SLSFiniteAlgebra I R = signedAdjacentNumerator I R / signedAdjacentDenominator I R` | Facade theorem for the finite response-type ratio | **Proved** |
| `ResponseTypeStats.beta2SLSFiniteAlgebra_eq_responseTypeWeightedSum'` / `beta2SLSFiniteAlgebra_eq_positiveResponseTypeAverage'` | wrappers around the corresponding `ResponseTypes.lean` theorems | Public facade wrappers for the response-type weighted-sum and positive-average finite algebra results | **Proved** |
| `ResponseTypeStats.PopulationBridge.beta2SLSPopulationBridge_eq_signedAdjacentRatio` / `beta2SLSPopulationBridge_eq_responseTypeWeightedSum` / `beta2SLSPopulationBridge_eq_positiveResponseTypeAverage` | wrappers around bridge + finite algebra theorems | Public facade wrappers upgrading the saturated finite-support population bridge to the signed-ratio, response-type weighted-sum, and positive-average conclusions | **Proved** |

## 9i0a-UniformTwoWayPanel. `Panel/UniformTwoWayPanel.lean` вҖ” Uniform Balanced Two-Way Panel Algebra

Paper-agnostic finite balanced-panel helpers for TWFE and two-way Mundlak arguments under the uniform unit-period measure. Lives directly under `Panel/` (general panel substrate, not estimand-characterization-specific) and exposes bridge lemmas showing it is the equal-unit-weight specialization of `WeightedTwoWayPanel`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.BalancedPanel Unit Time` | `structure { unit_card_ge_two, time_card_ge_two }` | Balanced finite panel side conditions from `def:po-estimand-wooldridge-two-way-population`: at least two units and at least two periods | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.uniformWeights` | `0 < Fintype.card Unit вҶ’ WeightedTwoWayPanel.UnitWeights Unit` | Equal unit-weight probability vector `p_i = 1 / |Unit|`, used to instantiate the weighted two-way panel substrate | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.unitMean` / `timeMean` / `grandMean` | `(Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ ...` | Uniform finite-panel unit means, time means, and grand mean for a scalar array | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.ddot` | `(Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ` | Uniform double-demeaned residual `V_it - unitMean V i - timeMean V t + grandMean V` | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.inner` | `(Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„қ` | Unnormalized uniform finite unit-period inner product used for orthogonality statements | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.ddot_eq_weighted` / `inner_eq_card_smul_weighted` | bridge identities for `uniformWeights` | Uniform double demeaning equals weighted double demeaning with equal unit weights, and the unnormalized uniform inner product is `|Unit|` times the weighted inner product | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.finiteResidualizedCoefficient` | `(Dtilde Ytilde : Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„қ` | Finite scalar residualized coefficient `inner Dtilde Ytilde / inner Dtilde Dtilde` for supplied residualized regressor and outcome arrays | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.finite_residualized_coefficient_eq_of_normalEqs` | `normal equations + residual bridges вҶ’ ОІ = finiteResidualizedCoefficient Dtilde Ytilde` | Paper-agnostic finite scalar FWL handoff: from raw-regressor and nuisance normal equations, an explicitly residualized regressor orthogonal to the nuisance class, an outcome residual bridge, and positive residualized sum of squares, derives the finite residualized-ratio coefficient. Used by Wooldridge `twfe_twm_equivalence` instead of re-deriving scalar FWL algebra locally | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.IsUnitTimeAdditive` | `(Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Prop` | Alias for `Causalean.Panel.Weighted.IsUnitTimeAdditive`, the unit/time fixed-effect span `h_it = a_i + b_t` | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.unitTimeProjection` | `(Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ` | Removed uniform unit/time component `unitMean + timeMean - grandMean` | **Defined** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.ddot_reconstruct` | `ddot V i t + unitMean V i + timeMean V t - grandMean V = V i t` | Pointwise reconstruction identity for uniform double demeaning | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.unitTimeProjection_additive` | `IsUnitTimeAdditive (unitTimeProjection V)` | The removed component belongs to the unit/time additive nuisance class | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.sub_ddot_eq_unitTimeProjection` | `V i t - ddot V i t = unitTimeProjection V i t` | Pointwise form showing the part removed by uniform residualization is the unit/time projection | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.ddot_orthogonal_unit` | `BalancedPanel Unit Time вҶ’ вҲ‘ i, вҲ‘ t, ddot V i t * a i = 0` | Uniform double-demeaned arrays are orthogonal to arbitrary unit-only functions | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.ddot_orthogonal_time` | `BalancedPanel Unit Time вҶ’ вҲ‘ i, вҲ‘ t, ddot V i t * b t = 0` | Uniform double-demeaned arrays are orthogonal to arbitrary time-only functions | **Proved** |
| `UniformTwoWayPanel.ddot_orthogonal_unit_time` | `BalancedPanel Unit Time вҶ’ IsUnitTimeAdditive h вҶ’ inner (ddot V) h = 0` | Uniform double-demeaned arrays are orthogonal to the whole unit/time additive nuisance span | **Proved** |

## 9i0a-FlexibleDIDMundlak. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/FlexibleDIDMundlak/{TWFE,VectorTWFE,VectorMundlak,DID,PopulationBridge,PopulationOrigin}.lean` вҖ” Wooldridge TWFE/Mundlak and Flexible DID

Specific-paper formalization scaffold for `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/wooldridge_twfe_mundlak_did.md`. `TWFE.lean` proves the scalar finite-panel slice of Wooldridge's TWFE and two-way Mundlak equivalence by instantiating the shared `UniformTwoWayPanel.finite_residualized_coefficient_eq_of_normalEqs` finite FWL handoff. `DID.lean` is a finite-cell composition theorem: it now DERIVES the untreated-prediction recovery from no-anticipation + conditional parallel trends + additivity (no longer an assumed field), eliminates the former opaque `Prop`+`_holds` placeholders, and derives the POLS/ETWFE cell links from finite-cell residual normal equations justified by a saturated block-diagonalization lemma. **G5 de-scaffold (2026-05-30):** `m0`'s exact untreated fit is no longer a posited field вҖ” `SaturatedUntreatedRegression` now carries the genuine weighted least-squares projection origin (`untreatedWeight`, `untreatedWeight_pos`, `untreatedNormalEq`: `m0` solves the untreated-only normal equations against the additive class `H0`), and the exact fit `m0 = YgMean` on untreated cells is the DERIVED theorem `untreatedFit` (the projection is exact because no-anticipation + conditional parallel trends place the untreated observed mean inside `H0`). **G9 de-scaffold (2026-05-30):** the finite imputation estimand is now bridged to the population conditional expectation вҖ” `PopulationBridge.lean` proves `thetaImp g t = eventCondExp Ој cohortEvent О”` (Оё^imp = E[О”вҲЈG=g]) via the law of iterated expectations over the finite covariate partition, under explicit identification hypotheses (covariate weights are conditional cell probabilities; cell residuals are within-cell conditional means). **G1 de-scaffold (2026-05-30):** the scalar-only restriction is removed вҖ” `VectorTWFE.lean` introduces the genuine K-vector TWFE problem with a `Matrix K K в„қ`-valued residualized Gram matrix `Q_ddotX`, the full-rank condition `IsUnit (gram X).det`, and the matrix normal equation; existence and uniqueness of the closed-form coefficient `(gram X)вҒ»В№ (ОЈ ddot X ddot Y)` are proved, and `ScalarTWFEProblem` is shown to be the `K = Fin 1` specialization (`ScalarTWFEProblem.toVector` + `toVector_betaTWFE`). **`VectorMundlak.lean` (2026-05-30)** completes Theorem A in vector form: the matrix Frisch-Waugh-Lovell handoff `matrix_fwl_eq_of_normalEqs` (generalizing the scalar finite FWL), the K-vector two-way Mundlak nuisance span `IsVectorTwoWayMundlakNuisance`, the coding-free `VectorTWMFit`, and the equivalence `vec_twfe_twm_equivalence : fit.beta = P.betaTWFE` plus the optional-controls-invariance corollary. The remaining deferred Wooldridge gap is the population conditional-expectation origin of `m0` itself (G5 measure layer).

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/TWFE.lean`

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time` | `structure { panel, Y, X, ddotX_ss_pos }` | Scalar balanced-panel TWFE problem: outcome, scalar regressor, panel side conditions, and positive residualized sum of squares standing in for full rank of `Q_ddotX` | **Defined** |
| `ScalarTWFEProblem.twfeDenominator` | `ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time вҶ’ в„қ` | Residualized-design denominator `вҲ‘ ddot X_it^2` | **Defined** |
| `ScalarTWFEProblem.twfeNumerator` | `ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time вҶ’ в„қ` | Residualized numerator `вҲ‘ ddot X_it * ddot Y_it` | **Defined** |
| `ScalarTWFEProblem.betaTWFE` | `ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time вҶ’ в„қ` | Scalar population TWFE coefficient, the finite FWL ratio | **Defined** |
| `ScalarTWFEProblem.twfeNormalEq` | `ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time вҶ’ в„қ вҶ’ Prop` | Scalar double-demeaned normal equation for a proposed TWFE coefficient | **Defined** |
| `ScalarTWFEProblem.betaTWFE_normalEq` | `P.twfeNormalEq P.betaTWFE` | The closed-form scalar TWFE coefficient satisfies the double-demeaned normal equation | **Proved** |
| `ScalarTWFEProblem.betaTWFE_unique` | `P.twfeNormalEq ОІ вҶ’ ОІ = P.betaTWFE` | Positive residualized sum of squares gives uniqueness of the scalar TWFE normal-equation solution | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.IsTwoWayMundlakNuisance` | `(X) вҶ’ (Z вҶ’ Unit вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (M вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Prop` | Scalar two-way Mundlak nuisance span: constants, unit means of `X`, time means of `X`, optional unit-only controls, and optional time-only controls | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.mundlak_nuisance_unit_time` | `IsTwoWayMundlakNuisance X Zvar Mvar h вҶ’ IsUnitTimeAdditive h` | Every scalar two-way Mundlak nuisance term is unit/time additive | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ScalarTWMFit` | `structure { beta, nuisance, nuisance_mem, normal_X, normal_H }` | Coding-free scalar two-way Mundlak fit, represented by a coefficient, nuisance function, and normal equations against `X` and the nuisance span | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.twfe_twm_residual_common` | `IsUnitTimeAdditive (X - ddot X) вҲ§ вҲҖ h вҲҲ H_twm, inner (ddot X) h = 0` | Residualizing `X` against the two-way Mundlak nuisance span leaves the double-demeaned residual | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.twfe_twm_equivalence` | `ScalarTWMFit P Zvar Mvar вҶ’ fit.beta = P.betaTWFE` | Scalar finite-panel TWFE--two-way Mundlak equivalence (`thm:po-estimand-wooldridge-twfe-twm-equivalence`, scalar slice) | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.twfe_twm_optional_controls_invariant` | `fitвӮҒ.beta = fitвӮӮ.beta` | Adding or removing optional unit-only/time-only controls does not change the scalar coefficient because both fits equal `betaTWFE` | **Proved** |

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/VectorTWFE.lean` вҖ” K-vector TWFE matrix normal equation (audit G1)

Closes G1: the K-vector generalization of the TWFE problem. The vector double-demeaned array is component-wise scalar double demeaning (`ddotVec X i t k = ddot (X В·В· k) i t`), so the uniform-panel orthogonality infrastructure is reused.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ddotVec` | `(Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ K вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ K вҶ’ в„қ` | Component-wise double-demeaned vector regressor | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.gram` | `(Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ K вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Matrix K K в„қ` | Residualized Gram matrix `Q_ddotX = ОЈ_it ddot(X_it) ddot(X_it)бөҖ` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.numer` | `(Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ K вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ K вҶ’ в„қ` | Residualized numerator vector `ОЈ_it ddot(X_it) ddot(Y_it)` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWFEProblem Unit Time K` | `structure { panel, Y, X, gram_unit }` | K-vector balanced-panel TWFE problem; the full-rank condition is `IsUnit (gram X).det`, matching nonsingular `Q_ddotX` in the source | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWFEProblem.betaTWFE` | `VectorTWFEProblem Unit Time K вҶ’ K вҶ’ в„қ` | Closed-form vector TWFE coefficient `(gram X)вҒ»В№ (ОЈ ddot X ddot Y)` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWFEProblem.vecTwfeNormalEq` | `VectorTWFEProblem Unit Time K вҶ’ (K вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Prop` | Coordinate-wise vector double-demeaned normal equation | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.vecNormalEq_iff_mulVec` | `(coordinate-wise normal eq) вҶ” (gram X).mulVec ОІ = numer X Y` | The coordinate normal equation equals the matrix normal equation `Q_ddotX ОІ = ОЈ ddot X ddot Y` | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWFEProblem.betaTWFE_normalEq` | `P.vecTwfeNormalEq P.betaTWFE` | Existence: the closed-form coefficient solves the matrix normal equation under Gram nonsingularity | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWFEProblem.betaTWFE_unique` | `P.vecTwfeNormalEq ОІ вҶ’ ОІ = P.betaTWFE` | Full-rank uniqueness of the matrix normal-equation solution | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ScalarTWFEProblem.toVector` | `ScalarTWFEProblem Unit Time вҶ’ VectorTWFEProblem Unit Time (Fin 1)` | The scalar problem embeds as the singleton-`K` vector problem; `IsUnit (gram).det` reduces to `ddotX_ss_pos` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ScalarTWFEProblem.toVector_betaTWFE` | `P.toVector.betaTWFE 0 = P.betaTWFE` | The singleton-coordinate vector coefficient recovers the scalar coefficient, so the scalar theorem is the `K = Fin 1` case | **Proved** |

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/VectorMundlak.lean` вҖ” K-vector two-way Mundlak equivalence (Theorem A, vector form)

Extends `twfe_twm_equivalence` to K-vector regressors via a matrix FWL handoff.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.gramOf` / `numerOf` | `(Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ K вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Matrix K K в„қ` / `вҖҰ вҶ’ (Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ K вҶ’ в„қ` | Generic residualized Gram matrix / numerator of a supplied residualized regressor | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.gram_eq_gramOf` / `numer_eq_numerOf` | `gram X = gramOf (ddotVec X)` / `numer X Y = numerOf (ddotVec X) (ddot Y)` | The TWFE Gram/numerator are the residualized instances of the generic versions | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.sum_dotRegressor` | `вҲ‘_it DtВ·k (вҲ‘_j DВ·j ОІ_j) = вҲ‘_j (вҲ‘_it DtВ·k DВ·j) ОІ_j` | A residualized regressor against a `ОІ`-combination factors through the cross-Gram | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.matrix_fwl_eq_of_normalEqs` | `(decompositions + per-coordinate orthogonality + IsUnit gram + normal eqs) вҶ’ ОІ = (gramOf Dtilde)вҒ»В№ (numerOf Dtilde Ytilde)` | Matrix Frisch-Waugh-Lovell handoff: vector coefficient satisfying pooled normal equations against `D` and nuisance class `H` equals the residualized matrix coefficient | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.IsVectorTwoWayMundlakNuisance` | `(X) вҶ’ (Z вҶ’ Unit вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (M вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (Unit вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Prop` | K-vector two-way Mundlak nuisance span: constants, unit/time means of every coordinate of `X`, optional unit-only and time-only controls | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.vector_mundlak_nuisance_unit_time` | `IsVectorTwoWayMundlakNuisance X Zvar Mvar h вҶ’ IsUnitTimeAdditive h` | Every vector Mundlak nuisance term is unit/time additive | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.sum_ite_one_mul` | `вҲ‘_k' (if k'=k then 1 else 0) * f k' = f k` | Coordinate-selection indicator collapse | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.VectorTWMFit` | `structure { beta, nuisance, nuisance_mem, normal_X, normal_H }` | Coding-free K-vector two-way Mundlak fit, normal equations against each regressor coordinate and the nuisance span | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.vec_twfe_twm_equivalence` | `VectorTWMFit P Zvar Mvar вҶ’ fit.beta = P.betaTWFE` | K-vector finite-panel TWFE--two-way Mundlak equivalence (`thm:po-estimand-wooldridge-twfe-twm-equivalence`, full vector form) | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.vec_twfe_twm_optional_controls_invariant` | `fitвӮҒ.beta = fitвӮӮ.beta` | Adding or removing optional unit-only/time-only controls does not change the vector coefficient | **Proved** |

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |

| `StaggeredATTCells.untreatedCells` | `StaggeredATTCells Cohort Time Covar вҶ’ Finset (Cohort Г— Time)` | Finite untreated design set (support of the weighted projection producing `m0`), selected by `untreatedCell` | **Defined** |
| `StaggeredATTCells.tauCell` | `StaggeredATTCells Cohort Time Covar вҶ’ Cohort вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ` | Covariate-weighted ATT cell `вҲ‘_c w_gc (Y_g - Y_0)` | **Defined** |
| `StaggeredATTCells.tauAgg` | `StaggeredATTCells Cohort Time Covar вҶ’ (Cohort вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„қ` | Weighted aggregate ATT over the treated-cell support | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.AggregateWeights P` | `structure { weight, nonneg_on_treated, sum_treated }` | Requested nonnegative treated-cell weights summing to one | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.NoAnticipation P` | `Prop` | Finite-cell no-anticipation predicate on `untreatedCell` observations; retained as a named assumption for the causal theorem interface | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P` | `Prop` | Additive untreated-mean representation `Y0Mean g t c = alpha g c + lambda t c`, the scaffold form of conditional parallel trends | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.SaturatedUntreatedRegression P` | `structure { m0, additive, untreatedWeight, untreatedWeight_pos, untreatedNormalEq, design_identifies, target_cell_support }` | Bundled untreated-regression witness. `untreatedNormalEq` (with positive weights `untreatedWeight`/`untreatedWeight_pos`) is the genuine finite-cell projection origin: `m0` solves the weighted least-squares normal equations of the observed outcome on the additive class `H0` over the untreated design. `untreatedFit` (exact fit) and `recovers_target_Y0` are now DERIVED theorems, not fields; `design_identifies` is the concrete connected/full-rank condition. The former opaque `Prop`+`_holds` placeholders (untreated_design_connected, full_rank_after_normalization, covariates_baseline) and the former posited `untreatedFit` field were removed. | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.SaturatedUntreatedRegression.untreatedFit` | `NoAnticipation P вҶ’ ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P вҶ’ untreatedCell g t вҶ’ m0 g t c = YgMean g t c` | **DERIVED exact untreated fit (G5).** The weighted projection reproduces the cohort-`g` mean on every untreated cell. Proof: no-anticipation + conditional parallel trends make the untreated observed mean additive, so the residual `m0 вҲ’ Y0Mean` is additive; plugging it into `untreatedNormalEq` forces its positively-weighted squared sum over the untreated design to zero, hence the residual vanishes cellwise | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.IsCellAdditive` | `(Cohort вҶ’ Time вҶ’ Covar вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Prop` | Additive cell function `d g t c = Оі g c + Оҙ t c` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.UntreatedDesignIdentifies P` | `Prop` | Connected/full-rank condition: an additive cell function vanishing on all untreated cells vanishes on all treated cells | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.SaturatedUntreatedRegression.recovers_target_Y0` | `NoAnticipation P вҶ’ ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P вҶ’ treatedCell g t вҶ’ m0 g t c = Y0Mean g t c` | Saturated regression recovers untreated means on treated cells, DERIVED from no-anticipation + conditional parallel trends + additivity + untreated fit + design identifiability | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.cellIndicator` | `(g : Cohort) вҶ’ (t : Time) вҶ’ Cohort вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ` | Saturated treated-cell indicator `1{(g',t')=(g,t)}` | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.cellIndicator_normalEq_eq_cellResidual` | `(full saturated indicator normal equation) вҶ” cellResidualNormalEq P S theta g t` | Saturated block-diagonalization: the full normal equation summed against a cell's indicator collapses (cross-cell terms vanish by orthogonality) to that cell's residual equation, justifying the POLS/ETWFE per-cell normal-equation fields | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.imputationTheta` | `(P) вҶ’ SaturatedUntreatedRegression P вҶ’ Cohort вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ` | Imputation residual mean using `observedMean - m0` within a treated cell | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.cellResidualNormalEq P S theta g t` | `Prop` | Finite-cell residual normal equation `вҲ‘_c w_gc (observedMean_gtc - m0_gtc - theta) = 0` for a candidate cell coefficient | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.cellResidualNormalEq_eq_imputationTheta` | `cellResidualNormalEq P S theta g t вҶ’ theta = imputationTheta P S g t` | A saturated treated-cell normal equation identifies the coefficient with the imputation residual mean, using `covarWeight_sum_one` | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.FlexibleDIDEstimands P S` | `structure { thetaImp, thetaPOLS, thetaETWFE, thetaImp_eq_imputation, pols_cell_normalEq, etwfe_cell_normalEq }` | Coding-free flexible imputation/POLS/ETWFE cell estimands; POLS and ETWFE coefficients now carry finite-cell residual normal equations instead of direct equality assumptions | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDEstimands.thetaPOLS_eq_imputationTheta` / `thetaETWFE_eq_imputationTheta` | `treatedCell g t вҶ’ thetaPOLS/ETWFE g t = imputationTheta P S g t` | Derived POLS and ETWFE cell equalities from their residual normal equations; the ETWFE normal equation is the residualized equation supplied by the FWL/TWFE-Mundlak bridge | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDEstimands.pols_cell_eq_imputation` / `etwfe_cell_eq_pols` | `treatedCell g t вҶ’ thetaPOLS = thetaImp` / `thetaETWFE = thetaPOLS` | Compatibility aliases; both equalities are now consequences of the normal-equation lemmas rather than structure fields | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.psiImp` / `psiPOLS` / `psiETWFE` | `(P) вҶ’ FlexibleDIDEstimands P S вҶ’ (Cohort вҶ’ Time вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„қ` | Aggregate estimands formed by weighting the imputation, POLS, and ETWFE cell coefficients over treated cells | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.imputationTheta_eq_tauCell` | `NoAnticipation P вҶ’ ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P вҶ’ treatedCell g t вҶ’ E.thetaImp g t = P.tauCell g t` | Imputation recovers a treated ATT cell from consistency and the DERIVED `recovers_target_Y0` | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.flexible_did_cell_characterization` | `NoAnticipation P вҶ’ ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P вҶ’ treatedCell g t вҶ’ thetaImp = thetaPOLS = thetaETWFE = tauCell` | Cell-level theorem: the untreated-prediction witness plus POLS/ETWFE residual normal equations imply all three cell estimands equal the ATT cell | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.flexible_did_aggregate_characterization` | `NoAnticipation P вҶ’ ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P вҶ’ AggregateWeights P вҶ’ psiImp = tauAgg вҲ§ psiPOLS = tauAgg вҲ§ psiETWFE = tauAgg` | Aggregate theorem obtained by summing the normal-equation-derived cell equalities over treated cells | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.flexible_did_cell_characterization_of_estimand_equalities` / `flexible_did_aggregate_characterization_of_estimand_equalities` | `NoAnticipation P вҶ’ ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P вҶ’ вҖҰ` (same signatures as cell/aggregate characterization respectively) | Compatibility aliases for the earlier scaffold names, now taking the explicit causal hypotheses | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.flexible_did_scaffold_characterization` | `NoAnticipation P вҶ’ ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P вҶ’ cell characterization вҲ§ aggregate characterization` | Finite-cell Wooldridge DID characterization from saturated untreated prediction plus POLS/ETWFE finite-cell residual normal equations | **Proved** |

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/PopulationBridge.lean` вҖ” finite estimand/projection вҶ’ population conditional expectation (audit G9 + G5 population half)

Bridges the finite-cell imputation estimand and the saturated projection `m0` to population conditional expectations, reusing the conditional finite-partition total law `Causalean.PO.eventCondExp_eq_sum_condProb_mul_eventCondExp`. Hypothesis-driven: identification hypotheses connect the finite scaffold's primitive means to a probability model вҖ” they are exactly the paper's *definitions* (Оё^imp = E[О”вҲЈG=g]; m0 = E[Y(вҲһ)вҲЈG,t,C]), not strengthenings.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.imputationTheta_eq_eventCondExp` | `(Ој, P, S, g, t, cohortEvent, covarCell, О”)`, partition + integrability + `hweight : covarWeight g c = Ој(cohortEvent вҲ© covarCell c)/Ој(cohortEvent)` + `hcell : observedMean вҲ’ m0 = eventCondExp Ој (cohortEvent вҲ© covarCell c) О”` вҶ’ `imputationTheta P S g t = eventCondExp Ој cohortEvent О”` | **G9 finiteвҶ’population bridge.** The finite imputation residual mean equals the population conditional expectation `E[О”вҲЈG=g]` on the cohort event, by the law of iterated expectations over the covariate partition | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.thetaImp_eq_eventCondExp` | `treatedCell g t вҶ’` (same hypotheses) `вҶ’ E.thetaImp g t = eventCondExp Ој cohortEvent О”` | **G9 (Оё^imp = E[О”вҲЈG=g]).** The `FlexibleDIDEstimands` imputation cell estimand equals the population conditional expectation on each treated cell; composes `thetaImp_eq_imputation` with `imputationTheta_eq_eventCondExp` | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.m0_eq_eventCondExp_treated` | `NoAnticipation P вҶ’ ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P вҶ’ treatedCell g t вҶ’` `hY0 : Y0Mean g t c = eventCondExp Ој (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` `вҶ’ S.m0 g t c = eventCondExp Ој (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` | **G5 population origin (treated cells).** The saturated projection's fitted value equals `E[Y_t(вҲһ)вҲЈG=g,t,C=c]`; causal content (additive extrapolation) carried by `recovers_target_Y0`, `hY0` reads `Y0Mean` as that conditional mean | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.m0_eq_eventCondExp_untreated` | `NoAnticipation P вҶ’ ConditionalParallelTrendsAdditive P вҶ’ untreatedCell g t вҶ’` `hY0 : Y0Mean g t c = eventCondExp Ој (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` `вҶ’ S.m0 g t c = eventCondExp Ој (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` | **G5 population origin (untreated cells).** Same conclusion via `untreatedFit` + no-anticipation | **Proved** |

### `FlexibleDIDMundlak/PopulationOrigin.lean` вҖ” `ofPopulation` constructor making the m0 conditional-expectation origin definitional (audit G5 measure layer)

Closes the *measure-layer* half of G5: instead of *assuming* `Y0Mean g t c = E[Y(вҲһ)вҲЈcell]` (the `hY0` hypothesis above), it builds a `StaggeredATTCells` whose cell means are *defined* as that `eventCondExp`, so `hY0` holds by `rfl`. Mirrors `DCDHPanel.ofPopulation`. The cell-weight data (`cohortShare`, `covarWeight`) is supplied as a witness (an FWL/partition construction orthogonal to the conditional-expectation origin); the pointwise consistency inputs are the paper's consistency axiom restricted to each cell.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.StaggeredATTCells.ofPopulation` | `(Ој : Measure О©) [IsProbabilityMeasure Ој] (cellEvent) (Y0pop Ygpop Yobspop : О© вҶ’ в„қ) (treatedCell untreatedCell) (cohortShare covarWeight) (hmeas) (weight side-conditions) (hcons_tr hcons_ut) вҶ’ StaggeredATTCells Cohort Time Covar` | **G5 (panel from a population model).** Defines `Y0Mean/YgMean/observedMean g t c := eventCondExp Ој (cellEvent g t c) {Y0pop/Ygpop/Yobspop}`; derives `consistency_treated`/`consistency_untreated` from pointwise PO consistency via `eventCondExp_congr_on`; weights passed as witnesses | **Defined** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.m0_eq_eventCondExp_treated_ofPopulation` | for `P = ofPopulation Ој вҖҰ`, `hNA вҶ’ hCPT вҶ’ treatedCell g t вҶ’ S.m0 g t c = eventCondExp Ој (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` | **G5 payoff (treated, definitional).** Applies `m0_eq_eventCondExp_treated` with `hY0 := rfl` вҖ” the conditional-expectation origin of `m0` is now definitional, not assumed | **Proved** |
| `FlexibleDIDMundlak.m0_eq_eventCondExp_untreated_ofPopulation` | for `P = ofPopulation Ој вҖҰ`, `hNA вҶ’ hCPT вҶ’ untreatedCell g t вҶ’ S.m0 g t c = eventCondExp Ој (cellEvent g t c) Y0pop` | **G5 payoff (untreated, definitional).** Untreated-cell analogue, `hY0 := rfl` | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-ImputationEventStudy. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/ImputationEventStudy/Imputation.lean` вҖ” Robust Imputation Event Studies (BJS)

Specific-paper finite-cell formalization of the Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess robust imputation event-study characterization. The module works directly with finite treated and untreated cell index types, arbitrary signed target weights, treated/untreated regressor rows, primitive observed-law means, a nuisance vector, and treated-cell effects. It proves the population imputation identification theorem (taking `PredictionIdentified` as a propositional existence hypothesis and extracting the witness internally) and the intentionally-weak `linear_unbiased_of_imputation_representation` algebra helper. The **source-strength** linear-unbiased representation theorem and the combined iff characterization now live in the companion `PanelBridge.lean` (В§9i0a-ImputationEventStudyBridge), where the finite left-null-space adjustment is rebased onto the panel `WeightedSupport`/`Subspace` substrate. A staggered-adoption two-way-FE grounding (closing the abstract-index scope gap) lives in `PanelGrounding.lean` (В§9i0a-ImputationEventStudyGrounding). The Gauss-Markov/BLUE efficiency half of `prop:po-estimand-bjs-linear-unbiased-imputation` is in `Efficiency.lean` (В§9i0a-ImputationEventStudyEfficiency), built on the finite Gauss-Markov layer В§13gm. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/borusyak_jaravel_spiess_imputation.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ImputationEventStudy.dot` | `(Regressor вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (Regressor вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„қ` | Finite regressor dot product used for untreated-outcome linear predictions | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.BJSPanel` | `structure { qT, qU, a, EY_T, EY_U, EY0_T, EY0_U, beta0, tau }` | Finite BJS treated/untreated cell design: regressor rows, arbitrary target weights, observed means, untreated potential-outcome means, nuisance vector, and treated-cell effects | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.theta` | `BJSPanel Treated Untreated Regressor вҶ’ в„қ` | Target weighted sum `вҲ‘ c, a c * tau c`; weights may be signed and need not sum to one | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.targetForTau` | `(Treated вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„қ` | Target functional evaluated at an arbitrary unrestricted treated-effect vector, used in the linear-unbiasedness statement | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.UntreatedOutcomeModel` | `Prop` | Deterministic mean version of the untreated-outcome linear model plus untreated-cell no-anticipation / observation equation | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.TreatmentEffectFixed` | `Prop` | Treated observed means equal untreated means plus the fixed/design-conditional treated-cell effect | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.ImputationWeights` | `structure { weight, row_identity }` | Witness-form prediction-span condition: for every target-relevant treated cell, its regressor row is a weighted combination of untreated rows | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.PredictionIdentified` | `Prop` | Propositional existence wrapper (`Nonempty ImputationWeights`) for valid target-relevant imputation weights; natural hypothesis for identification вҖ” callers supply existence, not a pre-built witness | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.psiImp` | `(Treated вҶ’ Untreated вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„қ` | Population observed-law imputation functional `вҲ‘ a_c (EY_T c - вҲ‘ h_cu EY_U u)` for arbitrary imputation weights | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.bjs_imputation_identification` | `UntreatedOutcomeModel вҶ’ PredictionIdentified вҶ’ TreatmentEffectFixed вҶ’ вҲғ h : ImputationWeights, psiImp h.weight = theta` | BJS population identification theorem: under the untreated model and prediction-span condition, some imputation weight matrix recovers the weighted treated-cell target; witness extracted internally via `Classical.choice` | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator` | `structure { vT, vU }` | Linear estimator coefficients on treated and untreated observed cell outcomes | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.value` | `(Treated вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (Untreated вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„қ` | Evaluates a treated-plus-untreated linear estimator on arbitrary outcome arrays | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.observedValue` | `в„қ` | Evaluates a linear estimator on the panel's observed-law treated and untreated means | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.modelValue` | `(Regressor вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (Treated вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„қ` | Model-implied value of the linear estimator for a nuisance vector and unrestricted treated-effect vector | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.unbiasedForAllTau` | `Prop` | Linear unbiasedness for every nuisance vector and every unrestricted treated-effect vector | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.vT_eq_a` | `unbiasedForAllTau вҶ’ вҲҖ c, vT c = a c` | Hoisted fact: unrestricted treatment-effect heterogeneity forces treated-cell weights to equal target weights (indicator-`tau` test); reused by the efficiency layer | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.nuisance_coord` | `unbiasedForAllTau вҶ’ вҲҖ r, (вҲ‘ c, a c * qT c r) + вҲ‘ u, vU u * qU u r = 0` | Hoisted fact: nuisance unbiasedness gives the BJS left-null-space coordinate constraint `aбөҖQ_T + vUбөҖQ_U = 0`; reused by the efficiency layer | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.LinearEstimator.HasImputationRepresentation` | `structure { weights, untreated_weight_representation }` | Explicit witness that a linear estimator's untreated coefficients are represented by valid BJS imputation weights; used as an algebra helper for the source-strength theorem | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.linear_unbiased_of_imputation_representation` | `unbiasedForAllTau вҶ’ HasImputationRepresentation вҶ’ вҲғ H, vT = a вҲ§ vU = -aбөҖH вҲ§ (вҲҖ YT YU, value YT YU = вҲ‘ c, a c * (YT c - вҲ‘ u, H.weight c u * YU u)) вҲ§ observedValue = psiImp H.weight` | Intentionally weak algebra helper: given a pre-built imputation representation witness, derives the estimator identity; source-strength derivation lives in `linear_unbiased_of_prediction_identified` | **Proved** |

## 9i0a-ImputationEventStudyBridge. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/ImputationEventStudy/PanelBridge.lean` вҖ” Imputation Event Study вҶ” panel substrate bridge

Connects the bespoke finite design-matrix algebra of the BJS imputation scaffold to the shared panel inner-product / subspace substrate (`Causalean/Panel/Weighted/`). The OLS imputation estimator is the *uniform-weight* special case of the panel WLS substrate, so the untreated cells carry the uniform `WeightedSupport`; the keystone lemma proves the BJS "left-null-space of `Q_U`" condition **is** `ip`-orthogonality to the regressor column span. The source-strength linear-unbiased theorem and the combined iff characterization (moved here from `Imputation.lean`, statements verbatim) consume that substrate-based left-null-space adjustment; `DecidableEq Untreated` is obtained internally via `Classical.decEq`, so their public signatures are unchanged.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `ImputationEventStudy.untreatedSupport` | `(Untreated : Type*) вҶ’ [Fintype, DecidableEq, Nonempty] вҶ’ WeightedSupport Untreated` | The untreated cells as the uniform `WeightedSupport` `Пү_u вүЎ 1/\|U\|` вҖ” the OLS = uniform-weight WLS instantiation through which BJS consumes the panel `ip`/`Subspace` API | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.regressorColumn` | `Regressor вҶ’ (Untreated вҶ’ в„қ)` | The `r`-th regressor column `u вҶҰ q_{ur}` as an array over untreated cells | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.columnSpan` | `Submodule в„қ (Untreated вҶ’ в„қ)` | Span of all regressor columns; orthogonality to it is the BJS left-null-space condition | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.ip_untreatedSupport_regressorColumn` | `ip v (regressorColumn r) = (\|U\|)вҒ»В№ * вҲ‘ u, v u * qU u r` | The panel inner product of an untreated vector with a regressor column equals the BJS left-null-space sum, rescaled by the uniform weight | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.ip_regressorColumn_eq_zero_iff` | `ip v (regressorColumn r) = 0 вҶ” вҲ‘ u, v u * qU u r = 0` | `ip`-orthogonality to a single column вҹә that column's left-null coordinate vanishes | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.columnSpan_ip_orthogonal_iff` | `(вҲҖ h вҲҲ columnSpan, ip v h = 0) вҶ” вҲҖ r, вҲ‘ u, v u * qU u r = 0` | **Keystone bridge**: a vector is `ip`-orthogonal to the regressor column span iff it satisfies the BJS left-null-space condition (the panel-substrate restatement of "`v` lies in the left null space of `Q_U`") | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.exists_imputationWeights_of_gap_orthogonal` | `(hc0 : a c0 вү  0) вҶ’ H0 вҶ’ (gap ip-orthogonal to columnSpan) вҶ’ вҲғ H, вҲҖ u, вҲ‘ c, a c * H.weight c u = -vU u` | Audit M3 left-null-space row adjustment, phrased through the panel substrate: one nonzero target row absorbs the orthogonal gap, yielding imputation weights representing the untreated coefficients | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.linear_unbiased_of_prediction_identified` | `unbiasedForAllTau вҶ’ PredictionIdentified вҶ’ (вҲғ c, a c вү  0) вҶ’ вҲғ H, vT = a вҲ§ vU = -aбөҖH вҲ§ (вҲҖ YT YU, value YT YU = вҲ‘ c, a c * (YT c - вҲ‘ u, H.weight c u * YU u)) вҲ§ observedValue = psiImp H.weight` | Source-strength BJS linear-unbiased representation theorem; now consumes `exists_imputationWeights_of_gap_orthogonal` (empty-`Untreated` case handled separately) instead of an inline finite-algebra construction | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.bjs_linear_unbiased_iff_imputation_form` | `PredictionIdentified вҶ’ (вҲғ c, a c вү  0) вҶ’ (unbiasedForAllTau вҶ” вҲғ H, vT = a вҲ§ vU = -aбөҖH вҲ§ (вҲҖ YT YU, value = вҲ‘ a_c (YT c - вҲ‘ h_cu YU u)) вҲ§ observedValue = psiImp H.weight)` | Combined iff characterization (`prop:po-estimand-bjs-linear-unbiased-imputation`, identification half): unbiased for every tau iff BJS imputation form; (вҹ№) is `linear_unbiased_of_prediction_identified`, (вҹё) is pure row-span algebra | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-ImputationEventStudyGrounding. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/ImputationEventStudy/PanelGrounding.lean` вҖ” Imputation Event Study staggered-adoption grounding

Closes the abstract-index scope gap (audit G5) *constructively*: exhibits `BJSPanel` as an instance built from genuine panel primitives вҖ” units `I`, periods `Fin T`, and a staggered-adoption path `g : I вҶ’ WithTop (Fin T)` via `Causalean.Panel.AdoptionPath`. Treated cells are those whose treatment has switched on (`g i вүӨ t`); untreated cells are those still untreated (`t < g i`), faithfully including every never-treated cell and every pre-adoption cell of a treated unit. The regressor design is the canonical two-way fixed-effect block `I вҠ• Fin T`, so the untreated-outcome model `E[Y(0)] = Оұ_i + О»_t` holds by construction and the BJS hypotheses discharge to a concrete panel.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `ImputationEventStudy.TreatedCell` | `(g : I вҶ’ WithTop (Fin T)) вҶ’ Type` | Subtype of cells `(i,t)` whose treatment has switched on (`AdoptionPath.le (g i) t`) | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.UntreatedCell` | `(g : I вҶ’ WithTop (Fin T)) вҶ’ Type` | Subtype of still-untreated cells (`AdoptionPath.lt (g i) t`); includes never-treated and pre-adoption cells | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.treated_not_untreated` | `le (g c.1) c.2 вҶ’ lt (g c.1) c.2 вҶ’ False` | The cell partition is exclusive: no cell is both adopted-by-`t` and untreated-at-`t` | **Proved** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.treated_or_untreated` | `вҲҖ c, le (g c.1) c.2 вҲЁ lt (g c.1) c.2` | The cell partition is exhaustive: every cell is treated or untreated | **Proved** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.feRow` | `(I Г— Fin T) вҶ’ (I вҠ• Fin T) вҶ’ в„қ` | Two-way fixed-effect design row for a cell: unit-`i` indicator stacked with time-`t` indicator | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.dot_feRow` | `dot (feRow c) (Sum.elim Оұ lam) = Оұ c.1 + lam c.2` | The FE row evaluates the additive fixed-effect model `Оұ_i + О»_t` | **Proved** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.ofStaggeredTWFE` | `(g) вҶ’ (Оұ : I вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (lam : Fin T вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (a tau : TreatedCell g вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ BJSPanel (TreatedCell g) (UntreatedCell g) (I вҠ• Fin T)` | Grounding constructor: builds a `BJSPanel` from an adoption path and two-way fixed effects | **Defined** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.ofStaggeredTWFE_untreatedModel` | `(ofStaggeredTWFE вҖҰ).UntreatedOutcomeModel` | The grounded panel satisfies the BJS untreated-outcome model (both cell means equal `Оұ_i + О»_t`; untreated cells have no anticipation) | **Proved** |
| `ImputationEventStudy.ofStaggeredTWFE_treatmentFixed` | `(ofStaggeredTWFE вҖҰ).TreatmentEffectFixed` | The grounded panel satisfies the BJS fixed-effect equation `E[Y_T] = E[Y_T(0)] + П„` | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-ImputationEventStudyEfficiency. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/ImputationEventStudy/Efficiency.lean` вҖ” Imputation Event Study Efficiency / BLUE half

The efficiency half of `prop:po-estimand-bjs-linear-unbiased-imputation`: under spherical errors the OLS imputation estimator is the minimum-variance linear unbiased estimator (Gauss-Markov / BLUE). The BJS imputation estimator is recognized as the OLS estimator for the *event-study design* `designFull` (a treated-cell indicator block stacked with the covariate block), so the result is a direct instantiation of the finite Gauss-Markov theorem `GaussMarkov.variance_blue_spherical` (В§13gm) at that design. The unbiasedness-to-design-constraint bridge uses the hoisted `LinearEstimator.vT_eq_a` and `LinearEstimator.nuisance_coord`. An additive fixed-effect-block bridge (`designFull_col_mem_indicatorSpan`) identifies the П„-columns of `designFull` with the panel `IndicatorSpan` substrate (`Causalean/Panel/Weighted/IndicatorSpan.lean`), connecting the event-study FE design to the panel indicator-span layer; the BLUE proof itself stays on the finite Gauss-Markov substrate (В§13gm), the correct home for the variance argument. No `sorry`/`admit`/`axiom`. Companion NL doc section C3.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BJSPanel.designFull` | `BJSPanel Treated Untreated Regressor вҶ’ Matrix (Treated вҠ• Untreated) (Treated вҠ• Regressor) в„қ` | Event-study design: rows are observed cells, columns are treated-cell fixed effects (П„ block, cell-indicator on treated rows / zero on untreated rows) stacked with covariates (ОІ block, `qT`/`qU`) | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.cFull` | `BJSPanel Treated Untreated Regressor вҶ’ (Treated вҠ• Regressor вҶ’ в„қ)` | Target functional in design coordinates: target weights `a` on the П„ block, zero on the covariate block | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.weightOf` | `LinearEstimator P вҶ’ (Treated вҠ• Untreated вҶ’ в„қ)` | A linear estimator's full weight vector over all observed cells (`Sum.elim vT vU`) | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.weightOf_vecMul_designFull` | `unbiasedForAllTau вҶ’ weightOf L бөҘ* designFull P = cFull P` | Unbiasedness bridge: an unbiased estimator's weight satisfies the design constraint (captures both `vT = a` and the nuisance left-null constraint) | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.bjs_ols_imputation_min_variance_spherical` | `(вҲҖ i, MemLp (Y i) 2 Ој) вҶ’ SphericalFamily Y Ој Пғ вҶ’ IsUnit ((designFull P)бөҖ * designFull P).det вҶ’ unbiasedForAllTau L вҶ’ Var[вҲ‘ olsWeight вҖҰ * Y] вүӨ Var[вҲ‘ weightOf L * Y]` | **BJS efficiency theorem (BLUE):** for a full-rank event-study design and spherical cell outcomes, the OLS imputation estimator has variance no larger than any linear unbiased estimator of the target | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.treatedClassifier` | `(Treated вҠ• Untreated) вҶ’ Option Treated` | Classifier on observed cells: each treated cell to its own label, every untreated cell to `none`; its `Some`-indicators are the П„-columns of `designFull` | **Defined** |
| `BJSPanel.designFull_col_eq_cellIndicator` | `(fun i => designFull P i (Sum.inl d)) = cellIndicator treatedClassifier (some d)` | Each treated-cell fixed-effect column of `designFull` is the panel cell indicator of the treated-cell classifier | **Proved** |
| `BJSPanel.designFull_col_mem_indicatorSpan` | `(fun i => designFull P i (Sum.inl d)) вҲҲ indicatorSpan treatedClassifier` | The treated-cell FE block of `designFull` lies in the panel `IndicatorSpan`: the BJS event-study FE design is the panel indicator-span substrate (additive; BLUE proof unchanged) | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-HeterogeneousTWFE. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/HeterogeneousTWFE/{FinitePanel,FWLBridge,PopulationBridge}.lean` вҖ” de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (2020) TWFE Signed Weights

Specific-paper finite-cell formalization of the de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille TWFE heterogeneous-effects warning. `FinitePanel.lean` works over finite group-time cells with positive weights `ПҖ_gt`, binary treatment `D_gt`, observed outcomes `Y_gt`, untreated outcomes `Y0_gt`, treatment effects `П„_gt`, and a residualized-treatment witness `DМғ_gt` against the group-plus-time fixed-effect span. It proves the finite FWL decomposition `ОІ^twfe = B0 + вҲ‘_{D=1} Пү_gt П„_gt`, the zero-untreated-residual-contrast corollary, the fact that treated-cell weights sum to one while inheriting the sign of `DМғ`, and the constructive sign-reversal theorem. `FWLBridge.lean` closes audit gaps **G2/G6**: for the uniform balanced panel it builds a genuine `DCDHPanel` whose `DМғ` is the *derived* Frisch-Waugh-Lovell residual (double-demeaned treatment `ddot D` from `UniformTwoWayPanel`), with the membership and orthogonality fields PROVED rather than posited вҖ” only nonzero double-demeaned treatment variation (`S_D > 0`), binary treatment, and consistency remain as primitives. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/de_chaisemartin_dhaultfoeuille_twfe.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.IsGTFE` | `(G вҶ’ T вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Prop` | Compatibility alias for `AdditiveSpan.IsUnitTimeAdditive`, specialized to group-time fixed effects `a_g + b_t` | **Defined** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.DCDHPanel G T` | `structure { pi, D, Y, Y0, tau, Dtilde : G вҶ’ T вҶ’ в„қ, pi_pos, pi_sum_one, D_binary, consistency, D_minus_resid_mem, Dtilde_orthogonal, SD_pos }` | Finite DCDH group-time panel plus residualized-treatment witness (`def:po-estimand-dcdh-panel`, `def:po-estimand-dcdh-residualized-treatment`) | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.SD P` | `DCDHPanel G T вҶ’ в„қ` | Residualized-treatment denominator `S_D = вҲ‘ ПҖ_gt DМғ_gtВІ` | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.betaTWFE P` | `DCDHPanel G T вҶ’ в„қ` | Finite FWL/TWFE coefficient `(вҲ‘ ПҖ_gt DМғ_gt Y_gt) / S_D` (`def:po-estimand-dcdh-twfe-coefficient`) | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.untreatedBias P` | `DCDHPanel G T вҶ’ в„қ` | Bias term `B0 = (вҲ‘ ПҖ_gt DМғ_gt Y0_gt) / S_D` in the DCDH decomposition | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.omega P g t` | `DCDHPanel G T вҶ’ G вҶ’ T вҶ’ в„қ` | Normalized residualized-treatment cell weight `Пү_gt = ПҖ_gt DМғ_gt / S_D`, interpreted on treated cells | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.treatedCells P` | `DCDHPanel G T вҶ’ Finset (G Г— T)` | Finite set of cells with `D_gt = 1`, used to state treated-cell sums without partial weights | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.DWeightedTau P` / `DCDHPanel.treatedWeightedTau P` | `DCDHPanel G T вҶ’ в„қ` | All-cell `D`-weighted treatment-effect component and equivalent treated-cell weighted sum | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast P` | `DCDHPanel G T вҶ’ Prop` | Exact DCDH bias-free condition `вҲ‘ ПҖ_gt DМғ_gt Y0_gt = 0` (`ass:po-estimand-dcdh-untreated-parallel`) | **Defined** |
| `DCDHPanel.inner_Dtilde_D_eq_SD` | `вҲ‘ ПҖ_gt DМғ_gt D_gt = P.SD` | Denominator identity derived by applying residualized-treatment orthogonality to `D вҲ’ DМғ` | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast_of_Y0_mem_gtfe` | `IsGTFE P.Y0 вҶ’ P.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast` | Additive group-plus-time untreated outcomes are a sufficient, not necessary, condition for zero untreated residual contrast | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.twfe_eq_untreatedBias_add_DWeightedTau` | `P.betaTWFE = P.untreatedBias + P.DWeightedTau` | All-cell version of the DCDH TWFE decomposition after substituting consistency | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.DWeightedTau_eq_treatedWeightedTau` | `P.DWeightedTau = P.treatedWeightedTau` | Binary-treatment filtering lemma: the all-cell effect component equals the treated-cell weighted sum | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.twfe_eq_untreatedBias_add_treated_weighted_tau` | `P.betaTWFE = P.untreatedBias + P.treatedWeightedTau` | Headline decomposition `ОІ^twfe = B0 + вҲ‘_{D=1} Пү_gt П„_gt` (`thm:po-estimand-dcdh-twfe-decomposition`) | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.twfe_eq_treated_weighted_tau_of_zeroUntreatedContrast` | `P.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast вҶ’ P.betaTWFE = P.treatedWeightedTau` | Bias-free DCDH causal weighted-sum corollary under the exact zero untreated residual contrast | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.treated_omega_sum_eq_one` | `вҲ‘_{gt вҲҲ treatedCells} omega P gt.1 gt.2 = 1` | Treated-cell DCDH weights sum to one (`prop:po-estimand-dcdh-weights-sum-one`) | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.treated_omega_neg_iff_Dtilde_neg` | `P.omega g t < 0 вҶ” P.Dtilde g t < 0` | Negative-sign direction of `sign(Пү_gt)=sign(DМғ_gt)`; needs only `ПҖ_gt>0`, `S_D>0` | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.treated_omega_pos_iff_Dtilde_pos` | `0 < P.omega g t вҶ” 0 < P.Dtilde g t` | Positive-sign direction, completing the sign triplet | **Proved** |
| `DCDHPanel.treated_omega_zero_iff_Dtilde_zero` | `P.omega g t = 0 вҶ” P.Dtilde g t = 0` | Zero-sign direction, completing the sign triplet | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.neg_component_of_exists_Dtilde_neg` | `(вҲғ g t, P.D g t = 1 вҲ§ P.Dtilde g t < 0) вҶ’ вҲ‘_{treated,Пү<0} Пү < 0` | Bridge: the paper's natural entry condition "some treated cell has DМғ_gt<0" implies the negative-weight-sum precondition of the sign-reversal theorem | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.two_cell_signed_weights_positive_effects_negative_sum` | `0 < c вҶ’ 0 < Оө вҶ’ ((1+c)Оө)/c < M вҶ’ 0 < Оө вҲ§ 0 < M вҲ§ (1+c)Оө + (-c)M < 0` | Explicit two-cell sign-reversal arithmetic from `prop:po-estimand-dcdh-sign-reversal` | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.exists_positive_effects_negative_weighted_sum_of_negative_component` | `(вҲ‘ w = 1) вҶ’ (вҲ‘_{w<0} w < 0) вҶ’ вҲғ П„, (вҲҖ i, 0 < П„ i) вҲ§ вҲ‘ i, w i П„ i < 0` | Reusable finite signed-average construction: a strictly negative total weight component can dominate with positive effects | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.exists_panel_with_positive_treated_effects_twfe_negative_of_negative_component` | `P.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast вҶ’ (вҲ‘_{treated,Пү<0} Пү < 0) вҶ’ вҲғ P', same ПҖ,D,Y0,DМғ, (вҲҖ g t, 0 < P'.tau g t), P'.zeroUntreatedResidualContrast вҲ§ P'.betaTWFE < 0` | Panel-level DCDH sign-reversal theorem: keeping the treatment/residualization geometry fixed, one can choose globally positive treated effects (for all g t) so TWFE is negative | **Proved** |

### `HeterogeneousTWFE/FWLBridge.lean` вҖ” `DМғ` as a derived FWL residual (audit G2/G6)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.uniformWeight G T` | `в„қ` | Uniform unit-period cell weight `1/(|G|В·|T|)` for the balanced-panel DCDH instance | **Defined** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.uniformWeight_pos` | `UniformTwoWayPanel.BalancedPanel G T вҶ’ 0 < uniformWeight G T` | Positivity of the uniform weight from the `вүҘ2` cardinality side conditions | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.ofTwoWayPanel` | `BalancedPanel G T вҶ’ (D Y Y0 tau) вҶ’ (D binary) вҶ’ (consistency) вҶ’ (0 < вҲ‘ uniformWeightВ·(ddot D)ВІ) вҶ’ DCDHPanel G T` | **G2/G6:** builds a genuine `DCDHPanel` whose `Dtilde := ddot D` (double-demeaned FWL residual); `D_minus_resid_mem` and `Dtilde_orthogonal` are DERIVED from `UniformTwoWayPanel` (`sub_ddot_eq_unitTimeProjection`, `unitTimeProjection_additive`, `ddot_orthogonal_unit_time`), not posited | **Defined** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.ofTwoWayPanel_Dtilde` | `(ofTwoWayPanel вҖҰ).Dtilde = ddot D` | The residualized treatment of the FWL-derived panel is exactly the double-demeaned treatment | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.ofTwoWayPanel_betaTWFE` | `(ofTwoWayPanel вҖҰ).betaTWFE = finiteResidualizedCoefficient (ddot D) Y` | The DCDH TWFE coefficient of the FWL-derived panel equals the uniform-panel FWL residualized coefficient of `Y` on `ddot D` (the uniform weight cancels) | **Proved** |

### `HeterogeneousTWFE/PopulationBridge.lean` вҖ” cell consistency from a potential-outcome contrast (audit G10)

Supplies the population origin of the `DCDHPanel.consistency` field and of `tau` as `Y(1) вҲ’ Y(0)`, at the level of cell-conditional means, reusing `Causalean.PO.eventCondExp_congr_on`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.cellMean_consistency` | `(Ој, A measurable, Y Y0 Y1, d вҲҲ {0,1})`, `hcons : вҲҖ Пү вҲҲ A, Y Пү = Y0 Пү + dВ·(Y1 Пү вҲ’ Y0 Пү)` вҶ’ `eventCondExp Ој A Y = eventCondExp Ој A Y0 + dВ·(eventCondExp Ој A Y1 вҲ’ eventCondExp Ој A Y0)` | **G10 finiteвҶ’population bridge.** On a cell where treatment is constant `D вүЎ d` and PO consistency holds pointwise, the cell-conditional means satisfy the DCDH consistency identity `ИІ = ИІ(0) + dВ·П„М„` with `П„М„ = E[Y(1)вҲЈA] вҲ’ E[Y(0)вҲЈA]` a genuine potential-outcome contrast. Proof: case split on `d`, `eventCondExp_congr_on`, `ring` | **Proved** |
| `HeterogeneousTWFE.DCDHPanel.ofPopulation` | `[IsProbabilityMeasure Ој]`, cell partition `cellEvent`, outcomes `Yfac Y0pop Y1pop`, binary cell treatment `d`, `Dtilde` witness; partition/positivity + pointwise consistency `hcons` + `Dtilde` orthogonality/`SD_pos` hypotheses вҶ’ `DCDHPanel G T` | **G10 `DCDHPanel` from a population model.** Builds a genuine panel with `Y/Y0 = E[В·вҲЈcell]`, `tau = E[Y(1)вҲЈcell] вҲ’ E[Y(0)вҲЈcell]` (a real PO contrast), `pi = Ој(cell)`. `consistency` is DERIVED via `cellMean_consistency`; `pi_sum_one` from `measure_biUnion_finset` over the partition. `Dtilde` (general-weight FWL residual) is a witness, as in the base panel вҖ” the uniform case is `ofTwoWayPanel` | **Defined** |
