## 9i0a-OLSWeightDecomposition. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/OLSWeightDecomposition/{FinitePartition,OverlapWeightedATE,GeneralCondExp}.lean` вҖ” SЕӮoczyЕ„ski (2022) Finite-Partition OLS Weights

Specific-paper specialization of the residualization framework to SЕӮoczyЕ„ski (2022, *Review of Economics and Statistics*). Layer A (`FinitePartition.lean`) is pure finite-cell algebra: given a finite covariate `G : рқ’ў` with cell probabilities `ПҖ_g`, treated shares `p_g`, and conditional treatment effects `П„_g`, the saturated-OLS estimand decomposes as `ОІ_sat = ОЈ Пү_g П„_g`, where `Пү_g вҲқ ПҖ_g p_g (1вҲ’p_g)`. On its own this layer carries no causal meaning (`ПҖ, p, П„` are formal reals); the causal content is injected in Layer B. Layer B (`OverlapWeightedATE.lean`, backed by `Support/*.lean`) instantiates the residualization core on a probability space with binary treatment, consistency, and finite-cell CMI, constructs the saturated residualization witnesses, and proves that the residualized coefficient equals the Layer A overlap-weighted ATE вҖ” with `П„_g = E[Y(1)вҲ’Y(0)вҲЈG=g]` built from real potential outcomes. All declarations live in `namespace Causalean.Panel.EstimandCharacterization.OLSWeightDecomposition`. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/sloczynski_ols_heterogeneous.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `FinitePartition рқ’ў` | `structure { ПҖ, p, П„ : рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ, ПҖ_nonneg, ПҖ_sum_one, p_nonneg, p_le_one, overlap_pos }` | SЕӮoczyЕ„ski finite covariate partition (`def:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-finite-partition`): cell probabilities `ПҖ_g`, treated shares `p_g вҲҲ [0,1]`, conditional treatment effects `П„_g`, plus the `ОЈПҖ=1` probability-vector constraint and the saturated-overlap relevance hypothesis `ОЈ ПҖ_g p_g (1вҲ’p_g) > 0` | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.cellOverlap P g` | `FinitePartition рқ’ў вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` | Within-cell treatment variance `p_g (1 вҲ’ p_g)` | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.overlapNumerator P` | `FinitePartition рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` | Numerator `ОЈ_g ПҖ_g В· p_g (1вҲ’p_g) В· П„_g` of the saturated-OLS estimand | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.overlapDenominator P` | `FinitePartition рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` | Denominator `ОЈ_g ПҖ_g В· p_g (1вҲ’p_g)` of the saturated-OLS estimand | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.overlapWeight P g` | `noncomputable FinitePartition рқ’ў вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` | Normalized weight `Пү_g = ПҖ_g p_g (1вҲ’p_g) / ОЈ_h ПҖ_h p_h (1вҲ’p_h)` | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.overlapWeightedATE P` | `noncomputable FinitePartition рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` | Saturated-OLS estimand `ОІ_sat = (ОЈ ПҖВ·p(1вҲ’p)В·П„) / (ОЈ ПҖВ·p(1вҲ’p))` in finite-cell form | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.overlapDenominator_pos` | `0 < P.overlapDenominator` | Denominator positivity, restated from `overlap_pos` after unfolding `cellOverlap` | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.omega_sum_eq_one` | `вҲ‘ g, P.overlapWeight g = 1` | The overlap weights sum to one. Proof: pull the common denominator out of the sum via `Finset.sum_div`, then cancel | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.finite_weights_eq_sum` | `P.overlapWeightedATE = вҲ‘ g, P.overlapWeight g * P.П„ g` | **Finite-partition OLS weights** (`prop:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-finite-weights`): the saturated-OLS estimand decomposes as `ОЈ Пү_g П„_g`. Proof: distribute the denominator over the numerator sum and rearrange via `ring` | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.equal_groups_weight_eq_pi` | `(вҲҖ g, P.p g = 1/2) вҶ’ вҲҖ g, P.overlapWeight g = P.ПҖ g` | If every `p_g = 1/2`, the normalized overlap weight equals the cell probability. Proof: each `cellOverlap g = 1/4`, denominator becomes `(1/4)В·ОЈПҖ_g = 1/4`, and the `1/4`'s cancel | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.equal_groups_collapses` | `(вҲҖ g, P.p g = 1/2) вҶ’ P.overlapWeightedATE = вҲ‘ g, P.ПҖ g * P.П„ g` | **Equal-cell-share collapse** (finite half of `prop:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-equal-groups`): if every `p_g = 1/2`, then `ОІ_sat = ОЈ ПҖ_g П„_g`. Proof: rewrite via `finite_weights_eq_sum`, then apply `equal_groups_weight_eq_pi` cell by cell | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.homogeneous_collapses` | `(вҲҖ g, P.П„ g = П„вӮҖ) вҶ’ P.overlapWeightedATE = П„вӮҖ` | **Homogeneous-effect collapse** (saturated half of `prop:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-homogeneous`): if `П„_g = П„вӮҖ` for every cell, then `ОІ_sat = П„вӮҖ`. Proof: pull `П„вӮҖ` out of the numerator, cancel with the denominator | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.perTreatedWeight P g` | `noncomputable FinitePartition рқ’ў вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` | **Per-treated-observation leverage factor** `1 вҲ’ p_g` in cell `g` (Remark `rem:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-group-size`): each treated unit in cell `g` contributes weight proportional to the untreated share | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.perUntreatedWeight P g` | `noncomputable FinitePartition рқ’ў вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` | **Per-untreated-observation leverage factor** `p_g` in cell `g`: each untreated unit in cell `g` contributes weight proportional to the treated share | **Defined** |
| `FinitePartition.cellOverlap_eq_perWeights_mul` | `P.cellOverlap g = P.perUntreatedWeight g * P.perTreatedWeight g` | The cell overlap variance factors as the product of the two per-unit leverage factors. Proof: `unfold` + `ring` | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.perTreatedWeight_antitone` | `P.p g вүӨ P.p h вҶ’ P.perTreatedWeight h вүӨ P.perTreatedWeight g` | **Headline monotonicity** (`rem:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-group-size`): the per-treated leverage factor `1 вҲ’ p_g` is antitone in `p_g` вҖ” the smaller the treated share, the larger each treated unit's weight. Formalizes the paper's title "Smaller Groups Get Larger Weights" for the treated side. Proof: `linarith` | **Proved** |
| `FinitePartition.perUntreatedWeight_monotone` | `P.p g вүӨ P.p h вҶ’ P.perUntreatedWeight g вүӨ P.perUntreatedWeight h` | The per-untreated leverage factor `p_g` is monotone in `p_g` вҖ” the larger the treated share, the larger each untreated unit's weight. Symmetrically to `perTreatedWeight_antitone`. Proof: `exact hph` | **Proved** |
| `OppositeGroupRepr` | `structure { ПҒ, П„_ATT, П„_ATU, ОІ_ols, wвӮҒ, wвӮҖ : в„қ, ПҒ_nonneg, ПҒ_le_one, twoComponent : ОІ_ols = wвӮҒВ·П„_ATT + wвӮҖВ·П„_ATU, weights_sum_one : wвӮҒ + wвӮҖ = 1, equalDispersion : wвӮҒ = 1вҲ’ПҒ }` | SЕӮoczyЕ„ski opposite-group representation (`ass:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-opposite-group`). **G2 closed:** the fields are now the paper's genuine structural assumption вҖ” the two-component representation `ОІ_ols = wвӮҒВ·П„_ATT + wвӮҖВ·П„_ATU` with `wвӮҒ+wвӮҖ=1` (recorded separately, per `rem:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-no-covariate-caveat`, NOT derived from the saturated formula, which is a different estimand) вҖ” plus the equal-dispersion specialization `wвӮҒ = 1вҲ’ПҒ`. The opposite-group identity is no longer a field: it is the derived theorem `represents`. The general dispersion-based weight formula is not specified by the source and is not formalized | **Defined** |
| `OppositeGroupRepr.tau_ATE R` | `OppositeGroupRepr вҶ’ в„қ` | ATE in two-component form, `П„_ATE = ПҒВ·П„_ATT + (1вҲ’ПҒ)В·П„_ATU` (`def:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-att-atu`) | **Defined** |
| `OppositeGroupRepr.w0_eq_rho` | `R.wвӮҖ = R.ПҒ` | The untreated weight equals the treated share in the equal-dispersion case, forced by `weights_sum_one` and `equalDispersion`. Proof: `rw [equalDispersion]` into `weights_sum_one`, `linarith` | **Proved** |
| `OppositeGroupRepr.represents` | `R.ОІ_ols = (1вҲ’R.ПҒ)В·R.П„_ATT + R.ПҒВ·R.П„_ATU` | **SЕӮoczyЕ„ski opposite-group identity** (`prop:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-opposite-group`), now **derived** (G2) rather than assumed: substitute the equal-dispersion weights `wвӮҒ = 1вҲ’ПҒ`, `wвӮҖ = ПҒ` into the two-component representation. Proof: `rw [twoComponent, equalDispersion, w0_eq_rho]` | **Proved** |
| `OppositeGroupRepr.equal_groups_eq_ATE` | `R.ПҒ = 1/2 вҶ’ R.ОІ_ols = R.tau_ATE` | **Equal-group-size collapse to the ATE** (top half of `prop:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-equal-groups`): if `ПҒ = 1/2`, the opposite-group OLS coefficient equals the ATE. Proof: substitute `ПҒ = 1/2` into both `represents` and `tau_ATE` and `ring` | **Proved** |
| `OppositeGroupRepr.homogeneous_eq_constant` | `R.П„_ATT = П„вӮҖ вҶ’ R.П„_ATU = П„вӮҖ вҶ’ R.ОІ_ols = П„вӮҖ` | **Homogeneous-effect collapse for the ATT/ATU representation** (ATT/ATU half of `prop:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-homogeneous`): if `П„_ATT = П„_ATU = П„вӮҖ`, then `ОІ_ols = П„вӮҖ` for every `ПҒ`. Proof: substitute into `represents` and `ring` | **Proved** |

### `OLSWeightDecomposition/GeneralCondExp.lean` вҖ” general continuous-covariate theorem via Пғ-algebra condExp (audit G4)

The abstract-conditioning version of `thm:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-overlap-weighted-ate` for an *arbitrary* covariate Пғ-algebra `m` (not the finite partition), using Mathlib's `Ој[f|m]` conditional expectation. Closes audit G4 (general continuous-X theorem, previously absent). Binary `D`, propensity `p := Ој[D|m]`, residual `DМғ := D вҲ’ p`. CMI is stated in the mean-independence **factoring form** `Ој[DВ·Y(d)|m] =бөҗ Ој[D|m]В·Ој[Y(d)|m]` вҖ” a documented *consequence* (weakening) of the paper's full CMI `E[Y(d)|D,X]=E[Y(d)|X]`, hence faithful. All declarations axiom-free.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `denom_identity` | `(hm : m вүӨ mО©) (D) (hDbin) (hD_int) вҶ’ вҲ« (D вҲ’ Ој[D|m])ВІ = вҲ« Ој[D|m]В·(1 вҲ’ Ој[D|m])` | **Denominator (A).** The residualized treatment variance integrates to the overlap factor. Proof: expand `(DвҲ’p)ВІ =бөҗ D вҲ’ 2pD + pВІ` (binary `DВІ=бөҗD`), pull `p` out of `Ој[В·|m]`, integrate via `integral_condExp` вҖ” gives `вҲ«p вҲ’ вҲ«pВІ = вҲ«p(1вҲ’p)` | **Proved** |
| `numer_identity` | `(hm) (D Y Y0 Y1) (hDbin) (integrabilities) (hcons) (hCMI1) (hCMI0) вҶ’ вҲ« (D вҲ’ Ој[D|m])В·Y = вҲ« Ој[D|m]В·(1вҲ’Ој[D|m])В·(Ој[Y1|m] вҲ’ Ој[Y0|m])` | **Numerator (B).** Proof: `(DвҲ’p)В·Y =бөҗ (1вҲ’p)(DВ·Y1) вҲ’ p((1вҲ’D)Y0)` by consistency+binary; pull out the `m`-measurable `1вҲ’p`,`p`; apply CMI factoring вҶ’ `вҲ«p(1вҲ’p)(M1вҲ’M0)` | **Proved** |
| `saturated_ols_overlap_weighted_ate` | `(hm) (D Y Y0 Y1) (hDbin) (integrabilities) (hcons) (hCMI1) (hCMI0) (_hoverlap : 0 < вҲ« Ој[D|m]В·(1вҲ’Ој[D|m])) вҶ’` `вҲ«(DвҲ’p)Y / вҲ«(DвҲ’p)ВІ = вҲ« p(1вҲ’p)(Ој[Y1|m]вҲ’Ој[Y0|m]) / вҲ« p(1вҲ’p)` | **G4 general overlap-weighted ATE.** The saturated FWL estimand equals the overlap-weighted conditional ATE for arbitrary `X`. Proof: rewrite numerator/denominator by (B),(A). The ratio identity is unconditional; `_hoverlap` is the paper's nondegeneracy regime | **Proved** |

### `OLSWeightDecomposition/OverlapWeightedATE.lean` (+ `Support/`) вҖ” probability-space bridge (Layer B)

`OverlapWeightedATE.lean` hosts the finite-cell headline theorem; the supporting measure-theoretic machinery lives in the `Support/` subfolder:

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| `Support/Basic.lean` | Saturated-control class, cell statistics, saturated propensity/mean regression, and elementary cell bounds |
| `Support/Integrals.lean` | Reusable finite-cell integral identities and saturated-sum reduction lemmas |
| `Support/Orthogonality.lean` | Per-cell orthogonality facts for treatment/outcome residuals and the observed-outcome `MemLp` bridge |
| `Support/Partition.lean` | Construction of `partitionOf` and the residualization witnesses `residWitnessD` / `residWitnessY` |
| `Support/PerCell.lean` | Per-cell denominator and numerator identities used in the final bridge proof |
| `OverlapWeightedATE.lean` | Public headline theorem `bridge_finite_residualized_eq_overlap` |

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `saturatedClass Ој G G_meas` | `LinearL2Class Ој` | Finite span of the cell indicators `рқҹҷ{G=g}` used as the saturated-control nuisance class | **Defined** |
| `cellMass` / `cellShare` / `cellTau` | `Measure О© вҶ’ вҖҰ вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` | Extract cell probability, treated share, and cell treatment effect from a probability-space representation | **Defined** |
| `propensity` / `meanReg` | `Measure О© вҶ’ вҖҰ вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Cell-wise saturated projections for treatment and outcome, represented as finite sums of cell indicators | **Defined** |
| `partitionOf` | `Measure О© вҶ’ вҖҰ вҶ’ FinitePartition рқ’ў` | Builds the Layer A finite partition from the probability-space cell statistics and overlap hypothesis | **Defined** |
| `residWitnessD` / `residWitnessY` | `ResidualizationWitness Ој (saturatedClass Ој G G_meas) D/Y` | Packages the saturated residualization witnesses; orthogonality is proved by reducing to per-cell indicator moments | **Proved** |
| `denom_per_cell` | `вҲ« (DвҲ’propensity)^2 В· рқҹҷ{G=g} = cellMass g * cellShare g * (1вҲ’cellShare g)` | Per-cell denominator identity for the FWL denominator | **Proved** |
| `num_per_cell` | `вҲ« (DвҲ’propensity) В· Y В· рқҹҷ{G=g} = cellMass g * cellShare g * (1вҲ’cellShare g) * cellTau g` | Per-cell numerator identity under consistency and finite-cell CMI | **Proved** |
| `bridge_finite_residualized_eq_overlap` | `residualizedCoefficient Ој (saturatedClass Ој G B_meas_G) (residWitnessY вҖҰ (Y_memLp_of_consistency вҖҰ)) (residWitnessD вҖҰ) = FinitePartition.overlapWeightedATE (partitionOf вҖҰ)` | Layer B bridge: assumes the finite-cell integrated-indicator condition implied by CMI (not an equivalence to the full conditional-mean clause), sums the per-cell numerator and denominator identities over `g`, removes the saturated outcome projection by residual orthogonality, and identifies the resulting ratio with the Layer A overlap-weighted ATE. **G7 fix:** `Y_memLp` is no longer a hypothesis вҖ” derived inline via `Y_memLp_of_consistency` from `B_consis+B_binary+B_Y0_L2+B_Y1_L2`. **G6 fix:** `B_Dtilde_pos` is no longer a hypothesis вҖ” denominator positivity derived from `B_overlap` via `hDen_sum`. Signature now has 8 hypotheses (was 10). **G4 (deferred):** general continuous-X theorem for arbitrary covariate deferred per `rem:po-estimand-sloczynski-ols-lean` | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-StaggeredTWFEDecomposition. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/{Panel,Pairwise,Decomposition,Bridge,Causal}.lean` вҖ” Goodman-Bacon (2021) TWFE staggered-timing decomposition

Specific-paper specialization of the residualization framework to Goodman-Bacon (2021, *Journal of Econometrics*). Layer A (`Panel.lean`, `Pairwise.lean`, `Decomposition.lean`) is pure finite-cell algebra over a balanced cohort-period law: given a `CohortPanel рқ’ў T` carrying cohort shares `p_g`, adoption dates `A_g вҲҲ WithTop (Fin T)`, and cell outcome means `Y_{gt}`, the population TWFE coefficient `ОІ^twfe := E[DМғВ·Y]/E[DМғВІ]` decomposes as a positively-weighted average of three families of 2Г—2 DID contrasts (treated-vs-never `О”^TN`, early-vs-late `О”^EL`, late-vs-early `О”^LE`). `Pairwise.lean` is the intermediate algebra layer: it first rewrites denominator/numerator quantities into ordered pairwise centered-treatment contributions, then states the local TN/treated-treated window lemmas needed by `Decomposition.lean`. Layer B (`Bridge.lean`) instantiates the residualization core on a probability space with the balanced cohort-period law and span(cohort + period indicators) as the saturated nuisance class. Layer C (`Causal.lean`) promotes the algebraic contrasts to causal statements about window-specific ATTs under consistency, no anticipation, and pairwise untreated parallel trends; the `LE` comparison carries the explicit bad-comparison term that motivates Goodman-Bacon's caveat about TWFE under treatment-effect heterogeneity. All declarations live in `namespace Causalean.Panel.EstimandCharacterization.StaggeredTWFEDecomposition`. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/goodman_bacon_twfe_timing.md`.

### `StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/Panel.lean` вҖ” cell-statistics record and primitives (Layer A1вҖ“A4)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `CompTag` | `inductive { TN, EL, LE }` | Three-valued tag for the type of admissible 2Г—2 comparison | **Defined** |
| `CohortPanel рқ’ў T` | `structure { p, A, Y, T_pos, p_pos, p_sum_one }` | Staggered-adoption cohort panel: cohort shares `p_g`, adoption dates `A_g : WithTop (Fin T)` (`вҠӨ = вҲһ`), cohort-period factual means `Y_{gt}`, plus positivity and probability-vector constraints | **Defined** |
| `AdoptionDate.{le, lt, isFin, isInf}` | `WithTop (Fin T) вҶ’ Fin T вҶ’ Prop` (or `вҶ’ Prop`) | Goodman-Bacon local helpers using the same `WithTop (Fin T)` convention as `AdoptionPath`: `A_g вүӨ t`, `t < A_g`, `A_g вү  вҠӨ`, and `A_g = вҠӨ` | **Defined** |
| `D` / `barD` / `pCohort` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ вҖҰ вҶ’ в„қ` | Treatment indicator `D_{gt} = 1_{A_g вүӨ t}`, cohort treatment share `\overline{D}_g = TвҒ»В№ вҲ‘_t D_{gt}`, and overall share `E[D] = вҲ‘_g p_g \overline{D}_g` | **Defined** |
| `Dtilde` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ Fin T вҶ’ в„қ` | Residualized treatment via the explicit double-demeaning formula `D вҲ’ \overline{D}_g вҲ’ E[DвҲЈT=t] + E[D]` | **Defined** |
| `VD` / `betaTWFE` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ в„қ` | Residualized-treatment variance `V_D = вҲ‘_{g,t} (p_g/T) DМғ_{gt}ВІ` and the TWFE estimand `ОІ^twfe = (вҲ‘ (p_g/T) DМғ Y) / V_D` (`def:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-twfe`) | **Defined** |
| `Ybar P g S` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ Finset (Fin T) вҶ’ в„қ` | Window mean `\overline{Y}_{g,S} = \|S\|вҒ»В№ вҲ‘_{t вҲҲ S} Y_{gt}` | **Defined** |
| `S0_TN` / `S1_TN` / `S0_EL` / `S1_EL` / `S0_LE` / `S1_LE` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ вҖҰ вҶ’ Finset (Fin T)` | Six comparison windows for the three 2Г—2 comparison types (`def:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-comparisons`) | **Defined** |
| `О”_TN` / `О”_EL` / `О”_LE` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` | The three 2Г—2 DID contrasts (treated-vs-never, early-vs-late, late-vs-early) | **Defined** |
| `lambdaTN` / `q` / `mu` / `lambdaEL` / `lambdaLE` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ вҖҰ вҶ’ в„қ` | Raw weights `О»^TN, О»^EL, О»^LE` and the timing-pair gap `q_{eв„“} = \overline{D}_e вҲ’ \overline{D}_в„“` plus splitting fraction `Ој_{eв„“} = (1вҲ’\overline{D}_e)/(1вҲ’q)` | **Defined** |
| `Lambda` / `w_TN` / `w_EL` / `w_LE` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ вҖҰ вҶ’ в„қ` | Aggregate raw-weight denominator `Оӣ` and the three normalized weights `w = О»/Оӣ` (`def:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-weights`) | **Defined** |
| `admissible` / `рқ’Ұ` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ CompTag Г— рқ’ў Г— рқ’ў вҶ’ Prop`, `Finset (CompTag Г— рқ’ў Г— рқ’ў)` | Admissibility predicate and the comparison index set, filtering admissible tagged cohort pairs | **Defined** |
| `weight` / `contrast` / `lambdaWeight` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ CompTag Г— рқ’ў Г— рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` | Unified normalized weight, 2Г—2 contrast, and raw weight on the full index, returning `0` outside the admissible set | **Defined** |

### `StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/Pairwise.lean` вҖ” pairwise centered-treatment algebra (Layer A intermediate)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `centeredD P g t` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ Fin T вҶ’ в„қ` | Cohort-demeaned treatment path `D_{gt} вҲ’ \overline D_g`; the object centered across cohorts at each period before applying the finite weighted covariance/variance identities | **Defined** |
| `vdPairContribution P g u` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` | Ordered-pair contribution `(p_g p_u)/(2T) В· ОЈ_t (centeredD_g(t) вҲ’ centeredD_u(t))ВІ` in the pairwise representation of `V_D` | **Defined** |
| `numPairContribution P g u` | `CohortPanel рқ’ў T вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` | Ordered-pair contribution `(p_g p_u)/(2T) В· ОЈ_t (centeredD_g(t) вҲ’ centeredD_u(t))В·(Y_g(t) вҲ’ Y_u(t))` in the pairwise representation of the TWFE numerator | **Defined** |
| `Dtilde_eq_centeredD_sub_weighted_mean` | `Dtilde P g t = centeredD P g t вҲ’ вҲ‘ h, P.p h * centeredD P h t` | Rewrites the explicit double-demeaning formula as cohort-weighted centering of the cohort-demeaned treatment path at each period | **Proved** |
| `VD_eq_pairwise_centeredD` | `VD P = вҲ‘ g, вҲ‘ u, vdPairContribution P g u` | First-stage denominator rewrite via `NormalizedWeights.weighted_center_var`; after this, `Decomposition.lean` only needs adoption-window grouping | **Proved** |
| `twfe_numerator_eq_pairwise_centeredD_Y` | `вҲ‘ (p_g/T)В·DМғВ·Y = вҲ‘ g, вҲ‘ u, numPairContribution P g u` | First-stage numerator rewrite via `NormalizedWeights.weighted_center_cov`; after this, `Decomposition.lean` only needs adoption-window grouping | **Proved** |
| `TN_pair_vd_contribution_eq_gap` | pairwise TN denominator contribution equals the treated-vs-never raw gap factor | Local denominator window algebra for a treated-vs-never pair; now factors through `vdPairContribution_add_swap`, `TN_centeredD_gap_eq`, and the proved `TN_time_variance_eq_gap` Bernoulli-variance helper | **Proved** |
| `TT_pair_vd_contribution_eq_gap` | pairwise treated-treated denominator contribution equals the raw timing-pair gap factor | Local denominator window algebra for a treated-treated ordered timing pair; proves the early-late treatment gap is the middle-window indicator and applies the finite binary-variance helper | **Proved** |
| `TN_pair_contribution_eq_lambda_delta` | pairwise TN numerator contribution equals `О»_TNВ·О”_TN` | Local numerator window algebra for a treated-vs-never pair; splits the centered binary covariance over `S0_TN`/`S1_TN` and rewrites the restricted sums as `Ybar` means | **Proved** |
| `TT_pair_contribution_eq_lambda_delta_sum` | pairwise treated-treated numerator contribution equals `О»_ELВ·О”_EL + О»_LEВ·О”_LE` | Local numerator window algebra for a treated-treated timing pair; partitions time into pre-early, middle, and post-late windows, uses the complement mean decomposition, and discharges the `mu` cardinal-share algebra | **Proved** |

### `StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/Decomposition.lean` вҖ” five Layer A theorems (A5.1вҖ“A5.5)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `weights_nonneg` | `0 вүӨ lambdaWeight P k` | All admissible raw weights are nonnegative. Proof: case-split on `CompTag`; uses `0 вүӨ p_g`, `0 вүӨ \overline{D}_g вүӨ 1`, and the cardinality bounds on `q_{eв„“}` | **Proved** |
| `raw_weight_sum_eq_VD` | `Lambda P = VD P` | **Denominator identity** of `thm:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-decomposition`: the residualized-treatment variance equals the sum of all pairwise raw weights (key algebraic content underlying the FWL denominator step) | `sorry` (placeholder) |
| `twfe_numerator_eq_lambda_delta_sum` | `вҲ‘ (p_g/T)В·DМғВ·Y = вҲ‘ О»^TNВ·О”^TN + вҲ‘ (О»^ELВ·О”^EL + О»^LEВ·О”^LE)` | **Numerator identity**: TWFE numerator decomposes by unordered cohort pairs into raw-weight Г— 2Г—2 DID contributions | `sorry` (placeholder) |
| `weights_sum_one` | `0 < VD P вҶ’ вҲ‘ k вҲҲ рқ’Ұ P, weight P k = 1` | Normalized comparison weights sum to one. Proof: divide `raw_weight_sum_eq_VD` by `Оӣ = V_D > 0` | **Proved** (modulo `raw_weight_sum_eq_VD`) |
| `twfe_eq_weighted_avg` | `betaTWFE P = вҲ‘ k вҲҲ рқ’Ұ P, weight P k * contrast P k` | **Headline theorem** (`thm:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-decomposition`): TWFE coefficient is the weighted average of the 2Г—2 DID contrasts, with the `VD P = 0` case handled by Lean's totalized division. Proof combines A5.2 + A5.3 with the definition of `weight` | **Proved** (modulo A5.2/A5.3) |

### `StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/Bridge/{Basic,Integrals,Orthogonality,Partition,PerCell}.lean` + `StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/Bridge.lean` вҖ” probability-space bridge (Layer B)

Layer B is split across five sub-files (mirroring Sloczynski's Bridge layout) plus a thin facade: `Bridge/Basic.lean` holds the panel class, cell statistics, and projections; `Bridge/Integrals.lean` holds reusable cell integral identities and the per-axis-to-whole-class orthogonality decoupler; `Bridge/Orthogonality.lean` holds per-axis residual orthogonality (under the balanced-cell hypothesis `IsBalancedPanelLaw`); `Bridge/Partition.lean` builds the `panelOf` Layer A panel and the residualization witnesses; `Bridge/PerCell.lean` proves the per-cohort-period denominator and numerator identities; `Bridge.lean` states the headline.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `panelClass Ој G T_rv G_meas T_meas` | `LinearL2Class Ој` | Linear `LВІ` class spanning (a.e.) the family of cohort indicators `рқҹҷ{G = g}` and period indicators `рқҹҷ{T_rv = t}`. Membership predicate uses two coefficient maps `cG : рқ’ў вҶ’ в„қ` and `cT : Fin T вҶ’ в„қ` (B1) | **Defined** |
| `cellMass` / `cohortMass` / `periodMass` / `cellMean` / `cohortBarD` | `Measure О© вҶ’ вҖҰ вҶ’ в„қ` | Extract cohort-period cell mass `Ој{G=g, T=t}.toReal`, cohort mass, period mass, cell mean of `Y`, and cohort treatment share from a probability-space representation (B2) | **Defined** |
| `panelOf` | `Measure О© вҶ’ вҖҰ вҶ’ CohortPanel рқ’ў T` | Builds the Layer A `CohortPanel` from the probability-space cell statistics plus an explicit adoption date `A : рқ’ў вҶ’ WithTop (Fin T)` and the balanced-law side conditions (B2) | **Defined** |
| `panelPropensity` / `panelMeanReg` | `Measure О© вҶ’ вҖҰ вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Cohort + period saturated projections for treatment and outcome (the closed-form residual via two-way demeaning); each lies in `panelClass` (B3) | **Defined** |
| `panelPropensity_mem_panelClass` / `panelMeanReg_mem_panelClass` | `(panelClass Ој G T_rv G_meas T_meas).mem (panelPropensity Ој D G T_rv)` (resp. `panelMeanReg`) | Membership lemmas for the projections, with explicit `(cG, cT)` coefficient maps | **Proved** |
| `IsBalancedPanelLaw Ој G T_rv` | `вҲҖ g t, cellMass = cohortMass В· periodMass` | The balanced cohort-period law hypothesis; Lean form of Goodman-Bacon (2021)'s `в„ҷ(G=g, T=t) = p_g В· q_t`. Used as `B_balanced` throughout the Layer B stack | **Defined** |
| `indicator_{cohort,period,panel_cell}_memLp` | `MemLp (рқҹҷ_{cell}) 2 Ој` | Cell indicators are square-integrable on a probability measure | **Proved** |
| `integral_{cohort,period,panel_cell}_indicator_one_eq_{cohort,period,cell}Mass` | `вҲ« рқҹҷ_{cell} dОј = mass` | Indicator integrates to its cell mass | **Proved** |
| `{cohort,period,panel_cell}_integral_div_mul_{cohort,period,cell}Mass` | `(вҲ« FВ·рқҹҷ_{cell} dОј / mass) В· mass = вҲ« FВ·рқҹҷ_{cell} dОј` | Division-multiplication cancels even at zero-mass cells (a.e.-vanishing indicator) | **Proved** |
| `cohortBarD_mul_cohortMass` | `cohortBarD g В· cohortMass g = вҲ« D В· рқҹҷ{G=g} dОј` | Defining identity of `cohortBarD` after multiplying back the cohort mass | **Proved** |
| `panelPropensity_eq_hat_of_mem` / `panelMeanReg_eq_hat_of_mem` | On cell `{G=g вҲ§ T_rv=t}`, projection equals the closed-form `panelPropensityHat` / `panelMeanRegHat` constant | Cell-constancy identities for the in-class projections; the two-axis analogues of Sloczynski's `propensity_eq_cellShare_of_mem` | **Proved** |
| `integral_mul_panelClass_eq_zero_of_axes` | per-axis orthogonality вҮ’ orthogonality against every member of `panelClass` | Decouples the cohort-axis and period-axis orthogonality from the witness-level obligation. Proof: split `V В· (ОЈ_g cG В· рқҹҷ{G=g} + ОЈ_t cT В· рқҹҷ{T_rv=t})` into two finite sums and apply per-axis vanishing | **Proved** |
| `integral_eq_sum_{cohort,period,panel_cell}` | `вҲ« F dОј = вҲ‘ over cells вҲ« F В· рқҹҷ_{cell} dОј` | Integrate by summing over cells of the appropriate axis(es) | **Proved** |
| `residD_{cohort,period}_orthogonal` / `residY_{cohort,period}_orthogonal` | `вҲ« Vtilde В· рқҹҷ_{axis} dОј = 0` | Per-axis orthogonality of the `D` / `Y` residual against cohort and period indicators. Cohort axis is automatic from the defining identity of `cohortBarD`; period axis uses `IsBalancedPanelLaw` to make the cross-cohort sum collapse to `вҲ« D dОј` (resp. `вҲ« Y dОј`) | **Proved** |
| `residWitnessD_panel` / `residWitnessY_panel` | `ResidualizationWitness Ој (panelClass Ој G T_rv G_meas T_meas) D/Y` | Full residualization witness against `panelClass`. Orthogonality field discharged via `integral_mul_panelClass_eq_zero_of_axes` plus the four per-axis lemmas, taking `IsBalancedPanelLaw` as input | **Proved** |
| `panelDtilde Ој D G T_rv g t` | `(вҲ« D В· рқҹҷ_{cell} dОј / cellMass) вҲ’ panelPropensityHat g t` | Cell-level residualized-treatment value; on `panelOf вҖҰ` panels equals the Layer A `Dtilde` under the side conditions in the bridge theorem | **Defined** |
| `denom_per_cell_panel` | `вҲ« VtildeВІ В· рқҹҷ_{cell g t} dОј = cellMass В· panelDtildeВІ` | Per-cell denominator identity: Vtilde is a.e. constant equal to `panelDtilde` on each cell (by the cell-constancy hypothesis on `D` and `panelPropensity_eq_hat_of_mem`); integrate against the cell indicator | **Proved** |
| `num_per_cell_panel` | `вҲ« Vtilde В· Y В· рқҹҷ_{cell g t} dОј = cellMass В· panelDtilde В· cellMean` | Per-cell numerator identity: same constancy argument as `denom_per_cell_panel`, then use `panel_cell_integral_div_mul_cellMass` to identify `вҲ« Y В· рқҹҷ_{cell} dОј = cellMass В· cellMean` | **Proved** |
| `bridge_Dtilde_sq_eq_VD` | `вҲ« wD.VtildeВІ dОј = VD (panelOf вҖҰ)` | Companion B4 identity: the FWL denominator equals the Layer A `V_D`. Proof: `integral_eq_sum_panel_cell` вҠ• `denom_per_cell_panel` per cell вҠ• `hLaw` (`cellMass = cohortMass / T`) вҠ• `hDtilde_eq` (`panelDtilde = Layer A Dtilde`) вҠ• `VD` definition | **Proved** |
| `bridge_VD_pos_iff_Dtilde_sq_pos` | `0 < VD (panelOf вҖҰ) вҶ” 0 < вҲ« wD.VtildeВІ dОј` | G9 bridge: `VD P > 0` iff the FWL denominator is positive (immediate via `bridge_Dtilde_sq_eq_VD`); lets callers derive `hDtilde_pos` from `hVD_pos` | **Proved** |
| `bridge_finite_residualized_eq_twfe` | `residualizedCoefficient Ој panelClass wY wD = betaTWFE (panelOf вҖҰ)` | **Headline B4**: the population FWL coefficient on the panel class equals the Layer A finite-cell `betaTWFE`. Proof: replace `wY.Vtilde` by `Y` in the numerator using `wD.orthogonal panelMeanReg_mem_panelClass`; expand both numerator and denominator by `integral_eq_sum_panel_cell` вҠ• per-cell identities; substitute `hLaw` / `hDtilde_eq`; identify with `betaTWFE` definition | **Proved** |

### `StaggeredTWFEDecomposition/Causal.lean` вҖ” causal characterization corollaries (Layer C)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Ybar0` | `(рқ’ў вҶ’ Fin T вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ Finset (Fin T) вҶ’ в„қ` | Window mean of a potential-outcome map (e.g. `Y(вҲһ)` or `Y(A_g)`); same shape as `Ybar` but operates on a generic potential-outcome map rather than `P.Y` | **Defined** |
| `ATT_window` | `(рқ’ў вҶ’ Fin T вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (рқ’ў вҶ’ Fin T вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ рқ’ў вҶ’ Finset (Fin T) вҶ’ в„қ` | Window-specific ATT `\|S\|вҒ»В№ вҲ‘_{t вҲҲ S} (Y1_{gt} вҲ’ Y0_{gt})` in the two-state layer where `Y0 = Y(вҲһ)` and `Y1 = Y(A_g)` (`def:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-window-att`) | **Defined** |
| `CausalAssumptions P Y0 Y1` | `Prop` | Bundles consistency on treated/untreated cells, no anticipation `t < A_g вҶ’ Y1_{gt} = Y0_{gt}`, and three pairwise untreated-parallel-trends fields (one per comparison type, matching the LaTeX `ass:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-causal`); this is the two-state simplification sufficient for the LE bad-comparison corollary, not the full `Y_gt(a)` adoption-date-indexed family | **Defined** |
| `CausalAssumptions.AdoptionDate.not_le_of_lt` / `lt_of_isInf` | `WithTop`-order helpers | `AdoptionDate.lt` rules out `AdoptionDate.le`; never-treated cohorts are untreated in every period | **Proved** |
| `CausalAssumptions.Ybar_eq_Ybar0_on_S0_TN` / `_of_inf` / `_on_S0_EL` / `_late_on_S1_EL` / `_late_on_S0_LE` | `Ybar P g S = Ybar0 Y0 g S` (under the appropriate side conditions) | Cell-by-cell consistency reduces factual `Ybar` to never-treated `Ybar0` on each cohort-window pair where the cohort is untreated | **Proved** |
| `CausalAssumptions.Ybar_eq_Ybar0_add_ATT_of_treated` | `(вҲҖ t вҲҲ S, A_g вүӨ t) вҶ’ Ybar P g S = Ybar0 Y0 g S + ATT_window Y0 Y1 g S` | On a treated window, factual `Ybar` decomposes as never-treated `Ybar0` plus the window ATT. Used to extract ATT contributions from each comparison's treated arm | **Proved** |
| `О”_TN_eq_ATT` | `О”_TN P g u = ATT_window Y0 Y1 g (S1_TN P g)` | **Layer C corollary 1** (`cor:po-estimand-goodman-bacon-causal-characterization`): under `CausalAssumptions`, the treated-vs-never contrast identifies the treated cohort's window ATT. Proof: reduce `Ybar` of untreated arms to `Ybar0`, apply `parallelTrends_TN` to cancel untreated trends, isolate the treated-arm ATT contribution | **Proved** |
| `О”_EL_eq_ATT` | `О”_EL P e в„“ = ATT_window Y0 Y1 e (S1_EL P e в„“)` | **Layer C corollary 2**: the early-vs-late-before-late contrast identifies the early cohort's window ATT. Proof: same shape as `О”_TN_eq_ATT`; uses the late-cohort untreated identity on both EL windows and `parallelTrends_EL` | **Proved** |
| `О”_LE_eq_bad_comparison` | `О”_LE P e в„“ = ATT_window Y0 Y1 в„“ (S1_LE P e в„“) вҲ’ (ATT_window Y0 Y1 e (S1_LE P e в„“) вҲ’ ATT_window Y0 Y1 e (S0_LE P e в„“))` | **Layer C corollary 3**: the late-vs-early-after-early contrast identifies the late cohort's ATT *minus* a bad-comparison term вҖ” the change in the already-treated early cohort's ATT across the comparison windows. Proof: decompose both arms' factual `Ybar` into `Ybar0 + ATT_window` (each cell is treated for both `e` and `в„“` in the appropriate windows), apply `parallelTrends_LE` to cancel untreated trends, isolate the ATT residuals | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0a-EventStudyContamination. `Panel/EstimandCharacterization/EventStudyContamination/{Setup,Conventional,CellGrid,Contamination,InteractionWeighted,Basic}.lean` вҖ” Sun-Abraham (2021) Event-Study Contamination and IW

Specific-paper finite-cell formalization for Sun-Abraham (2021). `Setup.lean` defines a finite staggered-adoption event-study system with adoption paths encoded as `WithTop (Fin T)`, admissible cohort-relative-time cells, definitional CATT primitives, and the named causal restrictions. `Conventional.lean` proves the residualized-design weight normalizations, conventional TWFE contamination representation, and apparent-pretrends corollary using finite-cell residualized indicator quantities; it consumes the FWL ratio `D.mu = conventionalMuRatio` and the `ConventionalResidualization` orthogonality conditions as hypotheses. `CellGrid.lean` **derives** both: it builds a genuine weighted-support projection on the cohort-relative-time cell grid (`Causalean/Panel/Weighted/*`) and proves `cellGrid_mu_eq_conventionalMuRatio` (closes gap G3) and `cellGrid_provides_residualization` (closes gaps G4/G9) вҖ” replacing the previously-assumed `D.mu = beta` bridge field and the assumed orthogonality fields. `Contamination.lean` exposes the de-scaffolded public theorems that take the genuine `CellGridResidualization` input directly. `InteractionWeighted.lean` keeps the IW DID construction separate and proves the cohort-level DID equality plus convex IW aggregation. `Basic.lean` is the import facade. Companion NL doc: `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimand_characterization/sun_abraham_event_study.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `EventStudyContamination.EventStudySystem` | `structure (T : в„•) { time, cohorts, cohortShare, cellMass, observedPathMean, observedMean, treatedMean, untreatedMean, untreatedPathMean }` | Finite staggered-adoption event-study system with integer period map, finite cohort support, `WithTop (Fin T)` adoption paths (`вҠӨ` as never treated), cell masses, observed means, treated potential-outcome means, and never-treated potential-outcome means | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.finitePath` / `isNeverTreated` / `isEventuallyTreated` / `relTime` / `absorbingTreatment` | adoption-path and relative-time helpers | Sun-Abraham-compatible wrappers around `AdoptionPath` plus relative event time; encode finite adoption paths, `вҲһ`, eventual treatment, and the absorbing treatment path `1{h вүӨ t}` | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.targetPeriods` / `baselinePeriods` / `AdmissibleCell` / `admissibleCells` / `cohortsAtEvent` | finite support helpers | Build finite target/baseline period sets, admissible cohort-relative-time cells, finite cell supports, and `G_e` cohort sets from an explicit event-time support | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.cellMassAtEvent` / `observedCellMean` / `meanCellContrast` / `CATT` / `pathTargetMean` / `pathBaselineMean` | finite-cell averages | Provide the finite mass, observed mean, treated-minus-untreated contrast, definitional CATT `CATT(g,e) = meanCellContrast(g,e)`, and path-level target/baseline means used by conventional and IW statements; this CATT averages over all periods realizing `(g,e)` and coincides with the source point-period CATT when that target-period set is singleton | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.Consistency` / `NoAnticipation` / `MeanParallelUntreated` / `EventStudyCausalRestrictions` | propositions/structure | Named Sun-Abraham causal restrictions: consistency, no anticipation, and mean parallel untreated paths whose additive component is stated using `AdditiveSpan.IsUnitTimeAdditive` | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.CATT_eq_zero_of_noAnticipation` | `NoAnticipation вҶ’ e < 0 вҶ’ CATT g e = 0` | No-anticipation implication that pre-treatment CATTs vanish | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.observedCellMean_eq_fixedEffects_add_CATT` | `EventStudyCausalRestrictions вҶ’ вҲғ alpha lambda, observedCellMean = cardвҒ»В№ * вҲ‘ target (alpha g + lambda t) + CATT(g,e)` | Averaged cell-mean decomposition used in the contamination proof | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.ConventionalDesign` | `structure { eventSupport, includedEvents, omittedEvent, displayedEvent, Rdot, mu }` | Finite conventional event-study design for the coefficient on `R^l`, carrying included/omitted relative times, residualized indicator values, and `mu_l` | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.cellAverage` / `IsEventStudyNuisance` / `ConventionalResidualization` / `FiniteIntegrable` | finite residualization hypotheses | Cell-average helper for nuisance functions, finite-cell nuisance span with shared `AdditiveSpan` unit/time base plus Sun-Abraham included event indicators, the residualized-design orthogonality conditions (now derived вҖ” see `cellGrid_provides_residualization`), and the finite integrability side condition | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.residualDenom` / `residualNumerator` / `conventionalMuRatio` / `omega` | finite residualized design quantities | Denominator `E[Rdot^l R^l]`, numerator `E[Rdot^l Y]`, residualized coefficient ratio, and Sun-Abraham cell weights `omega(l,g,e)` | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.desired_event_weights_sum_one` | `вҲ‘_{g вҲҲ G_l} omega(l,g,l) = 1` | Desired-event-time weight normalization | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.other_included_event_weights_sum_zero` | `e вҲҲ L вҶ’ e вү  l вҶ’ вҲ‘_{g вҲҲ G_e} omega(l,g,e) = 0` | Zero total weight on other included event-time indicators | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.contamination_representation` | `mu_l = вҲ‘_{(g,e)вҲҲC} omega(l,g,e) * CATT(g,e)` | Conventional Sun-Abraham TWFE event-study contamination representation, taking the FWL ratio `D.mu = conventionalMuRatio D` and the `ConventionalResidualization` orthogonality conditions as hypotheses (both now genuinely derivable via `CellGrid.lean`) | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.contamination_representation_split` | desired-event component plus `e вү  l` contamination component | Split form of the contamination representation | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.apparent_pretrends_from_post_treatment` | lead coefficient equals the sum over cells with `e вүҘ 0` under `EventStudyCausalRestrictions` and a negative displayed event time | Apparent-pretrends corollary; derives vanishing pre-treatment CATTs from no anticipation | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.CellIndex` / `cellTotalMass` / `cellSupport` (`CellGrid.lean`) | cell-grid empirical population | The admissible-cell subtype index, total admissible cell mass `Z`, and the `WeightedSupport` over the cell grid with weight `cellMassAtEvent / Z` (requires positive cell masses + nonempty admissible set) | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.ip_cellSupport` (`CellGrid.lean`) | `cellSupport.ip AвҲҳval BвҲҳval = (вҲ‘ cellMassAtEventВ·AВ·B)/Z` | The cell-grid weighted inner product reproduces the finite-cell sum (divided by total mass) вҖ” the bridge turning weighted-projection facts into the finite-cell orthogonality conditions | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.cellNuisance` / `cellAverage_mem_cellNuisance` / `eventIndicator_mem_cellNuisance` (`CellGrid.lean`) | cell-nuisance subspace + memberships | Span of cell-evaluated event-study nuisance functions; every cell-evaluated nuisance and every non-displayed included relative-time indicator lies in it | **Defined**/**Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.sum_admissible_mul_eventIndicator` (`CellGrid.lean`) | `вҲ‘ admissible fВ·1{e=e'} = вҲ‘_{G_{e'}} f g e'` | Cell-grid filtering identity; the engine behind `hDisplayedExpansion` and `hOtherIncludedOrthogonal` | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.cellRegressor` / `cellOutcome` / `CellGridResidualization` (`CellGrid.lean`) | genuine residualization input | Displayed-event indicator and observed-cell-mean as cell functions, and the honest hypothesis bundle: positive cell masses, `Rdot` **is** the weighted residual of the regressor against the nuisance span, and `D.mu` solves the weighted normal equations | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.cellGrid_mu_eq_conventionalMuRatio` (`CellGrid.lean`) | `CellGridResidualization вҶ’ D.mu = conventionalMuRatio D` | **Closes gap G3**: derives the FWL ratio from the weighted normal equations via `WeightedSupport.scalar_fwl_of_normalEqs` (no assumed `D.mu = beta`) | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.cellGrid_provides_residualization` (`CellGrid.lean`) | `CellGridResidualization вҶ’ ConventionalResidualization D` | **Closes gaps G4/G9**: derives the three finite-cell orthogonality conditions from `residualize_in_orthogonal` + the filtering identity (no assumed orthogonality fields) | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.contamination_representation_of_cellGrid` / `contamination_representation_split_of_cellGrid` / `apparent_pretrends_from_post_treatment_of_cellGrid` (`Contamination.lean`) | genuine public entry points taking `CellGridResidualization` | De-scaffolded contamination theorems: take the genuine cell-grid projection input and derive `ConventionalResidualization` + the FWL ratio internally before applying the finite-cell contamination algebra | **Proved** |
| `EventStudyContamination.PathConsistency` / `pathConsistency_observed_eq_untreated` | `absorbingTreatment h t = 0 вҶ’ observedPathMean h t = untreatedPathMean h t` | Path-level consistency primitive + lemma converting an untreated comparison path's observed mean to its never-treated potential-outcome mean; now DERIVES the IW observed/untreated bridge (replacing two assumed `IWSupport` fields) | **Defined**/**Proved** |
| `EventStudyContamination.targetPeriods_subsingleton_of_injective` / `CATT_eq_sourceCATT_of_injective` | `Function.Injective P.time вҶ’ CATT g e = treatedMean g t вҲ’ untreatedMean g t` at the unique `tвҲҲtargetPeriods g e` | Under injective `time`, the cell-averaged CATT collapses to the source point value CATT_{g,e} at period g+e | **Proved** |
| `EventStudyContamination.sum_convex_mem_Icc` | nonneg weights summing to one keep a weighted average in `[lo,hi]` | Finite convex-combination bound helper | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.IWDesign` / `IWSupport` / `IWComparisonParallelTrends` / `IWFiniteIntegrable` | IW DID data and assumptions | Interaction-weighted cohort set, comparison groups, aggregation weights, support restrictions, comparison-group parallel trends, and finite integrability side condition. NOTE: the former observed/untreated bridge fields `hComparisonObservedBaseline`/`hComparisonObservedTarget` were dropped from `IWSupport` вҖ” now derived via `PathConsistency` | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.observedTargetMean` / `observedBaselineMean` / `comparisonMass` / `comparisonMeanChange` / `DIDContrast` / `Delta` / `nuIW` | IW estimand primitives | Finite treated-cohort target/baseline means, comparison-group population mass, population-share weighted comparison-group mean change, cohort-specific DID contrast, definitional `Delta(g,l) = DIDContrast(g,l)`, and interaction-weighted event-study estimand `nu_l^IW` | **Defined** |
| `EventStudySystem.IW_Delta_eq_CATT` | `PathConsistency вҶ’ Delta(g,l) = CATT(g,l)` | Cohort-specific IW DID identification under consistency, no anticipation, IW parallel trends, support, path consistency, and finite integrability | **Proved** |
| `EventStudySystem.IW_convex_characterization` | `PathConsistency вҶ’ hLo вҶ’ hHi вҶ’ (вҲҖ g вҲҲ G_l^IW, Delta(g,l)=CATT(g,l)) вҲ§ nu_l^IW = вҲ‘ rho(g,l)*CATT(g,l) вҲ§ lo вүӨ nuIW вҲ§ nuIW вүӨ hi` | Convex IW characterization with nonnegative weights summing to one, no contamination from `e вү  l`, and a 4-way conjunction: now also takes `PathConsistency` and value bounds `hLo`/`hHi` and concludes `lo вүӨ nuIW вҲ§ nuIW вүӨ hi` (IW is genuinely a CONVEX combination of target CATTs вҖ” the paper's selling point, using the nonneg/sum-one weight hypotheses) | **Proved** |

---

## 9i0b. `PO/ID/Exact/RDD/RDDLimits.lean` вҖ” One-sided Limit Identification Engine

Generic topology + measure-theory engine shared by SharpRDD and FuzzyRDD.
All declarations live in `namespace Causalean.PO.RDDLimits`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `oneSidedLimit_eq_right` | `f =бөҗ[ПҖ.restrict (Ici c)] g вҶ’ ContinuousAt g c вҶ’ support_right вҶ’ Tendsto f (рқ“қ[>] c) (рқ“қ L) вҶ’ L = g c` | If `f` agrees a.e. with a continuous `g` on `Ici c` and there is positive mass to the right of `c`, any right-limit `L` of `f` at `c` equals `g c` | **Proved** |
| `oneSidedLimit_eq_left` | symmetric form for `Iio c` / `рқ“қ[<] c` | Left-side analogue of `oneSidedLimit_eq_right` | **Proved** |
| `value_eq_of_aeEq_right` | `f =бөҗ[ПҖ.restrict (Ici c)] g вҶ’ ContinuousAt f c вҶ’ ContinuousAt g c вҶ’ support_right вҶ’ f c = g c` | If both functions are continuous at `c` and agree a.e. on `Ici c` with positive right support, they are pointwise equal at `c` | **Proved** |
| `value_eq_of_aeEq_left` | symmetric form | Left-side analogue | **Proved** |

---

## 9i'. `PO/ID/Exact/RDD/SharpRDD.lean` вҖ” Sharp RDD Identification (PO framework)

Formalizes the sharp regression discontinuity example from `Basic Concepts.tex`
(`def:po-sharp-rdd-system`, `def:po-sharp-rdd-assumptions`, and
`prop:po-sharp-rdd`) in the bare potential-outcome framework.  This is the
first exact-ID example in the project to expose pointwise regression-function
representatives and one-sided topological limits at a cutoff.  The proof
faithfully derives `ОҪ =бөҗ[ПҖ.restrict] Ој_d` on each half-line from sharp cutoff +
consistency + the integral characterisation of regression representatives,
then upgrades a.e. agreement to limit equality via two-sided local support.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IsRegressionFunction` | imported from `PO/Analysis/Regression.lean` | Lightweight primitive for a regression representative `f(x) = E[g \| X=x]`; see `## 9i0. PO/Analysis/Regression.lean` for the full declaration and derived lemmas | **Imported** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem P` | `structure { Xvar, Dvar, Yvar, c, hYD }` | Sharp RDD subsystem: real running variable `X`, binary treatment `D`, real outcome `Y`, cutoff `c`, and distinctness of `Y` from the treatment node (for consistency rewrites) | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.{factualX, factualD, factualY}` | `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` / `P.О© вҶ’ Bool` / `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Empty-regime factual running variable, treatment, and outcome | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.YofD` | `Bool вҶ’ P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Treatment-specific potential outcome `Y(d) := Yvar.cfUnder Dvar d` | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.{measurable_factualX, measurable_factualD, measurable_factualY, measurable_YofD}` | `Measurable вҖҰ` | Basic measurability lemmas for factual and counterfactual maps | **Proved** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.dEvent` | `Bool вҶ’ Set P.О©` | Factual treatment event `{D = d}` | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, sharpCutoff, mu, nu, mu_isReg, nu_isReg, mu_continuousAt, support_right, support_left, nu_right_limit_exists, nu_left_limit_exists }` | Sharp RDD assumptions: consistency, a.s. sharp cutoff `D = 1{X вүҘ c}`, potential-outcome regressions `Ој_d` and observable regression `ОҪ` (each a witnessed `IsRegressionFunction`), continuity of each `Ој_d` at `c`, per-`Оө > 0` positive `(P.Ој.map X)`-mass on every one-sided open neighborhood of `c` (two-sided local support), and existence of both one-sided limits of `ОҪ` at `c` (matching the classical `lim` notation in the textbook statement) | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.tau_RDD` | `S.Assumptions вҶ’ в„қ` | Cutoff-local estimand `Ој_1(c) - Ој_0(c)`, by definition of the chosen treatment-specific regression representatives | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.{nu_right_limit, nu_left_limit}` | `S.Assumptions вҶ’ в„қ` | Chosen witnesses of `lim_{xвҶ“c} ОҪ(x)` and `lim_{xвҶ‘c} ОҪ(x)` via `Classical.choose` of the existence fields | **Defined** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.{tendsto_nu_right_limit, tendsto_nu_left_limit}` | `Tendsto ОҪ (рқ“қ[>] c) (рқ“қ (nu_right_limit hA))` etc. | Spec lemmas: the chosen witnesses are genuine one-sided limits | **Proved** |
| private slice-bridge lemmas | вҖ” | Sharp cutoff + consistency: on every measurable slice `XвҒ»В№ A` with `A вҠҶ Ici c` (resp. `A вҠҶ Iio c`), `factualY` agrees a.e. with `Y(1)` (resp. `Y(0)`), so the corresponding integrals вҖ” and hence the integral characterisations of `ОҪ` and `Ој_d` вҖ” coincide on these slices | **Proved** |
| private pushforward bridges | `setIntegral_pushforward_eq_right/left` | Translate the slice-level integral identity to the pushforward `(P.Ој.map X)` via `setIntegral_map`: integrals of `ОҪ` and `Ој_d` agree on every measurable subset of the corresponding half-line | **Proved** |
| private a.e. agreement | `nu_eq_mu_true_ae_restrict_Ici`, `nu_eq_mu_false_ae_restrict_Iio` | Apply `Integrable.ae_eq_of_forall_setIntegral_eq` on the restricted pushforward to upgrade the integral identity to `ОҪ =бөҗ[(P.Ој.map X).restrict (Ici c)] Ој_1` and the symmetric left version | **Proved** |
| private extraction lemmas | `exists_eq_in_Ioo_right/left` | For every `Оө > 0`, the support assumption combined with a.e. agreement yields a point in `(c, c+Оө)` (resp. `(c-Оө, c)`) where `ОҪ = Ој_d`, via `Measure.exists_mem_of_measure_ne_zero_of_ae` | **Proved** |
| private NeBot lemmas | `neBot_right/left` | The agreement-restricted right (resp. left) neighborhood filter `рқ“қ[>] c вҠ“ рқ“ҹ {ОҪ = Ој_d}` is non-trivial | **Proved** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.nu_right_limit_eq` | `Tendsto ОҪ (рқ“қ[>] c) (рқ“қ L) вҶ’ L = Ој_1 c` | Any right-limit of the observable regression at the cutoff equals `Ој_1 c`; proved by uniqueness of limits along the agreement subfilter, using continuity of `Ој_1` | **Proved** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.nu_left_limit_eq` | `Tendsto ОҪ (рқ“қ[<] c) (рқ“қ L) вҶ’ L = Ој_0 c` | Symmetric left-limit identification | **Proved** |
| `POSharpRDDSystem.rdd_identification` | `tau_RDD hA = nu_right_limit hA - nu_left_limit hA` | **Sharp RDD identification at the cutoff** (textbook form of `prop:po-sharp-rdd`): the cutoff-local effect equals the difference of the one-sided observable regression limits, `П„_RDD(c) = lim_{xвҶ“c} ОҪ(x) - lim_{xвҶ‘c} ОҪ(x)` | **Proved** |

---

## 9i''. `PO/ID/Exact/RDD/FuzzyRDD.lean` вҖ” Fuzzy RDD Identification (PO framework)

Formalizes the fuzzy regression discontinuity example from `Basic Concepts.tex`
(`def:po-fuzzy-rdd-system`, `def:po-fuzzy-rdd-assumptions`, and
`prop:po-fuzzy-rdd`) in the bare potential-outcome framework.  The Lean theorem
uses the regression-representative Wald form described in the implementation
plan: `Ој_D(z, В·)` represents `E[D(z) | X=В·]`, `Ој_Y(z, В·)` represents
`E[Y(D(z)) | X=В·]`, and the observable regressions `ОҪ_D`, `ОҪ_Y` are identified
with the corresponding right/left latent regressions at the cutoff.  Local
exclusion is encoded by the `Y(d)` interface; monotonicity is kept in the
assumption bundle to match the classical fuzzy RDD assumption list, though the
representative-level Wald identity itself only uses the bridge from consistency,
cutoff eligibility, continuity, support, and one-sided limit existence.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem P` | `structure { Xvar, Zvar, Dvar, Yvar, c, hYD, hZD, hDY }` | Fuzzy RDD subsystem: real running variable `X`, binary cutoff-eligibility instrument `Z`, binary treatment `D`, real outcome `Y`, cutoff `c`, and distinctness witnesses for consistency rewrites | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{factualX, factualZ, factualD, factualY}` | `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` / `P.О© вҶ’ Bool` / `P.О© вҶ’ Bool` / `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Empty-regime factual running variable, eligibility instrument, treatment, and outcome | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.DofZ` | `Bool вҶ’ P.О© вҶ’ Bool` | Instrument-specific potential treatment `D(z) := Dvar.cfUnder Zvar z` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.YofD` | `Bool вҶ’ P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Treatment-specific potential outcome `Y(d) := Yvar.cfUnder Dvar d` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.YofDofZ` | `Bool вҶ’ P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Composed potential outcome `Y(D(z))`, implemented as an if-then-else over the Boolean `D(z)` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{measurable_factualX, measurable_factualZ, measurable_factualD, measurable_factualY, measurable_DofZ, measurable_YofD, measurable_YofDofZ}` | `Measurable вҖҰ` | Basic measurability lemmas for factual variables and counterfactual/composed maps | **Proved** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.zEvent` | `Bool вҶ’ Set P.О©` | Factual eligibility event `{Z = z}` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.Assumptions S` | `structure { consistency, cutoffEligibility, monotonicity, muD, muY, nuD, nuY, *_isReg, *_continuousAt, support_right, support_left, one-sided limit witnesses, firstStageJump }` | Fuzzy RDD assumptions: consistency, a.s. cutoff rule `Z = 1{X вүҘ c}`, monotonicity `D(1) вүҘ D(0)`, latent and observable regression representatives with integral characterisations, continuity of latent regressions at `c`, two-sided local support for the law of `X`, existence of observable one-sided limits, and a nonzero latent first-stage jump at the cutoff | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.tau_FRD` | `S.Assumptions вҶ’ в„қ` | Regression-representative cutoff Wald estimand `(Ој_Y(1,c)-Ој_Y(0,c))/(Ој_D(1,c)-Ој_D(0,c))`, the formal counterpart used for `prop:po-fuzzy-rdd` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{nuD_right_limit, nuD_left_limit, nuY_right_limit, nuY_left_limit}` | `S.Assumptions вҶ’ в„қ` | Chosen witnesses of the four one-sided observable limits via `Classical.choose` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{tendsto_nuD_right_limit, tendsto_nuD_left_limit, tendsto_nuY_right_limit, tendsto_nuY_left_limit}` | `Tendsto вҖҰ` | Spec lemmas: the chosen witnesses are genuine one-sided limits of `ОҪ_D` and `ОҪ_Y` | **Proved** |
| private D-side bridge lemmas | вҖ” | Cutoff eligibility + consistency: on right/left measurable slices of `X`, factual treatment agrees a.e. with `D(1)`/`D(0)`, yielding integral and pushforward identities between `ОҪ_D` and `Ој_D` | **Proved** |
| private Y-side bridge lemmas | вҖ” | Consistency rewrites `Y` to `Y(factualD)` and then, on `{Z=z}`, to `Y(D(z))`; combined with the regression representative identities this gives the analogous bridges between `ОҪ_Y` and `Ој_Y` | **Proved** |
| private a.e. agreement and extraction lemmas | вҖ” | Apply `Integrable.ae_eq_of_forall_setIntegral_eq`, local support, and `Measure.exists_mem_of_measure_ne_zero_of_ae` to obtain agreement points arbitrarily close to `c` on both sides for treatment and outcome regressions | **Proved** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.{nuD_right_limit_eq, nuD_left_limit_eq, nuY_right_limit_eq, nuY_left_limit_eq}` | `Tendsto ОҪ_* (рқ“қ[>/<] c) (рқ“қ L) вҶ’ L = Ој_*(z,c)` | Any one-sided observable regression limit equals the corresponding latent regression representative at the cutoff, by uniqueness of limits along the agreement subfilter | **Proved** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.frd_identification` | `tau_FRD hA = (nuY_right_limit hA - nuY_left_limit hA) / (nuD_right_limit hA - nuD_left_limit hA)` | **Fuzzy RDD identification at the cutoff** (regression-representative form of `prop:po-fuzzy-rdd`): the cutoff Wald estimand equals the Wald ratio of one-sided observable regression limits | **Proved** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.YdiffComplier` | `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | `(Y(1) - Y(0)) В· 1_{D(1)=1 вҲ§ D(0)=0}`: the Y-difference weighted by the complier indicator; equals `Y(D(1)) - Y(D(0))` a.e. under monotonicity | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.Assumptions.{mu_Ydiff_complier, mu_Ydiff_complier_isReg, mu_Ydiff_complier_continuousAt}` | `в„қ вҶ’ в„қ`, `IsRegressionFunction`, `ContinuousAt` | Regression representative for `E[(Y(1)-Y(0))В·1_C \| X=В·]`, its `IsRegressionFunction` witness, and continuity at `c`; together they support the LATE bridge | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.tau_LATE` | `S.Assumptions вҶ’ в„қ` | Cutoff-local LATE `E[Y(1)-Y(0) \| X=c, C_c]` in regression-representative form: `mu_Ydiff_complier c / (muD true c - muD false c)` | **Defined** |
| `POFuzzyRDDSystem.tau_late_identification` | `tau_LATE hA = tau_FRD hA` | **Fuzzy RDD identifies cutoff-local LATE** (`prop:po-fuzzy-rdd`): under all fuzzy RDD assumptions including monotonicity, the Wald ratio `П„_FRD` equals the cutoff-local LATE `П„_LATE = E[Y(1)-Y(0)\|X=c, C_c]`; proved via `IsRegressionFunction.sub` + monotonicity case analysis + `aeEq_of_aeEq_response` + continuity at `c` | **Proved** |

---

## 9j. `PO/ID/Partial/Manski.lean` вҖ” Manski ATE Bounds under a Discrete IV (PO framework)

Formalizes the **Manski bound family** for the ATE under a discrete IV,
from `Basic Concepts.tex` В§`subsec:po-iv-manski`, В§`subsec:po-iv-manski-shape`,
and `rem:manski-bound-family` (lines ~1561вҖ“2010).  Split into one file per
shape restriction.  Instrument value space `Оұ` is arbitrary with
`MeasurableSingletonClass`; everything lives on the event-level
`eventCondExp` layer plus `POVar` consistency (no `CondIndepFun` stubs).

Layout:

- `Manski/Setup.lean` вҖ” data layer (`POManskiIVSystem`) + bound functionals.
- `Manski/Assumptions.lean` вҖ” `BaseAssumptions` + shape restrictions.
- `Manski/Helpers.lean` вҖ” shared utilities + conditional stratum bounds.
- `Manski/NonAsp.lean` вҖ” prop:po-iv-manski (baseline / mean-independence).
- `Manski/MTR.lean` вҖ” prop:po-iv-mtr (monotone treatment response).
- `Manski/MTS.lean` вҖ” prop:po-iv-mts (monotone treatment selection).
- `Manski/MIV.lean` вҖ” prop:po-iv-miv (monotone IV; integrated forms under `[Fintype Оұ]`).
- `Manski/Combined.lean` вҖ” cor:po-iv-mtr-mts, cor:po-iv-mtr-miv.
- `Manski.lean` вҖ” thin re-export.

### Setup (`Manski/Setup.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `POManskiIVSystem P Оұ` | `structure { Z, D, Y, hZ, hDbool, hYreal, hZD, hZY, hDY }` | Discrete-IV subsystem over a PO system: instrument `Z : Оұ`, binary `D`, real `Y`, pairwise distinctness | **Defined** |
| `zVar` / `dVar` / `yVar` | `POVar P Оұ` / `POVar P Bool` / `POVar P в„қ` | `POVar` wrappers around `Z`, `D`, `Y` | **Defined** |
| `YofD S d` | `P.О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Counterfactual outcome `Y(d) := yVar.cfUnder dVar d` | **Defined** |
| `factualZ` / `factualD` / `factualY` | `P.О© вҶ’ Оұ / Bool / в„қ` | Factual instrument / treatment / outcome | **Defined** |
| `zEvent S z` / `dEvent S d` | `Set P.О©` | Factual events `{Z = z}` / `{D = d}` | **Defined** |
| measurability lemmas | `measurable_{YofD, factualZ, factualD, factualY}`, `measurableSet_{zEvent, dEvent}` | One-line delegations to `POVar` | **Proved** |
| `ATE` | `в„қ := вҲ« (YofD true - YofD false) вҲӮP.Ој` | Target parameter, average treatment effect | **Defined** |
| `support S` | `Set Оұ := {z \| P.Ој (zEvent z) вү  0}` | Support of the instrument | **Defined** |
| `boundArm d c z` | `в„қ` | Unified arm bound functional `eventCondExp Ој (zEvent z) (factualY В· dVar.indicator d + c В· dVar.indicator (!d))`; specialises to the four named bounds below | **Defined** |
| `lowerBound1 lo z` | `в„қ := boundArm true lo z` | Observable `L_{1,z}`; now a one-liner on `boundArm` | **Defined** |
| `upperBound1 hi z` | `в„қ := boundArm true hi z` | Observable `U_{1,z}`; one-liner on `boundArm` | **Defined** |
| `lowerBound0 lo z` | `в„қ := boundArm false lo z` | Observable `L_{0,z}`; one-liner on `boundArm` | **Defined** |
| `upperBound0 hi z` | `в„қ := boundArm false hi z` | Observable `U_{0,z}`; one-liner on `boundArm` | **Defined** |

### Assumptions (`Manski/Assumptions.lean`)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `BaseAssumptions S` | `structure { consistency, lo, hi, hle, bounded_one, bounded_zero, integrable_{Y1,Y0} }` | Shared hypotheses: consistency, outcome bounds `lo вүӨ Y(d) вүӨ hi` a.s., integrability of `Y(1)` and `Y(0)`. Factual-`Y` integrability is *derived*, not bundled (see `integrable_factualY`) | **Defined** |
| `BaseAssumptions.bounded hA d` | `вҲҖбөҗ Пү вҲӮP.Ој, hA.lo вүӨ S.YofD d Пү вҲ§ S.YofD d Пү вүӨ hA.hi` | Binary-folded form of `bounded_one` / `bounded_zero`: `Y(d)` is a.s. in `[lo, hi]` uniformly in `d : Bool`. Derived by `cases d` | **Proved** |
| `BaseAssumptions.integrable_YofD hA d` | `Integrable (S.YofD d) P.Ој` | Binary-folded form of `integrable_Y1` / `integrable_Y0` | **Proved** |
| `BaseAssumptions.integrable_factualY hA` | `Integrable S.factualY P.Ој` | Factual outcome integrability, *derived* from arm integrability via consistency (`factualY = ОЈ_d Y(d)В·1{D=d}` a.e.); no longer an assumed field | **Proved** |
| `MeanIndep S` | `Prop { meanIndep_one, meanIndep_zero }` | Mean independence `E[Y(d) \| Z=z] = E[Y(d)]` on `eventCondExp`, for `d вҲҲ {0,1}` | **Defined** |
| `MTR S` | `Prop { monotone }` | Monotone treatment response: `Y(0) вүӨ Y(1)` a.s. | **Defined** |
| `MTS S` | `Prop { mts_one }` | Monotone treatment selection: `eventCondExp Ој (dEvent false) (YofD d) вүӨ eventCondExp Ој (dEvent true) (YofD d)` for `d вҲҲ {0,1}` | **Defined** |
| `MIV S` | `structure { inst, monotone }` | Monotone instrumental variable: bundled `LinearOrder вҶ‘S.support` and monotonicity of `u вҶҰ eventCondExp Ој (zEvent u) (YofD d)` on the support | **Defined** |

| pointwise fill-in lemmas | `dVar_inds_at_Пү` (private), `YofD_indD_plus_lo_le_YofD`, `YofD_le_YofD_indD_plus_hi` | a.e. "floor/ceiling fill-in" bounds; `dVar_inds_at_Пү` packages the binary case split for `(indicator d, indicator (!d))` | **Proved** |
| `boundArm_lo_le_cond_YofD` | `[IsFiniteMeasure], BaseAssumptions, d : Bool, z вҲҲ support вҶ’ boundArm d hA.lo z вүӨ eventCondExp Ој (zEvent z) (YofD d)` | Parametric conditional stratum lower bound; the mean-indep-free core used by `NonAsp` and `MIV` | **Proved** |

| `mtr_E_Y0_le_E_Y` | Step 4: `вҲ« Y(0) вүӨ вҲ« Y` symmetrically | **Proved** |
| `mtr_E_Y1_le_upper` | Step 2: `вҲ« Y(1) вүӨ pВ·E[Y\|D=1] + (1-p)В·hi` via total-law decomposition + consistency + ceiling bound | **Proved** |
| `mtr_lower_le_E_Y0` | Step 3: `(1-p)В·E[Y\|D=0] + pВ·lo вүӨ вҲ« Y(0)` | **Proved** |
| `mtr_nonneg_ATE` | `0 вүӨ ATE` from MTR a.s. inequality | **Proved** |
| `mtr_bounds_ATE` | Two-sided ATE sandwich: `0 вүӨ ATE вүӨ (pВ·E[Y\|D=1]+(1-p)В·hi) - ((1-p)В·E[Y\|D=0]+pВ·lo)` | **Proved** |

| `mts_bounds_ATE` | Two-sided MTS ATE sandwich, obtained by subtracting the four arm bounds | **Proved** |
