| Symbol | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `mLower1`, `mUpper1`, `mLower0`, `mUpper0` | Envelope functions `sSup { L_{d,u} \| u вүӨ z }` and `sInf { U_{d,u} \| z вүӨ u }` on the MIV linear order | **Defined** |
| `miv_mLower1_le_cond_Y1` | `mLower1 z вүӨ eventCondExp Ој (zEvent z) (YofD true)` on support; chains `Helpers` conditional bound with MIV monotonicity | **Proved** |
| `miv_cond_Y1_le_mUpper1` | analogous upper | **Proved** |
| `miv_mLower0_le_cond_Y0` | lower for arm 0 | **Proved** |
| `miv_cond_Y0_le_mUpper0` | upper for arm 0 | **Proved** |
| `miv_integral_mLower1_le_integral_Y1` | `[Fintype Оұ]`: `вҲ« mLower1(Z) вүӨ вҲ« Y(1)`; decomposes each side via `integral_eq_sum_measure_mul_eventCondExp` and compares stratum-wise, exploiting that off-support strata have measure `0` | **Proved** |
| `miv_integral_Y1_le_integral_mUpper1` | `[Fintype Оұ]`: `вҲ« Y(1) вүӨ вҲ« mUpper1(Z)`; symmetric to the lower bound | **Proved** |
| `miv_integral_mLower0_le_integral_Y0` | `[Fintype Оұ]`: arm 0 integrated lower | **Proved** |
| `miv_integral_Y0_le_integral_mUpper0` | `[Fintype Оұ]`: arm 0 integrated upper | **Proved** |
| `miv_bounds_ATE` | `[Fintype Оұ]`: `вҲ« (mLower1(Z) - mUpper0(Z)) вүӨ ATE вүӨ вҲ« (mUpper1(Z) - mLower0(Z))`; follows by linearity from the four integrated bounds | **Proved** |

### Combined (`Manski/Combined.lean`)

| Symbol | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `mtr_mts_bounds_ATE` | cor:po-iv-mtr-mts: `0 вүӨ ATE вүӨ E[Y\|D=1] - E[Y\|D=0]` under MTR + MTS; combines `mtr_nonneg_ATE` with `mts_E_Y1_le_condY1 вҲ© mts_condY0_le_E_Y0` | **Proved** |
| `mtr_miv_bounds_ATE` | `[Fintype Оұ]`: cor:po-iv-mtr-miv: `0 вүӨ ATE` (MTR) and `ATE вүӨ вҲ« (mUpper1(Z) - mLower0(Z))` (MIV); forwards from `miv_bounds_ATE` | **Proved** |

### IntervalForm (`Manski/IntervalForm.lean`) вҖ” `Set.Icc` restatements

Pure restatements of the ATE sandwiches as `S.ATE вҲҲ Set.Icc lo hi`, obtained by
feeding each `L вүӨ ATE вҲ§ ATE вүӨ U` pair through the
`Causalean.PartialID.mem_Icc_of_sandwich` bridge (`Basic.lean`). No new
mathematical content; same hypotheses/instances as each source theorem.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `manski_ATE_mem_Icc` | theorem | Closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the Manski no-assumption per-stratum-pair ATE sandwich (`manski_bounds_ATE`), via the `mem_Icc_of_sandwich` bridge | **Proved** |
| `manski_ATE_mem_Icc_ciSup` | theorem | Closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the sup/inf-aggregated Manski no-assumption ATE sandwich (`manski_bounds_ATE_ciSup`) | **Proved** |
| `mtr_mts_ATE_mem_Icc` | theorem | Closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the MTR + MTS ATE sandwich (`mtr_mts_bounds_ATE`): `ATE вҲҲ [0, E[Y\|D=1] - E[Y\|D=0]]` | **Proved** |
| `mtr_miv_ATE_mem_Icc` | theorem | `[Fintype Оұ]`: closed-interval `Set.Icc` form of the MTR + MIV ATE sandwich (`mtr_miv_bounds_ATE`): `ATE вҲҲ [0, вҲ« (mUpper1(Z) - mLower0(Z))]` | **Proved** |

**Scope note.** Two baseline aggregation forms are exposed in `NonAsp`: the
per-stratum-pair `manski_bounds_ATE` is strictly stronger (pick any
`zвӮҒ, zвӮҖ вҲҲ support`), while `manski_bounds_ATE_ciSup` matches the tex's
sup/inf form but requires `support.Nonempty` (Lean `вЁҶ`/`вЁ…` fall back to `0`
on empty index sets). The MIV integrated bounds require a `[Fintype Оұ]`
hypothesis on the instrument value space (standard Manski setting with a
discrete IV) and are discharged via the `integral_eq_sum_measure_mul_eventCondExp`
total law in `PO/Conditioning/EventCondExp.lean`; no outstanding `sorry` in the folder.

---

## 9j2. `PO/ID/Partial/` вҖ” Abstract Partial-Identification Bounds Engine

Reusable, PO-framework-free partial-identification primitives. Two files, both in
namespace `Causalean.PartialID`:

- `Basic.lean` вҖ” the sharp identified interval + a sharp-interval API, including the
  two scalar-sandwich bridge helpers (`mem_Icc_of_sandwich`, `mem_Icc_csInf_csSup`)
  that turn concrete `L вүӨ Оё вүӨ U` bounds into the engine's `Set.Icc` vocabulary.
- `Frechet.lean` вҖ” FrГ©chetвҖ“Hoeffding copula bounds + the easy Makarov lower bound.

### `Basic.lean`

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IdentifiedInterval obj feasible` | `Set в„қ` | Sharp identified interval: range of `obj` over all `{x // feasible x}`; the set of all attainable objective values | **Defined** |
| `mem_identifiedInterval hx` | `obj x вҲҲ IdentifiedInterval obj feasible` | If `x` is feasible then `obj x` is in the identified interval | **Proved** |
| `identifiedInterval_subset_Icc hL hU` | `IdentifiedInterval obj feasible вҠҶ Set.Icc L U` | Outer bound: uniform two-sided bounds `L вүӨ obj вүӨ U` on the feasible set imprison the identified set in `[L, U]` | **Proved** |
| `identifiedInterval_eq_Icc hL hU hLmem hUmem hconn` | `IdentifiedInterval obj feasible = Set.Icc L U` | Sharp bound (order-connected form): outer bound + both endpoints attained + identified set order-connected вҮ’ identified set is exactly `[L, U]` | **Proved** |
| `identifiedInterval_param_Icc hfeas hcont hL hU hbound` | `IdentifiedInterval obj feasible = Set.Icc L U` | Mixing-pattern constructor: feasible set = image of `[0,1]` under `Оі`, `objвҲҳОі` continuous, endpoint values `L,U` bracketing `obj` along the path вҮ’ sharp interval `[L,U]` (continuous image of a connected set is order-connected) | **Proved** |
| `mem_Icc_of_sandwich hlo hhi` | `Оё вҲҲ Set.Icc L U` | Scalar-sandwich bridge: the literal reading of a two-sided bound `L вүӨ Оё вүӨ U` as `Оё вҲҲ [L, U]`; names the inequality-pair вҶ’ `Set.Icc` step every concrete bound ends in. Consumers: `Manski`/`Proxy` `IntervalForm` | **Proved** |
| `mem_Icc_csInf_csSup hb ha hy` | `y вҲҲ Set.Icc (sInf s) (sSup s)` | Worst/best case over a nuisance: every member of a bounded set `s вҠҶ в„қ` lies between its infimum and supremum (the `csInf_le`/`le_csSup` step of a sharp bound). Consumers: `Lee/TrimBound`, `BalkePearl/IntervalForm` | **Proved** |

### `Frechet.lean`

Self-contained measure theory over a probability space `P` with two real random
variables `X, Y`; CDF/joint-CDF values written as `.toReal` of event measures.
Only the *easy* (fixed-joint-law) directions are asserted вҖ” no sharpness claim.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `frechet_upper u v` | `(P{XвүӨu вҲ§ YвүӨv}).toReal вүӨ min (P{XвүӨu}).toReal (P{YвүӨv}).toReal` | FrГ©chetвҖ“Hoeffding upper bound on the joint CDF (joint event вҠҶ each marginal event); needs no measurability вҖ” pure measure monotonicity | **Proved** |
| `frechet_lower hY u v` | `max ((P{XвүӨu}).toReal + (P{YвүӨv}).toReal - 1) 0 вүӨ (P{XвүӨu вҲ§ YвүӨv}).toReal` | FrГ©chetвҖ“Hoeffding lower bound on the joint CDF (inclusionвҖ“exclusion `P A + P B - 1 вүӨ P(AвҲ©B)`); only `Measurable Y` needed | **Proved** |
| `makarov_lower_param hY s a` | `max ((P{XвүӨa}).toReal - (P{Y<a-s}).toReal) 0 вүӨ (P{X-YвүӨs}).toReal` | Makarov lower bound on the CDF of the difference `X-Y`, per reference point `a` (from `{XвүӨa}вҲ©{a-sвүӨY} вҠҶ {X-YвүӨs}` + FrГ©chet lower); only `Measurable Y` needed | **Proved** |
| `makarov_lower_iSup hY s` | `вЁҶ a, max ((P{XвүӨa}).toReal - (P{Y<a-s}).toReal) 0 вүӨ (P{X-YвүӨs}).toReal` | Sup-convolution envelope form: the supremum over reference points still lower-bounds the CDF of `X-Y` | **Proved** |

### `RandomSet/Interval.lean`

Tier-0 random-set substrate for partial identification (the measure-theoretic
upgrade of `IdentifiedInterval`). An interval random set is a pair of measurable
endpoints `L, U : О© вҶ’ в„қ` with `L вүӨ U`, viewed as `Пү вҶҰ [L Пү, U Пү]`. Provides the
two facts a sharpness proof needs вҖ” measurable selection and the selection
(Aumann) expectation вҖ” both proved *elementarily* (no KuratowskiвҖ“Ryll-Nardzewski,
no atomlessness/Lyapunov). Endpoints are reported via `sInf`/`sSup` to match the
`SandwichInterval` convention and keep the support-function generalisation a
drop-in. Namespace `Causalean.PartialID.RandomSet`; all sorry-free + axiom-clean.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `randomInterval L U` | `О© вҶ’ Set в„қ` | The interval-valued random closed set `Пү вҶҰ [L Пү, U Пү]` | **Defined** |
| `IsSelection L U f` | `Prop` | `f` is a measurable everywhere-selection: `Measurable f вҲ§ вҲҖ Пү, f Пү вҲҲ [L Пү, U Пү]` | **Defined** |
| `isSelection_left hL hLU` | `IsSelection L U L` | The lower endpoint is always a selection вҮ’ the random set has a measurable selection | **Proved** |
| `isSelection_iff_exists_param hL hU hLU f` | `IsSelection L U f вҶ” вҲғ t, Measurable t вҲ§ (вҲҖ Пү, t Пү вҲҲ [0,1]) вҲ§ вҲҖ Пү, f Пү = L Пү + t ПүВ·(U ПүвҲ’L Пү)` | Measurable selection of an interval random set: every selection is `L + tВ·(UвҲ’L)` for a measurable `t : О© вҶ’ [0,1]`, and conversely (divide-by-width construction) | **Proved** |
| `selectionExpectation L U Ој` | `Set в„қ` | The selection (Aumann) expectation: integrals of integrable measurable selections, `{вҲ«f \| f a selection}` | **Defined** |
| `integral_le_integral_of_le hLint hUint hLU` | `(вҲ«L) вүӨ вҲ«U` | Endpoint integrals are ordered (monotonicity of the Bochner integral) | **Proved** |
| `selectionExpectation_eq_Icc hL hU hLint hUint hLU` | `selectionExpectation L U Ој = Set.Icc (вҲ«L) (вҲ«U)` | **Tier-0 core:** the selection expectation is exactly `[вҲ«L, вҲ«U]` (вҠҶ by integral monotonicity; вҠҮ realised by a *constant* `t вҲҲ [0,1]`, so no atomlessness needed) | **Proved** |
| `sInf_selectionExpectation вҖҰ` | `sInf (selectionExpectation L U Ој) = вҲ«L` | Sharp lower endpoint as the infimum over selections | **Proved** |
| `sSup_selectionExpectation вҖҰ` | `sSup (selectionExpectation L U Ој) = вҲ«U` | Sharp upper endpoint as the supremum over selections | **Proved** |

*Out of scope (flagged for later):* conditional selection expectation
`E[XвҲЈрқ’ў] = [E[LвҲЈрқ’ў], E[UвҲЈрқ’ў]]` (Tier-0.5, for regression envelopes such as
ManskiвҖ“Tamer interval regression), and the support-function / Artstein /
CLR-inference layers.

### `LP/ConicDuality.lean`

Abstract conic linear-programming duality over a pair of **real Hilbert spaces**,
the value-form backbone for sharp partial-ID bounds posed as
`min вҹӘc,xвҹ« s.t. A x = b, x вҲҲ K`. Built on Mathlib's `ProperCone` / `innerDual`.
Hilbert generality is already infinite-dimensional (e.g. `LВІ(Ој)` for proxy/bridge
functions). The *measure*-cone case (weak-вҲ— duality of measures against `C(X)`)
is **out of scope**: Mathlib gives `FiniteMeasure` only a `Module в„қвүҘ0` (no signed
`в„қ`-module), no ordered-cone instance, and a non-order weak-вҲ— topology вҖ” so the
nonnegative-measure `ProperCone` needs new infrastructure (a module note in the
file records this). Namespace `Causalean.PartialID`; weak duality + Farkas are
sorry-free, strong duality is a CQ-gated statement awaiting a modelling decision.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ConicProgram E F` | structure | Primal data `(K, A, b, c)`: cone `K : ProperCone в„қ E`, operator `A : E вҶ’L[в„қ] F`, rhs `b : F`, objective `c : E` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.PrimalFeasible x` | `Prop` | `A x = b вҲ§ x вҲҲ K` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.DualFeasible y` | `Prop` | reduced cost in the dual cone: `c вҲ’ AбөҖ y вҲҲ innerDual K` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.primalValue` | `в„қ` | `sInf { вҹӘc,xвҹ« : x primal feasible }` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.dualValue` | `в„қ` | `sSup { вҹӘb,yвҹ« : y dual feasible }` | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.weak_duality hx hy` | `вҹӘb,yвҹ« вүӨ вҹӘc,xвҹ«` | Pointwise weak duality: any dual-feasible value lower-bounds any primal-feasible value (via `mem_innerDual` + adjoint) | **Proved** |
| `ConicProgram.dualValue_le_primalValue hP hD` | `dualValue вүӨ primalValue` | Value form of weak duality (both feasible; no boundedness needed) | **Proved** |
| `ConicProgram.farkas` | `b вҲҲ ProperCone.map A K вҶ” вҲҖ y, AбөҖ y вҲҲ innerDual K вҶ’ 0 вүӨ вҹӘb,yвҹ«` | Feasibility alternative / strong-duality engine; restatement of `ProperCone.relative_hyperplane_separation`. `map` bakes in the closure of `A(K)` вҖ” exactly the CQ gap | **Proved** |
| `ConicProgram.augmentedImage` | `Set (F Г— в„қ)` | The augmented image cone `{(A x, вҹӘc,xвҹ«) : x вҲҲ K}`; its closedness is the primal-attainment CQ | **Defined** |
| `ConicProgram.strong_duality_primal_attained hP hbdd hCQ` | `вҲғ x, PrimalFeasible x вҲ§ вҹӘc,xвҹ« = primalValue` | Strong duality I (closedness CQ): the primal optimum is attained вҖ” an extremal feasible point exists. Via the closed value-slice + `IsClosed.csInf_mem` | **Proved** |
| `ConicProgram.strong_duality_zero_gap hP hbdd hCQ` | `primalValue = dualValue` | Strong duality II (closedness CQ вҖ” same as I): no duality gap. Closedness suffices (no Slater needed). Proof: for each `Оө>0`, separate `(b, primalValueвҲ’Оө)` from the augmented cone `map Гғ K` (`Гғ x = (A x, вҹӘc,xвҹ«)`) via `ProperCone.hyperplane_separation_point` on the normed product `F Г— в„қ`; Riesz-decompose the functional as `вҹӘyвӮҖ,vвҹ« + sВ·t`, rule out `s вүӨ 0`, and read off the dual-feasible `вҲ’(1/s)вҖўyвӮҖ`. | **Proved** |

---

## 9j2inf. `PO/ID/Partial/Inference/` вҖ” Confidence intervals for interval-identified parameters

First statistical-inference layer over the partial-identification *geometry*. Where the rest of `PartialID/` produces population sandwiches `L вүӨ ОёвӮҖ вүӨ U`, this folder turns them into finite-sample confidence intervals, formalizing the two coverage notions of the literature (HorowitzвҖ“Manski 2000 set coverage vs. ImbensвҖ“Manski 2004 parameter coverage). Built on `Stat/Concentration/ConfidenceInterval` (Hoeffding/Bernstein miss bounds) + a union bound.

### `Inference/Basic.lean` вҖ” coverage vocabulary + abstract honest-CI lemma (pure measure theory)

| Declaration | Type / statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `RandomCoversPoint A B Оё` | `Set О©` | Event that the random interval `[A Пү, B Пү]` contains the fixed value `Оё` (ImbensвҖ“Manski parameter-coverage notion) | **Defined** |
| `RandomCoversIcc A B L U` | `Set О©` | Event that `[A Пү, B Пү]` contains the whole population interval `[L, U]` (HorowitzвҖ“Manski set-coverage notion) | **Defined** |
| `randomCoversIcc_subset_randomCoversPoint` | `Оё вҲҲ [L,U] вҶ’ RandomCoversIcc A B L U вҠҶ RandomCoversPoint A B Оё` | Set coverage вҹ№ point coverage; the easy half of ImbensвҖ“Manski (2004, Lemma 1): parameter-coverage CIs are weakly smaller than set-coverage CIs | **Proved** |
| `randomCoversIcc_compl_subset` | `(RandomCoversIcc (loвҲ’wL) (hi+wU) L U)б¶ң вҠҶ {wL вүӨ |loвҲ’L|} вҲӘ {wU вүӨ |hiвҲ’U|}` | Non-coverage is contained in the two one-sided endpoint-failure events; geometric heart of the union bound | **Proved** |
| `honest_ci_set_cover` | `1 вҲ’ ОҙL вҲ’ ОҙU вүӨ Ој.real (RandomCoversIcc (loвҲ’wL) (hi+wU) L U)` | **Abstract honest CI.** Given one-sided miss bounds `Ој{wLвүӨ|loвҲ’L|}вүӨОҙL`, `Ој{wUвүӨ|hiвҲ’U|}вүӨОҙU`, the widened interval covers `[L,U]` w.p. `вүҘ1вҲ’ОҙLвҲ’ОҙU` (single union bound; stated in miss probabilities so any concentration inequality plugs in) | **Proved** |
| `honest_ci_point_cover` | `Оё вҲҲ [L,U] вҶ’ 1 вҲ’ ОҙL вҲ’ ОҙU вүӨ Ој.real (RandomCoversPoint (loвҲ’wL) (hi+wU) Оё)` | Parameter-coverage corollary: covering the set suffices to cover any point of it (conservative HorowitzвҖ“Manski parameter CI) | **Proved** |

### `Inference/IntervalCI.lean` вҖ” finite-sample plug-in instantiations (Hoeffding / Bernstein)

Plug-in Manski sandwich: bounding statistics `f_L, f_U` with population means `L = вҲ« f_L`, `U = вҲ« f_U`; estimate each endpoint by its sample mean and widen by a concentration half-width.

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `hoeffding_honest_ci_set_cover` | `1вҲ’ОҙLвҲ’ОҙU вүӨ Ој.real (RandomCoversIcc [XМ„вӮҷ(f_L)вҲ’wL, XМ„вӮҷ(f_U)+wU] L U)` | Honest set CI with Hoeffding half-widths for bounded statistics `f_LвҲҲ[aL,bL]`, `f_UвҲҲ[aU,bU]`; valid at every `n` | **Proved** |
| `hoeffding_honest_ci_point_cover` | `ОёвӮҖвҲҲ[вҲ«f_L,вҲ«f_U] вҶ’ 1вҲ’ОҙLвҲ’ОҙU вүӨ Ој.real (RandomCoversPoint вҖҰ ОёвӮҖ)` | Parameter-coverage version (Hoeffding) | **Proved** |
| `bernstein_honest_ci_point_cover` | as above with `bernsteinCIHalfWidth` | Variance-adaptive version; far tighter when the bounding statistics have small variance (leading term `2ПғвҲҡ(log(2/Оҙ)/n)`) | **Proved** |

### `Inference/ImbensManski.lean` вҖ” asymptotic one-sided sharp interval (ImbensвҖ“Manski 2004)

When the identified set has positive width `О” = UвҲ’L > 0`, covering the single true `ОёвӮҖ` (not the whole set) needs protection on only **one** side: the `O(n^{-1/2})` sampling error is asymptotically negligible vs the fixed `О”`, so non-coverage is one-sided and a *one-sided* critical value `c = z_{1вҲ’Оұ}` already gives `1вҲ’Оұ` coverage (vs the two-sided `z_{1вҲ’Оұ/2}` the set-coverage interval needs). Normalized endpoint statistics `SвӮ—,вӮҷ = вҲҡn(ОёМӮвӮ—вҲ’L)/ПғвӮ—`, `SбөӨ,вӮҷ = вҲҡn(ОёМӮбөӨвҲ’U)/ПғбөӨ вҮ’ N(0,1)`; offsets `aвӮҷ=вҲҡn(ОёвӮҖвҲ’L)/ПғвӮ—вүҘ0`, `bвӮҷ=вҲҡn(UвҲ’ОёвӮҖ)/ПғбөӨвүҘ0`. Sources: ImbensвҖ“Manski 2004 (Lemma 1), Stoye 2009, Molinari 2020 В§Pointwise-vs-Uniform.

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gaussianMeasure_zero_one_frontier_Iic c` | `gaussianMeasure 0 1 (frontier (Iic c)) = 0` | Standard normal gives zero mass to the boundary `{c}` of a half-line; continuity-point fact for one-sided portmanteau | **Proved** |
| `farEnd_vanishes_of_tendsto_atBot` | `SбөӨ вҮ’ N(0,1) вҶ’ t вҹ¶ atBot вҶ’ (Ој{SбөӨ,вӮҷ < tвӮҷ}).toReal вҹ¶ 0` | The `О”>0` mechanism: as the far threshold drifts to `вҲ’вҲһ` (`bвӮҷвҶ’вҲһ`), the far-endpoint failure probability vanishes. Bounds the moving tail by a fixed Gaussian tail (`gaussian_tail_small`) + portmanteau | **Proved** |
| `imbensManski_pointwise_coverage` | `вҖҰ вҶ’ вҲҖОө>0, вҲҖб¶  n, ОҰ(c) вҲ’ Оө вүӨ (Ој{SвӮ—,вӮҷвүӨc+aвӮҷ вҲ§ вҲ’(c+bвӮҷ)вүӨSбөӨ,вӮҷ}).toReal` | **Core asymptotic guarantee.** With `SвӮ—вҮ’N(0,1)`, `aвӮҷвүҘ0`, and the far end vanishing, IM coverage of `ОёвӮҖ` eventually exceeds `ОҰ(c)вҲ’Оө` where `ОҰ(c)=N(0,1)((-вҲһ,c])`. One-sided `c=z_{1вҲ’Оұ}` вҹ№ `вүҘ1вҲ’Оұ`. Pointwise (not uniform; Stoye's superefficiency caveat not addressed) | **Proved** |

Supporting lemma `Causalean.Stat.gaussian_tail_small` (Gaussian half-line tails `<Оө` for large cutoff) was factored out of the quantile-Bahadur rate proof into `Causalean/Stat/CLT/GaussianTail.lean` for reuse here.

---

## 9j2sf. `PO/ID/Partial/SupportFunction/` вҖ” Support-Function Identified Sets (convex/Hilbert spine)

The multivariate generalization of the scalar `Basic.lean` engine: when the
feasible set `C` is **convex** in a real inner-product space and the target is the
**linear functional** `x вҶҰ вҹӘd, xвҹ«`, the sharp identified interval is governed by
the *support function* `supportFn C d = sup { вҹӘd,xвҹ« : x вҲҲ C }`. Three files, all in
namespace `Causalean.PartialID`. Boundedness/nonemptiness side conditions are carried
explicitly (the real `sSup` convention), matching `SandwichInterval`/`RandomSet`.
This is the abstraction the proximal-L2-bridge, sensitivity-model, and reweighting
partial-ID problems bottom out in.

- `Basic.lean` вҖ” `supportFn` definition + the two monotone spine lemmas.
- `Calculus.lean` вҖ” the support-function algebra (direction + set operations).
- `Interval.lean` вҖ” sharp identified interval `[-h(-d), h(d)]`, width, point-identification, scalar-engine bridge.
- `AffineBall.lean` вҖ” closed-form endpoints over a Hilbert affine fiber `{A h = b}` intersected with a ball `вҖ–hвҖ– вүӨ B` (the proximal-L2-bridge identified set).

### `Basic.lean`

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `supportFn C d` | `в„қ := sSup ((fun x => вҹӘd,xвҹ«) '' C)` | Support function: best-case value of the linear target `вҹӘd,В·вҹ«` over the feasible set `C` | **Defined** |
| `supportFn_eq_iSup_image C d` | `supportFn C d = sSup (вҖҰ)` | Unfolding lemma exposing the `sSup`-of-image form | **Proved** |
| `le_supportFn hx hbdd` | `вҹӘd,xвҹ« вүӨ supportFn C d` | Lower sandwich: a feasible point's value is `вүӨ` the support value (needs the image bounded above) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_le hne hb` | `supportFn C d вүӨ b` | Upper sandwich: a uniform upper bound `b` on the functional over nonempty `C` bounds the support value | **Proved** |

### `Calculus.lean`

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `supportFn_singleton d xвӮҖ` | `supportFn {xвӮҖ} d = вҹӘd,xвӮҖвҹ«` | Support function of a point is the functional value | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_nonneg_of_zero_mem h0 hbdd` | `0 вүӨ supportFn C d` | `0 вҲҲ C` forces a nonnegative support value | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_smul_dir ht hne hbdd` | `supportFn C (tвҖўd) = t * supportFn C d` | Positive homogeneity in the direction (`t вүҘ 0`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_add_dir_le hne hbвӮҒ hbвӮӮ` | `supportFn C (dвӮҒ+dвӮӮ) вүӨ supportFn C dвӮҒ + supportFn C dвӮӮ` | Subadditivity / sublinearity in the direction | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_mono hCD hne hbdd` | `supportFn C d вүӨ supportFn D d` | Monotone in the set (`C вҠҶ D`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_union hC hD hbC hbD` | `supportFn (CвҲӘD) d = max (supportFn C d) (supportFn D d)` | Union rule | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_inter_le hne hbC hbD` | `supportFn (CвҲ©D) d вүӨ min (supportFn C d) (supportFn D d)` | Intersection rule (one-sided) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_translate xвӮҖ hne hbdd` | `supportFn ((xвӮҖ+В·) '' C) d = вҹӘd,xвӮҖвҹ« + supportFn C d` | Translation shifts the support value | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_smul_set ha hne hbdd` | `supportFn (aвҖўC) d = a * supportFn C d` | Positive set-scaling (`a вүҘ 0`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_minkowski hC hD hbC hbD` | `supportFn (C+D) d = supportFn C d + supportFn D d` | Additivity over Minkowski sums | **Proved** |

### `Interval.lean`

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `neg_supportFn_neg_le hx hbdd'` | `-supportFn C (-d) вүӨ вҹӘd,xвҹ«` | Lower endpoint `-h(-d)` bounds the functional from below (worst case) | **Proved** |
| `linearImage_subset_Icc hbdd hbdd'` | `(вҹӘd,В·вҹ«) '' C вҠҶ Set.Icc (-supportFn C (-d)) (supportFn C d)` | Outer bound: identified set sits inside the support interval | **Proved** |
| `linearImage_ordConnected hC` | `((вҹӘd,В·вҹ«) '' C).OrdConnected` | Convex `C` вҮ’ identified set has no gaps (linear image of convex is convex in `в„қ`) | **Proved** |
| `linearImage_eq_Icc hC hbdd hbdd' hUmem hLmem` | `(вҹӘd,В·вҹ«) '' C = Set.Icc (-supportFn C (-d)) (supportFn C d)` | **Sharp** interval (attainment form): convex + both endpoints attained вҮ’ identified set is exactly `[-h(-d), h(d)]` | **Proved** |
| `linearImage_eq_Icc_of_isCompact hcomp hC hne` | `(вҹӘd,В·вҹ«) '' C = Set.Icc (-supportFn C (-d)) (supportFn C d)` | **Sharp** interval (compact convex form): the workhorse вҖ” continuity gives bounded + attained automatically | **Proved** |
| `width C d` | `в„қ := supportFn C d + supportFn C (-d)` | Width of the identified set (upper minus lower endpoint) | **Defined** |
| `width_nonneg hne hbdd hbdd'` | `0 вүӨ width C d` | The identified width is nonnegative | **Proved** |
| `width_eq_zero_iff hne hbdd hbdd'` | `width C d = 0 вҶ” вҲҖ x вҲҲ C, вҹӘd,xвҹ« = supportFn C d` | **Point identification**: zero width iff the target is constant on `C` | **Proved** |
| `identifiedInterval_eq_image obj C` | `IdentifiedInterval obj (В· вҲҲ C) = obj '' C` | Bridge: the scalar `Basic.lean` engine's identified set is the set-image of the objective (so concrete theorems consume either vocabulary) | **Proved** |

### `AffineBall.lean`

Closed-form endpoints over the **bridge fiber** `affineBall A b B = {h | A h = b вҲ§ вҖ–hвҖ– вүӨ B}` in a real Hilbert space `H` (operator `A : H вҶ’L[в„қ] F`). Decomposing `h = hвӮҖ + v` (min-norm solution `hвӮҖ вҠҘ ker A`, free direction `v вҲҲ ker A`), the Pythagorean split + CauchyвҖ“Schwarz give the sharp value/width in terms of `P c = orthogonalProjection (ker A) c`. This is the `pid_proximal_l2_bridge` identified set.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `opKer A` | `Submodule в„қ H := LinearMap.ker вҶ‘A` | Free-direction subspace `ker A` of the operator (closed; has an orthogonal projection вҖ” instance provided) | **Defined** |
| `affineBall A b B` | `Set H := {h | A h = b вҲ§ вҖ–hвҖ– вүӨ B}` | The affine-ball feasible fiber: equation solutions inside the norm ball | **Defined** |
| `mem_affineBall` | `h вҲҲ affineBall A b B вҶ” A h = b вҲ§ вҖ–hвҖ– вүӨ B` | Membership unfolding (`@[simp]`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_affineBall_eq A hsol hperp hfit` | `supportFn (affineBall A b B) c = вҹӘc,hвӮҖвҹ« + вҲҡ(BВІвҲ’вҖ–hвӮҖвҖ–ВІ)В·вҖ–P cвҖ–` | Closed-form support value (best case), given min-norm solution `hвӮҖ вҠҘ ker A` fitting the ball | **Proved** |
| `width_affineBall_eq A hsol hperp hfit` | `width (affineBall A b B) c = 2В·вҲҡ(BВІвҲ’вҖ–hвӮҖвҖ–ВІ)В·вҖ–P cвҖ–` | Closed-form identified width | **Proved** |
| `affineBall_point_identified_iff A hsol hperp hfit` | `width (affineBall A b B) c = 0 вҶ” P c = 0` | **Point identification** (strict ball slack `вҖ–hвӮҖвҖ–<B`): collapse iff `c вҠҘ ker A`, i.e. `c` in the closed row space of `A` | **Proved** |

---

## 9j2rs. `PO/ID/Partial/RandomSet/` вҖ” Random sets, Hausdorff distance & the support-function bridge (BeresteanuвҖ“Molinari 2008)

The estimation/inference layer for set-identified parameters. Beresteanu & Molinari (2008) show the whole inference theory rests on one identity (HГ¶rmander's embedding, their eq. (A.1)): for compact convex `A,B`, `H(A,B) = sup_{вҖ–pвҖ–=1} |s(p,A) вҲ’ s(p,B)|`. This folder builds that bridge, its `d = 1` (interval) specialization (the scalar interval-data CLT, Theorems 3.1/3.2, in `IntervalCLT.lean`), and the general-`d` finite-grid support-process CLT on genuine random sets (`SetValued.lean` + `SupportProcess.lean`, Theorem A.2). Plan: `doc/beresteanu_molinari_plan.md`. All declarations **proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean**.

### `Interval.lean` вҖ” interval random sets & Aumann (selection) expectation (Tier-0, pre-existing)

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `randomInterval L U` | `О© вҶ’ Set в„қ := fun Пү => Icc (L Пү) (U Пү)` | The interval-valued random closed set `Пү вҶҰ [L Пү, U Пү]` | **Defined** |
| `IsSelection L U f` | `Prop` | `f` is a measurable everywhere-selection of `[L,U]` | **Defined** |
| `isSelection_iff_exists_param` | selection вҶ” `L + tВ·(UвҲ’L)`, `t : О©вҶ’[0,1]` measurable | Elementary measurable selection (no KuratowskiвҖ“Ryll-Nardzewski) | **Proved** |
| `selectionExpectation L U Ој` | `Set в„қ` | Selection (Aumann) expectation: integrals of integrable selections | **Defined** |
| `selectionExpectation_eq_Icc` | `= Icc (вҲ«L) (вҲ«U)` | **Aumann expectation `= [вҲ«L, вҲ«U]`** (`вҠҮ` uses a constant `t`, no atomlessness) | **Proved** |
| `sInf_selectionExpectation` / `sSup_selectionExpectation` | `= вҲ«L` / `= вҲ«U` | Sharp endpoints as inf/sup over selections | **Proved** |

### `Hausdorff.lean` вҖ” directed/symmetric Hausdorff distance + the geometric keystone

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `directedHausdorff A B` | `в„қ := sSup ((fun a => infDist a B) '' A)` | One-sided (directed) Hausdorff distance `sup_{aвҲҲA} infDist a B` | **Defined** |
| `hausdorffDist A B` | `в„қ := max (dбҙҙ A B) (dбҙҙ B A)` | Symmetric Hausdorff distance | **Defined** |
| `infDist_Icc hcd x` | `infDist x [c,d] = max 0 (max (cвҲ’x) (xвҲ’d))` | Distance from a real point to an interval (zero inside; signed gap outside) | **Proved** |
| `directedHausdorff_Icc hab hcd` | `dбҙҙ([a,b],[c,d]) = max 0 (max (cвҲ’a) (bвҲ’d))` | Directed Hausdorff between intervals (worst over-reach, attained at endpoints) | **Proved** |
| `hausdorffDist_Icc hab hcd` | `H([a,b],[c,d]) = max \|aвҲ’c\| \|bвҲ’d\|` | **The geometric keystone** (BeresteanuвҖ“Molinari eq. (A.1), `d=1`): set distance = larger endpoint gap | **Proved** |

### `Aumann.lean` вҖ” support function of an interval & the d=1 Artstein bridge

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `supportFn_Icc_one hab` | `supportFn [a,b] (1:в„қ) = b` | Support function of a real interval at `+1`: the upper endpoint | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_Icc_neg_one hab` | `supportFn [a,b] (-1:в„қ) = -a` | Support function at `вҲ’1`: the negated lower endpoint | **Proved** |
| `hausdorffDist_Icc_eq_supportFn hab hcd` | `H = max \|О”s(вҲ’1)\| \|О”s(1)\|` | **The `d=1` HГ¶rmander identity**: Hausdorff distance = sup over `{Вұ1}` of support-function differences (bridges `Hausdorff` to the `SupportFunction` engine) | **Proved** |
| `artstein_supportFn_one` | `s(+1, E[F]) = вҲ« s(+1, F Пү) вҲӮОј` | **Artstein identity** (`d=1`, dir `+1`): support of the Aumann expectation = expectation of the support (`= вҲ«U`) | **Proved** |
| `artstein_supportFn_neg_one` | `s(вҲ’1, E[F]) = вҲ« s(вҲ’1, F Пү) вҲӮОј` | **Artstein identity** (`d=1`, dir `вҲ’1`): `= вҲ’вҲ«L` | **Proved** |

### `IntervalCLT.lean` вҖ” scalar interval-data CLT (BeresteanuвҖ“Molinari Theorem 3.2)

The Tier-1 capstone: `вҲҡn В· H(ИІвӮҷ, E[Y]) вҮ’ max(|z_L|, |z_U|)`, a continuous-mapping image of the multivariate CLT (`Stat/CLT/GaussianLimit`) through the Hausdorff keystone.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `euclвӮӮ v` | `EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin 2)` | Pack a pair `Fin 2 вҶ’ в„қ` into `в„қВІ` (the `PiLp 2` synonym) | **Defined** |
| `maxAbs w` | `в„қ := max \|w 0\| \|w 1\|` | The max-abs functional on `в„қВІ` (the Hausdorff statistic in coordinates) | **Defined** |
| `continuous_maxAbs` / `measurable_maxAbs` | `Continuous`/`Measurable maxAbs` | `maxAbs` is continuous (hence measurable) | **Proved** |
| `normalizedSum_maxAbs_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (maxAbsвҲҳnormalizedSum) ((gaussianLimit ПҲ).map maxAbs) Ој _` | **Abstract continuous-mapping CLT**: `maxAbs` of the vector normalised sum вҮ’ pushforward of the Gaussian limit by `maxAbs` (via `clt_normalizedSum_vec` + `Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous`) | **Proved** |
| `sqrt_inv_centered` | `(вҲҡn)вҒ»В№В·(s вҲ’ nВ·c) = вҲҡnВ·(s/n вҲ’ c)` | Centered-sum / `вҲҡn` algebra (normalised centered sum = `вҲҡn Г— (sample mean вҲ’ pop mean)`) | **Proved** |
| `sampleMean S y n Пү` | `в„қ := (вҲ‘_{i<n} y (Zбөў Пү)) / n` | Sample mean of `y` over the first `n` draws | **Defined** |
| `intervalIFVec yL yU P` | `X вҶ’ в„қВІ` | Centered endpoint influence function `ПҲ(z) = (y_L(z) вҲ’ E y_L, y_U(z) вҲ’ E y_U)` | **Defined** |
| `sampleMean_le` | `y_L вүӨ y_U вҮ’ sampleMean yL вүӨ sampleMean yU` | Sample-mean interval is well-ordered | **Proved** |
| `maxAbs_normalizedSum_eq` | `maxAbs (normalizedSum) = вҲҡn В· H(ИІвӮҷ, E[Y])` | **The Hausdorff bridge**: the statistic is the scaled Hausdorff distance between the sample-mean interval `ИІвӮҷ` and `E[Y] = [E y_L, E y_U]` | **Proved** |
| `interval_data_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (fun n Пү => вҲҡnВ·H(ИІвӮҷ,E[Y])) ((gaussianLimit ПҲ).map maxAbs) Ој _` | **BeresteanuвҖ“Molinari Theorem 3.2**: `вҲҡn В· H(ИІвӮҷ, E[Y]) вҮ’ max(\|z_L\|, \|z_U\|)` (CLT вҲҳ bridge via `congr_ae`). Takes the centered-`ПҲ` multivariate-CLT hypotheses + statistic measurability | **Proved** |

### `SetValued.lean` вҖ” compact-convex set-valued RVs & the Minkowski-mean support bridge (Tier-2 substrate, general `d`)

The general-`d` substrate for the support-process CLT. A set-valued random variable takes **body** values (nonempty compact convex sets, the class `рқ’ҰвӮ–(E)`); on a compact set the linear functional `вҹӘd,В·вҹ«` attains its sup so `supportFn (F Пү) d` is well-defined. The one load-bearing fact is the **Minkowski-mean support bridge**, which turns the support value of an empirical Minkowski average into an ordinary sample mean. Pure convex geometry вҖ” no probability, no atomlessness.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IsBody C` | `structure` (`nonempty`, `isCompact`, `convex`) | A body: nonempty compact convex subset of `E` вҖ” the value type of an SVRV | **Defined** |
| `bddAbove_inner_image hC d` | `BddAbove ((вҹӘd,В·вҹ«) '' C)` | On a compact set the linear functional is bounded above (CauchyвҖ“Schwarz on the bounded set) | **Proved** |
| `IsBody.bddAbove h d` | `BddAbove ((вҹӘd,В·вҹ«) '' C)` | A body's support function is well-defined | **Proved** |
| `isBody_finsetSum s F h` | `IsBody (вҲ‘ i вҲҲ s, F i)` | A finite Minkowski sum of bodies is a body | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_finsetSum s F d h` | `supportFn (вҲ‘бөў Fбөў) d = вҲ‘бөў supportFn (Fбөў) d` | Support function commutes with finite Minkowski sums | **Proved** |
| `minkowskiMean s F` | `Set E := (\|s\|)вҒ»В№ вҖў (вҲ‘ i вҲҲ s, F i)` | The empirical Minkowski average `(1/n) вҖў вҲ‘бөў Fбөў` | **Defined** |
| `supportFn_minkowskiMean s F d h` | `supportFn (minkowskiMean s F) d = (\|s\|)вҒ»В№ В· вҲ‘бөў supportFn (Fбөў) d` | **The keystone**: support of a Minkowski average = average of supports (makes `s(p,FМ„вӮҷ)` an iid sample mean) | **Proved** |

### `SupportProcess.lean` вҖ” Tier-2 finite-direction support-process CLT (general `d`)

The general-`d` analogue of `IntervalCLT`, the honest finite-dimensional projection of BeresteanuвҖ“Molinari Theorem A.2 (the full `C(SбөҲвҒ»В№)` Banach CLT is the deferred Tier-3 gap). Over `k` fixed directions, the centered support process `ПҲ(z)_j = s(pвұј,F(z)) вҲ’ E s(pвұј,F)` вҮ’ a `k`-variate Gaussian; the Hausdorff-over-grid `в„“^вҲһ` statistic converges accordingly. The `SetValued` section instantiates the abstract shell on genuine random sets.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `maxAbsK w` | `в„қ := Finset.univ.sup' _ (fun j => \|w j\|)` | The `в„“^вҲһ`/Hausdorff-over-grid functional `supвұј \|wвұј\|` on `EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin k)` (`[NeZero k]`); generalizes `maxAbs` (`Fin 2`) | **Defined** |
| `continuous_maxAbsK` / `measurable_maxAbsK` | `Continuous`/`Measurable maxAbsK` | The grid functional is continuous (hence measurable) | **Proved** |
| `supportProcess_sup_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (maxAbsK вҲҳ normalizedSum) ((gaussianLimit ПҲ).map maxAbsK) Ој _` | **BM Theorem A.2, finite-grid form (abstract shell)**: `maxAbsK` of the vector normalised process вҮ’ `(gaussianLimit ПҲ).map maxAbsK` = law of `supвұј \|z(pвұј)\|`. General-`d` analogue of `normalizedSum_maxAbs_clt` | **Proved** |
| `supportProcess F EF p` | `X вҶ’ EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin k)` | The centered **support process** `ПҲ(x)_j = s(pвұј, F x) вҲ’ s(pвұј, EF)` over a grid of `k` directions; `вҲ«ПҲ=0` is the carried general-`d` Artstein condition | **Defined** |
| `supportProcess_ofLp` | `(supportProcess F EF p x).ofLp j = s(pвұј,F x) вҲ’ s(pвұј,EF)` | Coordinate access (`rfl`) | **Proved** |
| `supportProcess_normalizedSum_apply` | `normalizedSum (supportProcess ..) n Пү j = вҲҡn В· (s(pвұј, FМ„вӮҷ) вҲ’ s(pвұј, EF))` | **The Minkowski-mean bridge in coordinates**: the `j`-th coordinate of the normalised process is the rescaled support deviation of the empirical Minkowski average `FМ„вӮҷ = minkowskiMean (range n) (FвҲҳZ)`; via `supportFn_minkowskiMean` | **Proved** |
| `setValued_supportProcess_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (maxAbsK вҲҳ normalizedSum (supportProcess ..)) ((gaussianLimit ПҲ).map maxAbsK) Ој _` | **BM Theorem A.2 on genuine random sets**: the `в„“^вҲһ`-over-grid statistic of normalised support deviations вҮ’ `maxвұј\|z(pвұј)\|`; coordinates given by the bridge above (literal form `вҲҡnВ·maxвұј\|s(pвұј,FМ„вӮҷ)вҲ’s(pвұј,EF)\|`) | **Proved** |

### `GridTest.lean` вҖ” finite-grid specification test (BeresteanuвҖ“Molinari Theorem 2.2)

The two-sided test of `HвӮҖ : E[F] = EF` built on `setValued_supportProcess_clt`, rejecting when the rescaled Hausdorff-over-grid statistic exceeds a critical value.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gridTestStat S F EF p` | `в„• вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ` | The test statistic `TвӮҷ = maxAbsK(normalizedSum(supportProcess ..))` = `maxвұј вҲҡnВ·\|s(pвұј,FМ„вӮҷ)вҲ’s(pвұј,EF)\|` | **Defined** |
| `gridTestReject S F EF p n c` | `Set О© := {Пү \| c < TвӮҷ Пү}` | Rejection region at critical value `c` | **Defined** |
| `gridTestStat_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (gridTestStat ..) ((gaussianLimit ПҲ).map maxAbsK) Ој _` | Under `HвӮҖ`, `TвӮҷ вҮ’ maxвұј\|z(pвұј)\|`; thin restatement of `setValued_supportProcess_clt` | **Proved** |
| `gridTest_asymptotic_level` | `Ој(gridTestReject .. c) вҶ’ L(Ioi c)` (`L{c}=0`) | **BM Theorem 2.2 asymptotic level**: at a continuity point `c` of the limit law `L`, the rejection probability вҶ’ the limit-law tail `L(c,вҲһ)`; at the `(1вҲ’Оұ)`-quantile this is level `Оұ`. Via portmanteau (`tendsto_measure_of_null_frontier_of_tendsto'`) on `Ioi c` (frontier `{c}` null) | **Proved** |

Additional results landed in `IntervalCLT.lean` (moment discharge): `measurable_intervalIFVec`, `intervalIFVec_var_integrable`, `intervalIFVec_integrable`, `intervalIFVec_mean_zero`, `intervalIFVec_sum_aemeasurable`, `intervalIFVec_hHmeas` discharge the six analytic side-conditions of `interval_data_clt` from clean hypotheses, yielding the self-contained headline **`interval_data_clt_of_memLp`** (`вҲҡnВ·H(ИІвӮҷ,E[Y]) вҮ’ max(|z_L|,|z_U|)` requiring only `Measurable yL/yU`, `MemLp yL/yU 2 P`, `yL вүӨ yU`). All proved, axiom-clean.

### `IntervalInference.lean` вҖ” confidence regions & coverage (BeresteanuвҖ“Molinari В§2вҖ“3, Prop 2.7)

Confidence-region geometry for the interval-identified mean + the directed-Hausdorff CLT and its asymptotic coverage.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dilate A r` | `Set в„қ := A вҠ• [вҲ’r,r]` | Minkowski dilation; the BM region `UвӮҷ = ИІвӮҷ вҠ• B(0, c/вҲҡn)` | **Defined** |
| `dilate_Icc hab hr` | `[a,b] вҠ• [вҲ’r,r] = [aвҲ’r, b+r]` | Dilation of an interval (`aвүӨb`, `rвүҘ0`) | **Proved** |
| `subset_dilate_iff_directedHausdorff_le` | `[ОјL,ОјU] вҠҶ [yl,yu]вҠ•[вҲ’r,r] вҶ” dбҙҙ вүӨ r` | **BM Prop 2.7 core**: one-sided coverage вҶ” directed Hausdorff within radius | **Proved** |
| `subset_dilate_iff_hausdorff_le` | mutual containment `вҶ” H вүӨ r` | Symmetric (two-sided) coverage characterization | **Proved** |
| `coverage_event_eq` | `{E[Y]вҠҶUвӮҷ} = {вҲҡnВ·dбҙҙ(E[Y],ИІвӮҷ) вүӨ c}` | Coverage event = directed-statistic event (`nвүҘ1`, `cвүҘ0`) | **Proved** |
| `dirStat w` | `в„қ := max 0 (max wвӮҖ (вҲ’wвӮҒ))` | Directed-Hausdorff functional on `в„қВІ` (the `dбҙҙ` analogue of `maxAbs`) | **Defined** |
| `continuous_dirStat` / `measurable_dirStat` | вҖ” | `dirStat` continuous (hence measurable) | **Proved** |
| `normalizedSum_dirStat_clt` | `Tendsto_dist_vec (dirStatвҲҳnormalizedSum) ((gaussianLimit ПҲ).map dirStat) Ој _` | Directed continuous-mapping CLT | **Proved** |
| `dirStat_normalizedSum_eq` | `dirStat(normalizedSum) = вҲҡn В· dбҙҙ(E[Y], ИІвӮҷ)` | Directed Hausdorff bridge | **Proved** |
| `directedRegion_coverage` | `Ој{E[Y] вҠҶ UвӮҷ} вҶ’ L(вҲ’вҲһ,c]` | **BM Proposition 2.7**: coverage of the identified set converges to the limit-law mass of `Iic c` (`L = (gaussianLimit ПҲ).map dirStat`); at the `(1вҲ’Оұ)`-quantile (`L{c}=0`) this is asymptotic `1вҲ’Оұ` coverage. Via the directed CLT + portmanteau `tendsto_measure_of_null_frontier_of_tendsto'` (frontier `{c}` null) on `coverage_event_eq` | **Proved** |

**Frontier (Tier 3, deferred):** the full `C(SбөҲвҒ»В№)` Banach CLT (JainвҖ“Marcus) + BLP (Thm 4.3) вҖ” same Mathlib gap class as the bracketing-entropy chaining frontier (В§12ep.d).

## 9j2cs. `PO/ID/Partial/CriterionSet/` вҖ” Criterion-function set inference (ChernozhukovвҖ“HongвҖ“Tamer 2007)

The second pillar of partial-ID inference, complementary to the support-function/random-set program (В§9j2rs): a partially identified parameter is the **argmin set** of a population criterion `Q`, estimated by a **level set** of the sample criterion `QМӮвӮҷ`. Consistency is phrased in the *same* metric Hausdorff distance (`RandomSet.directedHausdorff`/`hausdorffDist`) that drives BeresteanuвҖ“Molinari, tying the two pillars to one geometry. All declarations **proved, sorry-free**.

### `Basic.lean` вҖ” criterion, identified set, level-set estimator

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `identifiedSet Q` | `Set Оҳ := {Оё \| Q Оё = 0}` | The identified set = zero set (argmin of a nonnegative criterion normalised to inf 0) | **Defined** |
| `levelSet Qn c` | `Set Оҳ := {Оё \| Qn Оё вүӨ c}` | The level-set estimator (sample analogue, relaxed by cutoff `c`) | **Defined** |
| `mem_identifiedSet` / `mem_levelSet` | membership `Iff.rfl` (`@[simp]`) | Unfolding lemmas | **Proved** |
| `levelSet_mono h` | `cвӮҒ вүӨ cвӮӮ вҶ’ levelSet Qn cвӮҒ вҠҶ levelSet Qn cвӮӮ` | Level set monotone in the cutoff | **Proved** |
| `identifiedSet_eq_levelSet_zero_of_nonneg hQ` | `identifiedSet Q = levelSet Q 0` | For `Q вүҘ 0` the identified set is the level-`0` set | **Proved** |
| `identifiedSet_subset_levelSet_self hc` | `identifiedSet Q вҠҶ levelSet Q c` (`cвүҘ0`) | Identified set sits inside any nonneg-cutoff level set of the same criterion | **Proved** |

### `Consistency.lean` вҖ” Hausdorff consistency of the level-set estimator

The mathematical core is a deterministic rate bound under a linear identifiability minorant; consistency is then a squeeze. Reuses the metric Hausdorff machinery of `RandomSet/Hausdorff.lean`.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `LinearMinorant Q Оҙ` | `Prop := вҲҖ Оё, Оҙ В· d(Оё, Оҳ_I) вүӨ Q Оё` | The CHT polynomial-minorant identifiability condition (degree `Оі=1`): `Q` grows вүҘ linearly away from `Оҳ_I` | **Defined** |
| `directedHausdorff_nonneg` / `hausdorffDist_nonneg` | `0 вүӨ В·` | Metric Hausdorff distances are nonnegative | **Proved** |
| `directedHausdorff_le_of_forall hM h` | `(вҲҖ aвҲҲA, infDist a B вүӨ M) вҶ’ dбҙҙ A B вүӨ M` | Directed Hausdorff bound from a uniform point-to-set bound | **Proved** |
| `directedHausdorff_eq_zero_of_subset h` | `A вҠҶ B вҶ’ dбҙҙ A B = 0` | Directed distance vanishes on a subset | **Proved** |
| `hausdorffDist_levelSet_le` | `H(levelSet Qn c, identifiedSet Q) вүӨ (c+Оө)/Оҙ` | **Deterministic CHT rate bound** under `LinearMinorant Q Оҙ` (`Оҙ>0`), sup-norm error `\|QnвҲ’Q\|вүӨОө`, cutoff `cвүҘОө`: estimator within `(c+Оө)/Оҙ` of `Оҳ_I` (minorant pins `ОҳМӮвӮҷ`; `Оҳ_I вҠҶ ОҳМӮвӮҷ` outright) | **Proved** |
| `tendsto_hausdorffDist_levelSet` | `Tendsto (fun n => H(levelSet (Qn n) (c n), Оҳ_I)) atTop (рқ“қ 0)` | **Hausdorff consistency**: with `ОөвӮҷвҶ’0` (ULLN input) and `ОөвӮҷ вүӨ cвӮҷ вҶ’ 0`, the level-set estimator is consistent for the identified set (squeeze on the rate bound) | **Proved** |

## 9j2sg. `PO/ID/Partial/SupportFunction/Sensitivity.lean` вҖ” Reweighting / Sensitivity Models (worst-case mean under a budget)

Sensitivity analysis replaces an unidentified reweighting `w` (the weights that would recover the estimand under unconfoundedness) by an **ambiguity set** `W` and reports the sharp range of the reweighted mean `вҹӘc, wвҹ«` over `W`. This is the **applications layer of `SupportFunction/`**: the support-function picture instantiated for sensitivity budgets (general robust-bound vocabulary + the ПҮВІ/LВІ ball), all in one file.

### Reweighting layer вҖ” sensitivity vocabulary + structural facts

The general ambiguity-set layer: the robust bounds are the support-interval endpoints, monotone in the budget and collapsing under no ambiguity.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `robustUpper W c` | `в„қ := supportFn W c` | Upper robust bound (worst case from above) of the reweighted mean over ambiguity set `W` | **Defined** |
| `robustLower W c` | `в„қ := -supportFn W (-c)` | Lower robust bound (worst case from below) | **Defined** |
| `robustLower_le_robustUpper` | `robustLower W c вүӨ robustUpper W c` | The robust interval is well-ordered (width `вүҘ 0`) | **Proved** |
| `le_robustUpper` / `robustLower_le` | `вҹӘc,wвҹ« вүӨ robustUpper W c` / `robustLower W c вүӨ вҹӘc,wвҹ«` | A feasible weight's reweighted mean lies in the robust interval | **Proved** |
| `robustUpper_mono` | `W вҠҶ W' вҶ’ robustUpper W c вүӨ robustUpper W' c` | **Budget monotonicity** (upper): a larger ambiguity set raises the upper bound | **Proved** |
| `robustLower_antitone` | `W вҠҶ W' вҶ’ robustLower W' c вүӨ robustLower W c` | **Budget monotonicity** (lower): a larger ambiguity set lowers the lower bound (interval widens) | **Proved** |
| `robustUpper_singleton` / `robustLower_singleton` | `robust* {wвӮҖ} c = вҹӘc,wвӮҖвҹ«` | **No ambiguity вҮ’ point identification** at `вҹӘc,wвӮҖвҹ«` (`@[simp]`) | **Proved** |
| `robustInterval_eq_image` | `(вҹӘc,В·вҹ«)''W = Icc (robustLower W c) (robustUpper W c)` | For compact convex `W` the robust bounds are sharp (the interval is the identified set) | **Proved** |

### ПҮВІ/LВІ divergence-ball model вҖ” closed form via the affine-ball engine

The ПҮВІ/LВІ ambiguity set `l2Ball e ПҒ = {w | вҹӘe,wвҹ« = 1 вҲ§ вҖ–w вҲ’ eвҖ– вүӨ ПҒ}` (unit base direction `e`, signed-reweighting relaxation of the ПҮВІ-ball). Substituting `v = w вҲ’ e` reduces it to the affine-ball fiber of `A = innerSL в„қ e`, giving the classic **mean Вұ `ПҒВ·SD`** band. Reading `e` as the constant and `c = Y` in `LВІ(P)`: `вҹӘe,cвҹ« = рқ”ј[Y]`, `вҖ–cвҖ–ВІвҲ’вҹӘe,cвҹ«ВІ = Var(Y)`.

| Symbol | Type | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `l2Ball e ПҒ` | `Set H := {w | вҹӘe,wвҹ« = 1 вҲ§ вҖ–wвҲ’eвҖ– вүӨ ПҒ}` | The ПҮВІ/LВІ ambiguity set: weights normalized against `e`, within an LВІ-ball of radius `ПҒ` | **Defined** |
| `mem_l2Ball` | `w вҲҲ l2Ball e ПҒ вҶ” вҹӘe,wвҹ« = 1 вҲ§ вҖ–wвҲ’eвҖ– вүӨ ПҒ` | Membership unfolding (`@[simp]`) | **Proved** |
| `opKer_innerSL_eq` | `opKer (innerSL в„қ e) = (в„қ вҲҷ e)б—®` | The free-direction kernel is the orthogonal complement of the base direction | **Proved** |
| `norm_orthogonalProjection_opKer_innerSL` | `вҖ–P_{ker} cвҖ– = вҲҡ(вҖ–cвҖ–ВІвҲ’вҹӘe,cвҹ«ВІ)` | Free-direction projection norm equals the standard deviation (under `вҖ–eвҖ–=1`) | **Proved** |
| `l2Ball_eq_translate` | `l2Ball e ПҒ = (e + В·)''(affineBall (innerSL в„қ e) 0 ПҒ)` | Reduction to the affine-ball engine (substitution `v = wвҲ’e`) | **Proved** |
| `supportFn_l2Ball_eq` | `supportFn (l2Ball e ПҒ) c = вҹӘe,cвҹ« + ПҒВ·вҲҡ(вҖ–cвҖ–ВІвҲ’вҹӘe,cвҹ«ВІ)` | **Closed-form worst case** (`mean + ПҒВ·SD`), `вҖ–eвҖ–=1`, `ПҒвүҘ0` | **Proved** |
| `width_l2Ball_eq` | `width (l2Ball e ПҒ) c = 2ПҒВ·вҲҡ(вҖ–cвҖ–ВІвҲ’вҹӘe,cвҹ«ВІ)` | Closed-form identified width (`2ПҒВ·SD`) | **Proved** |
| `l2Ball_point_identified_iff` | `width (l2Ball e ПҒ) c = 0 вҶ” вҖ–cвҖ–ВІ = вҹӘe,cвҹ«ВІ` | **Point identification** (`ПҒ>0`): collapse iff `c` collinear with `e` (outcome constant; equality in CauchyвҖ“Schwarz) | **Proved** |
