## 10c. `Mathlib/Indep.lean` вҖ” `IndepFun` / `CondIndepFun` plumbing (Block 1)

Generic facts about independence and conditional independence, candidates for
upstream inclusion in Mathlib.  Consumed by `SCM/Do/LocalMarkov.lean` to
discharge the latent-root branch of the local Markov property
(`full_local_markov_latent`).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.indepFun_pi_of_disjoint` | `S T : Finset О№`, `Disjoint S T` вҶ’ `IndepFun (coord-tuple on S) (coord-tuple on T) (Measure.pi Ој)` for a family of probability measures | Coordinate-tuple projections at disjoint `Finset`s are `IndepFun` under `Measure.pi`.  Derived from `iIndepFun_pi` + `iIndepFun.indepFun_finset` | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.finsetCoordProj` / `Causalean.measurable_finsetCoordProj` | `Finset О№ вҶ’ (вҲҖ i, О© i) вҶ’ ValuesOn S О©` and measurability | Generic finite-block tuple projection on a dependent product, packaged separately so product-measure independence lemmas can talk about coordinate blocks without depending on SCM-specific `ValuesOn` infrastructure | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.finsetCoordProjFromCondResidual` / `Causalean.measurable_finsetCoordProjFromCondResidual` / `Causalean.finsetCoordProjFromCondResidual_comp` | Reassemble `ПҖ_S` from `(ПҖ_U, ПҖ_{S \\ U})`, with measurability and composition identity | Residual-coordinate bookkeeping used to reduce overlap-in-conditioning to a disjoint residual product statement | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.condIndepFun_pi_cond_residual_of_disjoint` | pairwise-disjoint residual blocks `S0`, `T0`, `U` вҶ’ `CondIndepFun Пғ(ПҖ_U) (ПҖ_U,ПҖ_S0) (ПҖ_U,ПҖ_T0) (Measure.pi Ој)` | Focused finite-product conditional-independence fact: after conditioning on `ПҖ_U`, disjoint residual coordinate blocks are conditionally independent; proved from unconditional disjoint-block independence plus weak-union/prodMk lifting | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.condIndepFun_pi_of_inter_subset` | `S вҲ© T вҠҶ U вҶ’ CondIndepFun Пғ(ПҖ_U) ПҖ_S ПҖ_T (Measure.pi Ој)` | Raw finite-product conditional-independence fact: coordinate tuple on `S` is CI of tuple on `T` given tuple on `U` whenever the overlap is absorbed by conditioning; reduces to the residual disjoint-block helper | **Proved** (depends on `condIndepFun_pi_cond_residual_of_disjoint`) |
| `Causalean.comap_eq_bot_of_subsingleton` | `Subsingleton ОІ вҶ’ MeasurableSpace.comap (f : Оұ вҶ’ ОІ) inferInstance = вҠҘ` | The comap Пғ-algebra of a subsingleton-valued function is trivial вҖ” every preimage is either `вҲ…` or `univ`.  Used to collapse the `FullCondIndep M X Y вҲ… вҖҰ Ој` conditioning Пғ-algebra to `вҠҘ` | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.condIndepFun_bot_of_indepFun` | `IsProbabilityMeasure Ој` + `Measurable f` + `Measurable g` + `IndepFun f g Ој` вҶ’ `CondIndepFun вҠҘ bot_le f g Ој` | Bridge: `IndepFun` lifts to `CondIndepFun` with trivial (`вҠҘ`) conditioning Пғ-algebra on a standard Borel space.  Proof via `condIndepFun_iff_condExp_inter_preimage_eq_mul` + `condExp_bot` | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.indepFun_of_map` | `AEMeasurable ПҶ ОҪ` + measurable `X, Y` + `IndepFun (X вҲҳ ПҶ) (Y вҲҳ ПҶ) ОҪ` вҶ’ `IndepFun X Y (ОҪ.map ПҶ)` | Pushforward bridge for `IndepFun`: analogue of `LocalMarkov.condIndepFun_of_map` at the unconditional level.  Proof via `indepFun_iff_measure_inter_preimage_eq_mul` + `Measure.map_apply_of_aemeasurable` | **Proved** |

## 10d. `Mathlib/IndepIntegral.lean` вҖ” Drop-of-conditioning integral identity

Upstream candidate.  Contains no Causalean-specific definitions; namespaced
directly under `ProbabilityTheory` so it can be contributed back to Mathlib
verbatim.  Extracted from the IV/LATE first-stage and reduced-form proofs.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ProbabilityTheory.IndepFun.integral_restrict_preimage_eq_mul` | `IndepFun f g Ој`, `Measurable f`, `Measurable g`, `MeasurableSet E`, `Measurable h : ОІ вҶ’ в„қ` вҶ’ `вҲ« Пү in f вҒ»В№' E, h (g Пү) вҲӮОј = (Ој (f вҒ»В№' E)).toReal * вҲ« Пү, h (g Пү) вҲӮОј` | Drop-of-conditioning: under independence of `f` and `g`, conditioning on an event in the `f`-fibre factors out as a scalar multiple.  Proof rewrites the set integral as an indicator-weighted integral, applies `IndepFun.integral_fun_comp_mul_comp`, and evaluates via `integral_indicator_one` | **Proved** (upstream candidate вҖ” no Causalean types) |

## 10d''. `Mathlib/ELpNormMeasurable.lean` вҖ” Parametric LВІ-norm measurability

Upstream candidate.  Provides causal-agnostic measurability facts for the
parameter map `Пү вҶҰ вҖ–g ПүвҖ–_{LВІ(P)}` by rewriting the LВІ norm as a parametric
lintegral and applying Tonelli measurability.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.measurable_eLpNorm_two_toReal_of_uncurry` | `Measurable (Function.uncurry g)` вҶ’ `Measurable (fun Пү => (eLpNorm (g Пү) 2 P).toReal)` | Top-Пғ-algebra version. Uses `Measurable.lintegral_prod_right'` on `вҖ–g Пү xвҖ–вӮ‘^2`, rewrites `eLpNorm`, and post-processes with `.toReal`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.measurable_eLpNorm_two_toReal_of_uncurry_of_factor` | `Measurable[mО©.prod Пғ_X] (Function.uncurry g)` вҶ’ `Measurable[mО©] (fun Пү => (eLpNorm (g Пү) 2 P).toReal)` | Sub-Пғ-algebra product version. The theorem requires joint measurability with respect to the sub-Пғ-algebra product; coordinate/cylinder measurability alone is not enough to invoke Mathlib's parametric lintegral measurability lemma. | **Proved** |

## 10d'. `Mathlib/IIDCenteredSum.lean` вҖ” Conditional 2nd-moment bound for centered i.i.d. sums

Upstream candidate.  Isolates the central probabilistic inequality used by the
fold-B empirical-process bound in `Stat/SampleSplit/FoldBEmpiricalProcess.lean`: for a
finite family of i.i.d. samples independent of a sub-Пғ-algebra `m_A`, the
expected square of the centered scaled sum is bounded by the expected
`LВІ(P)`-norm squared of the integrand.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `lintegral_ofReal_inv_sqrt_smul_sum_sq_le` (private) | `0 < s.card`, unscaled square `вҲ«вҒ»(ОЈ Y_i)ВІ вүӨ s.card В· вҲ«вҒ» B` вҶ’ scaled `вҲ«вҒ» ((1/вҲҡs.card)В·ОЈ Y_i)ВІ вүӨ вҲ«вҒ» B` | Deterministic algebraic step: the `(1/вҲҡs.card)ВІ` prefactor cancels the `s.card` factor on the RHS via `lintegral_const_mul'` and `Real.sq_sqrt`. **Proved by Codex.** |
| `eLpNorm_two_sq_toReal_eq_integral_sq` (private) | `MemLp f 2 P` вҶ’ `ENNReal.ofReal ((eLpNorm f 2 P).toReal ^ 2) = ENNReal.ofReal (вҲ« f^2 dP)` | Converts the real `LВІ` norm square back to the square integral, using `MemLp.eLpNorm_eq_integral_rpow_norm` and nonnegativity of `вҲ« f^2`. **Proved.** |
| `centered_sq_lintegral_le_eLpNorm_two_sq` (private) | `MemLp f 2 P` вҶ’ `вҲ«вҒ» ofReal ((f - вҲ«f)^2) dP вүӨ ofReal (вҖ–fвҖ–ВІвӮӮ)` | Diagonal variance bound: rewrites the centered square lintegral as `variance f P`, applies `variance_le_expectation_sq`, then uses `eLpNorm_two_sq_toReal_eq_integral_sq`. **Proved.** |
| `pi_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` (private) | `MemLp f 2 P` вҶ’ under `Measure.pi (fun _ : s => P)`, `вҲ«вҒ» ofReal ((ОЈ_i (f(v_i)вҲ’вҲ«f))ВІ) вүӨ |s|В·ofReal(вҖ–fвҖ–ВІвӮӮ)` | Fixed-integrand finite-product estimate. Uses `variance_sum_pi` to eliminate cross terms, proves the centered coordinate mean is zero by `integral_map` along `measurePreserving_eval`, and bounds each variance by `variance_le_expectation_sq`. **Proved.** |
| `indep_trim_prod_map_eq` (private) | `Indep m_A (comap Z) Ој`, `m_A вүӨ mО©`, measurable `Z` вҶ’ `map (fun Пү => (Пү, ZПү)) Ој = (Ој.trim hm_A_le).prod (map Z Ој)` | Independent-joining bridge: converts sub-Пғ-algebra independence to `IndepFun id Z`, applies `indepFun_iff_map_prod_eq_prod_map_map`, and identifies the first marginal as the trimmed measure. **Proved.** |
| `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_unscaled_le_of_joined_law` (private) | Joined product law `(Пү, W_s Пү) вҲј (Ој.trim hm_A_le) Г— P^s` plus the public measurability/LВІ hypotheses вҶ’ unscaled bound | Product-space form: rewrites the original lintegral through the joined law, applies Tonelli, uses `pi_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` pointwise in `Пү`, pulls out `|s|`, and rewrites the trimmed lintegral back to `Ој`. **Proved.** |
| `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_unscaled_le` (private) | Same hypotheses as the public lemma but with conclusion `вҲ«вҒ» (ОЈ Y_i)ВІ вүӨ s.card В· вҲ«вҒ» вҖ–g ПүвҖ–ВІвӮӮ` (no `1/вҲҡs.card` factor) | Reduces the original hypotheses to the joined product law using `indep_trim_prod_map_eq` and `hW_iid_pi`, then calls `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_unscaled_le_of_joined_law`. **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` | `m_A вүӨ mО©`, `Indep m_A (comap (W on s)) Ој`, `Ој.map (W on s) = Measure.pi (fun _ => P)`, `Measurable[m_A.prod Пғ_X] (uncurry g)`, `вҲҖ Пү, MemLp (g Пү) 2 P` вҶ’ `вҲ«вҒ» Пү, ENNReal.ofReal (((1/вҲҡs.card) * ОЈ_{iвҲҲs} (g Пү (W i Пү) - вҲ«g Пү dP))ВІ) вҲӮОј вүӨ вҲ«вҒ» Пү, ENNReal.ofReal (вҖ–g ПүвҖ–ВІ_{LВІ(P)}) вҲӮОј` | Public theorem. **Proved.** |

## 10e. `Mathlib/ConvergenceInDistribution.lean` вҖ” Scalar Slutsky / varying maps

Upstream candidates for convergence in distribution.  Contains no Causalean-specific
definitions; the project-level `Tendsto_dist` wrappers in `Stat/Limit/Convergence.lean`
consume these statements.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `MeasureTheory.tendstoInMeasure_const_of_tendsto_real` | `aбөў вҶ’ aвӮҖ` вҶ’ constant random variables `Пү вҶҰ aбөў` converge in measure to `Пү вҶҰ aвӮҖ` | Deterministic topological convergence as convergence in measure for constant real-valued random variables | **Proved** |
| `MeasureTheory.TendstoInDistribution.const_mul_of_tendsto_const` | `Xбөў вҮ’ Z` and `aбөў вҶ’ aвӮҖ` вҶ’ `aбөўВ·Xбөў вҮ’ aвӮҖВ·Z` | Same-probability-space deterministic-scalar Slutsky theorem; proved by Mathlib's product Slutsky theorem `continuous_comp_prodMk_of_tendstoInMeasure_const` with multiplication as the continuous map | **Proved** |
| `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_map_mul_of_tendsto` | `ОҪбөў вҶ’ ОҪ` weakly and `aбөў вҶ’ aвӮҖ` вҶ’ `(x вҶҰ aбөўx)_#ОҪбөў вҶ’ (x вҶҰ aвӮҖx)_#ОҪ` | Measure-level deterministic-scalar Slutsky theorem for weak convergence on в„қ. Proved by rewriting `(x вҶҰ aбөўx)_#ОҪбөў` as the push-forward of `Оҙ_{aбөў} Г— ОҪбөў` by multiplication, then applying continuity of Dirac measures, product probability measures, and the ordinary continuous-mapping theorem | **Proved** |

## 10f. `Mathlib/FWL.lean` вҖ” FrischвҖ“WaughвҖ“Lovell on a finite-dim inner product space

Upstream candidate.  Self-contained algebraic content of the
FrischвҖ“WaughвҖ“Lovell theorem on a real inner-product space `V` with a
nuisance subspace `H` admitting an orthogonal projection.  Causal- and
panel-agnostic; depends only on Mathlib (`Submodule.orthogonalProjection`,
`Matrix.mulVec`, `Matrix.nonsing_inv`).  **Live consumer:**
`Panel/FWLInstanceL2.lean` (В§9i0a-FWLInstanceL2)
instantiates this development on `Lp в„қ 2 Ој` to give the conditional-mean
estimand papers a shared, measure-theory-native FWL engine.  (The finite
weighted-support tower `Weighted/FWL.lean` does **not** instantiate this file:
its inner product is only positive-semidefinite, so it carries its own FWL
built on `Mathlib/SemiInnerProjection`; the two are connected at the data level
via `Weighted/OfProbabilityMeasure.lean`.)  NL artifact:
`doc/basic_concepts/mathlib/fwl.md`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residualize H v` | `Submodule в„қ V вҶ’ V вҶ’ V` | Orthogonal residual operator `M_H v = v - P_H v` as a self-map of `V`, defined via `Submodule.orthogonalProjectionFn` (which lands in `V`, not `вҶҘH`) so the result is a `V`-vector. Requires `[H.HasOrthogonalProjection]`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.inner_residualize_of_mem` | `(v : V) {w : V} (hw : w вҲҲ H) вҶ’ вҹӘresidualize H v, wвҹ« = 0` | Residual orthogonality: `M_H v вҠҘ H`. Direct unfolding of `Submodule.orthogonalProjectionFn_inner_eq_zero`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residualize_inner_swap_right` | `(v w : V) вҶ’ вҹӘresidualize H v, wвҹ« = вҹӘresidualize H v, residualize H wвҹ«` | Symmetric residualization in the right slot. Splits `w = M_H w + P_H w` and uses orthogonality of the residual to `H`. The key identity used by FWL: residualizing only one side suffices. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.gramResid H X` | `Submodule в„қ V вҶ’ (Fin K вҶ’ V) вҶ’ Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) в„қ` | Residualized Gram matrix `Q_{XX} j k := вҹӘM_H (X j), M_H (X k)вҹ«`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residInnerVec H X Y` | `вҖҰ вҶ’ V вҶ’ (Fin K вҶ’ в„қ)` | Residualized inner-product vector `r_j := вҹӘM_H (X j), Yвҹ«`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fwlCoef H X Y` | `вҖҰ вҶ’ V вҶ’ (Fin K вҶ’ в„қ)` | The FWL coefficient `ОёМӮ := Q_{XX}вҒ»В№ *бөҘ residInnerVec H X Y`. Defined unconditionally; only the normal-equations identity below needs invertibility of `Q_{XX}`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.gramResid_mulVec_fwlCoef` | `(Y : V) (hQ : IsUnit (gramResid H X).det) вҶ’ (gramResid H X) *бөҘ fwlCoef H X Y = residInnerVec H X Y` | Residualized normal equations for the FWL coefficient. Pure matrix algebra: `A *бөҘ (AвҒ»В№ *бөҘ b) = b` via `Matrix.mulVec_mulVec`, `Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv`, `Matrix.one_mulVec`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residInnerVec_eq_residualize_right` | `(Y : V) вҶ’ residInnerVec H X Y = residInnerVec H X (residualize H Y)` | The FWL right-hand side is invariant under residualizing `Y`. Pointwise consequence of `residualize_inner_swap_right`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fittedValue X ОІ` | `(Fin K вҶ’ V) вҶ’ (Fin K вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ V` | Finite fitted value `вҲ‘ j, ОІ j вҖў X j`; helper lemmas record linearity under coefficient subtraction and commutation with residualization. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residualizedObjective H X Y ОІ` | `вҖҰ вҶ’ в„қ` | Residualized least-squares objective `вҹӘM_H Y вҲ’ вҲ‘ j ОІ_j вҖў M_H X_j, M_H Y вҲ’ вҲ‘ j ОІ_j вҖў M_H X_jвҹ«`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.originalObjective X Y ОІ h` | `вҖҰ вҶ’ V вҶ’ в„қ` | Original least-squares objective with an explicit nuisance term `h`: `вҹӘY вҲ’ XОІ вҲ’ h, Y вҲ’ XОІ вҲ’ hвҹ«`. | **Defined** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.residualizedObjective_eq_fwlCoef_add_error` | `(hQ : IsUnit (gramResid H X).det) вҶ’ residualizedObjective H X Y ОІ = residualizedObjective H X Y (fwlCoef H X Y) + вҹӘXМғ(fwlCoefвҲ’ОІ), XМғ(fwlCoefвҲ’ОІ)вҹ«` | Residualized completing-square identity. The nonnegative error term yields residualized minimization and, with Gram invertibility, coefficient uniqueness. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fwlCoef_residualized_minimizes` | `(hQ : IsUnit (gramResid H X).det) вҶ’ residualizedObjective H X Y (fwlCoef H X Y) вүӨ residualizedObjective H X Y ОІ` | Completing-square theorem: the matrix-defined FWL coefficient minimizes the residualized least-squares objective. Uses the residualized normal equations to kill the cross term. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.originalObjective_eq_residualizedObjective_add_projectionError` | `(hh : h вҲҲ H) вҶ’ originalObjective X Y ОІ h = residualizedObjective H X Y ОІ + вҹӘP_H (Y вҲ’ XОІ) вҲ’ h, P_H (Y вҲ’ XОІ) вҲ’ hвҹ«` | Pythagorean decomposition of the original objective into residualized loss plus squared nuisance-projection error. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fwlCoef_original_minimizes` | `(hQ) (hh : h вҲҲ H) вҶ’ originalObjective X Y (fwlCoef H X Y) (P_H (Y вҲ’ X fwlCoef)) вүӨ originalObjective X Y ОІ h` | Standard finite-dimensional FWL optimizer theorem: the residualized coefficient paired with the projection of its raw residual minimizes the original least-squares problem over coefficients and nuisance terms in `H`. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.fwlCoef_eq_of_original_minimizer` | `original-objective minimizer hypothesis вҶ’ ОІ = fwlCoef H X Y` | ОІ-block uniqueness theorem: any minimizer of the original least-squares problem has coefficient vector equal to the residualized FWL coefficient. | **Proved** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.FWL.original_minimizer_eq_fwlCoef_projection` | `original-objective minimizer hypothesis вҶ’ ОІ = fwlCoef H X Y вҲ§ h = P_H (Y вҲ’ X fwlCoef)` | Full standard FWL uniqueness characterization: every original-problem minimizer is exactly the residualized coefficient paired with the nuisance projection of its raw residual. | **Proved** |

## 10h. `Mathlib/TwoStateMarkov.lean` вҖ” two-state Markov transition matrix, stationary projection, geometric ergodicity

Project-generic shim (depends only on Mathlib; no causal/panel/Causalean
content) for the two-state Markov chain on `Fin 2` with rows
`(1 - a, a)` and `(b, 1 - b)`. Carries the two transition/stationary
matrix definitions and the substrate for long-T compact-uniform
convergence required by
`CausalSmith/Panel/Q1_SpectralThreshold/LongT.lean`: spectral
decomposition of the transition matrix plus uniform geometric
convergence to the stationary projection on compact subsets of the open
transition-parameter square `(0,1)ВІ`. **Both definitions and all 4
theorems are in place; all theorems proved.**

This file consolidates the two definitions (previously local to
`CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Panel/Q1_SpectralThreshold/Basic.lean`) and the
four ergodicity theorems (previously in
`CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Mathlib/MarkovErgodicity.lean`, now deleted)
into a single neutral Causalean module. `Q1_SpectralThreshold/Basic.lean`
re-exports the two definitions under the short names so existing
downstream code is unchanged.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.transitionMatrix` | `(a b : в„қ) вҶ’ Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) в„қ` | The 2Г—2 row-stochastic transition matrix with rows `(1 - a, a)` and `(b, 1 - b)`. `noncomputable`. | **Defined.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.stationaryProjection` | `(a b : в„қ) вҶ’ Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) в„қ` | The rank-one projector whose two rows both equal the stationary distribution `(b/(a+b), a/(a+b))`. `noncomputable`. | **Defined.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.transitionMatrix_pow_eq_spectral` | `(a b : в„қ) (ha_pos : 0 < a) (ha_lt_one : a < 1) (hb_pos : 0 < b) (hb_lt_one : b < 1) вҶ’ вҲҖ k, M^k = О  + (1 - a - b)^k вҖў (1 - О )` | Spectral decomposition: powers of the transition matrix split into the stationary projection plus a geometrically-decaying correction along `1 - О `. **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.one_minus_a_b_abs_lt_one` | `(0 < a) (0 < b) (a < 1) (b < 1) вҶ’ |1 - a - b| < 1` | Pointwise spectral gap on the open transition-parameter square. **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.one_minus_a_b_uniform_gap_on_compact` | `(K : Set (в„қ Г— в„қ)) (IsCompact K) (K вҠҶ (0,1)ВІ) вҶ’ вҲғ ПҒ < 1, вҲҖ p вҲҲ K, |1 - p.1 - p.2| вүӨ ПҒ` | Uniform spectral gap on compact subsets via `IsCompact.exists_isMaxOn` + averaging the maximum with `1`. **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.TwoStateMarkov.transitionMatrix_pow_tendsto_stationary_uniform` | `(K compact вҠҶ (0,1)ВІ) вҶ’ вҲҖ Оө > 0, вҲғ N, вҲҖ k вүҘ N, вҲҖ p вҲҲ K, вҲҖ i j, |((M p.1 p.2)^k - О  p.1 p.2) i j| вүӨ Оө` | Uniform geometric convergence of `M^k` to the stationary projection on compact subsets, combining the spectral decomposition (entries of `1 - О ` bounded by `1`) with the uniform gap (geometric decay rate `ПҒ < 1`). **Proved.** |

## 10g. `Mathlib/SemiInnerProjection.lean` вҖ” semidefinite finite-dimensional projections

Upstream candidate for finite-dimensional projection under a symmetric
positive-semidefinite bilinear form.  This is the generic support-quotient
architecture consumed by `Panel/Subspace.lean`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.exists_orthogonalProjection_of_posSemidef` | finite-dimensional `V`, bilinear form `B`, symmetry, `вҲҖ x, 0 вүӨ B x x`, submodule `H` вҶ’ `вҲғ P : V вҶ’вӮ—[в„қ] V, P X вҲҲ H вҲ§ B (X - P X) h = 0` | Generic semidefinite projection existence. Proof uses the restricted Gram map `H вҶ’вӮ— Dual в„қ H`, shows each right-hand side lies in its range via the PSD radical fact and dual-annihilator/range duality, then chooses a linear right inverse of the range restriction. | **Proved.** |

---

## 10i. `Mathlib/StandardGaussian.lean` вҖ” standard Gaussian on a finite-dim inner-product space

Constructs the standard Gaussian measure on an arbitrary finite-dimensional
real inner-product space `E` (mean zero, covariance = inner product) and proves
its basic moments. Construction: take `b := stdOrthonormalBasis в„қ E`, build the
product of `n = finrank в„қ E` standard real Gaussians on `Fin n вҶ’ в„қ`, transport
to `EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin n)` via `toLp`, then pull back to `E` along the
isometry `b.repr.symm`. Upstream candidate.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Causalean.Mathlib.stdGaussian` | `[FiniteDimensional в„қ E] [BorelSpace E] вҶ’ Measure E` | The standard Gaussian measure on `E`: `(prod of gaussianReal 0 1).map toLp` pulled back along `b.repr.symm`. | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.isProbabilityMeasure_stdGaussian` | `IsProbabilityMeasure (stdGaussian E)` | Probability measure (pushforward of a `Measure.pi` of probability measures). | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.isGaussian_stdGaussian` | `IsGaussian (stdGaussian E)` | Gaussian law; via `iIndepFun.hasGaussianLaw` for the product, then `isGaussian_map_equiv`. | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.stdGaussian_mean` | `вҲ« x, x вҲӮ(stdGaussian E) = 0` | Mean is zero; each coordinate `gaussianReal 0 1` has mean 0, pushed through the continuous-linear maps. | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.covarianceBilin_stdGaussian` | `covarianceBilin (stdGaussian E) u v = вҹӘu, vвҹ«_в„қ` | Covariance bilinear form equals the inner product. Via `covarianceBilin_map` (adjoint of isometry = inverse), `covarianceBilin_apply_pi`, coordinate covariances (Kronecker delta from independence + unit variance), and `OrthonormalBasis.repr` isometry. | **Proved.** |
| `Causalean.Mathlib.stdGaussian_map_normSq_eq_pi` | `(stdGaussian E).map вҖ–В·вҖ–ВІ = (Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (finrank в„қ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)).map (вҲ‘ (w i)ВІ)` | Exposes the product-of-1-D-Gaussians structure of the squared-norm law (rotation-invariance of `вҖ–В·вҖ–ВІ` strips the orthonormal-basis transport), used for ПҮВІ atomlessness. | **Proved.** |

---

## 10j. `Mathlib/OperatorSqrt.lean` вҖ” positive square root of a positive operator

Builds the **operator square root** of a positive symmetric operator on a
finite-dimensional real inner-product space вҖ” a result Mathlib lacks (it has the
spectral theorem and `LinearMap.IsPositive`, but no operator sqrt). Construction:
in the orthonormal eigenbasis `b` with real eigenvalues `Ој` (both from
`LinearMap.IsSymmetric.eigenvectorBasis`/`eigenvalues`), `posSqrt` acts as
`вҲҡ(Ој i)` on each eigenvector (via `Basis.constr`). Upstream candidate. All in
namespace `LinearMap.IsPositive` (with `hT : T.IsPositive`).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `eigBasis` / `eigVals` | `OrthonormalBasis (Fin (finrank в„қ E)) в„қ E` / `Fin _ вҶ’ в„қ` | Orthonormal eigenbasis and real eigenvalues of the positive operator `T`. | **Proved.** |
| `eigVals_nonneg` | `0 вүӨ hT.eigVals i` | Eigenvalues of a positive operator are nonnegative (`Ој i = вҹӘT (b i), b iвҹ« вүҘ 0`). | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrt` | `E вҶ’вӮ—[в„қ] E` | The square root: `posSqrt (b i) = вҲҡ(Ој i) вҖў b i`, extended by `Basis.constr`. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrt_mul_self` | `hT.posSqrt вҲҳвӮ— hT.posSqrt = T` | Square law, via `Real.mul_self_sqrt` and `Basis.ext`. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrt_inner` | `вҹӘposSqrt x, yвҹ« = вҲ‘ i, вҲҡ(Ој i) вҹӘb i,xвҹ« вҹӘb i,yвҹ«` | Symmetric weighted-sum form of the quadratic data. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrt_isSymmetric` / `posSqrt_isPositive` | `IsSymmetric` / `IsPositive` | `posSqrt` is itself symmetric and positive. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrtCLM` | `E вҶ’L[в„қ] E` | `posSqrt` packaged as a continuous linear map. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrtCLM_isSelfAdjoint` / `posSqrtCLM_adjoint` | `IsSelfAdjoint posSqrtCLM` / `adjoint posSqrtCLM = posSqrtCLM` | Self-adjointness of the continuous square root. | **Proved.** |
| `posSqrtCLM_comp_self` | `posSqrtCLM вҲҳL posSqrtCLM = T.toContinuousLinearMap` | Continuous square law. | **Proved.** |

---

## 10j'. `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Cholesky.lean` вҖ” real Cholesky factorization (existence + uniqueness)

The **Cholesky factorization** of a real symmetric positive-definite matrix `M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) в„қ`: there is a unique upper-triangular matrix `U` with strictly positive diagonal such that `M = UбөҖ U` (here "upper-triangular" means `j < i вҶ’ U i j = 0`, all strictly-below-diagonal entries vanish). Existence is derived from Mathlib's LDL decomposition (`LDL.lower_conj_diag`) by absorbing the square roots of the diagonal factor: `U = вҲҡD В· LбөҖ` where `L = LDL.lower` and `D = LDL.diag`. Uniqueness reduces to the fact that an orthogonal upper-triangular matrix with positive diagonal is the identity. Mathlib has LDL but no Cholesky; upstream candidate. Built for the causal-disentanglement formalization. Namespace `Causalean.Mathlib.LinearAlgebra`. Sorry-free (axioms: `propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IsUpperTri` / `isUpperTri_iff_blockTriangular` | `вҲҖ i j, j < i вҶ’ U i j = 0` / `вҶ” U.BlockTriangular id` | Strict upper-triangularity predicate and its identification with Mathlib's `BlockTriangular id`. | **Proved.** |
| `ldl_lowerInv_diag_one` | `LDL.lowerInv hM i i = 1` | The Gram-Schmidt lower-inverse matrix is unitriangular (diagonal entries are `1`). | **Proved.** |
| `ldl_diagEntries_pos` | `0 < LDL.diagEntries hM i` | The LDL diagonal entries of a positive-definite matrix are strictly positive (each is `xбөҖMx > 0` for the nonzero `x = lowerInv` row). | **Proved.** |
| `ldl_lowerInv_blockTriangular` / `ldl_lower_blockTriangular` | `(вҖҰ).BlockTriangular toDual` | `LDL.lowerInv` and `LDL.lower` are lower-triangular. | **Proved.** |
| `ldl_lower_diag_one` | `LDL.lower hM i i = 1` | `LDL.lower` is unitriangular (inverse of a unitriangular lower-triangular matrix). | **Proved.** |
| `cholesky_exists` | `вҲғ U, (вҲҖ i j, j<i вҶ’ U i j = 0) вҲ§ (вҲҖ i, 0 < U i i) вҲ§ M = UбөҖ U` | **Existence.** Every real positive-definite matrix factors as `UбөҖ U` with `U` upper-triangular, positive-diagonal. | **Proved.** |
| `orthogonal_upperTri_pos_diag_eq_one` | `WбөҖ W = 1 вҶ’ upper-tri вҶ’ pos-diag вҶ’ W = 1` | **Key uniqueness lemma.** An orthogonal upper-triangular matrix with positive diagonal is the identity (its transpose is both upper and lower triangular, hence diagonal, hence `Вұ1`, hence `1`). | **Proved.** |
| `cholesky_unique` | `M = UбөҖ U = VбөҖ V вҶ’ (both upper-tri pos-diag) вҶ’ U = V` | **Uniqueness.** The factorization is unique: `W = V UвҒ»В№` is orthogonal upper-triangular with positive diagonal, so `W = 1`. | **Proved.** |

---

## 10k. `Mathlib/MeasureTheory/PartitionIntegral.lean` вҖ” finite-partition integral algebra

Decomposing an integral over the fibers of a finite-valued measurable map `H : О© вҶ’ О№` (a finite partition of the sample space). Causal-agnostic, Mathlib-shaped measure theory (a Mathlib-PR candidate); relocated here from `Stat/` so any layer (estimation, panel cell algebra, partial-ID) can use it without pulling in `Stat/` or `PO/`. Complements `Causalean.PO.integral_eq_sum_measure_mul_eventCondExp` (the `eventCondExp`/measure-weighted form for an explicit set family): here the form is measurable-map / set-integral, plus the cell-constant pull-out the `PO` lemma does not provide. Index type carries `[Fintype О№] [MeasurableSpace О№] [MeasurableSingletonClass О№]`; no measurability of the cell-weight `c : О№ вҶ’ в„қ` is needed (it is constant on each fiber). Namespace `Causalean.Mathlib.MeasureTheory`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `integral_eq_sum_setIntegral_fiber` | `вҲ« f = вҲ‘ h, вҲ«_{HвҒ»В№{h}} f` | **Fiber decomposition.** For integrable `f` and finite-valued measurable `H`, the integral splits as the sum of set-integrals over the fibers. Wraps `integral_iUnion_fintype` over the fiber cover of `univ`. **Proved.** |
| `integral_cellConst_mul` | `вҲ« c(H Пү)В·f Пү = вҲ‘ h, c h В· вҲ«_{HвҒ»В№{h}} f` | **Cell-constant pull-out.** An integrand weighted by a cell-dependent value `c (H Пү)` decomposes with `c h` pulled out per cell. Workhorse for panel cell-by-cell regression algebra and cell-weighted-average estimands. No measurability of `c` required. **Proved.** |
| `integral_cellConst` | `вҲ« c(H Пү) = вҲ‘ h, c h В· Ој(HвҒ»В№{h})` | **Cell-weight aggregation.** For a finite measure, the integral of a cell-constant function is the cell-weighted sum of cell masses. Special case of `integral_cellConst_mul` with `f вүЎ 1`. **Proved.** |

## 10k'. `Mathlib/MeasureTheory/CondExpIndicatorTower.lean` вҖ” conditioning вҶ” inner-regression weighting

Causal-agnostic conditional-expectation identity (a Mathlib-PR candidate; namespace `MeasureTheory`): the measure-theoretic kernel behind "regression adjustment = inverse-propensity weighting". Reused by `regression_adjustment` (В§9g) and earmarked for the ATT/DTR sequential analogs.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `condExp_setIndicator_condExp_of_le` | `(hm : m вүӨ m') (hm' : m' вүӨ m0) [SigmaFinite (Ој.trim hm')] (hs : MeasurableSet[m'] s) (hf : Integrable f Ој) вҶ’ Ој[s.indicator f \| m] =бөҗ Ој[s.indicator (Ој[f\|m']) \| m]` | **Masked outcome вҶ” inner regression.** Conditioning the masked `1_sВ·f` on coarse `m` equals conditioning its inner regression `Ој[f\|m']` (with `s` `m'`-measurable). Proof: inner `condExp_indicator` + tower `condExp_condExp_of_le`. With `m=Пғ(X)`, `m'=Пғ(D,X)`, `s={D=d}`, dividing by `Ој[1_s\|m]` turns the LHS into the IPW/adjustment functional, the RHS into the regression. **Proved.** |

## 12. `Stat/` вҖ” Causal-agnostic statistical primitives

Foundation layer for the estimation and inference modules: i.i.d. samples, convergence
modes, asymptotic linearity, Neyman orthogonality, and one-shot sample
splits. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation.tex` Sections 1вҖ“5. Files
here are deliberately project-agnostic and are candidates for upstream
contribution to Mathlib.

**Folder layout.** The top level keeps only the foundational `Sample.lean` and
the `SampleSplit.lean` umbrella; everything else is grouped by topic into
subfolders: `Limit/`
(convergence modes, continuous mapping, WLLN), `CLT/` (asymptotic linearity,
multivariate CLT, Gaussian limit, ПҮВІ), `Inference/` (О”-method, variance
estimation, studentization, Wald), `Quantile/` (cdf/quantile inference stack),
`MEstimation/` (Z/M-estimator CLT), `Orthogonality/` (Neyman orthogonality,
conditional-op helpers), `SampleSplit/` (cross-fitting + fold-restricted CLT),
`Minimax/` (Le Cam / Fano / Assouad lower bounds, В§12w),
and `Concentration/` (vendored toolbox, В§12'). Module paths below reflect this.

### 12a0. `Stat/Coupling/ProductLossMonotoneCoupling/` вҖ” Product-loss monotone couplings

Coupling-based statistical primitives for extremal dependence with fixed real marginals.
This stack builds explicit comonotone and countermonotone quantile couplings, proves
the sharp FrГ©chet-Hoeffding cdf envelopes, derives Hoeffding's covariance identity,
and concludes that the product expectation is maximized and minimized by those two
monotone couplings under finite second moments.

### 12a. `Stat/Sample.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample О© X Ој P` | structure | I.i.d. sequence `Z : в„• вҶ’ О© вҶ’ X` on a single ambient space, with mutual independence (`iIndepFun`), identical distribution (`IdentDistrib`), and law-match (`Ој.map (Z 0) = P`). Matches `def:est-iid-sample`. |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean` | `def` | `(1/n) ОЈ_{i<n} f (Z i Пү)` for a real-valued statistic `f`. |

### 12b. `Stat/Limit/Convergence.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_inProb`, `Tendsto_L2`, `Tendsto_dist` | `def`s | Aliases for convergence in probability / `LВІ` / distribution (LГ©vyвҖ“Prokhorov-style at the pushforward-measure level). |
| `Tendsto_dist.const_mul_tendsto` | `Xn вҮ’ Q` and `aвӮҷ вҶ’ aвӮҖ` вҮ’ `aвӮҷВ·Xn вҮ’ (x вҶҰ aвӮҖx)_#Q` | Project wrapper around `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_map_mul_of_tendsto`; handles the pushforward-map bookkeeping for `Tendsto_dist`. **Proved** (depends on Mathlib stub). |
| `IsBigOp Xn rn Ој`, `IsLittleOp Xn rn Ој` | `def`s | `O_p(rn)` and `o_p(rn)` per `def:est-stoch-order`. |
| `IsLittleOp.mul_isBigOp` | `o_p(rn)В·O_p(sn) = o_p(rnВ·sn)` | Requires `0 < rn n`, `0 < sn n` for all `n`. **Proved.** |
| `IsBigOp.add` | `O_p(rn) + O_p(rn) = O_p(rn)` | **Proved.** |
| `IsBigOp.mul_isLittleOp_one_isLittleOp` | Slutsky-style: `O_p(1)В·o_p(1) = o_p(1)` | **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.tightness` | `Xn вҮ’ Q` вҮ’ `Xn = O_p(1)` | Tightness of a single distributional limit on в„қ: pick a tail set `{|x| вүҘ N}` with small `Q`-mass via continuity from above on the antitone family `{|x| вүҘ n}`, then portmanteau on the closed tail to bound `limsup Ој.map(Xn n) {|x| > N} вүӨ Q {|x| вүҘ N}`. **Proved by Codex.** О”-method helper. |
| `IsBigOp.const_mul_tendsto_zero` | `Xn = O_p(1)` вҲ§ `aвӮҷ вҶ’ 0` вҮ’ `aвӮҷВ·Xn = o_p(1)` | Degenerate Slutsky: bounds the bad event `{Оө < |aвӮҷ Xn|}` by `{M < |Xn|}` once `|aвӮҷ| < Оө/M`, using only the deterministic shrinkage `|aвӮҷ| вҶ’ 0`. **Proved by Codex.** О”-method helper. |

### 12b'. `Stat/Limit/ConvergenceVec.lean`

Vector analogue of `Convergence.lean`'s `Tendsto_dist` plus the Slutsky lemmas needed by the multivariate О”-method (`Stat/Inference/DeltaMethod.lean`) and the vector asymptotic-normality corollary (`Stat/CLT/AsymptoticLinearityVec.lean`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_dist_vec` | `def` | Vector convergence in distribution: `Ој.map (Xn n)` converges weakly to `Q` in `ProbabilityMeasure E` for `E` a pseudo-metric space with `OpensMeasurableSpace`. Direct port of scalar `Tendsto_dist`. |
| `Tendsto_dist_vec.add_isLittleOp_one` | vector Slutsky absorption | `Xn вҮ’ Q` and `вҖ–Yn вҲ’ XnвҖ– = o_p(1)` вҮ’ `Yn вҮ’ Q`, on `[NormedAddCommGroup E] [BorelSpace E]`. **Proved by Codex** as a metric-space port of the scalar proof using the bounded-Lipschitz portmanteau characterization (`tendsto_iff_forall_lipschitz_integral_tendsto`); integrand `F : E вҶ’ в„қ` is split over `{вҖ–F (Yn) вҲ’ F (Xn)вҖ– < LВ·Оө/2}` and its complement, with the complement controlled via Lipschitz pullback through `вҖ–Yn вҲ’ XnвҖ–`. |
| `Tendsto_dist_vec.congr_ae` | eventual a.e. congruence | Vector analogue of `Tendsto_dist.congr_ae`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous` | continuous-map pushforward | `g : E вҶ’ F` continuous вҮ’ `Tendsto_dist_vec Xn Q Ој` вҮ’ `Tendsto_dist_vec (g вҲҳ Xn) (Q.map g) Ој`. **Proved by Codex** via `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_map_of_tendsto_of_continuous`. Used by the multivariate О”-method to push forward by the FrГ©chet derivative `Dg`. |

### 12c0. `Stat/CLT/GaussianTail.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `gaussian_tail_small` | `0<v вҶ’ 0<Оө вҶ’ вҲғ R>0, N(0,v)((-вҲһ,-R]) вүӨ Оө вҲ§ N(0,v)([R,вҲһ)) вүӨ Оө` | Gaussian half-line tail control: both one-sided tails of `N(0,v)` go below any `Оө` for a large cutoff (continuity-from-above on half-line families shrinking to `вҲ…`). Factored out of the quantile-Bahadur rate proof; reused by `PartialID/Inference/ImbensManski.lean` | **Proved** |

### 12c. `Stat/CLT/AsymptoticLinearity.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gaussianMeasure m ПғВІ` | `def` | Real-typed wrapper around `ProbabilityTheory.gaussianReal m ПғВІ.toNNReal`; negative variances clip to `0` (Dirac at `m`). **Proved.** |
| `instIsProbabilityMeasureGaussianMeasure` | instance | `IsProbabilityMeasure (gaussianMeasure m v)` for every real `m, v`, inherited from Mathlib's `gaussianReal`. **Proved.** |
| `IsAsymLinear Оёn ОёвӮҖ ПҲ S I` | structure | Asymptotic linearity per `def:est-asym-linear`: `mean_zero`, `finite_var` (encoded as `Integrable (ПҲВІ)`), `remainder = o_p(1)` over an explicit index family `I` (classically `I n = {0, ..., n-1}`). |
| `IsAsymLinear.normalizedSum S ПҲ I n` | `def` | The normalised partial sum `(1/вҲҡ|I n|) ОЈ_{iвҲҲI(n)} ПҲ(Z_i)` along an i.i.d. sample and an index family `I`. |
| `IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator Оёn ОёвӮҖ I n` | `def` | The rescaled estimator `вҲҡ|I n| (Оёn n вҲ’ ОёвӮҖ)` for index family `I`. |
| `IIDSample.clt_normalized_sum` | `(1/вҲҡn) ОЈ ПҲ(Zбөў) вҮ’ N(0, вҲ«ПҲВІ dP)` | CLT contact point: needs `ПҲ` measurable, mean-zero, square-integrable. **Proved** via `ProbabilityTheory.central_limit` from the upstream `RemyDegenne/CLT` package, with variance normalisation and a zero-variance degenerate branch. |
| `Tendsto_dist.add_isLittleOp_one` | Slutsky absorption | If `Xn вҮ’ Q` in distribution and `Yn вҲ’ Xn = o_p(1)`, then `Yn вҮ’ Q`. **Proved** at the `Tendsto_dist` (measure-level) wrapper via the bounded-Lipschitz characterisation of weak convergence. |
| `Tendsto_dist.congr_ae` | eventual a.e. congruence | If `Xn вҮ’ Q` and `Xn n =бөҗ Yn n` eventually, then `Yn вҮ’ Q`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.const_mul_tendsto_gaussian` | deterministic scalar Slutsky for Gaussian targets | If `Xn вҮ’ N(0, v)` and deterministic `a n вҶ’ aвӮҖ`, then `a n В· Xn вҮ’ N(0, aвӮҖВІv)`. **Proved** (depends on `MeasureTheory.ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_map_mul_of_tendsto`) by `Tendsto_dist.const_mul_tendsto` plus `gaussianReal_map_const_mul`. |
| `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal` | `вҲҡ|I n|(Оёn вҲ’ ОёвӮҖ) вҮ’ N(0, вҲ«ПҲВІ dP)` | Headline corollary `prop:est-al-implies-an`: combines `clt_normalized_sum` with `Tendsto_dist.add_isLittleOp_one` to absorb the asymptotically-linear remainder (classical corollary uses `I n = Finset.range n`). **Proved.** |

### 12c'. `Stat/CLT/AsymptoticLinearityVec.lean`

Vector-Оё companion to `AsymptoticLinearity.lean`. Adds the vector-valued asymptotic-linearity predicate `IsAsymLinearVec` parametric in a Euclidean space `E`, plus a bridge to the scalar form when `E = в„қ`, and the headline multivariate asymptotic-normality corollary.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsAsymLinearVec Оёn ОёвӮҖ ПҲ S I` | structure | Vector-valued asymptotic linearity at `ОёвӮҖ : E` with vector IF `ПҲ : X вҶ’ E`. Fields: `mean_zero` (Bochner integral), `finite_var` (`вҖ–ПҲвҖ–ВІ вҲҲ LВ№`), `remainder` (norm `o_p(1)`). |
| `IsAsymLinearVec.normalizedSum`, `rescaledEstimator` | `def`s | Vector versions of the scalar accessors. |
| `IsAsymLinearVec.toScalar` | bridge | When `E = в„қ`, `IsAsymLinearVec` reduces to scalar `IsAsymLinear`. **Proved**: field-by-field rewrite using `вҖ–xвҖ– = |x|` and `r вҖў x = r * x` on `в„қ`. |
| `IsAsymLinear.toVec` | bridge | Scalar-to-vector lift. **Proved** (mirror of `toScalar`). |
| `IsAsymLinearVec.tendsto_normal_vec` | `theorem` | Multivariate analogue of `tendsto_normal`: given a vector CLT contact `_hCLT` (the partial-sum pushforward converges to `Q : ProbabilityMeasure E`), the rescaled estimator pushforward also converges to `Q`. **Proved by Codex** by absorbing `_h.remainder` into `_hCLT` via `Tendsto_dist_vec.add_isLittleOp_one`. The multivariate CLT itself is left to the caller. |

### 12d. `Stat/Orthogonality/Orthogonality.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `NeymanOrthogonal m О·вӮҖ ОёвӮҖ P` | `def` | Moment condition `вҲ« m О·вӮҖ x ОёвӮҖ dP = 0` plus vanishing GГўteaux derivative along every direction `О· вҲҲ H`. Restricted realization sets are imposed at instantiation (cf. `H_Оө` in `Estimation/ATE/InfluenceFunction.lean`). |

### 12e. `Stat/SampleSplit.lean` (umbrella)

Re-export umbrella over per-strategy split modules:
- `Stat/SampleSplit/OneShot.lean` вҖ” `OneShotSplit` (the original API, unchanged).
- `Stat/SampleSplit/KFold.lean` вҖ” `KFoldSplit` (K-fold cross-fitting).

Existing consumers that import `Causalean.Stat.SampleSplit` continue to work.

#### 12e.OneShot

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OneShotSplit S` | structure | Fold-size schedule `nвӮҒ : в„• вҶ’ в„•` with `bound`, `grow`, `cogrow` per `def:est-one-shot-split`. |
| `OneShotSplit.foldA n` | `def` | `{0, вҖҰ, nвӮҒ n вҲ’ 1}`. |
| `OneShotSplit.foldB n` | `def` | `{nвӮҒ n, вҖҰ, n вҲ’ 1}` (filtered range). |
| `OneShotSplit.foldA_disjoint_foldB` | `Disjoint (foldA n) (foldB n)` | Pure combinatorics. **Proved.** |
| `OneShotSplit.folds_indep` | `IndepFun (Z\|foldA) (Z\|foldB) Ој` | Direct corollary of `iIndepFun.indepFun_finset` plus disjointness. **Proved.** |
| `OneShotSplit.foldB_card` | `(foldB n).card = n - nвӮҒ n` | Cardinality of the suffix-shaped fold-B (filter on `range n`). **Proved.** Used to derive eventual positivity of `(foldB n).card` from `cogrow`. |
| `OneShotSplit.foldB_card_tendsto` | `Tendsto (fun n => (foldB n).card) atTop atTop` | Direct corollary of `cogrow` + `foldB_card`. **Proved.** |

#### 12e.2. `Stat/SampleSplit/KFold.lean`

K-fold sample split (cross-fitting). Sibling to `OneShot.lean`. Used by `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DMLCrossFit.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `KFoldSplit S K` | structure | K disjoint folds with `partition`, `cover`, `grow`, `ratio` (`fold(n,k).card/n вҶ’ 1/K`). |
| `KFoldSplit.trainComplement n k` | `def` | `{0,вҖҰ,n-1} \ fold(n,k)`. |
| `KFoldSplit.fold_disjoint_trainComplement` | `lemma` | Disjointness via `Finset.mem_sdiff`. **Proved.** |
| `KFoldSplit.folds_indep` | `theorem` | `(Z i)_{i вҲҲ fold(n,k)}` independent of `(Z i)_{i вҲҲ trainComplement(n,k)}` under `Ој`. **Proved** (one-line `S.indep.indepFun_finset` mirroring `OneShotSplit.folds_indep`). |

### 12f. `Stat/Orthogonality/ConditionalOp.lean`

Envelope-based stochastic-order helpers + CauchyвҖ“Schwarz packaging. Consumed by `PlugIn.lean` (single-factor bias) and `Remainder.lean` (product-rate AIPW bias). Causal-agnostic; candidate for upstream Mathlib contribution once stable.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsBigOp.of_sqEnvelope` | `(Xn n)ВІ вүӨ cn n a.s.` вҲ§ `cn = O_p(rnВІ)` вҮ’ `Xn = O_p(rn)` | Square-envelope вҮ’ `O_p`, using a.e. event inclusion and limsup monotonicity. **Proved.** |
| `IsLittleOp.of_sqEnvelope` | `(Xn n)ВІ вүӨ cn n a.s.` вҲ§ `cn = o_p(rnВІ)` вҮ’ `Xn = o_p(rn)` | Square-envelope вҮ’ `o_p`, using a.e. event inclusion and convergence-to-zero monotonicity. **Proved.** |
| `abs_integral_le_eLpNorm_two` | `IsProbabilityMeasure ОҪ` вҮ’ `\|вҲ« f dОҪ\| вүӨ вҖ–fвҖ–_{LВІ(ОҪ)}` | Constant-case CauchyвҖ“Schwarz via the LВ№/LВІ comparison on probability spaces. **Proved.** |
| `integral_abs_mul_le_eLpNorm_mul_eLpNorm` | `вҲ« \|fВ·g\| dОҪ вүӨ вҖ–fвҖ–вӮӮ В· вҖ–gвҖ–вӮӮ` | CauchyвҖ“Schwarz for LВІВ·LВІ via Mathlib HГ¶lder. **Proved.** |
| `integral_op_of_eLpNorm_op` | `eLpNorm (fn) 2 ОҪ = o_p(rn)` вҮ’ `вҲ« fn dОҪ = o_p(rn)` | Lifts LВІ-rate to integrated bias by pointwise domination. **Proved.** |
| `integral_abs_mul_op_of_eLpNorm_op` | `eLpNorm (fn) 2 ОҪ = o_p(sn)` вҲ§ fixed `g вҲҲ LВІ` вҮ’ `вҲ« \|fnВ·g\| dОҪ = o_p(sn В· вҖ–gвҖ–вӮӮ)` | Lifts LВІ-rate to integrated absolute product, splitting the zero/nonzero fixed-norm cases. **Proved.** |

### 12g. `Stat/SampleSplit/PartialFoldCLT.lean`

Fold-restricted CLT for one-shot sample splits. Provides the CLT contact and asymptotic-normality corollary at rate `вҲҡ|B(n)|` for split estimators (PlugIn / DML), plus a вҲҡn-rate conversion under a fixed split ratio `|B(n)|/n вҶ’ c вҲҲ (0,1)` with the standard variance inflation `ПғВІ/c`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.clt_normalizedFoldB` | `(1/вҲҡ\|B(n)\|) ОЈ_{iвҲҲB(n)} ПҲ(Z_i) вҮ’ N(0, вҲ«ПҲВІdP)` | Fold-B CLT. **Proved** by reindexing fold-B through a finite-set characteristic-function bridge and composing the existing `IIDSample.clt_normalized_sum` CLT wrapper along `\|B(n)\| вҶ’ вҲһ`. |
| `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB` | AL on fold B вҮ’ `вҲҡ\|B(n)\|В·(Оёn вҲ’ ОёвӮҖ) вҮ’ N(0, ПғВІ)` | Mirror of `tendsto_normal` for `I = split.foldB`. **Proved by Codex** (4-line composition of `clt_normalizedFoldB` + `Tendsto_dist.add_isLittleOp_one`). |
| `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal_foldB_sqrt_n` | `\|B(n)\|/n вҶ’ c вҲҲ (0,1)` вҲ§ AL on fold B вҮ’ `вҲҡnВ·(Оёn вҲ’ ОёвӮҖ) вҮ’ N(0, ПғВІ/c)` | вҲҡn-rate conversion under fixed split ratio. **Proved** (depends on the Mathlib varying-map stub); handles fold-B measurability, deterministic `вҲҡn/вҲҡ\|B(n)\| вҶ’ 1/вҲҡc`, eventual a.e. equality, and variance algebra. |

### 12h. `Stat/SampleSplit/FoldBEmpiricalProcess.lean`

Empirical-process bounds on a centered evaluation-fold sum of a training-fold-measurable random function. Consumed by `DMLChernozhukov.lean` (one-shot) and `DMLCrossFit.lean` (K-fold). The file is structured in three layers:

1. **Abstract layer (private)** вҖ” parameterised over an arbitrary evaluation-fold schedule `eval : в„• вҶ’ Finset в„•`, training Пғ-algebra `m_train : в„• вҶ’ MeasurableSpace О©`, plus independence and iid-product-law hypotheses.
2. **OneShot wrappers** вҖ” preserved public signatures `foldB_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` and `sqrtFoldB_integral_isLittleOp_one`, now thin instantiations of the abstract layer.
3. **K-fold corollaries** вҖ” new public lemmas under `KFoldSplit.*` namespace.

#### Abstract layer (private)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `centeredEvalSum_sq_lintegral_le_core` / `centeredEvalSum_sq_lintegral_le` | `вҲ«вҒ» Пү, ((1/вҲҡ\|eval n\|) ОЈ_{eval n} (g(Z_i) вҲ’ вҲ«g dP))ВІ вҲӮОј вүӨ вҲ«вҒ» Пү, вҖ–g(n,Пү)вҖ–ВІ_{LВІ(P)} вҲӮОј` | Second-moment bound on the centered eval-sum, parametric in `(eval, m_train, h_train_le, h_indep, h_iid)`. **Proved** via the generic `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` helper in `Causalean/Mathlib/IIDCenteredSum.lean`. |
| `evalSum_truncated_isLittleOp_one` | bounded `g` with `вҲ«вҒ» вҖ–gвҖ–ВІвӮӮ вҶ’ 0` вҮ’ centered eval-sum is `o_p(1)` | Markov / Chebyshev in `ENNReal` plus bounded convergence. **Proved by Codex (A0)** via `MeasureTheory.meas_ge_le_lintegral_div`. Eventual `0 < (eval n).card` from a separate `h_eval_card_grow` hypothesis. |
| `evalSum_isLittleOp_one` | `вҖ–g(n,Пү)вҖ–_{LВІ(P)} = o_p(1)` (random-norm rate) вҮ’ centered eval-sum is `o_p(1)` | Truncation at LВІ-norm вүӨ 1, then `evalSum_truncated_isLittleOp_one` + `IsLittleOp.of_eq_on_asymptotic`. **Proved.** |
| `evalSum_bias_isLittleOp_one` | `(eval n).card / n вҶ’ c вҲҲ (0,1)` вҲ§ `вҖ–fвҖ–_{LВІ(P)} = o_p(n^{-1/2})` вҮ’ `вҲҡ\|eval n\|В·вҲ«f dP = o_p(1)` | Bias term, fully parametric. **Proved.** |

#### OneShot wrappers (public; signatures preserved for backward compat)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `foldB_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` | fold-A-measurable `f` with `вҖ–fвҖ–_{LВІ(P)} = o_p(1)` вҮ’ `(1/вҲҡ\|B(n)\|) ОЈ_{i вҲҲ B(n)} (f(n,Пү,Z_i Пү) вҲ’ вҲ«f dP) = o_p(1)` | Thin wrapper around the abstract layer, instantiating `eval n := split.foldB n`, `m_train n := comap (foldA n) Z`, `h_indep := split.folds_indep n` (definitional `IndepFun`-to-`Indep` bridge), `h_iid := S.indep.precomp + IdentDistrib.map_eq + S.law`, `h_eval_card_grow := split.foldB_card_tendsto`. **Proved.** |
| `sqrtFoldB_integral_isLittleOp_one` | `\|B(n)\|/n вҶ’ c вҲҲ (0,1)` вҲ§ `вҖ–fвҖ–_{LВІ(P)} = o_p(n^{-1/2})` вҮ’ `вҲҡ\|B(n)\|В·вҲ«f dP = o_p(1)` | OneShot bias wrapper. **Proved.** |

#### KFold corollaries (public; new)

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `KFoldSplit.fold_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` | per `k : Fin K`, training-complement-measurable `f` with `вҖ–fвҖ–_{LВІ(P)} = o_p(1)` вҮ’ `(1/вҲҡ\|fold n k\|) ОЈ_{fold n k} (f(Z_i) вҲ’ вҲ«f dP) = o_p(1)` | K-fold per-evaluation-fold centered sum. Instantiates the abstract layer with `eval n := split.fold n k`, `m_train n := comap (trainComplement n k) Z`, `h_indep` via `split.folds_indep n k` (with `.symm` since the K-fold version is stated `IndepFun fold trainComplement`), `h_eval_card_grow := split.grow k`. **Proved.** |
| `KFoldSplit.sqrtFold_integral_isLittleOp_one` | `1 < K` вҲ§ `вҖ–fвҖ–_{LВІ(P)} = o_p(n^{-1/2})` вҮ’ per `k`, `вҲҡ\|fold n k\|В·вҲ«f dP = o_p(1)` | K-fold per-fold bias. Uses `c := (K:в„қ)вҒ»В№` вҲҲ (0, 1) (whence the `1 < K` requirement) and `split.ratio k`. **Proved.** |

### 12i. `Stat/MEstimation/InfluenceFunction.lean`

Light data structure packaging the three properties an influence function must satisfy: measurability, mean-zero, and finite second moment. Mirrors `def:sp-pathwise-diff` from `doc/basic_concepts/Semi-parametric Inference/semi_parametric_inference.tex` at the *assumption* level вҖ” pathwise differentiability is deferred.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `InfluenceFunction P ПҲ` | `Prop`-valued structure | An influence function for parameter `Оё : E` under law `P : Measure X` is a measurable, mean-zero, square-integrable function `ПҲ : X вҶ’ E`. Fields: `measurable`, `mean_zero`, `finite_var`. |

### 12j. `Stat/Inference/DeltaMethod.lean`

Smooth-function CLT. Two forms; both proved.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `deltaMethod_scalar` | `theorem` | If `вҲҡn (T_n вҲ’ tвӮҖ) вҮ’ N(0, ПғВІ)` and `g : в„қ вҶ’ в„қ` differentiable at `tвӮҖ` with derivative `g'`, then `вҲҡn (g T_n вҲ’ g tвӮҖ) вҮ’ N(0, g'ВІ ПғВІ)`. **Proved by Codex.** Pipeline: tightness of `вҲҡn (T_n вҲ’ tвӮҖ)` via `Tendsto_dist.tightness`; consistency `T_n вҲ’ tвӮҖ вҶ’_p 0` via `IsBigOp.const_mul_tendsto_zero` against `(вҲҡn)вҒ»В№ вҶ’ 0`; remainder `Rn := вҲҡn В· (g(T_n) вҲ’ g tвӮҖ вҲ’ g' (T_n вҲ’ tвӮҖ))` is `o_p(1)` from `HasDerivAt.isLittleO_sub` on the localizing event `{|T_n вҲ’ tвӮҖ| < ПҒ}`; finally `Tendsto_dist.const_mul_tendsto_gaussian` for `g' В· вҲҡn (T_n вҲ’ tвӮҖ)` and `Tendsto_dist.add_isLittleOp_one` to absorb `Rn`. |
| `deltaMethod` | `theorem` | Multivariate form: `вҲҡn вҖў (T_n вҲ’ tвӮҖ) вҮ’ Q` (vector limit) and `g : E вҶ’ F` FrГ©chet-differentiable at `tвӮҖ` with derivative `Dg : E вҶ’L[в„қ] F` вҮ’ `вҲҡn вҖў (g T_n вҲ’ g tвӮҖ) вҮ’ Q.map Dg`. **Proved by Codex.** Vector port of the scalar pipeline: `вҖ–вҲҡn вҖў (T_n вҲ’ tвӮҖ)вҖ–` is `O_p(1)` via `Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous` along `вҖ–В·вҖ– : E вҶ’ в„қ` plus `Tendsto_dist.tightness`; consistency `T_n вҲ’ tвӮҖ вҶ’_p 0` in norm; remainder controlled by `HasFDerivAt.isLittleO_sub`; linear pushforward `Dg` via `Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous` again; vector Slutsky absorption via `Tendsto_dist_vec.add_isLittleOp_one`. |

### 12j'. `Stat/Inference/HadamardDeriv.lean` + `Stat/Inference/FunctionalDelta.lean`

**Directional (Hadamard) functional delta method.** The FrГ©chet delta method (12j) cannot reach lattice functionals: an interval-identified parameter's sharp endpoints are `sup'`/`inf'` of separately estimable quantities (`PartialID/.../IntersectionBound.lean` `iInter_Icc_eq_Icc : вӢӮвұј [Lвұј,Uвұј] = [sup' L, inf' U]`; Manski MIV envelopes; combined MTR+MTS bounds), and `max`/`min` are only *Hadamard directionally* differentiable вҖ” with a **nonlinear** derivative at a tie, which is exactly the binding/kink regime where bound inference is delicate (HiranoвҖ“Porter 2012; FangвҖ“Santos 2019). References: van der Vaart (1998) В§20.2; Shapiro (1991).

`HadamardDeriv.lean` (analysis core, no probability):

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasHadamardDirDerivAt ПҶ ПҶ' Оё` | def | Sequential Hadamard directional differentiability over normed spaces: for every `h`, every `hвӮҷ вҶ’ h`, every `tвӮҷ вҶ’ 0вҒә`, `(tвӮҷ)вҒ»В№ вҖў (ПҶ(Оё + tвӮҷвҖўhвӮҷ) вҲ’ ПҶОё) вҶ’ ПҶ' h`. `ПҶ'` is positively homogeneous, *not* assumed linear. |
| `HasFDerivAt.hasHadamardDirDerivAt` | theorem | **FrГ©chet вҮ’ Hadamard**: a FrГ©chet-differentiable `ПҶ` (derivative `L`) is Hadamard directionally differentiable with derivative `L`. *(One `sorry`: the `(tвӮҷ)вҒ»В№ вҖў rвӮҷ вҶ’ 0` remainder step вҖ” standard `IsLittleO` divide-by-rate, off the critical path; the `max`/`min` results below do not use it.)* |
| `maxDirDeriv a b` / `minDirDeriv a b` | def | Directional derivative of `max`/`min : в„қГ—в„қ вҶ’ в„қ` at `(a,b)`: a coordinate projection off the diagonal, and `max`/`min` itself (sublinear) at the tie `a=b`. |
| `continuous_maxDirDeriv` / `continuous_minDirDeriv` | theorem | The directional derivatives are continuous. |
| `hasHadamardDirDerivAt_max` / `hasHadamardDirDerivAt_min` | theorem | `max`/`min` are Hadamard directionally differentiable at every `(a,b)` with the derivatives above. **Sorry-free, axiom-clean.** Tie case: `max(b+t hвӮҒ, b+t hвӮӮ) = b + tВ·max(hвӮҒ,hвӮӮ)`; off-diagonal: the dominating coordinate is eventually selected. |

`FunctionalDelta.lean` (the delta theorem; consumes the existing `Tendsto_dist_vec` weak-convergence infra):

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `isLittleOp_one_of_measure_ne_tendsto_zero` | theorem | Reusable Slutsky device: if `Ој{YвӮҷ вү  XвӮҷ} вҶ’ 0` then `YвӮҷ вҲ’ XвӮҷ = o_p(1)`. The ingredient for the *off-diagonal* case (lattice functional selects one coordinate except when the estimated ordering is wrong, an event of vanishing probability). **Proved.** |
| `instIsProbabilityMeasure_map_max` / `_min` | instance | The lattice pushforward `Q.map (z вҶҰ max/min z.1 z.2)` of a probability measure `Q` is a probability measure. Global instance, so the `Tendsto_dist` conclusions below need *not* assume it. |
| `sqrt_mul_max_sub` / `sqrt_mul_min_sub` | lemma | **The exact tie identity** `вҲҡnВ·(max/min ГўвӮҷ bМӮвӮҷ вҲ’ a) = max/min(вҲҡn(ГўвӮҷвҲ’a), вҲҡn(bМӮвӮҷвҲ’a))` вҖ” the rescaled image equals the lattice functional applied to the joint rescaled deviation, with no remainder. |
| `maxStat_aemeasurable` / `minStat_aemeasurable` | lemma | The image statistic `вҲҡnВ·(max/min ГўвӮҷ bМӮвӮҷ вҲ’ a)` is `AEMeasurable` for every `n`, *derived* from the joint deviation's measurability via the tie identity вҖ” so the delta theorems need *not* assume it. |
| `deltaMethod_max_tie` | theorem | **Directional delta method for `max` at a tie.** If `вҲҡn вҖў ((ГўвӮҷ,bМӮвӮҷ) вҲ’ (a,a)) вҮ’ Q` on `в„қГ—в„қ`, then `вҲҡnВ·(max ГўвӮҷ bМӮвӮҷ вҲ’ a) вҮ’ Q.map (z вҶҰ max z.1 z.2)` (generally *non-Gaussian* вҖ” the law of `max(ZвӮҒ,ZвӮӮ)`). The binding/kink case the FrГ©chet method cannot reach. **Sorry-free, axiom-clean.** Proof: the exact identity `вҲҡn(max ГўвӮҷ bМӮвӮҷ вҲ’ a) = max(вҲҡn(ГўвӮҷвҲ’a), вҲҡn(bМӮвӮҷвҲ’a)) = maxвҲҳSвӮҷ` (`sqrt_mul_max_sub`), then continuous mapping (`Tendsto_dist_vec.map_continuous`) + `congr_ae`; no linearization remainder. Hypotheses: only the joint-deviation measurability `hSn_meas` and the joint CLT `hCLT` вҖ” the pushforward-probability instance and the image-statistic measurability are now discharged automatically (via the instance / `maxStat_aemeasurable`). |
| `deltaMethod_min_tie` | theorem | Companion for `min`. **Sorry-free, axiom-clean.** Same discharged-assumption shape. |

*Off-diagonal (`a вү  b`) and the abstract `sup'`/`inf'`-over-finite-families forms are the natural follow-ups; the tie case is the novel/hard one and the reusable `isLittleOp_one_of_measure_ne_tendsto_zero` is the device for the off-diagonal reduction.*

### 12k. `Stat/MEstimation/ZEstimator.lean`

Parametric-inference regularity bundle (structure layer). Houses only `ZEstimatorRegularity`; the headline theorem `zEstimator_clt` lives downstream in `Stat/MEstimation/ZEstimatorCLT.lean` to keep the import DAG acyclic (the proof needs `Stat/MEstimation/EmpiricalExpansion.lean`, which itself imports this file for the structure).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ZEstimatorRegularity ПҲ ОёвӮҖ P` | structure | Identification (`вҲ« ПҲ(В·;ОёвӮҖ) dP = 0`), Jacobian `JвӮҖ : E вҶ’L[в„қ] E` + invertibility witness, FrГ©chet-derivative spec, finite var, measurability, plus three smoothness fields: `jacobian_continuity` (`ContinuousAt` of `Оё вҶҰ вҲ« ПҲ(В·;Оё) dP` at `ОёвӮҖ`; redundant with `JвӮҖ_spec` but exposed for downstream convenience), `psi_int_neighborhood` (`ПҲ(В·;Оё)` is `P`-integrable on a neighborhood of `ОёвӮҖ`), and `score_envelope` (integrable `LВІ` derivative envelope: вҲғ `Оҙ>0`, вҲғ nonneg measurable `F : X вҶ’ в„қ` with `FВІвҲҲLВ№(P)` such that `вҖ–ПҲОёzвҲ’ПҲОёвӮҖzвҖ– вүӨ вҖ–ОёвҲ’ОёвӮҖвҖ–В·F z` for all `z : X` and all `вҖ–ОёвҲ’ОёвӮҖвҖ–<Оҙ` (pointwise вҖ” produced by MVT on smooth scores; needed to transfer the bound to random sample-dependent `Оёn`) вҖ” this is the LaTeX-spec form of `def:par-smoothness` and is what discharges `score_diff_L2_isLittleOp_sqrt` via Markov + iid LLN on `FВІ`). Reference: `def:par-smoothness` (van der Vaart 1998 В§5.6). |

### 12l. `Stat/MEstimation/EmpiricalExpansion.lean`

Empirical-process / linearization layer feeding `zEstimator_clt`. References: `def:par-smoothness`, `thm:par-z-clt` in `doc/basic_concepts/Semi-parametric Inference/parametric_inference.tex`; van der Vaart (1998) В§5.6 Thm 5.41; Newey-McFadden (1994) В§7.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `StochEquicontAt ПҲ ОёвӮҖ P Ој S Оёn` | `def` | Asymptotic-equicontinuity predicate at `ОёвӮҖ` along `Оёn`: `вҲҖ Оө>0, вҲғ Оҙ>0` such that the empirical-process gap `(вҲҡn)вҒ»В№ ОЈ (ПҲ(Оёn,Z_i)вҲ’ПҲ(ОёвӮҖ,Z_i)) вҲ’ вҲҡn вҲ« (ПҲ(Оёn,В·)вҲ’ПҲ(ОёвӮҖ,В·)) dP` exceeding `Оө` on `{вҖ–ОёnвҲ’ОёвӮҖвҖ–<Оҙ}` has vanishing probability as `nвҶ’вҲһ`. Standard Donsker-class hypothesis (van der Vaart 1998 Lemma 19.24 / В§19.4); exposed as a hypothesis pending Mathlib's empirical-process layer. |
| `score_diff_L2_isLittleOp_sqrt` | `theorem` | Under `ZEstimatorRegularity` (in particular `score_envelope`) + consistency `Оёn вҶ’_p ОёвӮҖ`, the sample mean square `(1/n) ОЈ_{i<n} вҖ–ПҲ(Оёn,Z_i) вҲ’ ПҲ(ОёвӮҖ,Z_i)вҖ–ВІ` is `o_p(1)`. Proof: dominated by `вҖ–ОёnвҲ’ОёвӮҖвҖ–ВІ В· (1/n) ОЈ F(Z_i)ВІ` on the `Оҙ`-good event (pointwise envelope bound), where the second factor satisfies a Markov bound `Ој{Mn>M} вүӨ K/M` with `K := вҲ«FВІ dP` (using `IIDSample.identDist + S.law` to push `вҲ«Пү F(S.Z i Пү)ВІ dОј = вҲ«z F zВІ dP`); the `Оҙ`-bad event has probability вҶ’ 0 by `hConsistent (П„/2)`. **Proved.** |
| `empiricalScoreDiff_isLittleOp_sqrt` | `theorem` | Centered empirical-process control: under `ZEstimatorRegularity`, consistency, and `hStochEquicont : StochEquicontAt ПҲ ОёвӮҖ P Ој S Оёn`, `вҖ–(1/вҲҡn) ОЈ (ПҲ(Оёn,Z_i) вҲ’ ПҲ(ОёвӮҖ,Z_i)) вҲ’ вҲҡn В· вҲ« (ПҲ(Оёn,В·) вҲ’ ПҲ(ОёвӮҖ,В·)) dPвҖ– = o_p(1)`. (Subtracting `вҲҡn В· вҲ«` is the iid bias correction.) Proof: combine `hStochEquicont(Оө)` with `hConsistent(Оҙ/2)` to remove the conditioning on `{вҖ–ОёnвҲ’ОёвӮҖвҖ–<Оҙ}` via measure-union bound. **Proved.** |
| `populationScoreDiff_eq_jacobian_plus_remainder` | `theorem` | Population FrГ©chet expansion: `вҲ« (ПҲ(Оё,В·) вҲ’ ПҲ(ОёвӮҖ,В·)) dP вҲ’ JвӮҖ (Оё вҲ’ ОёвӮҖ) =o[рқ“қ ОёвӮҖ] вҖ–Оё вҲ’ ОёвӮҖвҖ–`. Rephrasing of `JвӮҖ_spec` as `Asymptotics.IsLittleO`. **Proved.** |
| `localStochasticExpansion` | `theorem` | Headline expansion: under `ZEstimatorRegularity`, consistency, `hStochEquicont`, and the rate `hRate : вҖ–Оёn вҲ’ ОёвӮҖвҖ– = O_p(1/вҲҡn)`, the norm `вҖ–(1/вҲҡn) ОЈ (ПҲ(Оёn,Z_i) вҲ’ ПҲ(ОёвӮҖ,Z_i)) вҲ’ вҲҡn В· JвӮҖ (Оёn вҲ’ ОёвӮҖ)вҖ–` is `o_p(1)`. Combines (b)+(c)+`hRate` via triangle. Plugged directly into `zEstimator_clt`. **Proved.** |

### 12l'. `Stat/MEstimation/ZEstimatorCLT.lean`

Headline theorem layer for the parametric-inference workhorse. Z- / M-estimator CLT for vector-valued estimating equations. OLS, MLE, IV-2SLS instantiate it. Imports `Stat/MEstimation/EmpiricalExpansion.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `zEstimator_clt` | `theorem` | Under `ZEstimatorRegularity`, consistency, asymptotic equicontinuity (`hStochEquicont : StochEquicontAt ПҲ ОёвӮҖ P Ој S Оёn`), the rate hypothesis `hRate : вҖ–Оёn вҲ’ ОёвӮҖвҖ– = O_p(1/вҲҡn)`, and empirical-moment vanishing, `Оёn` is asymptotically linear at `ОёвӮҖ` with IF `вҲ’JвӮҖвҒ»В№ В· ПҲ(В·; ОёвӮҖ)` (Chernozhukov form). Conclusion is `IsAsymLinearVec`. Reference: van der Vaart 1998 Thm 5.41. **Proved.** All three `IsAsymLinearVec` fields discharged: `mean_zero` (push `вҲ’JвӮҖвҒ»В№` through Bochner integral via `identification`), `finite_var` (operator-norm bound on `JвӮҖ_inv` + `finite_var`), and `remainder` (combine `localStochasticExpansion` + `hMoment`, then bound the rescaled remainder by `JвӮҖ_inv` and the finite-dim left-inverse derived from `JвӮҖ_inverse`). The Donsker-step content is encapsulated in the `hStochEquicont` hypothesis, to be discharged per-instance until Mathlib's empirical-process layer lands. |

### 12l''. `Stat/MEstimation/ExtremumConsistency.lean` вҖ” discharging the CLT's consistency and equicontinuity hypotheses

Additive bridge: re-exposes the headline CLTs (`zEstimator_clt`, `gmm_asymptotically_linear`) with their two *opaque* hypotheses derived from clean primitives. **Consistency** `hConsistent` is derived from the primitive extremum conditions via the empirical-process layer's `mEstimator_consistent_of_glivenkoCantelli` (В§12ep.c) вҖ” classical reduction (NeweyвҖ“McFadden 1994 Thm 2.1; van der Vaart 1998 Thm 5.7): consistency follows from a GlivenkoвҖ“Cantelli criterion plus a well-separated population maximum. **Equicontinuity** `hStochEquicont : StochEquicontAt` is derived from the *class-level* Donsker property `AsymptoticEquicont` (В§12ep.d) via `stochEquicontAt_of_asymptoticEquicont` вҖ” the estimator-indexed modulus is reconstructed from the function-class property plus consistency. The original `hConsistent`/`hStochEquicont`-taking theorems are unchanged and remain available for callers who establish those by other means. Imports `Stat/GMM/AsymptoticNormality.lean` + `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/MEstimatorConsistency.lean` + `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/Equicontinuity/Modulus.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `consistent_lt_norm_of_le_dist` | `(вҲҖ Оө>0, Ој{Оө вүӨ dist (Оёn n) ОёвӮҖ} вҶ’ 0) вҮ’ (вҲҖ Оө>0, Ој{Оө < вҖ–Оёn n вҲ’ ОёвӮҖвҖ–} вҶ’ 0)` | **Format bridge** between the consistency conclusion of the empirical-process layer (`вүӨ dist`) and the form the CLT layer consumes (`< вҖ–В·вҖ–`); on a normed group `dist = вҖ–В·вҲ’В·вҖ–` and `{Оө<вҖ–В·вҖ–} вҠҶ {ОөвүӨdist}`, so the measures squeeze to 0. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `zEstimator_clt_of_extremum` | same `IsAsymLinearVec` conclusion as `zEstimator_clt` | **Z-estimator CLT from extremum primitives.** Replaces `hConsistent` by `(m, hGC : GlivenkoCantelli S m, hArgmax : Оёn maximizes PвӮҷ m over ОёвӮҖ, hSep : well-separated population max at ОёвӮҖ)`; consistency is derived and fed to `zEstimator_clt`. The Donsker modulus `hStochEquicont`, rate `hRate`, and score FOC `hMoment` are unchanged. Typically `ПҲ = вҲӮm/вҲӮОё`, so one estimator satisfies both `hMoment` and `hArgmax`; `m`/`ПҲ` kept independent to avoid differentiability bookkeeping. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `gmm_asymptotically_linear_of_extremum` | same `IsAsymLinearVec Оёn ОёвӮҖ prob.influence вҖҰ` conclusion as `gmm_asymptotically_linear` | **GMM analogue.** Discharges `hConsistent` from a GlivenkoвҖ“Cantelli GMM criterion `m` (classically `m Оё = вҲ’бёЎвӮҷ(Оё)бөҖ W бёЎвӮҷ(Оё)`) with well-separated population max of which `Оёn` is a sample maximiser. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `zEstimator_clt_of_donsker` | same `IsAsymLinearVec` conclusion as `zEstimator_clt` | **Z-estimator CLT with the equicontinuity hypothesis discharged.** Replaces `hStochEquicont` by the class-level `hAEC : AsymptoticEquicont ПҲ ОёвӮҖ P Ој S` (В§12ep.d); `StochEquicontAt` is reconstructed via `stochEquicontAt_of_asymptoticEquicont` from `hAEC` + `hConsistent`. Keeps `hConsistent`, `hRate`, `hMoment`. **Proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** |
| `zEstimator_clt_of_extremum_donsker` | same `IsAsymLinearVec` conclusion as `zEstimator_clt` | **Both opaque hypotheses discharged.** Assumes neither `hConsistent` nor `hStochEquicont`: takes the extremum package `(m, hGC, hArgmax, hSep)` and the Donsker property `hAEC`. The single derived consistency conclusion is threaded into *both* the linearisation and the `StochEquicontAt` reduction. Fully primitive Z-estimator CLT. **Proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** |
| `gmm_asymptotically_linear_of_donsker` | same `IsAsymLinearVec Оёn ОёвӮҖ prob.influence вҖҰ` conclusion as `gmm_asymptotically_linear` | **GMM analogue of `zEstimator_clt_of_donsker`.** `hStochEquicont` replaced by the class-level `hAEC : AsymptoticEquicont prob.score prob.ОёвӮҖ P Ој S`. **Proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** |
| `gmm_asymptotically_linear_of_extremum_donsker` | same `IsAsymLinearVec Оёn ОёвӮҖ prob.influence вҖҰ` conclusion as `gmm_asymptotically_linear` | **GMM analogue of `zEstimator_clt_of_extremum_donsker`.** Neither `hConsistent` nor `hStochEquicont` assumed: consistency from the GC GMM criterion, equicontinuity from `hAEC`. Fully primitive GMM asymptotic-linearity theorem. **Proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** |

### 12m. `Stat/Limit/ContinuousMapping.lean`

Estimator-agnostic convergence-in-probability primitives. Extracted from the (formerly `private`) helpers in `Estimation/NPIV/DR/AsymptoticNormal.lean` so any estimator can reuse them. Consumed by `Stat/Inference/Studentize.lean`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_inProb.comp_continuousAt` | `Xn вҶ’_p c` вҲ§ `ContinuousAt g c` вҮ’ `gвҲҳXn вҶ’_p g c` | Continuous-mapping theorem in probability at a **constant** limit `c`. Оө-Оҙ pullback of `ContinuousAt` through `tendstoInMeasure_iff_dist`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.pi_comp_continuousAt` | finite `О№`, coordinatewise `YвӮҷбөў вҶ’_p cбөў`, `ContinuousAt g c` вҮ’ `g(YвӮҷ) вҶ’_p g(c)` | Finite-Pi continuous-mapping theorem in probability. Converts coordinate tails to a finite union bound, then applies continuity in the product metric. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.matrix_comp_continuousAt` | entrywise `MвӮҷбөўвұј вҶ’_p MвӮҖбөўвұј`, `ContinuousAt g MвӮҖ` вҮ’ `g(MвӮҷ) вҶ’_p g(MвӮҖ)` | Matrix-valued corollary of the finite-Pi theorem, reindexing entries by `KГ—K`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.inv` | `Yn вҶ’_p YвӮҖ` вҲ§ `YвӮҖ вү  0` вҮ’ `1/Yn вҶ’_p 1/YвӮҖ` | Reciprocal continuity (special case of `comp_continuousAt` via `continuousAt_invвӮҖ`). **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.sub_const` | `Yn вҶ’_p YвӮҖ` вҮ’ `Yn вҲ’ YвӮҖ вҶ’_p 0` | Center a convergence-in-probability statement at its constant limit. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.sub` | `Xn вҶ’_p a` вҲ§ `Yn вҶ’_p b` вҮ’ `Xn вҲ’ Yn вҶ’_p a вҲ’ b` | Difference of two convergent-in-probability sequences (constant limits); Оө/2 union bound (Mathlib lacks `TendstoInMeasure.sub`). **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.isLittleOp_one` | `Yn вҶ’_p 0` вҮ’ `Yn = o_p(1)` | Convergence in probability to zero is `o_p(1)`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.isBigOp_one` | `Xn вҶ’_p c` вҮ’ `Xn = O_p(1)` | In-probability tightness at a constant limit. **Proved.** |

### 12n. `Stat/Limit/WLLN.lean`

Generic weak law of large numbers for `IIDSample`, plus the consistent-variance corollary. Generalizes the Panel-specific `iidPanel_sampleMean_ae` (boundedness weakened to integrability; a.s. limit converted to in-probability). Supplies the `ПғМӮвӮҷ вҶ’_p ПғВІ` consistency input that the studentized/Wald layer takes as a hypothesis.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.measurable_sampleMean` | `Measurable g` вҮ’ `Measurable (S.sampleMean g N)` | Measurability of the sample mean (finite sum of `g вҲҳ Z i`, scaled). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_tendsto_inProb` | `Integrable (gвҲҳZвӮҖ)` вҮ’ `S.sampleMean g вҶ’_p вҲ« g dP` | WLLN: i.i.d. sample mean converges in probability to the population mean. Wraps `strong_law_ae_real` (a.s.) then `tendstoInMeasure_of_tendsto_ae` on the finite measure `Ој`; law transfer via `integral_map` + `S.law`. Requires `[IsProbabilityMeasure P]`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleSecondMoment_tendsto_inProb` | `Integrable (ПҲВІвҲҳZвӮҖ)` вҮ’ `S.sampleMean ПҲВІ вҶ’_p вҲ« ПҲВІ dP` | Consistent variance for a known influence function `ПҲ`: `(1/n)ОЈПҲ(Zбөў)ВІ вҶ’_p ПғВІ`. Direct corollary with `g := ПҲВІ`. **Proved.** |

### 12n'. `Stat/SampleSplit/FoldBWLLN.lean`

Fold-B weak law of large numbers: the analogue of `sampleMean_tendsto_inProb` averaged over the evaluation fold `B(n)` of a `OneShotSplit` (a growing index subset) instead of the full sample. Unlike the full-sample WLLN (an SLLN/a.e. argument) this is a finite-variance Chebyshev argument over the fold, so it restricts cleanly to `B(n)`. Supplies the missing substrate for fold-B sample-mean consistency (e.g. the Jacobian-consistency input of the partially linear feasible-DML estimator).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `OneShotSplit.foldB_sampleMean_tendsto_inProb` | `MemLp g 2 P` вҮ’ `(1/\|B(n)\|)ОЈ_{iвҲҲB(n)} g(Zбөў) вҶ’_p вҲ« g dP` | **Fold-B WLLN.** For a fixed finite-variance `g`, the fold-B sample mean converges in probability to the population mean. Proof: per-index variance/mean transport via `IdentDistrib.variance_eq`/`map`, `IndepFun.variance_sum` over the finset `B(n)` gives `Var = Var(g)/\|B(n)\|`, Chebyshev (`meas_ge_le_variance_div_sq`) and `\|B(n)\|вҶ’вҲһ` (`foldB_card_tendsto`) close it (empty folds handled via the eventual filter). **Proved.** |

### 12n''. `Stat/Inference/VarianceEstimation.lean`

Consistent variance/covariance estimation for the i.i.d. sample type вҖ” the `ПғМӮ вҶ’вӮҡ ПғвӮҖ` input the studentized/Wald layer takes as a hypothesis. Builds directly on the WLLN.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_mul_tendsto_inProb` | `(1/N)ОЈ gвӮҒ(Zбөў)gвӮӮ(Zбөў) вҶ’вӮҡ вҲ« gвӮҒgвӮӮ dP` | Empirical mean of a product (WLLN with `g := gвӮҒВ·gвӮӮ`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.abs_apply_mul_le_norm_sq` | `\|ПҶ(ПҲ x)В·ПҶ'(ПҲ x)\| вүӨ (вҖ–ПҶвҖ–вҖ–ПҶ'вҖ–)В·вҖ–ПҲ xвҖ–ВІ` | Operator-norm domination helper. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleCov_entry_tendsto_inProb` | `(1/N)ОЈ ПҶ(ПҲ(Zбөў))ПҶ'(ПҲ(Zбөў)) вҶ’вӮҡ вҲ« ПҶ(ПҲ)ПҶ'(ПҲ) dP` | **Covariance consistency** for `ПҲ : X вҶ’ E` via continuous coordinate functionals `ПҶ ПҶ' : E вҶ’L[в„қ] в„қ` (specializes to entrywise ОЈМӮвӮ–вӮ— via `EuclideanSpace.proj`). **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_inProb.sqrt` | `Vn вҶ’вӮҡ vвӮҖ вҮ’ вҲҡVn вҶ’вӮҡ вҲҡvвӮҖ` | Continuous-mapping for `Real.sqrt`. **Proved.** |
| `sqrt_var_tendsto_inProb` | `ПғМӮВІ вҶ’вӮҡ ПғвӮҖВІ вҲ§ ПғвӮҖ>0 вҮ’ вҲҡ(ПғМӮВІ) вҶ’вӮҡ ПғвӮҖ` | **Studentization-ready** standard-error consistency; feeds `div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian`. **Proved.** |

### 12n'''. `Stat/CLT/MultivariateCLT.lean`

Multivariate CLT via the **CramГ©rвҖ“Wold device**, the vector analogue of `clt_normalized_sum`. Discharges the multivariate CLT contact `_hCLT` taken as a hypothesis by `IsAsymLinearVec.tendsto_normal_vec` and `deltaMethod` (vector О”-method). Weak convergence on a finite-dim real inner-product space `E` is reduced to pointwise charFun convergence (LГ©vy continuity, `ProbabilityMeasure.tendsto_iff_tendsto_charFun` from the `Clt` package); each direction's charFun is the scalar charFun of the projected sum `вҹӘt, vecNSвҹ«`, handled by the scalar CLT.

**Limit-law status:** the CramГ©rвҖ“Wold reduction and per-direction charFun limit `вҶ’ exp(вҲ’ВҪвҲ«вҹӘt,ПҲвҹ«ВІ dP)` are fully proven, sorry-free. The theorems here keep the target `Q` abstract, parameterized by `charFun Q t = exp(вҲ’ВҪвҲ«вҹӘt,ПҲвҹ«ВІ dP)`. That abstract hypothesis is **discharged concretely** in `Stat/CLT/GaussianLimit.lean` (see 12n'''c): `gaussianLimit ПҲ` is the covariance-`ОЈ` Gaussian built explicitly, and `IIDSample.clt_normalizedSum_vec` is the multivariate CLT against it with no abstract `Q`/charFun hypothesis remaining.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `inner_normalizedSum` | `вҹӘt, vecNS n Пүвҹ« = scalarNS_{вҹӘt,ПҲвҹ«} n Пү` | Projection identity. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.normalizedSum_vec_charFun_tendsto` | `charFun(Ој.map vecNS)(t) вҶ’ exp(вҲ’ВҪвҲ«вҹӘt,ПҲвҹ«ВІ dP)` | **Per-direction charFun limit** from the scalar CLT via `integral_map`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist_vec.of_charFun_tendsto` | pointwise charFun convergence вҮ’ `Tendsto_dist_vec` | **CramГ©rвҖ“Wold wrapper**. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.clt_normalizedSum_vec_of_charFun` | Gaussian-charFun `Q` вҮ’ `vecNS вҮ’ Q` | **Multivariate CLT contact**; discharges `_hCLT`. **Proved.** |
| `IsAsymLinearVec.tendsto_normal_vec_clt` | vector asym-linearity + Gaussian-charFun `Q` вҮ’ rescaled estimator вҮ’ Q | **End-to-end vector asymptotic normality** (no CLT hypothesis). **Proved.** |

### 12n'''b. `Stat/CLT/GaussianCharFunBridge.lean`

Verified glue converting a *concrete* centered Gaussian into the abstract `hQ` characteristic-function target taken as a hypothesis by `MultivariateCLT`/`WaldVec`/`RatioDeltaMethod`. The multivariate-Gaussian charFun formula is `ProbabilityTheory.IsGaussian.charFun_eq'` (`Mathlib/Probability/Distributions/Gaussian/CharFun.lean`), proven at the current pin. The concrete `Q` is now constructed in `Stat/CLT/GaussianLimit.lean` (12n'''c), completing de-abstraction.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `charFun_isGaussian_centered` | `IsGaussian Q` вҲ§ `вҲ« x вҲӮQ = 0` вҮ’ `charFun Q t = exp(вҲ’ВҪВ·covarianceBilin Q t t)` | centered-Gaussian charFun; specialises Mathlib `IsGaussian.charFun_eq'`. **Proved** (axioms: propext/choice/Quot). |
| `charFun_isGaussian_of_cov_eq` | + `вҲҖ t, covarianceBilin Q t t = вҲ«вҹӘt,ПҲвҹ«ВІdP` вҮ’ `charFun Q t = exp(вҲ’ВҪвҲ«вҹӘt,ПҲвҹ«ВІdP)` | matches the exact `hQ` shape of the multivariate CLT contact, ready to discharge it once `Q` is built. **Proved.** |

### 12n'''c. `Stat/CLT/SecondMomentOperator.lean` + `Stat/CLT/GaussianLimit.lean`

**De-abstraction of the multivariate CLT.** Together with `Mathlib/OperatorSqrt.lean` (operator вҲҡ, 10j) and `Mathlib/StandardGaussian.lean` (10i), these build the concrete covariance-`ОЈ` Gaussian limit and discharge the abstract `hQ` of `MultivariateCLT`. `SecondMomentOperator` is the operator `ОЈ t = вҲ« вҹӘt,ПҲ xвҹ« вҖў ПҲ x вҲӮP`; `GaussianLimit` sets `gaussianLimit ПҲ := stdGaussian.map вҲҡОЈ` and proves its charFun is the CLT target.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `secondMomentLM` | `E вҶ’вӮ—[в„қ] E` | Second-moment operator `ОЈ t = вҲ«вҹӘt,ПҲвҹ«вҖўПҲ вҲӮP` (Bochner integral); linearity via `integral_add`/`integral_smul`. **Proved.** |
| `secondMomentLM_inner` | `вҹӘОЈ t, sвҹ« = вҲ«вҹӘt,ПҲвҹ«вҹӘs,ПҲвҹ« вҲӮP` | Quadratic-form identity, via `integral_inner`. **Proved.** |
| `secondMomentLM_isPositive` | `(secondMomentLM ПҲ).IsPositive` | `ОЈ` is positive (symmetric + `вҹӘОЈt,tвҹ« = вҲ«вҹӘt,ПҲвҹ«ВІ вүҘ 0`). **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit` | `Measure E` | The CLT limit: `(stdGaussian E).map вҲҡОЈ`, centered Gaussian with covariance `ОЈ`. `IsGaussian`/`IsProbabilityMeasure` instances. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit_mean` | `вҲ« x, x вҲӮ(gaussianLimit ПҲ) = 0` | Centered (mean pushes through the continuous-linear вҲҡОЈ; `stdGaussian_mean`). **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit_covarianceBilin` | `covarianceBilin (gaussianLimit ПҲ) t t = вҲ«вҹӘt,ПҲвҹ«вҹӘt,ПҲвҹ« вҲӮP` | Covariance recovers the asymptotic-variance integral, via `covarianceBilin_map`, `posSqrtCLM_adjoint` (self-adjoint), `posSqrtCLM_comp_self` (вҲҡОЈвҲҳвҲҡОЈ=ОЈ), `covarianceBilin_stdGaussian`. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit_charFun` | `charFun (gaussianLimit ПҲ) t = exp(вҲ’ВҪвҲ«вҹӘt,ПҲвҹ«ВІ dP)` | Exactly the abstract `hQ`; via `charFun_isGaussian_of_cov_eq`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.clt_normalizedSum_vec` | centered sq-int `ПҲ` вҮ’ `vecNS вҮ’ gaussianLimit ПҲ` | **Multivariate CLT against the concrete Gaussian limit** вҖ” no abstract `Q`/charFun hypothesis. **Proved** (axiom-clean). |

### 12n''''. `Stat/Inference/WaldVec.lean`

Multivariate Wald / confidence-ellipsoid asymptotic coverage вҖ” the vector analogue of the scalar `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage`. The Wald statistic `WвӮҷ = (вҲҡn(ОёМӮвӮҷвҲ’ОёвӮҖ))бөҖ ОЈМӮвӮҷвҒ»В№ (вҲҡn(ОёМӮвӮҷвҲ’ОёвӮҖ))` is already в„қ-valued, so an ellipsoid `{Оё : Wald вүӨ c}` has coverage `ПҮ(Iic c)` via one-sided portmanteau. The construction of `WвӮҷ` and the proof `WвӮҷ вҮ’ ПҮ` (continuous mapping of the joint limit through `(t,S) вҶҰ tбөҖSвҒ»В№t`) is the caller's input, exactly as the scalar `wald_coverage` takes `SвӮҷ вҮ’ N(0,1)`. The limit `ПҮ` is kept abstract here, but identifying the image of the concrete Gaussian `gaussianLimit` (12n'''c) under the quadratic form `S вҶҰ SбөҖОЈвҒ»В№S` as `ПҮВІ_d` is now **closed** in `Stat/Inference/ChiSquaredWald.lean` (12n'''d).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage_Iic` | `WвӮҷ вҮ’ ПҮ` вҲ§ `ПҮ(вҲӮIic c)=0` вҲ§ bridge вҮ’ `coverProb вҶ’ ПҮ(Iic c)` | **Ellipsoid coverage** via one-sided portmanteau on `Set.Iic c`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage_Iic_of_noAtoms` | `WвӮҷ вҮ’ ПҮ` (`[NoAtoms ПҮ]`) вҲ§ bridge вҮ’ `coverProb вҶ’ ПҮ(Iic c)` | Same, boundary-null discharged automatically for any atomless limit (e.g. non-degenerate ПҮВІ). **Proved.** |

### 12n'''d. `Stat/CLT/ChiSquared.lean` + `Stat/Inference/ChiSquaredWald.lean`

**ПҮВІ identification of the Wald limit.** `ChiSquared` *defines* the ПҮВІ_d law and `ChiSquaredWald` whitens `gaussianLimit` through the Wald quadratic form to recognise its image as ПҮВІ_d, closing the open step flagged in `WaldVec`. ПҮВІ_d is defined as the law of `вҖ–WвҖ–ВІ` for `W ~ stdGaussian` (textbook squared-norm definition; the `Gamma(d/2,1/2)` density form is not developed вҖ” Mathlib has no ПҮВІ). The whitening: `gaussianLimit = stdGaussian.map вҲҡОЈ`, and with `ОЈ` non-degenerate (injective вҮ’ invertible in finite dim), `вҹӘвҲҡОЈ w, ОЈвҒ»В№(вҲҡОЈ w)вҹ« = вҖ–wвҖ–ВІ` by self-adjointness of `вҲҡОЈ`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `chiSqDist` | `в„• вҶ’ Measure в„қ` | The ПҮВІ_d law: `(stdGaussian (EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin d))).map вҖ–В·вҖ–ВІ`. `IsProbabilityMeasure` instance. **Proved.** |
| `stdGaussian_map_linearIsometryEquiv` | `(stdGaussian E).map О№ = stdGaussian F` for `О№ : E вүғвӮ—бөў F` | `stdGaussian` is isometry-invariant; matched characteristic functions (`charFun_isGaussian_centered` + `Measure.ext_of_charFun`, covariance preserved by `О№`). **Proved.** |
| `stdGaussian_map_normSq` | `(stdGaussian E).map вҖ–В·вҖ–ВІ = chiSqDist (finrank в„қ E)` | The squared-norm law depends only on dimension. **Proved.** |
| `noAtoms_pi_normSq` / `noAtoms_chiSqDist` | `1 вүӨ d вҮ’ NoAtoms (chiSqDist d)` | ПҮВІ_d is atomless for `d вүҘ 1` (squared first coordinate atomless, Fubini over the independent remainder; reduced to a product of 1-D Gaussians via `stdGaussian_map_normSq_eq_pi`). **Proved.** |
| `secondMomentInv` | `E вҶ’L[в„қ] E` | `ОЈвҒ»В№ = (вҲҡОЈ)вҒ»В№вҲҳ(вҲҡОЈ)вҒ»В№` from injectivity of `ОЈ`; `secondMomentInv_secondMomentLM` / `secondMomentLM_secondMomentInv` certify it is the two-sided inverse. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianLimit_waldForm_map` | `(gaussianLimit ПҲ).map (S вҶҰ вҹӘS, ОЈвҒ»В№ Sвҹ«) = chiSqDist (finrank в„қ E)` | **The ПҮВІ identification** (whitening through `вҲҡОЈ`). **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage_chiSq` | `WвӮҷ вҮ’ ПҮВІ_d` (`1вүӨd`) вҲ§ bridge вҮ’ `coverProb вҶ’ ПҮВІ_d(Iic c)` | WaldVec ellipsoid coverage specialised to the identified atomless limit `chiSqDist d`. **Proved.** |

### 12n'''e. `Stat/CLT/ChiSquaredProjection.lean` вҖ” squared-norm of a projected Gaussian is ПҮВІ

The law of `вҖ–P zвҖ–ВІ` for an **orthogonal projection** `P` (self-adjoint idempotent) and `z ~ stdGaussian` is `ПҮВІ_{rank P}`. Reusable beyond GMM (F-tests, score/LM tests, rank-deficient Wald).
