Proof: corestrict `P` to its range `S`, transport by the orthonormal-basis isometry `S вүғвӮ—бөў EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin r)`; the pushforward of `stdGaussian E` is `stdGaussian S` (centered, covariance = inner product, via `Measure.ext_of_charFun` вҖ” the crux is that the corestriction's adjoint is the inclusion because `P` fixes its range), then `stdGaussian_map_normSq`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `stdGaussian_map_normSq_orthogonalProjection` | `IsSelfAdjoint P` вҲ§ `P вҲҳL P = P` вҮ’ `(stdGaussian E).map (вҖ–PВ·вҖ–ВІ) = chiSqDist (finrank в„қ (range P))` | **Projected-Gaussian ПҮВІ.** The squared norm of an orthogonally-projected standard Gaussian is `ПҮВІ` with rank-of-projection degrees of freedom. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 12o. `Stat/Inference/Studentize.lean`

Generic studentized-CLT and Wald-coverage engine, abstracted off the concrete TRAE-DR estimator (the NPIV/DR theorems now instantiate these). Portmanteau / Gaussian-boundary helpers are made public here.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Tendsto_dist.tendsto_measure_of_null_frontier` | `Xn вҮ’ Q` вҲ§ `Q(вҲӮE)=0` вҮ’ `(Ој.map Xn)(E) вҶ’ Q(E)` | Continuity-set portmanteau for the project's `Tendsto_dist` wrapper. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianMeasure_zero_one_singleton` | `N(0,1)({x}) = 0` | Standard normal has no atoms. **Proved.** |
| `gaussianMeasure_zero_one_frontier_Icc` | `0 < z` вҮ’ `N(0,1)(вҲӮ[вҲ’z,z]) = 0` | Zero boundary mass for a symmetric interval. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian` | `Xn вҮ’ N(0,ПғвӮҖВІ)` вҲ§ `ПғМӮ вҶ’_p ПғвӮҖ` вҲ§ `ПғвӮҖ>0` вҮ’ `Xn/ПғМӮ вҮ’ N(0,1)` | **Studentized CLT** (random-denominator Slutsky). Scales `Xn` by `1/ПғвӮҖ` (`const_mul_tendsto_gaussian`); the remainder `XnВ·(1/ПғМӮ вҲ’ 1/ПғвӮҖ)` is `O_p(1)В·o_p(1)` via `tightness` + `comp_continuousAt`; absorbs with `add_isLittleOp_one`. **Proved.** |
| `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage` | `Sn вҮ’ N(0,1)` вҲ§ `coverProb вҲ’ Ој{SnвҲҲ[вҲ’z,z]} вҶ’ 0` вҮ’ `coverProb вҶ’ N(0,1)([вҲ’z,z])` | **Wald coverage** (generic): portmanteau on the closed interval plus the caller-supplied event-equivalence bridge. Specialize at `z = z_{1вҲ’Оұ/2}` for `1 вҲ’ Оұ` coverage. **Proved.** |

### 12o'. `Stat/Bootstrap/` вҖ” Nonparametric bootstrap standard error

The econometric workhorse use of the bootstrap (`vce(bootstrap)`): estimate the standard error of an asymptotically-linear estimator by the bootstrap, then form a studentized / Wald interval. The key fact is that the nonparametric (multinomial / Efron) bootstrap variance of `вҲҡn(XМ„* вҲ’ XМ„)` is **exactly** the empirical variance `(1/n)ОЈ(ПҲ(Zбөў) вҲ’ ПҲМ„вӮҷ)ВІ` вҖ” not an asymptotic approximation вҖ” so a *consistent* bootstrap SE plugs straight into the generic studentized CLT / Wald coverage of `Stat/Inference/Studentize.lean`. The nonparametric percentile / percentile-t refinements (which need conditional weak convergence) remain out of scope, but the **Gaussian multiplier (wild) bootstrap** below now supplies an *exact* distributional law on the conditioned sample. Three files: `Variance.lean` (the exact variance identity + consistency), `CI.lean` (studentized CLT + Wald coverage), and `GaussianMultiplier.lean` (exact multiplier-bootstrap law).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.bootstrapVar` | `noncomputable def`, `в„• вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Bootstrap variance: the conditional variance `E*[ПҲВІ] вҲ’ (E*ПҲ)ВІ = sampleMean(ПҲВІ) вҲ’ (sampleMean ПҲ)ВІ` of a single multinomial-bootstrap draw `ПҲ(Z*вӮҒ)` over the empirical distribution. |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapVar_eq_centered` | `bootstrapVar = (1/n)ОЈ(ПҲ(Zбөў) вҲ’ ПҲМ„вӮҷ)ВІ` | **Exact variance identity** (`E*[(ПҲвҲ’E*ПҲ)ВІ]`), the centered empirical second moment; holds for every `n` (`field_simp`/`ring` after the centered-sum expansion). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapVar_nonneg` | `0 вүӨ bootstrapVar S ПҲ n Пү` | Nonnegativity вҖ” it is a sum of squares (`bootstrapVar_eq_centered`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapVar_tendsto_inProb` | meas/int/sq-int/mean-zero `ПҲ` вҮ’ `bootstrapVar вҶ’вӮҡ вҲ« ПҲВІ dP` | **Consistency of the bootstrap variance**: `sampleMean(ПҲВІ) вҶ’вӮҡ вҲ«ПҲВІ` and `(sampleMean ПҲ)ВІ вҶ’вӮҡ (вҲ«ПҲ)ВІ=0`, subtracted via `Tendsto_inProb.sub`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapSE` | `noncomputable def`, `вҲҡ(bootstrapVar)` | Bootstrap standard error of `вҲҡn ОёМӮ` вҖ” the bootstrap estimate of the asymptotic SD `вҲҡ(вҲ«ПҲВІ)`. |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapSE_tendsto_inProb` | same hyps вҮ’ `bootstrapSE вҶ’вӮҡ вҲҡ(вҲ« ПҲВІ dP)` | **SE consistency**; `Tendsto_inProb.sqrt` of the variance consistency. Supplies the `ПғМӮ вҶ’вӮҡ ПғвӮҖ` input of `div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.bootstrapStudentized` | `noncomputable def`, `вҲҡn(ОёМӮвҲ’ОёвӮҖ)/ПғМӮвӮҷ` | The bootstrap studentized statistic (full-sample index family). |
| `bootstrap_studentized_tendsto` | `IsAsymLinear Оёn ОёвӮҖ ПҲ S range` вҲ§ `0<вҲ«ПҲВІ` вҮ’ `вҲҡn(ОёМӮвҲ’ОёвӮҖ)/ПғМӮвӮҷ вҮ’ N(0,1)` | **Bootstrap studentized CLT**: `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal` (numerator `вҮ’ N(0,вҲ«ПҲВІ)`) + bootstrap-SE consistency through `Tendsto_dist.div_tendsto_inProb_gaussian`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `bootstrap_wald_coverage` | + `z>0` вҲ§ coverage-event bridge вҮ’ `coverProb вҶ’ N(0,1)([вҲ’z,z])` | **Bootstrap Wald asymptotic coverage**; instantiates `Tendsto_dist.wald_coverage` on the bootstrap studentized statistic. `z = z_{1вҲ’Оұ/2}` gives the `1вҲ’Оұ` interval `ОёМӮвӮҷ Вұ z_{1вҲ’Оұ/2}В·ПғМӮвӮҷ/вҲҡn`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12o''. `Stat/Bootstrap/GaussianMultiplier.lean` вҖ” exact Gaussian multiplier (wild) bootstrap law

The multiplier/wild bootstrap (van der Vaart В§23; Mammen) attaches i.i.d. mean-zero multipliers `Оҫбөў` to the recentred data `aбөў = xбөў вҲ’ xМ„вӮҷ`. With **standard Gaussian** multipliers the perturbed statistic is, on a *fixed* data vector, **exactly** a centred Gaussian вҖ” no CLT and no conditional-distribution machinery, because a linear combination of independent Gaussians is Gaussian with variances adding. Both declarations **proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `map_weighted_sum_gaussian` | `iIndepFun Оҫ`, each `Ој.map (Оҫ i) = gaussianReal 0 1` вҮ’ `Ој.map (Пү вҶҰ вҲ‘ i, a i В· Оҫ i Пү) = gaussianReal 0 вҹЁвҲ‘ i, (a i)ВІ, _вҹ©` | **Core fact**: a weighted sum of i.i.d. standard Gaussians has law `N(0, вҲ‘ aбөўВІ)`. `Finset` induction peeling one summand: each `aбөў Оҫбөў ~ N(0, aбөўВІ)` (`gaussianReal_map_const_mul`), the partial sum is independent of the new term (`iIndepFun.indepFun_finset_sum_of_notMem`), and their laws convolve (`gaussianReal_add_gaussianReal_of_indepFun`). |
| `multiplierBootstrap_law` | as above with `aбөў = n^{-1/2}(xбөў вҲ’ xМ„)` вҮ’ law of `TвӮҷ* = n^{-1/2} вҲ‘ i, (xбөў вҲ’ xМ„) Оҫбөў` is `gaussianReal 0 вҹЁsвӮҷВІ, _вҹ©` | **Exact law of the studentized multiplier-bootstrap mean**: the bootstrap statistic is exactly `N(0, sвӮҷВІ)` with `sвӮҷВІ = (1/n) вҲ‘ (xбөў вҲ’ xМ„)ВІ` the fixed sample variance. Since `sвӮҷВІ вҶ’вӮҡ ПғВІ`, this is the distributional bootstrap counterpart of the CLT limit `N(0, ПғВІ)`. |

---

#### 12p. `Stat/Quantile/Quantile.lean` вҖ” Quantile function (generalized inverse of a cdf)

Causal-agnostic statistical primitive: the lower quantile function of a real
probability measure, `quantile Ој П„ := sInf {x | П„ вүӨ cdf Ој x}`, the
left-continuous generalized inverse of Mathlib's `ProbabilityTheory.cdf`.
Mathlib has the cdf but no generalized inverse; this file supplies it plus the
order (Galois) characterisation. Project-agnostic; upstream-candidate.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `quantileSet Ој П„` | `def`, `Set в„қ` | The super-level set `{x | П„ вүӨ cdf Ој x}` defining the quantile. |
| `quantile Ој П„` | `noncomputable def`, `в„қ` | `sInf (quantileSet Ој П„)` вҖ” the `П„`-quantile (left-continuous generalized inverse of the cdf). |
| `quantileSet_up_closed` | `lemma` | `x вҲҲ quantileSet Ој П„ вҶ’ x вүӨ x' вҶ’ x' вҲҲ quantileSet Ој П„` (cdf monotone). **Proved.** |
| `bddBelow_quantileSet` | `lemma` | For `0 < П„`, the super-level set is bounded below (cdf вҶ’ 0 at вҲ’вҲһ). **Proved.** |
| `nonempty_quantileSet` | `lemma` | For `П„ < 1`, the super-level set is nonempty (cdf вҶ’ 1 at +вҲһ). **Proved.** |
| `le_cdf_quantile` | `lemma` | For `П„ < 1`, `П„ вүӨ cdf Ој (quantile Ој П„)`. Uses right-continuity of the cdf. **Proved.** |
| `le_cdf_of_quantile_le` | `lemma` | For `П„ < 1`, `quantile Ој П„ вүӨ x вҶ’ П„ вүӨ cdf Ој x`. **Proved.** |
| `quantile_le_of_le_cdf` | `lemma` | For `0 < П„`, `П„ вүӨ cdf Ој x вҶ’ quantile Ој П„ вүӨ x` (`csInf_le`). **Proved.** |
| `quantile_le_iff` | `theorem` | **Galois connection** on `(0,1)`: `quantile Ој П„ вүӨ x вҶ” П„ вүӨ cdf Ој x`. **Proved.** |
| `quantile_mono` | `lemma` | Quantile monotone in `П„` on `(0,1)`. **Proved.** |

#### 12q. `Stat/Quantile/EmpiricalCDF.lean` вҖ” Empirical cumulative distribution function

Sample analogue of `cdf P`, `FМӮвӮҷ(y) = (1/n) ОЈ 1{Z_i вүӨ y}` (the `sampleMean` of the indicator `cdfStat y`), with its fixed-`y` influence function `cdfIF P y z = 1{z вүӨ y} вҲ’ F(y)`. Because `вҲҡn (FМӮвӮҷ(y) вҲ’ F(y))` *is* the normalized influence-function sum, the empirical cdf is asymptotically linear with an identically-zero remainder, giving the fixed-`y` CLT directly. Causal-agnostic base layer for `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantile.lean`; upstream-candidate.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cdfStat y` | `noncomputable def`, `в„қ вҶ’ в„қ` | The lower-ray indicator `1{В· вүӨ y}` (= `Set.indicator (Iic y) 1`). |
| `measurable_cdfStat`, `cdfStat_nonneg`, `cdfStat_le_one`, `cdfStat_sq` | `lemma`s | Measurability; `0 вүӨ 1{В·вүӨy} вүӨ 1`; idempotence `(1{В·вүӨy})ВІ = 1{В·вүӨy}`. **Proved.** |
| `integrable_cdfStat` | `lemma` | `cdfStat y` is integrable (bounded on a finite measure). **Proved.** |
| `integral_cdfStat` | `lemma` | `вҲ« 1{В·вүӨy} dP = cdf P y` (= `P.real (Iic y)`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empiricalCDF S y` | `noncomputable def`, `в„• вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ` | The empirical cdf `FМӮвӮҷ(y)`, the `sampleMean` of `cdfStat y`. |
| `cdfIF P y` | `noncomputable def`, `в„қ вҶ’ в„қ` | Fixed-`y` influence function `1{z вүӨ y} вҲ’ F(y)`. |
| `measurable_cdfIF`, `cdfIF_mean_zero`, `abs_cdfIF_le_one`, `cdfIF_sq_integrable` | `lemma`s | Measurability; `вҲ« cdfIF dP = 0`; `|cdfIF| вүӨ 1`; square-integrability. **Proved.** |
| `cdfIF_variance` | `lemma` | `вҲ« cdfIFВІ dP = F(y)(1 вҲ’ F(y))`, the Bernoulli variance of `{Z вүӨ y}`. **Proved.** |
| `isLittleOp_zero` | `lemma` | The zero sequence is `o_p(1)`. **Proved.** |
| `rescaledEmpiricalCDF_eq_normalizedSum` | `lemma` | **Key identity** `вҲҡn (FМӮвӮҷ(y) вҲ’ F(y)) = (1/вҲҡn) ОЈ cdfIF(Z_i)`. **Proved.** |
| `empiricalCDF_isAsymLinear` | `lemma` | `FМӮвӮҷ(y)` is `IsAsymLinear` at `F(y)` with IF `cdfIF P y` (remainder вүЎ 0). **Proved.** |
| `empiricalCDF_tendsto_normal` | `theorem` | **Empirical-cdf CLT** `вҲҡn (FМӮвӮҷ(y) вҲ’ F(y)) вҮ’ N(0, F(y)(1вҲ’F(y)))`. **Proved** (instance of `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal`). |
| `empiricalCDF_tendsto_inProb` | `theorem` | **Consistency** (WLLN) `FМӮвӮҷ(y) вҶ’вӮҡ F(y)`. **Proved.** |

#### 12r. `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantile.lean` вҖ” Sample-quantile asymptotics (Bahadur representation)

`вҲҡn`-asymptotic normality of a quantile-estimator sequence `qМӮвӮҷ` for the `П„`-quantile of `P`, via the Bahadur representation with influence function `ПҲ_П„(z) = (П„ вҲ’ 1{z вүӨ qвӮҖ})/fвӮҖ` and asymptotic variance `П„(1вҲ’П„)/fвӮҖВІ`. The hard empirical-process (Donsker / asymptotic-equicontinuity) content вҖ” the `o_p(n^{-1/2})` Bahadur remainder вҖ” is **exposed as a hypothesis** in the `QuantileRegularity` bundle, mirroring the `StochEquicontAt` pattern of `Stat/MEstimation/EmpiricalExpansion.lean`; everything downstream is proved. Reusable by any quantile estimator (plain sample quantile, IPW-weighted QTE quantile, вҖҰ) satisfying the expansion. References: Bahadur (1966); van der Vaart (1998) В§21.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `quantileIF П„ qвӮҖ fвӮҖ` | `noncomputable def`, `в„қ вҶ’ в„қ` | Sample-quantile influence function `(П„ вҲ’ 1{z вүӨ qвӮҖ})/fвӮҖ`. |
| `measurable_quantileIF` | `lemma` | Measurability of `quantileIF`. **Proved.** |
| `quantileIF_mean_zero` | `lemma` | `вҲ« ПҲ_П„ dP = 0`, given `cdf P qвӮҖ = П„`. **Proved.** |
| `quantileIF_sq_integrable` | `lemma` | `ПҲ_П„` is square-integrable (bounded by `(|П„|+1)/|fвӮҖ|`). **Proved.** |
| `quantileIF_variance` | `lemma` | `вҲ« ПҲ_П„ВІ dP = П„(1вҲ’П„)/fвӮҖВІ`, the classical sample-quantile asymptotic variance. **Proved.** |
| `QuantileRegularity S qn П„ qвӮҖ fвӮҖ` | `structure` (`Prop`) | Regularity bundle: `П„ вҲҲ (0,1)`, density `fвӮҖ > 0`, `cdf P qвӮҖ = П„`, `HasDerivAt (cdf P) fвӮҖ qвӮҖ`, and `bahadur` вҖ” the exposed Donsker remainder (= `IsAsymLinear.remainder` for `(qМӮвӮҷ, qвӮҖ, ПҲ_П„)`). |
| `QuantileRegularity.isAsymLinear` | `lemma` | The bundle is an `IsAsymLinear` witness for `qМӮвӮҷ` at `qвӮҖ` with IF `ПҲ_П„`. **Proved.** |
| `QuantileRegularity.tendsto_normal` | `theorem` | **Sample-quantile CLT** `вҲҡn (qМӮвӮҷ вҲ’ qвӮҖ) вҮ’ N(0, П„(1вҲ’П„)/fвӮҖВІ)`. **Proved** (specializes `IsAsymLinear.tendsto_normal` with the closed-form variance). |

#### 12s. `Stat/Quantile/EmpiricalQuantile.lean` вҖ” Sample quantile (empirical-cdf inverse)

The `П„`-sample quantile `qМӮвӮҷ(П„) = quantile ОҪвӮҷ П„`, the generalized inverse of the empirical cdf, where `ОҪвӮҷ = (1/n) ОЈ_{i<n} Оҙ_{Zбөў}` is the empirical measure. Supplies the deterministic/structural facts (switching relation, monotonicity, atom bound) feeding the Bahadur derivation. **Fully proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.empiricalMeasure S n Пү` | `noncomputable def` | Empirical measure `(1/n) ОЈ_{i<n} Оҙ_{Zбөў}`. |
| `IIDSample.empiricalMeasure_isProbabilityMeasure` | `lemma` | For `0 < n`, `ОҪвӮҷ` is a probability measure. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empiricalMeasure_real_Iic` | `lemma` | `ОҪвӮҷ.real (Iic y) = FМӮвӮҷ(y)`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empiricalMeasure_cdf` | `lemma` | **cdf bridge** `cdf ОҪвӮҷ = FМӮвӮҷ`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile S П„` | `noncomputable def` | Sample quantile `qМӮвӮҷ(П„) = quantile ОҪвӮҷ П„`. |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_le_iff` | `lemma` | **Switching relation** `qМӮвӮҷ(П„) вүӨ x вҶ” П„ вүӨ FМӮвӮҷ(x)` (`0<П„<1`, `0<n`). **Proved** (from `quantile_le_iff` + cdf bridge). |
| `IIDSample.empiricalCDF_monotone` | `lemma` | `y вҶҰ FМӮвӮҷ(y)` is monotone. **Proved**. |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_atom_bound` | `lemma` | **Atom bound** `\|FМӮвӮҷ(qМӮвӮҷ) вҲ’ П„\| вүӨ 1/n` a.s., under atomless `P` (`Continuous (cdf P)`); needs `[IsProbabilityMeasure Ој]`. **Proved** (no-atoms from continuous cdf via `measure_singleton`/`leftLim`; a.s. tie-free from `IndepFun`+`NoAtoms`; discrete count `card(Cle q)=card(Clt)+card(Ceq)`, ties `вүӨ1`, strict part `вүӨ nП„` via threshold below `q`). |

#### 12t. `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantileBahadur.lean` вҖ” Bahadur representation (DERIVED)

Derives the Bahadur remainder of the **sample** quantile from elementary tools (Chebyshev + monotone-grid oscillation) вҖ” no Donsker/empirical-process hypothesis. Turns `SampleQuantile.QuantileRegularity.bahadur` from a hypothesis into a theorem for `qМӮвӮҷ`. **Fully proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.** This file is now a thin re-export umbrella; the derivation is split by layer under `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantileBahadur/`: `Oscillation.lean` (L2 Chebyshev increment + L3 monotone-grid oscillation, incl. `SampleQuantileReg`/`empProcess`), `Rate.lean` (L4 root-`n` rate), `Linearity.lean` (L5 inversion + L6 assembly). Existing imports of `Causalean.Stat.Quantile.SampleQuantileBahadur` are unaffected. The declarations below are listed by their owning layer.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SampleQuantileReg P П„ qвӮҖ fвӮҖ` | `structure` (`Prop`) | Regularity: `П„вҲҲ(0,1)`, `fвӮҖ>0`, `cdf P qвӮҖ=П„`, `HasDerivAt (cdf P) fвӮҖ qвӮҖ`, **and** `cont` (atomless `P`). |
| `IIDSample.empProcess S n Пү y` | `noncomputable def` | Centered scaled empirical process `GвӮҷ(y) = вҲҡn (FМӮвӮҷ(y) вҲ’ F(y))`. |
| `IIDSample.empProcess_increment_tendsto_zero` | `lemma` (L2) | `GвӮҷ(qвӮҖ+u/вҲҡn) вҲ’ GвӮҷ(qвӮҖ) вҶ’вӮҡ 0` (Chebyshev second-moment via `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empProcess_cell_sandwich`, `empProcess_node_max_tendsto_zero`, `exists_grid_bracket` | `lemma`s | L3 helpers: per-cell monotone sandwich; finite-grid node-max `вҶ’вӮҡ 0`; uniform-mesh bracketing. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.empProcess_oscillation` | `lemma` (L3, **crux**) | At random `UвӮҷ=O_p(1)`: `GвӮҷ(qвӮҖ+UвӮҷ/вҲҡn) вҲ’ GвӮҷ(qвӮҖ) вҶ’вӮҡ 0` (monotone-grid sandwich). **Proved.** |
| `cdf_increment_sqrt_tendsto` (in `.Oscillation`), `Tendsto_dist.limsup_measure_closed_le`, `gaussian_tail_small`, `IIDSample.empProcess_q0_tendsto_normal`/`_bigO` | `lemma`s | L4 helpers: Taylor limit `вҲҡn(F(qвӮҖ+a/вҲҡn)вҲ’F(qвӮҖ))вҶ’fвӮҖa` (single canonical lemma defined in `Oscillation.lean`, reused by `Rate.lean` вҖ” the former duplicate `_pre`/`Rate` copies are consolidated); closed-set portmanteau; Gaussian-tail smallness; `GвӮҷ(qвӮҖ) вҮ’ N(0,П„(1вҲ’П„))` + its `O_p(1)`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_rate` | `lemma` (L4) | `вҲҡn(qМӮвӮҷвҲ’qвӮҖ) = O_p(1)` (two-sided tail via switching + portmanteau). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_atom_term_tendsto_zero`, `sampleQuantile_taylor_remainder_tendsto_zero`, `Tendsto_inProb.add_zero_zero`/`of_isLittleOp_one`/`const_mul_zero` | `lemma`s | L5/L6 helpers: `вҲҡn(FМӮвӮҷ(qМӮвӮҷ)вҲ’П„)вҶ’вӮҡ0`; `вҲҡnВ·R(qМӮвӮҷ)вҶ’вӮҡ0`; `Tendsto_inProb` `+`/bridge/scalar-mul algebra. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_inversion` | `lemma` (L5) | `GвӮҷ(qМӮвӮҷ) = вҲ’fвӮҖВ·вҲҡn(qМӮвӮҷвҲ’qвӮҖ) + o_p(1)` (switching + atom bound + Taylor). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.normalizedSum_quantileIF_eq` | `lemma` | `(1/вҲҡn) ОЈ ПҲ_П„(Zбөў) = вҲ’GвӮҷ(qвӮҖ)/fвӮҖ`. **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_isAsymLinear` | `theorem` (L6) | **Derived** `IsAsymLinear` for `qМӮвӮҷ` (Bahadur remainder proved `o_p(1)`: `fвӮҖВ·RМғвӮҷ = L5 вҲ’ L3`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantile_quantileRegularity` | `theorem` | `qМӮвӮҷ` satisfies `QuantileRegularity` with `bahadur` derived вҮ’ scalar CLT `вҲҡn(qМӮвӮҷвҲ’qвӮҖ)вҮ’N(0,П„(1вҲ’П„)/fвӮҖВІ)` for free. **Proved.** |

#### 12u. `Stat/Quantile/SampleQuantileJoint.lean` вҖ” Joint normality of a quantile vector

Joint asymptotic normality of `(qМӮвӮҷ(П„вӮҒ),вҖҰ,qМӮвӮҷ(П„_k))` via the per-coordinate derived Bahadur + the project multivariate CLT (CramГ©rвҖ“Wold). Target `Q` kept abstract via Gaussian charFun, as in the rest of the multivariate-CLT stack. **Fully proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `eucl v` | `noncomputable abbrev` | Pack `Fin k вҶ’ в„қ` into `EuclideanSpace в„қ (Fin k)` (the `PiLp 2` equiv). |
| `eucl_apply`, `norm_sq_eucl`, `norm_eucl_le_sum_abs` | `lemma`s | `(eucl v) j = v j`; `вҖ–eucl vвҖ–ВІ = вҲ‘ vвұјВІ`; `вҖ–eucl vвҖ– вүӨ вҲ‘ \|vвұј\|`. **Proved.** |
| `quantileIFVec П„ q f` | `noncomputable def` | Joint IF `ПҲ(z)_j = (П„вұјвҲ’1{zвүӨqвұј})/fвұј`. |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantileVec S П„` | `noncomputable def` | Sample-quantile vector `(qМӮвӮҷ(П„вұј))_j`. |
| `measurable_quantileIFVec` | `lemma` | Measurability of `ПҲ`. **Proved.** |
| `quantileIF_cross` | `lemma` | **Covariance entry** `вҲ« ПҲ_{П„вұј}ПҲ_{П„вӮ—} dP = (min(П„вұј,П„вӮ—)вҲ’П„вұјП„вӮ—)/(fвұјfвӮ—)` (`1{вүӨqвұј}1{вүӨqвӮ—}=1{вүӨmin}`, `F(min)=min(П„)` by monotonicity). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantileVec_isAsymLinearVec` | `theorem` | Vector asymptotic linearity (coordinatewise from scalar Bahadur; `eucl` commutes with `вҖў`/`вҲ’`/`вҲ‘`; norm `вүӨ вҲ‘\|RвӮҷвұј\|`, each `o_p(1)`). **Proved.** |
| `IIDSample.sampleQuantileVec_tendsto_normal` | `theorem` | **Headline** `вҲҡn(qМӮвӮҷ(П„вұј)вҲ’qвұј)_j вҮ’ N(0,ОЈ)`, `ОЈ_{jl}=(min(П„вұј,П„вӮ—)вҲ’П„вұјП„вӮ—)/(fвұјfвӮ—)` (`Q` abstract Gaussian-charFun via `tendsto_normal_vec_clt`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 12v. `Stat/GMM/` вҖ” Generalized Method of Moments (Hansen 1982)

Abstract GMM stack over a moment model `g : E вҶ’ X вҶ’ F` (parameter `Оё : E`,
moment vector valued in `F`, over-identified `dim E вүӨ dim F`).  Three layers:
the pure operator-algebra **optimal-weighting theorem** (`VarianceAlgebra`), the
statistical **problem bundle + efficiency** (`Setup`), and **asymptotic
normality** as a corollary of the Z-estimator CLT (`AsymptoticNormality`).  All
declarations are namespace `Causalean.Stat`.  Spec: Hansen (1982); Newey &
McFadden (1994), В§3.  All **sorry-free and axiom-clean**.

#### 12v.1. `Stat/GMM/VarianceAlgebra.lean` вҖ” sandwich/efficient variance algebra

Pure finite-dimensional operator algebra (no measure theory) on continuous
linear maps between real inner-product spaces.  Inverses are carried as *data
with two-sided witnesses* (mirroring `ZEstimatorRegularity.JвӮҖ_inv`), so no
operator inverse is constructed.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gmmBread G W` | `(adjoint G) вҲҳL W вҲҳL G : E вҶ’L E` | The **bread** `GбөҖWG` of Jacobian `G : E вҶ’L F` and weighting `W : F вҶ’L F`; symmetric when `W` is, invertible at full-rank `G`, `W вү» 0`. |
| `gmmSandwich G W Cov breadInv` | `breadInv вҲҳL GбөҖ вҲҳL W вҲҳL Cov вҲҳL W вҲҳL G вҲҳL breadInv` | The **sandwich asymptotic variance** `(GбөҖWG)вҒ»В№ GбөҖW ОЈ WG (GбөҖWG)вҒ»В№`, given a two-sided inverse `breadInv` of the bread. (`Cov` = covariance `ОЈ`, since `ОЈ` is a reserved Lean token.) |
| `adjoint_inv_self` | `adjoint M = M` вҲ§ `M вҲҳL N = id` вҮ’ `adjoint N = N` | Self-adjointness of a right inverse of a self-adjoint operator (left-inverse uniqueness). Single shared copy in `VarianceAlgebra.lean` (imported by `OverID.lean`; the former duplicate in `OverID.lean` is removed). |
| `adjoint_gmmBread` | `adjoint W = W` вҮ’ `adjoint (gmmBread G W) = gmmBread G W` | (private) The bread is self-adjoint when `W` is. |
| `gmm_efficiency` | `(gmmSandwich G W Cov breadInv вҲ’ effInv).IsPositive` | **GMM optimal-weighting theorem (Hansen 1982, Thm 3.2).** The sandwich variance dominates the efficient variance `effInv = (GбөҖCovвҒ»В№G)вҒ»В№` in the LГ¶wner order, with equality at `W = CovвҒ»В№`. Proof: `V(W) вҲ’ Vвҳ… = (AвҲ’B)ОЈ(AвҲ’B)бөҖ вӘ° 0` with `A = (GбөҖWG)вҒ»В№GбөҖW`, `B = (GбөҖОЈвҒ»В№G)вҒ»В№GбөҖОЈвҒ»В№`; the cross terms collapse to `Vвҳ…` because `ОЈ`/`ОЈвҒ»В№` cancel against the bread, and positivity is `IsPositive.conj_adjoint` on `ОЈ вӘ° 0`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12v.2. `Stat/GMM/Setup.lean` вҖ” GMM problem, influence function, efficiency

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gmmScore G W g` | `fun Оё x => adjoint G (W (g Оё x))` | The **combined GMM score** `ПҲ(Оё,x) = GбөҖW g(Оё,x)`; its empirical zero is the (linearized) GMM estimator (FOC of `бёЎбөҖWбёЎ`). |
| `gmmIF G W breadInv g ОёвӮҖ` | `fun x => вҲ’(breadInv (adjoint G (W (g ОёвӮҖ x))))` | The **GMM influence function** `вҲ’(GбөҖWG)вҒ»В№GбөҖW g(ОёвӮҖ,В·)`. |
| `GMMProblem P` | structure | Moment model bundle: `g`, truth `ОёвӮҖ`, weighting `W` (`hWsa : adjoint W = W`), Jacobian `G`, identification `вҲ« g(ОёвӮҖ) dP = 0`, measurability/finite-variance of `g(ОёвӮҖ)`, FrГ©chet-derivative spec `jac_spec`, covariance `Cov` with second-moment characterization `hCov`, and two-sided inverse witnesses for `Cov`, `GбөҖWG`, `GбөҖCovвҒ»В№G`. |
| `GMMProblem.score` / `influence` | `noncomputable def`s | The problem's combined score / influence function. |
| `GMMProblem.asympVar` | `gmmSandwich G W Cov breadInv` | The **sandwich asymptotic variance** of the problem. |
| `GMMProblem.effVar` | `effInv` | The **efficient variance** `(GбөҖCovвҒ»В№G)вҒ»В№`. |
| `GMMProblem.cov_isPositive` | `prob.Cov.IsPositive` | The covariance is a positive operator вҖ” it is a second moment (`вҹӘCov t,tвҹ« = вҲ« вҹӘt,gвҹ«ВІ вүҘ 0`, symmetric). **Proved.** |
| `GMMProblem.efficiency` | `(prob.asympVar вҲ’ prob.effVar).IsPositive` | **Hansen optimal-weighting theorem, statistical form**: the sandwich variance dominates the efficient variance in the LГ¶wner order; the efficient GMM weighting is `W = CovвҒ»В№`. Instantiates `gmm_efficiency`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12v.3. `Stat/GMM/AsymptoticNormality.lean` вҖ” GMM CLT via the combined-score Z-estimator

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gmm_asymptotically_linear` | `IsAsymLinearVec Оёn ОёвӮҖ prob.influence S (range n)` | **GMM asymptotic linearity.** If `Оёn` solves the combined empirical moment `(1/n) ОЈ GбөҖW g(Z_i;Оёn) = 0`, is consistent at rate `O_p(1/вҲҡn)`, and the combined score `prob.score` satisfies `ZEstimatorRegularity` (with `reg.JвӮҖ_inv = breadInv`), then `Оёn` is asymptotically linear with IF `вҲ’(GбөҖWG)вҒ»В№GбөҖW g(ОёвӮҖ,В·)`. Hence `вҲҡn(ОёnвҲ’ОёвӮҖ) вҮ’ N(0, asympVar)` via `IsAsymLinearVec.tendsto_normal_vec`. Reduces GMM to `zEstimator_clt` on `gmmScore`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12v.4. `Stat/GMM/OverID.lean` вҖ” over-identification (J / HansenвҖ“Sargan) test

The whitened J-test: with the moment covariance normalised to the identity (the change of variables the `ОЈМӮвҒ»В№`-weighting performs), the residual maker `M = I вҲ’ G(GбөҖG)вҒ»В№GбөҖ` is an orthogonal projection of rank `m вҲ’ k`, so the J-statistic limit `вҖ–MwвҖ–ВІ` is `ПҮВІ_{mвҲ’k}` via `stdGaussian_map_normSq_orthogonalProjection`. The general-covariance case is the change of variables `z вҶҰ ОЈ^{-1/2} z`. Inverses carried as data+witnesses (`heL`, `heR`).

**Deferred (known gap).** This file establishes only the *limit law* `вҖ–MwвҖ–ВІ вҮ’ ПҮВІ_{mвҲ’k}` for a standard Gaussian `w` on the moment space. Wiring the **empirical** J-statistic `nВ·бёЎвӮҷ(ОёМӮ)бөҖ ОЈМӮвҒ»В№ бёЎвӮҷ(ОёМӮ)` to this limit вҖ” i.e. proving the empirical moments converge to `Mw` under the efficient weighting and applying continuous mapping вҖ” is left as future work (needs the moment-CLT contact point + the whitening change-of-variables at the estimator). The ПҮВІ target law itself is fully proved.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `gmmHatMatrix G effInv` | `G вҲҳL effInv вҲҳL adjoint G` | The **hat matrix** `H = G(GбөҖG)вҒ»В№GбөҖ`, orthogonal projection onto `range G`. |
| `gmmResidualMaker G effInv` | `id вҲ’ gmmHatMatrix G effInv` | The **residual maker** `M = I вҲ’ H`, orthogonal projection onto `(range G)^вҠҘ`. |
| `gmmResidualMaker_isSelfAdjoint` / `_idempotent` | `IsSelfAdjoint M` / `M вҲҳL M = M` | `M` is an orthogonal projection (uses `effInv` self-adjoint + the inverse witnesses). **Proved.** |
| `gmmResidualMaker_finrank_range` | `finrank (range M) = finrank F вҲ’ finrank E` | **HansenвҖ“Sargan dof** `m вҲ’ k`. Proof: `range M = ker H`, `range H = range G`, `G` injective (from `heL`); rankвҖ“nullity (`LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker`) + `LinearMap.finrank_range_of_inj`. **Proved.** |
| `jStatistic_chiSq` | `(stdGaussian F).map (вҖ–MВ·вҖ–ВІ) = chiSqDist (finrank F вҲ’ finrank E)` | **Headline: the whitened GMM J-statistic limit law is `ПҮВІ_{mвҲ’k}`.** Applies the projected-Gaussian ПҮВІ lemma to `M`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 12w. `Stat/Minimax/` вҖ” Minimax lower bounds (Le Cam two-point)

The converse direction of the inference stack: machinery to certify that *no*
estimator can do better than a given rate (optimality / sharpness), as opposed
to the achievability results in В§12cвҖ“В§12v. The whole program reduces to one
chain: *risk lower bound вҶҗ reduction to testing вҶҗ testing error вүҘ 1 вҲ’ TV вҶҗ TV вүӨ f(KL)*.
The first three links are elementary (this folder); the KL bridge (Pinsker)
lives in `Pinsker.lean`. Project-agnostic; candidates for upstream Mathlib.

Three reduction tiers share the same testing core: **Le Cam** (`LeCam.lean`, В§12w.2)
packs two hypotheses; **Fano** (`Fano.lean`, В§12w.7) packs many pairwise-separated
ones (rates from `log`-cardinality); **Assouad** (`Assouad.lean`, В§12w.8) packs a
`d`-cube of coordinate-wise tests (rates that grow with dimension). Fano and Assouad
are both proven *unconditionally* from the elementary testing bound вҖ” no entropy or
product-measure machinery вҖ” so the divergence input enters only through `tvDist`.

#### 12w.1. `Stat/Minimax/TotalVariation.lean` вҖ” statistical TV distance

Mathlib only carries `SignedMeasure.totalVariation` (Jordan norm); this file develops the testing-oriented statistical TV between two measures.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `tvDist Ој ОҪ` | `вЁҶ A measurable, \|Ој.real A вҲ’ ОҪ.real A\|` | **Total variation distance**: sup over measurable sets of the probability gap. |
| `abs_measureReal_sub_le_one` | `\|Ој.real A вҲ’ ОҪ.real A\| вүӨ 1` | Each defining term is bounded by 1 (probability measures). **Proved.** |
| `bddAbove_tvDist_range` | `BddAbove (range вҖҰ)` | The defining family is bounded above by 1. **Proved.** |
| `abs_measureReal_sub_le_tvDist` | `\|Ој.real A вҲ’ ОҪ.real A\| вүӨ tvDist Ој ОҪ` | Each measurable set's gap is `вүӨ tvDist` (via `le_ciSup`). **Proved.** |
| `measureReal_sub_le_tvDist` | `ОҪ.real A вҲ’ Ој.real A вүӨ tvDist Ој ОҪ` | Signed-gap form. **Proved.** |
| `tvDist_nonneg`, `tvDist_le_one` | `0 вүӨ tvDist Ој ОҪ вүӨ 1` | Range. **Proved.** |
| `tvDist_symm` | `tvDist Ој ОҪ = tvDist ОҪ Ој` | Symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `one_sub_tvDist_le_test` | `1 вҲ’ tvDist Ој ОҪ вүӨ Ој.real A + ОҪ.real Aб¶ң` | **The testing bound**: the total error of any test (rejection region `A`) is at least `1 вҲ’ tvDist`. The single inequality driving the two-point method. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.2. `Stat/Minimax/LeCam.lean` вҖ” Le Cam two-point method

The reduction from estimation to testing. Parameters live in a `[PseudoMetricSpace Оҳ] [OpensMeasurableSpace Оҳ]`; estimators are arbitrary measurable `est : О© вҶ’ Оҳ`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `measurableSet_error` | `MeasurableSet {Пү \| s вүӨ dist (est Пү) Оё}` | The error region of a measurable estimator is measurable (closed-set preimage). **Proved.** |
| `one_sub_tvDist_le_error_sum` | `1 вҲ’ tvDist PвӮҖ PвӮҒ вүӨ PвӮҖ.real {s вүӨ dist(est,ОёвӮҖ)} + PвӮҒ.real {s вүӨ dist(est,ОёвӮҒ)}` | **Summed form.** Under `2s`-separation `2s вүӨ dist ОёвӮҖ ОёвӮҒ`, the two error probabilities of any estimator sum to at least `1 вҲ’ tvDist`. Proof = testing bound + triangle inequality + monotonicity. **Proved.** |
| `half_one_sub_tvDist_le_max_error` | `(1 вҲ’ tvDist PвӮҖ PвӮҒ)/2 вүӨ max (PвӮҖ.real {вҖҰ}) (PвӮҒ.real {вҖҰ})` | **Headline (max form).** Every estimator has worst-case error probability at least `ВҪ(1 вҲ’ tvDist PвӮҖ PвӮҒ)`: the canonical minimax lower bound. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.3. `Stat/Minimax/Pinsker.lean` вҖ” KL bridge

Bridges `tvDist` to Mathlib's `InformationTheory.klDiv`, making the two-point bound usable for concrete families (KL is computable for Gaussians etc.). **Pinsker's inequality is now proven unconditionally** вҖ” both the scalar core (`klFun_lower_bound`) and the measure-theoretic half (ScheffГ© `вүӨ` + weighted CauchyвҖ“Schwarz). `PinskerBound` remains as a reusable `Prop` packaging but is no longer a substrate gate.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `klFun_mul_lower_aux` | `0 вүӨ (x+2)В·klFun x вҲ’ (3/2)(xвҲ’1)ВІ` for `x вүҘ 0` | Cleared-denominator Pinsker scalar bound. Proved by full two-level calculus on `ПҶ` (`HasDerivAt` for ПҶ, ПҶвҖІ; `ПҶвҖі = 2(log x + 1/x вҲ’ 1) вүҘ 0` via `log_le_sub_one`; monotonicity вҮ’ `ПҶ вүҘ ПҶ(1)=0`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `klFun_lower_bound` | `(3/2)(xвҲ’1)ВІ/(x+2) вүӨ klFun x` for `x вүҘ 0` | Division-form Pinsker scalar engine. **Proved.** |
| `PinskerBound Ој ОҪ` | `Prop`: `tvDist Ој ОҪ вүӨ вҲҡ((klDiv Ој ОҪ).toReal/2)` | Reusable `Prop` packaging of the Pinsker bound for a pair. **Proven** by `pinskerBound_of_ac_of_ne_top`. |
| `pinskerBound_of_ac_of_ne_top` | `Ој вүӘ ОҪ` + `klDiv Ој ОҪ вү  вҠӨ` вҮ’ `PinskerBound Ој ОҪ` | **Pinsker's inequality, proven unconditionally**: ScheffГ© `вүӨ` (`tvDist_le_half_integral_abs_rnDeriv`) + weighted CauchyвҖ“Schwarz (`вҲҡ(p+2)` weight) + `klFun_lower_bound`. Both hyps are genuinely needed (`.toReal` collapses `klDiv = вҠӨ` to `0`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `pinskerBound_pi_iid` | one-sample `Ој вүӘ ОҪ` + `Integrable (llr Ој ОҪ) Ој` вҮ’ `PinskerBound (Ој^{вҠ—n}) (ОҪ^{вҠ—n})` | i.i.d.-product Pinsker: product AC + `klDiv вү  вҠӨ` discharged from marginals via `ProductKLLeCam.pi_iid_*`, then the unconditional inequality. **Proved.** |
| `klForm_two_point_lower_bound_of_pinsker` | from `PinskerBound PвӮҖ PвӮҒ` + 2s-separation: worst-case error `вүҘ (1 вҲ’ вҲҡ(KL/2))/2` | KL-form minimax lower bound from a supplied `PinskerBound` term; composes with `half_one_sub_tvDist_le_max_error`. **Proved from hypothesis.** |
| `klForm_two_point_lower_bound` | `PвӮҖ вүӘ PвӮҒ` + `klDiv PвӮҖ PвӮҒ вү  вҠӨ` + 2s-separation вҮ’ worst-case error `вүҘ (1 вҲ’ вҲҡ(KL/2))/2` | **Unconditional** KL-form minimax lower bound: derives the Pinsker bridge via `pinskerBound_of_ac_of_ne_top` (no `PinskerBound` hypothesis). **Proved.** |

#### 12w.4. `Stat/Minimax/Scheffe.lean` вҖ” variational TV identity (вүӨ direction)

The ScheffГ© `вүӨ` bound вҖ” the analytic workhorse for both the ПҮВІ route (`TV вүӨ ВҪвҲҡПҮВІ`) and discharging `PinskerBound`. Proved via the symmetric splitting `вҲ«_A f = вҲ’вҲ«_{Aб¶ң} f` (sidesteps posPart/negPart machinery). The reverse `вүҘ` (full equality) was deliberately skipped вҖ” the `вүӨ` suffices downstream.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `abs_setIntegral_le_half_integral_abs_of_integral_eq_zero` | for integrable `f`, `вҲ« f вҲӮОҪ = 0`, measurable `A`: `\|вҲ« x in A, f вҲӮОҪ\| вүӨ ВҪвҲ«\|f\| вҲӮОҪ` | Generic, probability-free; clean Mathlib-contribution candidate. **Proved.** |
| `measureReal_sub_eq_setIntegral_rnDeriv_sub_one` | under `Ој вүӘ ОҪ`: `Ој.real A вҲ’ ОҪ.real A = вҲ« x in A, ((Ој.rnDeriv ОҪ x).toReal вҲ’ 1) вҲӮОҪ` | Signed-gap as a density integral. **Proved.** |
| `tvDist_le_half_integral_abs_rnDeriv` | `tvDist Ој ОҪ вүӨ ВҪвҲ«\|(Ој.rnDeriv ОҪ x).toReal вҲ’ 1\| вҲӮОҪ` (prob measures, `Ој вүӘ ОҪ`) | **ScheffГ© `вүӨ`.** **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.5. `Stat/Minimax/ChiSquared.lean` вҖ” ПҮВІ divergence link

`chiSqDiv` and the ПҮВІ control of total variation вҖ” the divergence that makes two-point indistinguishability computable (and tensorizes over iid samples). Named `chiSqDiv` to avoid colliding with the ПҮВІ *distribution* `chiSqDist` (В§12n'''d).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `chiSqDiv Ој ОҪ` | `вҲ« ((Ој.rnDeriv ОҪ x).toReal вҲ’ 1)ВІ вҲӮОҪ` | ПҮВІ-divergence of Ој relative to ОҪ. |
| `chiSqDiv_nonneg` | `0 вүӨ chiSqDiv Ој ОҪ` | Integrand is a square. **Proved.** |
| `tvDist_le_half_sqrt_chiSqDiv` | `tvDist Ој ОҪ вүӨ ВҪВ·вҲҡ(chiSqDiv Ој ОҪ)` (prob, `ОјвүӘОҪ`, sq-deviation integrable) | **CauchyвҖ“Schwarz on ScheffГ©** (HГ¶lder `2,2` with `gвүЎ1`, `вҲ«1ВІвҲӮОҪ=1`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chiSqDiv_eq` | `chiSqDiv Ој ОҪ = (вҲ« (Ој.rnDeriv ОҪ x).toRealВІ вҲӮОҪ) вҲ’ 1` (`ОјвүӘОҪ`) | Expanded form via `вҲ« dОј/dОҪ вҲӮОҪ = 1`. **Proved.** |

| `rnDeriv_prod_eq` | `(ОјвӮҒ.prod ОјвӮӮ).rnDeriv (ОҪвӮҒ.prod ОҪвӮӮ) =бөҗ fun z вҶҰ rnDeriv ОјвӮҒ ОҪвӮҒ z.1 В· rnDeriv ОјвӮӮ ОҪвӮӮ z.2` | Product-measure RN-derivative factorization (assembled from `withDensity_rnDeriv_eq` + `prod_withDensityвӮҖ` + `rnDeriv_withDensityвӮҖ`; Mathlib has no packaged version). Reusable. **Proved.** |
| `chiSqDiv_prod` | `1 + chiSqDiv (ОјвӮҒ.prod ОјвӮӮ) (ОҪвӮҒ.prod ОҪвӮӮ) = (1+chiSqDiv ОјвӮҒ ОҪвӮҒ)(1+chiSqDiv ОјвӮӮ ОҪвӮӮ)` | **ПҮВІ tensorization** (binary). Via `rnDeriv_prod_eq` + `chiSqDiv_eq` + Fubini (`integral_prod_mul`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chiSqDiv_map_measurableEquiv` | `chiSqDiv (Ој.map e) (ОҪ.map e) = chiSqDiv Ој ОҪ` for `e : О© вүғбөҗ О©'` (Пғ-finite) | **ПҮВІ is invariant under a measurable equivalence** (relabelling the sample space). Via `integral_map_equiv` + `MeasurableEmbedding.rnDeriv_map`. Reusable. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chiSqDiv_self` | `chiSqDiv ПҒ ПҒ = 0` (Пғ-finite) | Self-divergence vanishes (`rnDeriv_self =бөҗ 1`); the `n=0` base case. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `pi_iid_absolutelyContinuous` | `Ој вүӘ ОҪ вҮ’ Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n вҶҰ Ој) вүӘ Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n вҶҰ ОҪ)` | i.i.d. product a.c. (Mathlib has no `pi`-level a.c. lemma); induction via `piFinSuccAbove` + `AbsolutelyContinuous.prod`/`.map`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid` | `1 + chiSqDiv (Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n вҶҰ Ој)) (Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n вҶҰ ОҪ)) = (1 + chiSqDiv Ој ОҪ)вҒҝ` (finite `О©`, prob, `ОјвүӘОҪ`) | **n-fold i.i.d. ПҮВІ tensorization** вҖ” the iid scaling consumed by `n`-sample two-point/Le Cam bounds. Induction on `Fin n` via `measurePreserving_piFinSuccAbove` + `chiSqDiv_map_measurableEquiv` + binary `chiSqDiv_prod`; integrability discharged for free by `Integrable.of_finite` (restricted to `[Fintype О©] [MeasurableSingletonClass О©]`). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `pi_iid_integrable_sq_dev` | `Integrable ((d(Ој^вҠ—n)/d(ОҪ^вҠ—n) вҲ’ 1)ВІ) (ОҪ^вҠ—n)` from the one-sample integrability `hint` (general `[MeasurableSpace S]`, probs, `ОјвүӘОҪ`) | **Integrability propagation** to the i.i.d. product: the squared density deviation stays integrable under `n`-fold products (LВІ(ОҪ)-tensorization of the density). Supplies the side-condition that `one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid` got for free on a finite space. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid_general` | `1 + chiSqDiv (Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n вҶҰ Ој)) (Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n вҶҰ ОҪ)) = (1 + chiSqDiv Ој ОҪ)вҒҝ` (general `[MeasurableSpace S]`, probs, `ОјвүӘОҪ`, sq-deviation integrable) | **n-fold i.i.d. ПҮВІ tensorization on a GENERAL (possibly continuous) measurable space** вҖ” the continuum analogue of `one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid` (whose `[Fintype]` only discharged integrability for free). Same `chiSqDiv_prod` induction, with integrability supplied by `hint` + `pi_iid_integrable_sq_dev`. Needed by minimax two-point lower bounds whose sample space is a continuum (e.g. a covariate space). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `chiSqDiv_prod_ancillary` | `chiSqDiv (Ој.prod ПҒ) (ОҪ.prod ПҒ) = chiSqDiv Ој ОҪ` (probs, `ОјвүӘОҪ`, `hint` for `Ој,ОҪ`) | **Ancillary factor invariance**: a common product factor `ПҒ` cancels (`1 + ПҮВІ(ПҒвҖ–ПҒ) = 1`). The formal "an ancillary observation carries no information"; the bridge from finite-cell to continuous-`X` (В§13sa.6). **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.5b. `Stat/Minimax/ChiSquaredFinite.lean` вҖ” finite / product specializations of ПҮВІ

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `rnDeriv_mul_measure_singleton` | `ОҪ {x} В· (dОј/dОҪ)(x) = Ој {x}` (`ОјвүӘОҪ`, measurable singletons) | **Discrete RadonвҖ“Nikodym bridge** (a genuine Mathlib gap): pointwise density inversion on atoms, via `setLIntegral_rnDeriv` + `restrict_singleton`/`lintegral_dirac'`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `finite_one_add_chiSqDiv` | `1 + chiSqDiv Ој ОҪ = вҲ‘ x, (Ој.real {x})ВІ / (ОҪ.real {x})` (finite `О©`, probs, `ОјвүӘОҪ`) | **Finite ПҮВІ formula**: turns ПҮВІ into a finite sum of squared point-mass ratios (`chiSqDiv_eq` + `integral_fintype` + the bridge). The enabling step for the Ingster bound. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `pi_real_singleton` | `(Measure.pi Ој).real {Пү} = вҲҸ i, (Ој i).real {Пү i}` (finite index, probs) | **Product point mass** (real-valued): `{Пү} = univ.pi {Пүбөў}` + `Measure.pi_pi` + `ENNReal.toReal_prod`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.6. `Stat/Minimax/MinimaxRisk.lean` вҖ” real-functional / n-sample packaging

Causal-agnostic specialization of Le Cam to a real-valued functional estimated from `n` i.i.d. samples вҖ” the shape consumed by concrete minimax lower bounds (the causal structure-agnostic ATE theorem lives in `Estimation/`, not here).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `real_two_point_lower_bound` | `2s вүӨ \|ОёвӮҖвҲ’ОёвӮҒ\|` вҮ’ `(1вҲ’tvDist PвӮҖ PвӮҒ)/2 вүӨ max (PвӮҖ.real{sвүӨ\|estвҲ’ОёвӮҖ\|}) (PвӮҒ.real{sвүӨ\|estвҲ’ОёвӮҒ\|})` | Le Cam at `Оҳ=в„қ` (`dist a b = \|aвҲ’b\|`). **Proved.** |
| `two_point_lower_bound_of_tvDist_le` | same with explicit `tvDist PвӮҖ PвӮҒ вүӨ c` вҮ’ bound `(1вҲ’c)/2` | Convenience for plugging a TV upper bound. **Proved.** |
| `iid_two_point_lower_bound` | for laws `PвӮҖ,PвӮҒ` on `S`, functional `П„`, `n` samples (`Measure.pi (fun _:Fin n вҶҰ Pвұј)`), `2s вүӨ \|П„ PвӮҖ вҲ’ П„ PвӮҒ\|` вҮ’ worst-case miss prob `вүҘ ВҪ(1 вҲ’ tvDist)` between the `n`-fold laws | **The n-sample minimax form.** **Proved.** |
| `two_point_lower_bound_of_chiSqDiv_le` | `chiSqDiv PвӮҖ PвӮҒ вүӨ c` (+ `вүӘ`, integrability) вҮ’ worst-case miss prob `вүҘ (1 вҲ’ ВҪвҲҡc)/2` | **ПҮВІ-form bound** (via `tvDist вүӨ ВҪвҲҡПҮВІ`); the directly-certifiable form since ПҮВІ is computable and tensorizes. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.7. `Stat/Minimax/Fano.lean` вҖ” multiple-hypothesis (M-ary) lower bound

Generalizes Le Cam from *two* hypotheses to *many* pairwise-`2s`-separated ones вҖ” the testing/TV form of Fano's lemma (Tsybakov В§2.6, Le CamвҖ“BirgГ©). An estimator induces disjoint acceptance regions `A i = {Пү | dist(est Пү, Оё i) < s}`; comparing each `Pбөў(A i)` to a *reference* hypothesis `iвӮҖ` costs one `tvDist`, bounding the total correct-recovery mass. Proven **unconditionally** from `measureReal_sub_le_tvDist` вҖ” no entropy/mutual-information machinery; divergence enters only through `tvDist` (bound it by KL/ПҮВІ via В§12w.3/В§12w.5). Uses a reference index `iвӮҖ` rather than the centroid mixture, so it does **not** depend on `Mixture.lean` (the symmetric mixture-centroid form is a strict sharpening that can be layered on).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `acceptanceRegion est Оё s` | `{Пү \| dist (est Пү) Оё < s}` | Points the estimator places strictly within `s` of `Оё`. |
| `measurableSet_acceptanceRegion` | `MeasurableSet (acceptanceRegion est Оё s)` | Complement of the (closed) error region. **Proved.** |
| `acceptanceRegion_disjoint` / `_pairwiseDisjoint` | `2s вүӨ dist ОёвӮҖ ОёвӮҒ вҮ’ Disjoint (A ОёвӮҖ) (A ОёвӮҒ)`; pairwise version over a family | A point within `s` of both targets would force them within `2s`. **Proved.** |
| `sum_refReal_acceptance_le` | `вҲ‘бөў (P iвӮҖ).real (A i) вүӨ 1` | Reference mass over disjoint regions is `вүӨ 1` (finite additivity + `вүӨ univ`). **Proved.** |
| `sum_correct_le` | `вҲ‘бөў (P i).real (A i) вүӨ 1 + вҲ‘бөў tvDist (P iвӮҖ) (P i)` | **Heart of Fano**: each `Pбөў(A i)` vs reference costs one `tvDist`. **Proved.** |
| `fano_average_error` | `1 вҲ’ (1 + вҲ‘бөў tvDist (P iвӮҖ) (P i))/N вүӨ (вҲ‘бөў (P i).real {s вүӨ dist(est,Оё i)})/N` (`N = card О№`) | **Average-error minimax lower bound** over the family. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `fano_exists_error` | under `tvDist (P iвӮҖ) (P i) вүӨ ОІ`: `вҲғ i, 1 вҲ’ 1/N вҲ’ ОІ вүӨ (P i).real {s вүӨ dist(est,Оё i)}` | **Usable form**: large `N` + small `ОІ` вҮ’ some hypothesis forces error. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.8. `Stat/Minimax/Assouad.lean` вҖ” hypercube lower bound (Assouad's lemma)

Packs a `d`-cube `{P П„ : П„ вҲҲ (Fin d вҶ’ Bool)}` in which each coordinate is an independent two-point test вҖ” converting a `d`-fold product into a `d`-fold *sum* of testing bounds (rates that grow with dimension). A cube estimator `est : О© вҶ’ (Fin d вҶ’ Bool)` decodes each bit; its expected **Hamming risk** counts mis-decoded coordinates. Proof = coordinate-flip averaging: pairing each vertex `П„` with its `j`-flip via an involution (`flipPerm`), the two coordinate-`j` masses are `(P П„).real S + (P (flip j П„)).real Sб¶ң`, lower-bounded by `1 вҲ’ tvDist` via `one_sub_tvDist_le_test`. **Unconditional**, no product-measure machinery.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `flipBit j П„` / `flipPerm j` | `Function.update П„ j (!П„ j)`; as an `Equiv.Perm` | Flip the `j`-th cube coordinate (an involution; the perm reindexes cube-sums). **Proved involutive.** |
| `hammingRisk P est П„` | `вҲ‘вұј (P П„).real {Пү \| est Пү j вү  П„ j}` | Expected number of coordinates the estimator decodes incorrectly at vertex `П„`. |
| `measurableSet_decode_ne` | `MeasurableSet {Пү \| est Пү j вү  b}` from `MeasurableSet {Пү \| est Пү j = b}` | Decoding-error sets are complements of level sets. **Proved.** |
| `decode_ne_flip_compl` | `{Пү \| est Пү j вү  (flip j П„) j} = {Пү \| est Пү j вү  П„ j}б¶ң` | The `j`-th error set at the flipped vertex is the complement at `П„` (`a вү  !b вҶ” a = b`). **Proved.** |
| `hammingRisk_pair_ge` | `1 вҲ’ tvDist (P П„) (P (flip j П„)) вүӨ (P П„).real SвұјП„ + (P (flip j П„)).real Sвұј(flip)` | **Per-vertex pair bound** = two-point testing bound on the `j`-th decoded bit. **Proved.** |
| `sum_decode_ge` | `вҲ‘_П„ (1 вҲ’ tvDist (P П„)(P (flip j П„))) вүӨ 2В·вҲ‘_П„ (P П„).real {est j вү  П„ j}` | Per-coordinate cube-sum bound via the flip involution (`Equiv.sum_comp`). **Proved.** |
| `assouad_average` | under `tvDist (P П„)(P (flip j П„)) вүӨ ОІ`: `(d/2)(1 вҲ’ ОІ) вүӨ (вҲ‘_П„ hammingRisk P est П„)/card` | **Assouad (average form).** Large `d` + small `ОІ` вҮ’ many mis-decoded coordinates. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `assouad_exists` | same hypotheses: `вҲғ П„, (d/2)(1 вҲ’ ОІ) вүӨ hammingRisk P est П„` | **Existence form**: some vertex forces large Hamming risk. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

#### 12w.9. `Stat/Minimax/Mixture.lean` вҖ” finite mixtures of measures

Generic finite mixture `вҲ‘бөў wбөў вҖў Pбөў` and the **domination lemma** behind the fuzzy-hypothesis / second-moment method: a mixture over in-class hypotheses with a common functional value never assigns more mass than the worst single hypothesis, so an averaged risk is `вүӨ` the supremum. Consumed by the structure-agnostic ATE lower bound (В§13sa) for the `dominated` (realizability) obligation of `TwoPointWitness`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mixture w P` | `вҲ‘ i, w i вҖў P i` (`w : Fin K вҶ’ в„қвүҘ0вҲһ`) | Finite mixture of measures with `в„қвүҘ0вҲһ` weights. |
| `mixture_apply` | `mixture w P A = вҲ‘ i, w i В· P i A` | Mass on a set is the weighted sum of the parts' masses. **Proved.** |
| `mixture_isProbabilityMeasure` | `вҲ‘ i, w i = 1 вҮ’ IsProbabilityMeasure (mixture w P)` (parts prob) | A convex combination of probability measures is a probability measure. **Proved.** |
| `mixtureReal_le` | `вҲ‘ i, w i = 1, (вҲҖ i, (P i).real A вүӨ B) вҮ’ (mixture w P).real A вүӨ B` (parts prob) | **Domination**: a weighted average of per-part masses is `вүӨ` a common bound. The averaging step in the fuzzy-hypothesis reduction. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `exists_real_ge_mixture` | `вҲ‘ i, w i = 1 вҮ’ вҲғ i, (mixture w P).real A вүӨ (P i).real A` (parts prob, `[Nonempty О№]`) | **Witness extraction**: a finite average is `вүӨ` its maximal component, so some part carries at least the mixture's mass. Pulls a single hard DGP out of a mixture hypothesis. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

## 12ep. `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/` вҖ” Uniform LLN (GlivenkoвҖ“Cantelli) & extremum-estimator consistency

Empirical-process layer written for the econometrics workflow (van der Vaart
1998, Ch. 19; Newey & McFadden 1994, В§2). A *function class* is an indexed family
`f : О№ вҶ’ X вҶ’ в„қ`; the empirical process measures how far the sample means `PвӮҷ f_i`
sit from the population means `P f_i = вҲ« f_i dP`, **uniformly over the class**.
This is the uniform law of large numbers that the M-estimation CLT stack
(`Stat/MEstimation/`) previously had to assume. All declarations **proved,
sorry-free**.

### 12ep.a `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/Basic.lean` вҖ” vocabulary

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.empiricalProcess f i n` | `def`, `О© вҶ’ в„қ` | The centred-scaled process `вҲҡn В· (PвӮҷ f_i вҲ’ P f_i)` at class member `i` вҖ” the object whose weak limit (a Gaussian process) is the subject of Donsker theory. |
| `IIDSample.supDeviation f n` | `def`, `О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Finite-class sup deviation `вЁҶ i, \|PвӮҷ f_i вҲ’ P f_i\|` (meaningful as a statistic when `О№` is finite). |
| `GlivenkoCantelli S f` | `def`, `Prop` | **(Weak) uniform LLN**: for every `Оө > 0`, `Ој {Пү | вҲғ i, Оө вүӨ \|PвӮҷ f_i вҲ’ P f_i\|} вҶ’ 0`. Stated existentially (not via `вЁҶ`) so it is meaningful for infinite/uncountable classes without lattice-junk artefacts. |
| `L1Bracketing f P Оө` | `structure` | A finite `LВ№(P)` `Оө`-bracketing: `m` brackets `[lo j, hi j]` (`j : Fin m`) with integrable endpoints, `P`-mean width `вҲ« (hi j вҲ’ lo j) dP вүӨ Оө`, and an `assign : О№ вҶ’ Fin m` sandwiching each member `lo (assign i) вүӨ f i вүӨ hi (assign i)`. |
| `HasL1Bracketing f P` | `def`, `Prop` | Finite `LВ№(P)` bracketing numbers: an `Оө`-bracketing exists for every `Оө > 0`. The classical sufficient condition for GlivenkoвҖ“Cantelli. |

### 12ep.b `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/GlivenkoCantelli.lean` вҖ” uniform LLN theorems

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `glivenkoCantelli_of_fintype` | `[Finite О№]`, each `f i` measurable & `P`-integrable вҮ’ `GlivenkoCantelli S f` | A **finite** class of integrable functions is GlivenkoвҖ“Cantelli. Union bound over coordinates, each vanishing by the pointwise WLLN (`IIDSample.sampleMean_tendsto_inProb`). **Proved.** |
| `glivenkoCantelli_of_hasL1Bracketing` | each `f i` measurable, `HasL1Bracketing f P` вҮ’ `GlivenkoCantelli S f` | A class with **finite `LВ№(P)` bracketing numbers** is GlivenkoвҖ“Cantelli (van der Vaart 1998, Thm 19.4; NeweyвҖ“McFadden Lemma 2.4). Each member is sandwiched in a bracket of mean-width `вүӨ Оө/2`, so its deviation is controlled by the `2m` bracket-endpoint deviations plus `Оө/2`; the endpoints vanish by the finite case. **Proved.** |

Private helpers: `tendsto_measure_zero_of_le` (measure squeeze), `integrable_pullback` (transfer `P`-integrability to `Ој`), `dev_tendsto_zero` (single-statistic WLLN rephrased from the `edist`/`в„қвүҘ0вҲһ` `TendstoInMeasure` to the real `\|В·\|` event), `sampleMean_mono`.

### 12ep.c `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/MEstimatorConsistency.lean` вҖ” extremum-estimator consistency

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mEstimator_consistent_of_glivenkoCantelli` | `GlivenkoCantelli S m` + sample-argmax domination over `ОёвӮҖ` + well-separated population max вҮ’ `вҲҖ Оө>0, Ој {Пү | Оө вүӨ dist (ОёМӮвӮҷ Пү) ОёвӮҖ} вҶ’ 0` | **Consistency of extremum / M-estimators** (NeweyвҖ“McFadden 1994, Thm 2.1; van der Vaart 1998, Thm 5.7). `ОёМӮвӮҷ` maximizing the sample criterion `Оё вҶҰ PвӮҷ m(Оё,В·)` is consistent for the population maximizer `ОёвӮҖ`. **Consumes** the `GlivenkoCantelli` engine: the uniform-convergence hypothesis is exactly that predicate. Proof is the classical `M(ОёвӮҖ) вҲ’ M(ОёМӮвӮҷ) вүӨ 2В·sup_Оё\|MвӮҷвҲ’M\|` sandwich versus the separation gap `О·`. **Proved, sorry-free.** |
| `mEstimator_consistent_of_bracketing` | finite `LВ№(P)` bracketing of `m` + argmax domination + well-separated max вҮ’ consistency | **Bracketing headline corollary**: composes `glivenkoCantelli_of_hasL1Bracketing` with the consistency theorem, so a verifiable bracketing condition (instead of an assumed uniform LLN) yields consistency directly. **Proved, sorry-free.** |

### 12ep.d `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/Equicontinuity/` вҖ” stochastic equicontinuity & the `StochEquicontAt` reduction

The empirical-process / Donsker layer at the **parametric** scale, discharging the
`StochEquicontAt` hypothesis consumed by `zEstimator_clt` and
`gmm_asymptotically_linear`. **Key structural finding:** `StochEquicontAt`
(fixed `Оҙ`, inner limit `0`, gap evaluated at the *random* estimator `Оёn`) is only
true when `Оёn вҶ’_p ОёвӮҖ` вҖ” it secretly bundles consistency. The honest decomposition
is **class-level asymptotic equicontinuity (`AsymptoticEquicont`, independent of
`Оёn`) + consistency вҹ№ `StochEquicontAt`**. The uniform-in-`n` control of the
empirical process comes from the variance identity `Var(вҲҡn(PвӮҷвҲ’P)f) = Var_P f` via
Chebyshev вҖ” no CLT and no chaining for the base cases. All declarations below are
**proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean**. `E` is a finite-dimensional real inner-product
space (the `Z`-estimator parameter/value space).

`Process.lean` вҖ” vocabulary:

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.empProcVec S f n` | `def`, `О© вҶ’ E` | **Vector-valued centered empirical process** `GвӮҷ(f)(Пү) = (вҲҡn)вҒ»В№ вҖў ОЈ_{i<n} f(Zбөў Пү) вҲ’ вҲҡn вҖў вҲ« f dP`; the `E`-valued analogue of `empiricalProcess`. |
| `IIDSample.empProcVec_eq_stochEquicont_gap` | `rfl` bridge | `empProcVec` of the score difference `ПҲ(Оё,В·)вҲ’ПҲ(ОёвӮҖ,В·)` equals the centered gap `R_n` of `StochEquicontAt`. |

`SecondMoment.lean` вҖ” the uniform-in-`n` Chebyshev workhorse:

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.measurable_empProcVec` | `Measurable (fun Пү => empProcVec f n Пү)` | Measurability of the process in `Пү`. |
| `empProcVec_sq_lintegral_le` | `f вҲҲ LВІ(P)` вҮ’ `вҲ«вҒ» вҖ–GвӮҷ(f)вҖ–ВІ dОј вүӨ ENNReal.ofReal (вҲ« вҖ–fвҖ–ВІ dP)` | **Second-moment / variance identity at the empirical scale**, uniformly in `n`. Proved coordinate-wise: Parseval (`OrthonormalBasis.sum_sq_inner_right`) reduces `вҖ–GвӮҷ(f)вҖ–ВІ` to a sum of scalar centered i.i.d. sums, each bounded by `Causalean.Mathlib.iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le`. No CLT, no chaining. |
| `empProcVec_chebyshev` | `0<Оө` вҮ’ `Ој {вҖ–GвӮҷ(f)вҖ– > Оө} вүӨ ENNReal.ofReal (вҲ« вҖ–fвҖ–ВІ dP) / ENNReal.ofReal (ОөВІ)` | **Chebyshev bound** (second moment + Markov, `meas_ge_le_lintegral_div`). The uniform-in-`n` modulus that drives equicontinuity: as `вҖ–fвҖ–_{LВІ(P)}` shrinks, the tail shrinks at a rate independent of sample size. |

`Modulus.lean` вҖ” the class-level predicate and the reduction:

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `AsymptoticEquicont ПҲ ОёвӮҖ P Ој S` | `def`, `Prop` | **Class-level asymptotic equicontinuity** (the Donsker content, independent of any estimator): `вҲҖ Оө>0, вҲҖ О·>0, вҲғ Оҙ>0, вҲҖб¶  n, Ој {Пү | вҲғ Оё, вҖ–ОёвҲ’ОёвӮҖвҖ–<Оҙ вҲ§ Оө < вҖ–GвӮҷ(ПҲ(Оё,В·)вҲ’ПҲ(ОёвӮҖ,В·))(Пү)вҖ–} вүӨ О·`. Existential over `Оё` (not a non-measurable `sup`) so outer-measure bookkeeping is automatic. |
| `empProcVec_atEstimator_tendsto_zero` | `AsymptoticEquicont` + `Оёn вҶ’_p ОёвӮҖ` вҮ’ `GвӮҷ(ПҲ(Оёn,В·)вҲ’ПҲ(ОёвӮҖ,В·)) вҶ’_p 0` (unconditional) | The empirical process at the estimator vanishes вҖ” textbook union bound (split on `{вҖ–ОёnвҲ’ОёвӮҖвҖ–вүҘОҙ}` via consistency; on the complement the gap is witnessed by `Оё=Оёn`). |
| `stochEquicontAt_of_asymptoticEquicont` | `AsymptoticEquicont` + `Оёn вҶ’_p ОёвӮҖ` вҮ’ `StochEquicontAt ПҲ ОёвӮҖ P Ој S Оёn` | **The reduction.** Discharges the estimator-indexed `StochEquicontAt` hypothesis of `zEstimator_clt`/`gmm_asymptotically_linear` from the clean class-level Donsker property plus consistency. |
| `empProcVec_isLittleOp_of_L2` | deterministic `Оёn` with `вҲ« вҖ–ПҲ(Оёn,В·)вҲ’ПҲ(ОёвӮҖ,В·)вҖ–ВІ dP вҶ’ 0` вҮ’ gap is `o_p(1)` | **Deterministic-curve equicontinuity** (non-vacuousness witness): direct from `empProcVec_chebyshev`, no chaining, no consistency. The base case certifying the `AsymptoticEquicont` machinery is satisfiable. |

`MaximalInequality.lean` вҖ” sub-exponential maximal inequality (chaining primitive):

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.measure_abs_ge_le` | `Ој.real {Оө вүӨ \|X\|} вүӨ 2 exp(вҲ’ОөВІ/(2(v+bОө)))` | Two-sided sub-exponential tail, from the one-sided `measure_ge_le` applied to `X` and `вҲ’X`. |
| `measure_exists_abs_ge_le` | finite family вҮ’ `Ој.real {вҲғ iвҲҲt, Оө вүӨ \|Yбөў\|} вүӨ t.card В· 2 exp(вҲ’ОөВІ/(2(v+bОө)))` | **Maximal inequality** for the max of finitely many mean-zero sub-exponential variables (union bound). The reusable primitive a bracketing/chaining maximal inequality is built from. |
| `measure_sup'_ge_le` | same bound for `Ој.real {Оө вүӨ t.sup' (fun i => \|Yбөў\|)}` | `sup'` form of the maximal inequality. |

**Remaining frontier.** Discharging `AsymptoticEquicont` for an *infinite* class
over a continuum of `Оё` (genuine bracketing-entropy / Ossiander chaining, van der
Vaart 1998 Lemma 19.34) is the real Mathlib gap вҖ” the `вҲҡn` bracketing bias is
intrinsic and cannot be tamed without chaining (finite classes are trivially
equicontinuous). `MaximalInequality.lean` provides the single-scale primitive for
that future chaining argument.

### 12ep.e `Stat/EmpiricalProcess/CrossFitRate.lean` вҖ” conditional / cross-fit `O_p` rate

Paper-agnostic workhorse behind every cross-fit / DML "remainder-is-negligible"
step: the centered empirical mean of a (possibly cross-fit / conditional) score is
`O_p(n^{-1/2} В· (second-moment)^{1/2})`, stated over an arbitrary score and
Пғ-field. Reuses the existing `O_p` predicate `Causalean.Stat.IsBigOp` (no second
predicate) and the conditional second-moment engine
`Causalean.Mathlib.iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le`. The Markov "LВІ-envelope вҮ’ `O_p`"
step (previously inlined privately in `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DMLCrossFit.lean`)
is extracted here as the reusable primitive. All declarations **proved, sorry-free,
axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsBigOp.mono_rate` | `0 вүӨ rвӮҷ вүӨ sвӮҷ`, `O_p(rвӮҷ)` вҮ’ `O_p(sвӮҷ)` | **Weaken to a larger rate** вҖ” a larger envelope is a weaker statement. |
| `IsBigOp.scale_rate` | `c>0`, `O_p(cВ·rвӮҷ)` вҮ’ `O_p(rвӮҷ)` | **Absorb a positive constant rate factor** into the witness `M`. |
| `IsBigOp.const_rate_collapse` | `0 вүӨ N`, `O_p(fun _ => N)` вҮ’ `O_p(fun _ => 1)` | **Collapse a fixed constant rate to the unit rate** (normalizes fold-sum `O_p(1)` conclusions). |
| `IsBigOp.const_mul` | `O_p(rвӮҷ)` вҮ’ `cВ·XвӮҷ = O_p(rвӮҷ)` | **Constant multiple** of the random sequence keeps the rate. |
| `IsBigOp.add'` | `O_p(rвӮҷ) + O_p(sвӮҷ) = O_p(rвӮҷ+sвӮҷ)` (nonneg rates) | **Additivity at the sum rate** (`IsBigOp.add` is the `rвӮҷ=sвӮҷ` special case). |
| `IsBigOp.of_abs_le` | pointwise `\|XвӮҷ\| вүӨ \|YвӮҷ\|`, `YвӮҷ = O_p(rвӮҷ)` вҮ’ `XвӮҷ = O_p(rвӮҷ)` | **Domination rule** for stochastic big-O. |
| `IsBigOp.zero` | `0 = O_p(rвӮҷ)` | Constant-zero sequence is stochastically bounded at any rate. |
| `IsBigOp.finset_sum` | finite sum of terms each `O_p(rвӮҷ)` вҮ’ sum is `O_p(rвӮҷ)` | Finite-sum bookkeeping for stochastic big-O; constants absorb the finite index set. |
| `IsBigOp.mul` | `0вүӨrвӮҷ`, `0вүӨsвӮҷ`, `XвӮҷ=O_p(rвӮҷ)`, `YвӮҷ=O_p(sвӮҷ)` вҮ’ `XвӮҷYвӮҷ=O_p(rвӮҷsвӮҷ)` | **Product rule** for stochastic big-O at two nonnegative rates. |
| `IsBigOp.of_sq_lintegral_le` | `XвӮҷ` a.e.-meas., `0 вүӨ VвӮҷ`, `вҲ«вҒ» (XвӮҷ)ВІ dОј вүӨ VвӮҷ` вҮ’ `XвӮҷ = O_p(вҲҡVвӮҷ)` | **The Markov primitive.** A deterministic second-moment envelope yields the matching `O_p` rate (Chebyshev: `Ој{MвҲҡVвӮҷ<\|XвӮҷ\|} вүӨ 1/MВІ`). The reusable core every cross-fit rate proof consumes. |
| `isBigOp_centered_crossFit_sum` | eval set `s n` вҠҘ sub-Пғ-field `m_A n`, conditionally iid; score `g n` `(m_A n вҠ— Пғ_X)`-measurable & `LВІ(P)`; deterministic `VвӮҷ вүҘ вҲ«_О© вҖ–g n ПүвҖ–ВІ_{LВІ(P)} dОј` вҮ’ centered fold sum `= O_p(вҲҡVвӮҷ)` | **Lemma B вҖ” the conditional / cross-fit lift** (the deliverable). `вҲ« g n Пү dP = рқ”ј[g\|m_A n](Пү)` is the conditional mean (eval fold вҠҘ `m_A n`), so the sum is `вҲҡ\|s n\|В·(в„ҷвӮҷg вҲ’ рқ”ј[g\|m_A n])`. Second moment via the engine, rate via `of_sq_lintegral_le`. Estimand-agnostic; weak-overlap clipped-AIPW is the first consumer. |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_sub_sq_lintegral_le` | iid sample, `f вҲҲ LВІ(P)`, `0<n` вҮ’ `вҲ«вҒ» (в„ҷвӮҷf вҲ’ вҲ«f dP)ВІ dОј вүӨ E_P[fВІ]/n` | **Lemma A second-moment bound.** `(1/вҲҡn)ОЈ(f(Zбөў)вҲ’m) = вҲҡn(в„ҷвӮҷfвҲ’m)`, so the engine bound divides by `n`. |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_sub_meas_ge_le` | `0<n`, `t>0` вҮ’ `Ој{t вүӨ \|в„ҷвӮҷf вҲ’ вҲ«f dP\|} вүӨ E_P[fВІ]/(nВ·tВІ)` | **Chebyshev tail** for the centered sample mean (second-moment bound + Markov). |
| `IIDSample.sampleMean_sub_isBigOp` | iid sample, `f вҲҲ LВІ(P)` вҮ’ `(в„ҷвӮҷf вҲ’ вҲ«f dP) = O_p(вҲҡ(E_P[fВІ]/n))` | **Lemma A вҖ” the unconditional `O_p` rate.** Feeding the centered statistic `fвҲ’вҲ«f` (whose `E_P[(В·)ВІ]=Var_P f`) gives the sharp `O_p(вҲҡ(Var_P f/n))` form. The `(в„ҷвӮҷвҲ’P)ПҶ^bd` consumer. |

## 12x. `Stat/UStatistic/` вҖ” U-statistics: Hoeffding decomposition, exact variance, and the CLT

The reusable statistical primitive behind variance estimators, rank/sign statistics,
and the higher-order influence-function estimators (В§HOIF). The development has two
layers with a one-way bridge:

- **Order-`m` (the general theory).** `OrderM/` builds the fixed-order U-statistic on
  ordered injective `m`-tuples, its HГЎjek expansion, the exact order-`m` second moment,
  and вҖ” the **headline** вҖ” the fixed-order central limit theorem
  `uStatisticOrder_clt_of_regular` (`OrderM/CLT.lean`): a regular order-`m` U-statistic
  is `вҲҡn`-asymptotically normal with the HГЎjek variance `вҲ« ПҲВІ dP`. Its one genuinely
  probabilistic input, negligibility of the higher-order remainder, is discharged from
  a first-order-degeneracy `LВІ вүӨ C/n` keystone (`OrderM/FirstDegenKernel.lean` вҶ’
  `OrderM/RemainderSecondMoment.lean` вҶ’ `OrderM/RemainderNegligible.lean`).
- **Order-2 (a thin `m = 2` face).** `Basic.lean`/`Variance.lean` keep the natural
  pairwise-kernel API (`h : X вҶ’ X вҶ’ в„қ`, off-diagonal `uStatistic`, `DegenKernel`, `О¶`)
  that the HOIF estimators consume. The order-2 *variance* now routes through the
  order-`m` `ExactVariance` internally (via `pairKernel`); the order-2 CLT is recovered
  as the `m = 2` specialization `uStatistic_clt_of_symmetric_via_orderM`
  (`OrderM/OrderTwo.lean`).

_Per-declaration tables below are generated from docstrings вҖ” do not hand-edit; run `npm run doc:gen`. Edit the Lean docstring to change a description._

### 12x.1. `Stat/UStatistic/Basic.lean` вҖ” order-2 Hoeffding decomposition

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.Basic -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.uStatistic` | `(X вҶ’ X вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„• вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ` | The order-2 U-statistic with kernel `h` over the first `n` sample points: `(n(nвҲ’1))вҒ»В№ ОЈ_{(i,j) вҲҲ offDiag (range n)} h(Zбөў, Zвұј)`. The off-diagonal sum ranges over ordered pairs `i вү  j`; for a symmetric kernel this is the usual `(n choose 2)вҒ»В№ ОЈ_{i<j}`. |
| `Stat.uMean` | `(X вҶ’ X вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Measure X вҶ’ в„қ` | Population mean of the kernel: `Оё = вҲ¬ h dP dP`. |
| `Stat.uProj` | `(X вҶ’ X вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Measure X вҶ’ X вҶ’ в„қ` | First Hoeffding projection (centered): `hвӮҒ(x) = вҲ« h(x, В·) dP вҲ’ Оё`. |
| `Stat.uDegen` | `(X вҶ’ X вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Measure X вҶ’ X вҶ’ X вҶ’ в„қ` | Degenerate second-order kernel: `g(x, y) = h(x, y) вҲ’ Оё вҲ’ hвӮҒ(x) вҲ’ hвӮҒ(y)`. |
| `Stat.hoeffding_decomp` | `h x y = Causalean.Stat.uMean h P + Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x + Causalean.Stat.uProj h P y + Causalean.Stat.uDegen h P x y` | Hoeffding decomposition (pointwise identity). `h(x, y) = Оё + hвӮҒ(x) + hвӮҒ(y) + g(x, y)`. Purely algebraic. |
| `Stat.uProj_integral_eq_zero` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => вҲ« (y : X), h x y вҲӮP) P вҶ’ вҲ« (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x вҲӮP = 0` | The first projection integrates to zero: `вҲ« hвӮҒ dP = 0`. |
| `Stat.uProj_integrable` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => вҲ« (y : X), h x y вҲӮP) P вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (Causalean.Stat.uProj h P) P` | The first projection is integrable whenever `x вҶҰ вҲ« h(x, В·) dP` is. |
| `Stat.uDegen_integral_right_eq_zero` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => вҲ« (y : X), h x y вҲӮP) P вҶ’ вҲҖ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P вҶ’ вҲ« (y : X), Causalean.Stat.uDegen h P x y вҲӮP = 0` | Degeneracy of the second-order kernel. For every fixed `x`, `вҲ« g(x, В·) dP = 0`. This is the defining property that makes the U-statistic's quadratic remainder negligible at the `вҲҡn` scale. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.3. `Stat/UStatistic/Variance.lean` вҖ” order-2 exact variance (thin `m = 2` face for HOIF)

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.Variance -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.DegenKernel` | `MeasureTheory.Measure X вҶ’ (X вҶ’ X вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Prop` | Bundled hypotheses on a degenerate order-2 kernel `g`. |
| `Stat.DegenKernel.integrable` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => g p.1 p.2) (P.prod P)` | `g` is LВ№ on the product law (probability space, from LВІ). |
| `Stat.DegenKernel.deg'` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g вҶ’ вҲҖ (y : X), вҲ« (x : X), g x y вҲӮP = 0` | Left degeneracy, from symmetry and right degeneracy. |
| `Stat.DegenKernel.toOrderDegenKernel` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel g)` | Bridge to the order-`m` theory (`m = 2`). A degenerate order-2 kernel `g` gives a completely-degenerate order-`2` kernel `pairKernel g` (`z вҶҰ g (z 0) (z 1)`): symmetry of `pairKernel g` under `Equiv.Perm (Fin 2)` is `hg.symm`; the single-coordinate integrals are `hg.deg` / `hg.deg'`; square-integrability transports across the `Fin 2 вҶ’ X` вүғ `X Г— X` product-law equivalence. |

| `Stat.IIDSample.zeta_eq_zetaOrder` | `Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zeta P g = Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel g)` | The order-2 second moment `О¶` equals the order-`m` second moment `О¶_m` of the paired kernel: `вҲ¬ gВІ dP dP = вҲ« (pairKernel g)ВІ dPВІ`. A change of variables along the `Fin 2 вҶ’ X` вүғ `X Г— X` measure equivalence. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.zeta_nonneg` | `0 вүӨ Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zeta P g` | `О¶ вүҘ 0`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_offDiag_sum_sq` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g вҶ’ вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҲ« (Пү : О©), (вҲ‘ p вҲҲ (Finset.range n).offDiag, g (S.Z p.1 Пү) (S.Z p.2 Пү)) ^ 2 вҲӮОј = 2 * вҶ‘(Finset.range n).offDiag.card * Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zeta P g` | Second moment of the off-diagonal sum. `E[(ОЈ_{p вҲҲ offDiag} g(Z_{p.1},Z_{p.2}))ВІ] = 2 В· \|offDiag\| В· О¶`. The `m = 2` case of `integral_injectiveTuples_sum_sq_degen`, using `sum_injectiveTuples_two_eq_offDiag`, `toOrderDegenKernel`, and `zeta_eq_zetaOrder` (`2! = 2`, `injectiveTupleCount 2 n = \|offDiag\| = n(nвҲ’1)`). |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_sq` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g вҶ’ вҲҖ {n : в„•}, 2 вүӨ n вҶ’ вҲ« (Пү : О©), (вҲҡвҶ‘n * Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S g n Пү) ^ 2 вҲӮОј = 2 * Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zeta P g / (вҶ‘n - 1)` | LВІ bound on the rescaled degenerate U-statistic. For `n вүҘ 2`, `E[(вҲҡn В· GвӮҷ)ВІ] = 2О¶ / (nвҲ’1)`. The `m = 2` case of `integral_rescaled_order_sq_degen` (`n В· 2! В· О¶ / n^{(2)} = 2О¶/(nвҲ’1)`), via `uStatisticOrder_two_eq_uStatistic` and `zeta_eq_zetaOrder`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.memLp_rescaled` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g вҶ’ вҲҖ (n : в„•), MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun Пү => вҲҡвҶ‘n * Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S g n Пү) 2 Ој` | The rescaled degenerate U-statistic is in `LВІ`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_eq_zero` | `Causalean.Stat.DegenKernel P g вҶ’ вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҲ« (Пү : О©), вҲҡвҶ‘n * Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S g n Пү вҲӮОј = 0` | The rescaled degenerate U-statistic has mean zero. |

### 12x.4. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/Basic.lean` вҖ” fixed-order U-statistic on injective tuples

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.Basic -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.injectiveTuples` | `(m n : в„•) вҶ’ Finset (Fin m вҶ’ Fin n)` | Ordered injective `m`-tuples from the first `n` sample indices. |
| `Stat.injectiveTupleCount` | `в„• вҶ’ в„• вҶ’ в„қ` | The number of ordered injective `m`-tuples from the first `n` sample indices. |
| `Stat.injectiveSubtypeEquivEmbedding` | `(m n : в„•) вҶ’ { t // Function.Injective t } вүғ (Fin m вҶӘ Fin n)` | Injective functions `Fin m вҶ’ Fin n` as elements of the finite embedding type. |
| `Stat.injectiveTuples_card_eq_descFactorial` | `(Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n).card = n.descFactorial m` | The ordered injective tuple count is the falling factorial `n (n-1) ...`. |
| `Stat.injectiveTupleCount_eq_descFactorial` | `Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n = вҶ‘(n.descFactorial m)` | The real-valued ordered injective tuple count is the falling factorial. |
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder` | `{m : в„•} вҶ’ ((Fin m вҶ’ X) вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„• вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ` | The fixed-order U-statistic averages a kernel over ordered injective sample tuples from the first `n` observations. |
| `Stat.uMeanOrder` | `((Fin m вҶ’ X) вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Measure X вҶ’ в„қ` | Population mean of an order-`m` kernel under the product law. |
| `Stat.insertCoord` | `(j : Fin m) вҶ’ X вҶ’ ({ k // k вү  j } вҶ’ X) вҶ’ Fin m вҶ’ X` | Insert one distinguished coordinate into the remaining coordinates. |

| `Stat.uProjOrder` | `((Fin m вҶ’ X) вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Measure X вҶ’ X вҶ’ в„қ` | First Hoeffding projection of a positive-order kernel, using coordinate `0` as the distinguished coordinate. |

| `Stat.uProjOrderAt_integral_eq_zero` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => вҲ« (tail : { k // k вү  j } вҶ’ X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) вҲӮMeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) P вҶ’ вҲ« (x : X), вҲ« (tail : { k // k вү  j } вҶ’ X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) вҲӮMeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P вҲӮP = Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P вҶ’ вҲ« (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P x вҲӮP = 0` | The coordinatewise first projection integrates to zero once the slice-averaged representation of the population mean is available. The additional equality is the standard finite-product/Fubini identity for the chosen coordinate. |

| `Stat.uInfluenceOrder_integral_eq_zero` | `(вҲҖ (j : Fin m), MeasureTheory.Integrable (Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P) P) вҶ’ (вҲҖ (j : Fin m), вҲ« (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P x вҲӮP = 0) вҶ’ вҲ« (x : X), вҲ‘ j, Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P x вҲӮP = 0` | The summed first-order influence function is centered if every coordinatewise first projection is centered. |
| `Stat.integral_pi_eval_eq` | `MeasureTheory.Integrable f P вҶ’ (вҲ« (z : О№ вҶ’ X), f (z i) вҲӮMeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) = вҲ« (x : X), f x вҲӮP` | Integrating a function of one coordinate under a finite product law recovers the one-dimensional integral. |

|---|---|---|

| `Stat.uInfluenceOrder_eq_card_mul_of_common_projection` | `(вҲҖ (j : Fin m) (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j h P x = ПҶ x) вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P = fun x => вҶ‘m * ПҶ x` | If all coordinatewise first projections agree with a common projection, then the order-`m` influence function is `m` times that projection. |
| `Stat.uRemainderOrder` | `{m : в„•} вҶ’ [NeZero m] вҶ’ ((Fin m вҶ’ X) вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„• вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ` | The higher-order remainder statistic for an order-`m` U-statistic. |
| `Stat.OrderDegenKernel` | `MeasureTheory.Measure X вҶ’ {m : в„•} вҶ’ [NeZero m] вҶ’ ((Fin m вҶ’ X) вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Prop` | A fully degenerate order-`m` kernel under product measure `P^m`. |
| `Stat.OrderDegenerateNegligible` | `{m : в„•} вҶ’ [NeZero m] вҶ’ ((Fin m вҶ’ X) вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Prop` | The higher-order order-`m` HГЎjek remainder is negligible at the `вҲҡn` scale. |
| `Stat.injectiveTupleCount_ne_zero` | `m вүӨ n вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n вү  0` | If `m вүӨ n`, there is at least one ordered injective `m`-tuple in `Fin n`. |
| `Stat.injectiveTuples_fiber_card_eq` | `{t вҲҲ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n \| t j = y}.card = {t вҲҲ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n \| t j = y'}.card` | Fibres of a fixed coordinate map on ordered injective tuples have the same cardinality. The bijection composes tuples with the codomain transposition swapping the two fibre values. |
| `Stat.sum_injectiveTuples_apply_eq_range` | `m вүӨ n вҶ’ вҲҖ (j : Fin m) (f : в„• вҶ’ в„қ), вҲ‘ t вҲҲ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n, f вҶ‘(t j) = Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n / вҶ‘n * вҲ‘ i вҲҲ Finset.range n, f i` | For each coordinate of an ordered injective tuple, every sample index appears equally often. |
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_sub_uMean_eq` | `m вүӨ n вҶ’ вҲҖ (Пү : О©), Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h n Пү - Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P = (вҶ‘n)вҒ»В№ * вҲ‘ i вҲҲ Finset.range n, Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P (S.Z i Пү) + Causalean.Stat.uRemainderOrder S h n Пү` | HГЎjek decomposition for a fixed-order U-statistic. For positive order `m` and sample size `n вүҘ m`, the centered statistic equals its linear HГЎjek projection plus the higher-order residual U-statistic. |
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_remainder_eq` | `m вүӨ n вҶ’ (fun Пү => вҲҡвҶ‘(Finset.range n).card * (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h n Пү - Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) - (вҲҡвҶ‘(Finset.range n).card)вҒ»В№ * вҲ‘ i вҲҲ Finset.range n, Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P (S.Z i Пү)) = fun Пү => вҲҡвҶ‘n * Causalean.Stat.uRemainderOrder S h n Пү` | The HГЎjek remainder in `IsAsymLinear` form is the rescaled higher-order residual U-statistic. |
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_isAsymLinear` | `вҲ« (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x вҲӮP = 0 вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2) P вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenerateNegligible S h вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) S fun r => Finset.range r` | Fixed-order U-statistic asymptotic linearity. If the summed first projection is centered and square-integrable, and the higher-order residual is negligible at the `вҲҡn` scale, then the fixed-order U-statistic is asymptotically linear with influence function equal to the sum of coordinatewise first projections. |

### 12x.7. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/ExactVariance.lean` вҖ” exact order-`m` second moment

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.ExactVariance -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.IIDSample.crossterm_eq_zeta_of_image_eq` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g вҶ’ вҲҖ {n : в„•} {t q : Fin m вҶ’ Fin n}, Function.Injective t вҶ’ Function.Injective q вҶ’ Finset.image t Finset.univ = Finset.image q Finset.univ вҶ’ вҲ« (Пү : О©), (g fun j => S.Z (вҶ‘(t j)) Пү) * g fun j => S.Z (вҶ‘(q j)) Пү вҲӮОј = Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P g` | Equal-image cross term. If two ordered injective `m`-tuples have the same image, then `q` is a reordering of `t`, so by symmetry `g(Z_q) = g(Z_t)` and the cross expectation is `О¶_m = zetaOrder P g`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.crossterm_eq_zero_of_image_ne` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g вҶ’ вҲҖ {n : в„•} {t q : Fin m вҶ’ Fin n}, Function.Injective t вҶ’ Function.Injective q вҶ’ Finset.image t Finset.univ вү  Finset.image q Finset.univ вҶ’ вҲ« (Пү : О©), (g fun j => S.Z (вҶ‘(t j)) Пү) * g fun j => S.Z (вҶ‘(q j)) Пү вҲӮОј = 0` | Distinct-image cross term. If two ordered injective `m`-tuples have different images, complete degeneracy kills the cross expectation: some index of `t` is absent from `q`, and integrating that coordinate out gives `0`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.card_injectiveTuples_image_eq` | `Function.Injective t вҶ’ {q вҲҲ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n \| Finset.image q Finset.univ = Finset.image t Finset.univ}.card = m.factorial` | For an ordered injective `m`-tuple `t`, the ordered injective tuples with the same image are exactly its `m!` reorderings. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_injectiveTuples_sum_sq_degen` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g вҶ’ вҲҖ (n : в„•), вҲ« (Пү : О©), (вҲ‘ t вҲҲ Causalean.Stat.injectiveTuples m n, g fun j => S.Z (вҶ‘(t j)) Пү) ^ 2 вҲӮОј = вҶ‘m.factorial * Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n * Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P g` | Exact second moment of the injective-tuple sum. `E[(ОЈ_{t} g(Z_t))ВІ] = m! В· n^{(m)} В· О¶_m`. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_order_sq_degen` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenKernel P g вҶ’ вҲҖ {n : в„•}, m вүӨ n вҶ’ вҲ« (Пү : О©), (вҲҡвҶ‘n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n Пү) ^ 2 вҲӮОј = вҶ‘n * вҶ‘m.factorial * Causalean.Stat.IIDSample.zetaOrder P g / Causalean.Stat.injectiveTupleCount m n` | Exact variance of the rescaled degenerate fixed-order U-statistic. `E[(вҲҡn В· UвӮҷ)ВІ] = n В· m! В· О¶_m / n^{(m)}`. For `m = 2` this is `2О¶ / (nвҲ’1)`. |
