### 12x.9. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/RemainderSecondMoment.lean` вҖ” keystone `LВІ вүӨ C/n` bound

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.RemainderSecondMoment -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.IIDSample.crossterm_eq_zero_of_shared_le_one` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g вҶ’ вҲҖ {n : в„•} {t q : Fin m вҶ’ Fin n}, Function.Injective t вҶ’ Function.Injective q вҶ’ (Finset.image t Finset.univ вҲ© Finset.image q Finset.univ).card вүӨ 1 вҶ’ вҲ« (Пү : О©), (g fun j => S.Z (вҶ‘(t j)) Пү) * g fun j => S.Z (вҶ‘(q j)) Пү вҲӮОј = 0` | Cross-term vanishing. If two ordered injective `m`-tuples share at most one sample index, the expected product of the corresponding kernel terms is zero. Zero shared indices вҮ’ independence + mean zero; one shared index вҮ’ condition on it and use first-order degeneracy of each factor. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_order_sq_le` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g вҶ’ вҲғ C, 0 вүӨ C вҲ§ вҲҖ {n : в„•}, m вүӨ n вҶ’ вҲ« (Пү : О©), (вҲҡвҶ‘n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n Пү) ^ 2 вҲӮОј вүӨ C / вҶ‘n` | `LВІ` bound on the rescaled higher-order remainder. For a first-order degenerate order-`m` kernel there is a constant `C` (depending only on `m` and `О¶_m = E[gВІ]`) with `E[(вҲҡn В· UвӮҷ)ВІ] вүӨ C / n` for all `n вүҘ m`. This is the keystone estimate; it packages cross-term vanishing, the CauchyвҖ“Schwarz bound `\|E[g_t g_q]\| вүӨ О¶_m`, the `O(n^{2m-2})` count of surviving tuple pairs, and the `n В· (n^{(m)})вҒ»ВІ` normalization. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.10. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/RemainderNegligible.lean` вҖ” negligibility discharge

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.RemainderNegligible -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.IIDSample.integral_rescaled_order_eq_zero` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g вҶ’ вҲҖ {n : в„•}, m вүӨ n вҶ’ вҲ« (Пү : О©), вҲҡвҶ‘n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n Пү вҲӮОј = 0` | The rescaled higher-order remainder has mean zero. |
| `Stat.IIDSample.orderDegenerateNegligible_of_firstDegen` | `Causalean.Stat.OrderFirstDegenKernel P g вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.IsLittleOp (fun n Пү => вҲҡвҶ‘n * Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S g n Пү) (fun x => 1) Ој` | Negligibility of the higher-order remainder. Under first-order degeneracy, `вҲҡn В· UвӮҷ вҶ’ 0` in probability, i.e. it is `o_p(1)`. Proof: `LВІ` boundedness (`memLp_rescaled_sqKernel`) with mean zero and variance `вүӨ C/n вҶ’ 0` (`integral_rescaled_order_sq_le`), via Chebyshev вҖ” mirror the order-2 `degenerateNegligible_of_degenKernel`. |
| `Stat.orderDegenerateNegligible_of_residual` | `Measurable (Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P z ^ 2) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) вҶ’ (вҲҖ (j : Fin m), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => вҲ« (tail : { k // k вү  j } вҶ’ X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) вҲӮMeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) P) вҶ’ (вҲҖ (j : Fin m), вҲ« (x : X), вҲ« (tail : { k // k вү  j } вҶ’ X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) вҲӮMeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P вҲӮP = Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) вҶ’ (вҲҖ (j : Fin m) (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun tail => h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail)) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P)) вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenerateNegligible S h` | The higher-order remainder of a fixed-order U-statistic is negligible. For an order-`m` kernel `h` whose residual `uDegenOrder h P` is measurable and square-integrable and whose slice/Fubini integrability side conditions hold, the rescaled remainder `вҲҡn В· GвӮҷ` is `o_p(1)`. This discharges the `OrderDegenerateNegligible` hypothesis consumed by the order-`m` CLT `uStatisticOrder_clt` (`Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.CLT`). |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.11. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/CLT.lean` вҖ” headline: fixed-order U-statistic CLT

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Stat.UStatistic.OrderM.CLT -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_clt` | `Measurable (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) вҶ’ вҲ« (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x вҲӮP = 0 вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2) P вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.OrderDegenerateNegligible S h вҶ’ вҲҖ (hОёn_meas : вҲҖ (n : в„•), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) (fun r => Finset.range r) n) Ој), (вҲҖ (n : в„•), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.normalizedSum S (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) (fun r => Finset.range r) n) Ој) вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.Tendsto_dist (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) fun r => Finset.range r) (Causalean.Stat.gaussianMeasure 0 (вҲ« (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2 вҲӮP)) Ој hОёn_meas` | Fixed-order U-statistic CLT. If the first-order influence function is measurable, centered, square-integrable, and the higher-order residual is negligible at the `вҲҡn` scale, then the U-statistic is asymptotically normal with variance `вҲ« ПҲВІ dP`, where `ПҲ` is the sum of coordinatewise first projections. |
| `Stat.uStatisticOrder_clt_of_regular` | `Measurable (Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun z => Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder h P z ^ 2) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) вҶ’ (вҲҖ (j : Fin m), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => вҲ« (tail : { k // k вү  j } вҶ’ X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) вҲӮMeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P) P) вҶ’ (вҲҖ (j : Fin m), вҲ« (x : X), вҲ« (tail : { k // k вү  j } вҶ’ X), h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail) вҲӮMeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P вҲӮP = Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) вҶ’ (вҲҖ (j : Fin m) (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun tail => h (Causalean.Stat.insertCoord j x tail)) (MeasureTheory.Measure.pi fun x => P)) вҶ’ Measurable (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) вҶ’ вҲ« (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x вҲӮP = 0 вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2) P вҶ’ вҲҖ (hОёn_meas : вҲҖ (n : в„•), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) (fun r => Finset.range r) n) Ој), (вҲҖ (n : в„•), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.normalizedSum S (Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P) (fun r => Finset.range r) n) Ој) вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.Tendsto_dist (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatisticOrder S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMeanOrder h P) fun r => Finset.range r) (Causalean.Stat.gaussianMeasure 0 (вҲ« (x : X), Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder h P x ^ 2 вҲӮP)) Ој hОёn_meas` | Fixed-order U-statistic CLT (end-to-end). For an order-`m` kernel `h` whose residual is regular (measurable, square-integrable, with the slice/Fubini side conditions) and whose summed first projection is measurable, centered, and square-integrable, the fixed-order U-statistic is `вҲҡn`-asymptotically normal with variance `вҲ« ПҲВІ dP`, `ПҲ = ОЈвұј uProjOrderAt j h P`. This composes `uStatisticOrder_clt` with `orderDegenerateNegligible_of_residual`, so no negligibility hypothesis is required from the caller вҖ” the order-`m` analogue of `uStatistic_clt_of_symmetric`. |
<!-- /GEN -->

### 12x.12. `Stat/UStatistic/OrderM/OrderTwo.lean` вҖ” `m = 2` bridge to the order-2 CLT

|---|---|---|

Requires product-integrability of `h` so that the joint integral over `Fin 2 вҶ’ X` agrees (via Fubini) with the iterated integral defining `uMean`. |
| `Stat.uProjOrderAt_pairKernel_of_symm` | `(вҲҖ (x y : X), h x y = h y x) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => h p.1 p.2) (P.prod P) вҶ’ (вҲҖ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P) вҶ’ вҲҖ (j : Fin 2), Causalean.Stat.uProjOrderAt j (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h) P = Causalean.Stat.uProj h P` | Each coordinate first projection of `pairKernel h` equals the order-2 first projection of `h`, for a symmetric kernel. | `Stat.uInfluenceOrder_pairKernel_of_symm` | `(вҲҖ (x y : X), h x y = h y x) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => h p.1 p.2) (P.prod P) вҶ’ (вҲҖ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P) вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.uInfluenceOrder (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h) P = fun x => 2 * Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x` | The order-`2` influence function of `pairKernel h` is `2 В· hвӮҒ`, matching the order-2 CLT influence function `ПҲ = 2 В· uProj h P`. |
| `Stat.uDegenOrder_pairKernel_of_symm` | `(вҲҖ (x y : X), h x y = h y x) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => h p.1 p.2) (P.prod P) вҶ’ (вҲҖ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P) вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.uDegenOrder (Causalean.Stat.pairKernel h) P = fun z => Causalean.Stat.uDegen h P (z 0) (z 1)` | The order-`2` degenerate residual of `pairKernel h` is the order-2 degenerate kernel `uDegen h P`, evaluated at the two coordinates. |
| `Stat.uStatistic_clt_of_symmetric_via_orderM` | `(вҲҖ (x y : X), h x y = h y x) вҶ’ (Measurable fun p => h p.1 p.2) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun p => Causalean.Stat.uDegen h P p.1 p.2 ^ 2) (P.prod P) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => вҲ« (y : X), h x y вҲӮP) P вҶ’ (вҲҖ (x : X), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => h x y) P) вҶ’ MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x ^ 2) P вҶ’ вҲҖ (hОёn_meas : вҲҖ (n : в„•), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMean h P) (fun m => Finset.range m) n) Ој), (вҲҖ (n : в„•), AEMeasurable (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.normalizedSum S (fun x => 2 * Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x) (fun m => Finset.range m) n) Ој) вҶ’ Causalean.Stat.Tendsto_dist (Causalean.Stat.IsAsymLinear.rescaledEstimator (Causalean.Stat.uStatistic S h) (Causalean.Stat.uMean h P) fun m => Finset.range m) (Causalean.Stat.gaussianMeasure 0 (вҲ« (x : X), (fun x => 2 * Causalean.Stat.uProj h P x) x ^ 2 вҲӮP)) Ој hОёn_meas` | Order-2 U-statistic CLT, derived from the fixed-order-`m` CLT. For a symmetric, square-integrable kernel `h` with finite first-projection variance `О¶вӮҒ = вҲ« hвӮҒВІ dP`, the order-2 U-statistic is `вҲҡn`-asymptotically normal, `вҲҡn (UвӮҷ вҲ’ Оё) вҮ’ N(0, вҲ« (2 hвӮҒ)ВІ dP) = N(0, 4 О¶вӮҒ)`. |
<!-- /GEN -->

## 12wo. `Stat/PolynomialTail/` вҖ” Polynomial lower-tail inverse-moment asymptotics

Causal-agnostic real-analysis / measure-theory infrastructure for the small-value
(lower-tail) asymptotics of a bounded positive random variable.  This is the moment
calculus underlying *any* inverse-weighted estimand whose weight is floored at `1/О»` вҖ”
not tied to one paper.  It specializes to (and quantitatively refines) the qualitative
`Overlap`/positivity assumption in `Identification/`: taking `U = min(e(X), 1вҲ’e(X))`
recovers the variance/rate ingredients of IPW, AIPW, and HГЎjek estimators under thin
overlap, trimming/truncation bias, and weak-overlap minimax rates.  Fix a probability
measure `P` and a measurable `U : О© вҶ’ в„қ` with `0 < U вүӨ 1` a.s.  The two-sided
polynomial lower-tail hypothesis `PolyTail P U Оә tвӮҖ cm cp` says
`cmВ·tбөҸ вүӨ P{U вүӨ t} вүӨ cpВ·tбөҸ` on `(0,tвӮҖ]` (`0 < Оә`, `0 < tвӮҖ < 1`, `0 < cm < cp`).  The
library bounds the two truncated inverse moments (`О»`-truncation, `0 < О» вүӨ tвӮҖ`)

    I P U О» = вҲ« U/(max U О»)ВІ   (= 1/U on {UвүҘО»}, U/О»ВІ on {U<О»}),
    J P U О» = вҲ« (max U О»)вҒ»В№    (= 1/U on {UвүҘО»}, 1/О»  on {U<О»}),

and the leftover trimming weight `wО» = 1 вҲ’ U/(max U О») вҲҲ [0,1]` (supported on `{U<О»}`).
The key trichotomy is in the tail exponent `Оә`: `О»^{Оә-1}` (Оә<1), `log(1/О»)` (Оә=1),
bounded (Оә>1).  The lower bounds genuinely use **both** tail constants (the gap
`cm < cp` breaks any one-sided argument).  Method: Mathlib's layer-cake theorem
`MeasureTheory.Integrable.integral_eq_integral_Ioc_meas_le` reduces `J` to a
threshold integral of `P{U вүӨ tвҒ»В№}`, evaluated against `вҲ« t^{-Оә}` (`integral_rpow` /
`integral_inv`).  All declarations **sorry-free and axiom-clean**.

### 12wo.1. `Stat/PolynomialTail/Basic.lean` вҖ” setup, integrands, basic facts

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PolyTail P U Оә tвӮҖ cm cp` | `structure : Prop` | The polynomial lower-tail hypothesis: `cmВ·tбөҸ вүӨ P{UвүӨt} вүӨ cpВ·tбөҸ` on `(0,tвӮҖ]`, with `0<Оә`, `0<tвӮҖ<1`, `0<cm<cp`. Fields `kappa_pos/t0_pos/t0_lt_one/cm_pos/cm_lt_cp/tail_lower/tail_upper`. |
| `PolyTail.cp_pos` | `0 < cp` | Upper constant positive. |
| `TailSetup P U` | `structure : Prop` | Structural hypotheses on `U`: `Measurable U`, `0 < U` a.s., `U вүӨ 1` a.s. |
| `truncDen U О» Пү` | `def := max (U Пү) О»` | Truncated denominator. |
| `invMomentI P U О»` | `def := вҲ« U/(max U О»)ВІ` | Truncated inverse second moment. |
| `invMomentJ P U О»` | `def := вҲ« (max U О»)вҒ»В№` | Truncated inverse first moment. |
| `trimWeight U О» Пү` | `def := 1 вҲ’ U/(max U О»)` | Leftover weight `вҲҲ[0,1]`, supported on `{U<О»}`. |
| `measurable_*` | measurability | The three integrands and the denominator are measurable. |
| `measureReal_lt_le` | `P{U<О»} вүӨ cpВ·О»бөҸ` | Mass of the open lower level set (monotonicity + upper tail). |
| `invMomentI_integrand_mem` / `invMomentJ_integrand_mem` / `trimWeight_mem` | pointwise `вҲҲ[0,1/О»ВІ]` / `[0,1/О»]` / `[0,1]` | Pointwise integrand sandwiches. |
| `integrable_invMomentI_integrand` / `вҖҰJвҖҰ` / `integrable_trimWeight` | `Integrable вҖҰ P` | Integrability (bounded a.e. on a probability space). |

### 12wo.2. `Stat/PolynomialTail/LayerCakeReduction.lean` вҖ” layer-cake reduction

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `tailIntegral P U О»` | `def := вҲ« t in (1,О»вҒ»В№], P{U вүӨ tвҒ»В№}` | The threshold ("tail") integral. |
| `monotone_cdf` | `Monotone (s вҶҰ P{UвүӨs})` | The lower CDF is monotone. |
| `measurable_tailIntegrand`, `tailIntegrand_nonneg`, `tailIntegrand_le_one`, `integrableOn_tailIntegrand` | regularity | Measurability/`вҲҲ[0,1]`/integrability of `t вҶҰ P{UвүӨtвҒ»В№}`. |
| `invMomentJ_eq_one_add_tailIntegral` | `J P U О» = 1 + tailIntegral P U О»` (`0<О»<1`) | **Master layer-cake reduction.** Levels `tвүӨ1` contribute exactly `1` (`(max U О»)вҒ»В№ вүҘ 1` a.s.); levels `(1,О»вҒ»В№]` reproduce `P{UвүӨtвҒ»В№}`. |
| `tailIntegral_nonneg` | `0 вүӨ tailIntegral P U О»` | Nonnegativity. |

### 12wo.3. `Stat/PolynomialTail/PowerIntegral.lean` вҖ” power-law interval integrals

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `zero_notMem_uIcc` | `0 вҲү [[a,b]]` (`0<aвүӨb`) | Side condition for `integral_rpow`/`integral_inv`. |
| `integral_rpow_neg_Ioc` | `вҲ« t in (a,b], t^{-Оә} = (b^{1-Оә}вҲ’a^{1-Оә})/(1вҲ’Оә)` (`Оәвү 1`, `0<aвүӨb`) | Power integral via `integral_rpow`. |
| `integral_inv_neg_Ioc` | `вҲ« t in (a,b], t^{-1} = log b вҲ’ log a` (`0<aвүӨb`) | `Оә=1` case via `integral_inv`. |

### 12wo.4. `Stat/PolynomialTail/TailIntegralBounds.lean` вҖ” tail-integral sandwich

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `inv_rpow_eq_rpow_neg` | `(tвҒ»В№)^Оә = t^{-Оә}` (`t>0`) | rpow identity. |
| `tailIntegrand_upper_window` / `tailIntegrand_lower_window` | `cmВ·t^{-Оә} вүӨ P{UвүӨtвҒ»В№} вүӨ cpВ·t^{-Оә}` on `(tвӮҖвҒ»В№,О»вҒ»В№]` | Polynomial-window integrand sandwich. |
| `integrableOn_rpow_neg_window` | `IntegrableOn (t^{-Оә}) (tвӮҖвҒ»В№,О»вҒ»В№]` | Integrability of the power integrand. |
| `tailIntegral_le` | `tailIntegral вүӨ (tвӮҖвҒ»В№вҲ’1) + cpВ·вҲ«_{(tвӮҖвҒ»В№,О»вҒ»В№]} t^{-Оә}` | **Upper** sandwich (bounded shell `(1,tвӮҖвҒ»В№]` + polynomial window). |
| `tailIntegral_ge` | `cmВ·вҲ«_{(tвӮҖвҒ»В№,О»вҒ»В№]} t^{-Оә} вүӨ tailIntegral` | **Lower** sandwich. |

### 12wo.5. `Stat/PolynomialTail/MomentJBounds.lean` вҖ” three-regime bounds for `J`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `powInt_ne_one` | `вҲ«_{(tвӮҖвҒ»В№,О»вҒ»В№]} t^{-Оә} = (О»^{Оә-1}вҲ’tвӮҖ^{Оә-1})/(1вҲ’Оә)` (`Оәвү 1`) | Regime power-integral value. |
| `powInt_eq_one` | `вҖҰ = log(1/О») вҲ’ log(1/tвӮҖ)` (`Оә=1`) | Regime power-integral value. |
| `invMomentJ_bounds_lt` | `вҲғ 0<aвүӨA, вҲҖО»вҲҲ(0,tвӮҖ], aВ·О»^{Оә-1} вүӨ J вүӨ AВ·О»^{Оә-1}` (`Оә<1`) | **Оә<1.** Constants depend only on `(Оә,cm,cp,tвӮҖ)`: `A=tвӮҖвҒ»В№+cp/(1вҲ’Оә)`, `a=min(cm/(1вҲ’Оә), tвӮҖ^{1вҲ’Оә})`. |
| `invMomentJ_bounds_eq` | `вҲғ 0<aвүӨA, aВ·log(1/О») вүӨ J вүӨ AВ·log(1/О»)+A` (`Оә=1`) | **Оә=1.** |
| `invMomentJ_bounds_gt` | `вҲғ 0<aвүӨA, a вүӨ J вүӨ A` (`Оә>1`) | **Оә>1.** вҖ” inverse first moment is *bounded* (`a=1` since `JвүҘ1`). |

### 12wo.6. `Stat/PolynomialTail/MomentIBounds.lean` вҖ” bounds for `I` + IsTheta

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `invMomentI_le_invMomentJ` | `I P U О» вүӨ J P U О»` | Pointwise `U/(max U О»)ВІ вүӨ (max U О»)вҒ»В№`. Gives the **upper** half of the `I` bounds from the `J` bounds. |
| `invMomentJ_sub_invMomentI_le` | `J P U О» вҲ’ I P U О» вүӨ cpВ·О»^{Оә-1}` | Gap controlled by tail mass: pointwise `= wО»/(max U О») вүӨ О»вҒ»В№В·wО»`, and `вҲ«wО» вүӨ P{U<О»} вүӨ cpВ·О»бөҸ`. The bridge for the `Оә=1` `I`-lower bound. |
| `invMomentI_antitone` | `I P U О»вӮӮ вүӨ I P U О»вӮҒ` (`О»вӮҒвүӨО»вӮӮ`) | `I` decreases as `О»` grows (`max U О»` grows). Bridge for the `Оә>1` constant `I`-floor. |
| `invMomentI_ge_shell` | `(cm/2)В·(cm/(2cp))^{1/Оә}В·О»^{Оә-1} вүӨ I P U О»` | **Shell lower bound.** The shell `(ПҒО»,О»]`, `ПҒ=(cm/(2cp))^{1/Оә}`, has mass `вүҘ(cm/2)О»бөҸ` (uses **both** tails) on which `U/О»ВІ вүҘ ПҒ/О»`. |
| `invMomentI_bounds_lt` | `вҲғ 0<aвүӨA, вҲҖО»вҲҲ(0,tвӮҖ], aВ·О»^{Оә-1} вүӨ I вүӨ AВ·О»^{Оә-1}` (`Оә<1`) | Lower = shell, upper = `I вүӨ J`. |
| `invMomentI_bounds_eq` | `вҲғ 0<aвүӨA, aВ·log(1/О») вүӨ I вүӨ AВ·log(1/О»)+A` (`Оә=1`) | Lower = `I вүҘ J вҲ’ cp` (relation) uniformized with the shell floor; upper = `I вүӨ J`. |
| `invMomentI_bounds_gt` | `вҲғ 0<aвүӨA, a вүӨ I вүӨ A` (`Оә>1`) | Bounded inverse second moment: lower `I(О»)вүҘI(tвӮҖ)вүҘ` shell-at-`tвӮҖ` (antitone); upper = `I вүӨ J`. |
| `isTheta_of_two_sided` | two-sided bound вҹ№ `f =Оҳ[рқ“қ[>]0] g` | Generic `IsTheta` packager. |
| `invMomentJ_isTheta_lt` / `invMomentI_isTheta_lt` | `J вүҚ О»^{Оә-1}`, `I вүҚ О»^{Оә-1}` (`Оә<1`) | **IsTheta** on `рқ“қ[>] 0`. |
| `invMomentJ_isTheta_gt` / `invMomentI_isTheta_gt` | `J вүҚ 1`, `I вүҚ 1` (`Оә>1`) | **IsTheta** (bounded regime). |

### 12wo.7. `Stat/PolynomialTail/TrimBias.lean` вҖ” general bias bound

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `trimWeight_bias_bound` | `\|вҲ« wО»В·g вҲӮP\| вүӨ CОІВ·cpВ·О»^{Оә+ОІ}` | For `g` with HГ¶lder envelope `\|g\|вүӨCОІВ·О»^ОІ` on `{U<О»}`: tail-mass (`вүӨcpВ·О»бөҸ`) Г— envelope. Elementary (no layer cake). |

## 12'. `Stat/Concentration/` вҖ” Vendored concentration toolbox (Phase-4a)

Selectively vendored from
[auto-res/lean-rademacher](https://github.com/auto-res/lean-rademacher)
(commit `72d28921dc960f47691640fb973303a1be9d13ca`, MIT License). Audit
trail and porting policy live in
[Causalean/Stat/Concentration/UPSTREAM.md](../Causalean/Stat/Concentration/UPSTREAM.md).
Phase 4a covers definitions and the headline statements only; lower-level
proof chains are scaffolded with `sorry` and filled by Codex in a
separate pass.

#### 12'.a `Stat/Concentration/Rademacher.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Signs n` | `def`, `Type` | `{вҲ’1, +1}`-valued sign vectors of length `n`. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity n f S` | `noncomputable def`, `в„қ` | `(1 / 2вҒҝ) ОЈ_Пғ supбөў |(1/n) ОЈвӮ– Пғ k В· f i (S k)|`. |
| `rademacherComplexity n f Ој X` | `noncomputable def`, `в„қ` | Population version: `E[empiricalRademacherComplexity n f (X вҲҳ Пү)]` under `Пү вҲј ОјвҒҝ`. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_without_abs` | `noncomputable def`, `в„қ` | Variant without the outer absolute value (used in some symmetrization steps). |
| `uniformDeviation n f Ој X S` | `noncomputable def`, `в„қ` | `supбөў |(1/n) ОЈвӮ– f i (S k) вҲ’ E[f i вҲҳ X]|`. |
| `uniformDeviation_measurable` | `theorem` | Measurability of `uniformDeviation` for countable function classes with each `f i` measurable. **Proved.** |

#### 12'.a' `Stat/Concentration/ERMOracle.lean` вҖ” generic ERM oracle inequality

The method-agnostic learning-theory rate: empirical risk minimization over a countable
hypothesis class with bounded loss has excess risk controlled by the Rademacher complexity of
the loss class plus a McDiarmid tail. The engine that turns a complexity bound for a specific
method's class into a concrete excess-risk rate.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `erm_excess_le_two_uniformDeviation` | `theorem` | **Deterministic ERM basic inequality:** an empirical minimizer beating a comparator on the sample has excess population risk `вүӨ 2В·uniformDeviation`. **Proved.** |
| `erm_oracle_inequality` | `theorem` | **Oracle inequality:** `ОјвҒҝ{ 4В·рқ”ҜвӮҷ + 2Оө < R(ДҘ) вҲ’ R(hвӢҶ) } вүӨ exp(вҲ’ОөВІ t n)`, chaining the basic inequality with FoML's symmetrization + McDiarmid tail (`рқ”ҜвӮҷ` = Rademacher complexity of the loss class). **Proved.** |
| `erm_oracle_inequality_separable` | `theorem` | Separable-class version (`SeparableSpace`/`FirstCountableTopology О№`, loss continuous in the parameter) вҖ” the form that applies to the uncountable-but-separable LВІ/LВ№-ball linear classes (kernel, lasso). **Proved.** |

#### 12'.b `Stat/Concentration/BoundedDifference.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `uniformDeviation_bounded_difference` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | `uniformDeviation n f Ој X` has bounded differences with constant `(1/n)В·2b` in each coordinate, when each `f i` is bounded by `b`. |
| `bounded_difference_of_bounded` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | The map `Пү вҶҰ supбөў |ОЈвӮ– f i (X (Пү k)) вҲ’ nВ·E[f i вҲҳ X]|` has bounded differences with constant `2b`. |

#### 12'.c `Stat/Concentration/McDiarmid.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `mcdiarmid_inequality_pos` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | McDiarmid upper-tail: bounded-difference family `(X i)` вҮ’ `P(f вҲ’ E f вүҘ Оө) вүӨ exp(вҲ’2ОөВІ t)` whenever `t ОЈ cбөўВІ вүӨ 1`. |
| `mcdiarmid_inequality_neg` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | Symmetric lower-tail: `P(f вҲ’ E f вүӨ вҲ’Оө) вүӨ exp(вҲ’2ОөВІ t)`. |
| `mcdiarmid_inequality_pos'` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | Single-`X` form on the product measure `ОјвҒҝ` вҖ” the version actually consumed by `OSL/Modulus.lean`. |

#### 12'.d `Stat/Concentration/Symmetrization.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `expectation_le_rademacher` | `theorem` (`sorry`) | `E[supбөў |ОЈвӮ– f i (X (Пү k)) вҲ’ nВ·E f i|] вүӨ 2 n В· rademacherComplexity n f Ој X`. Wainwright Thm 4.10. |

#### 12'.e `Stat/Concentration/Separable.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `separableSpaceSup_eq` | `theorem` | For continuous bounded `f : X вҶ’ E` with `X` separable, `вЁҶ x, f x = вЁҶ i, f (denseSeq X i)`. **Proved.** |
| `separableSpaceSup_eq_real` | `theorem` | Real-valued specialisation handling the unbounded case. **Proved.** |

#### 12'.e2 `Stat/Concentration/CoveringNumber.lean`

Metric covering numbers for totally bounded sets, ported from
`auto-res/lean-rademacher` for the Dudley entropy-integral chain.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `coveringNumber_exists` | `TotallyBounded A`, `Оө > 0` вҶ’ `вҲғ n t, t.card = n вҲ§ A вҠҶ вӢғ y вҲҲ t, Metric.ball y Оө` | Extracts a finite Оө-net for a totally bounded set and records its cardinality. |
| `coveringNumber` | `TotallyBounded A вҶ’ в„қ вҶ’ в„•` | Minimal finite Оө-cover cardinality for positive Оө, and zero for nonpositive Оө. |
| `coveringNumber_eq` | `Оө > 0` вҶ’ `coveringNumber ha Оө = Nat.find вҖҰ` | Unfolds the positive-radius branch of `coveringNumber`. |
| `converingNumber_antitone` | `AntitoneOn (coveringNumber ha) (Set.Ioi 0)` | Covering number is antitone in the radius on positive radii. |
| `coveringNumber_nonzero` | `A.Nonempty`, `Оө > 0` вҶ’ `0 < coveringNumber ha Оө` | Nonempty totally bounded sets have positive covering number at positive radius. |
| `converingNumber_aemeasurable` | `AEMeasurable (coveringNumber ha) volume` | Measurability of the covering-number function on the real radius line. |
| `coveringFinset` | `TotallyBounded A`, `Оө > 0` вҶ’ `Finset X` | Chooses a finite Оө-net attaining `coveringNumber`. |
| `coveringFinset_cover` | `A вҠҶ вӢғ y вҲҲ coveringFinset ha hОө, Metric.ball y Оө` | The chosen finset covers the target set by Оө-balls. |
| `coveringFinset_card` | `(coveringFinset ha hОө).card = coveringNumber ha Оө` | The chosen finset has cardinality equal to the covering number. |

#### 12'.e3 `Stat/Concentration/DudleyEntropy.lean`

The Dudley entropy-integral chaining bound controls empirical Rademacher
complexity by the covering-number entropy integral of the empirical
pseudometric function space.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dudley_entropy_integral'` | `Оө > 0`, total boundedness, positive sample size, empirical-norm radius `c`, and `Оө < c/2` вҶ’ empirical Rademacher complexity without absolute value is bounded by `4Оө` plus the entropy integral from `Оө` to `c/2` | Core Dudley entropy-integral bound for a fixed sample and function class. |
| `dudley_entropy_integral_bound` | Same bound with the public sample-size parameter name `n` | Headline wrapper ported from FoML `Main.lean`. |

#### 12'.e3b `Stat/Concentration/SqrtLogIntegral.lean`

Elementary real-analysis evaluation used to simplify Dudley entropy-integral
upper bounds with logarithmic kernels.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sqrt_le_add_one_div_two` | `0 вүӨ t вҶ’ sqrt t вүӨ (t + 1) / 2` | AM-GM bound for the square root, proved from nonnegativity of `(sqrt t - 1)^2`. |
| `log_div_nonneg_of_mem_Icc` | `0 < Оө`, `x вҲҲ [Оө, Оҙ]` вҶ’ `0 вүӨ log (Оҙ / x)` | Positivity of the logarithmic ratio kernel on the integration interval. |
| `integral_log_div_eq` | `0 < Оө`, `Оө вүӨ Оҙ` вҶ’ `вҲ« x in Оө..Оҙ, log (Оҙ / x) = Оҙ - Оө - Оө * log (Оҙ / Оө)` | Exact closed-form evaluation of the logarithmic ratio integral. |
| `integral_log_div_le` | `0 < Оө`, `Оө вүӨ Оҙ` вҶ’ `вҲ« x in Оө..Оҙ, log (Оҙ / x) вүӨ Оҙ - Оө` | Interval-length upper bound obtained from `log (Оҙ / Оө) вүҘ 0`. |
| `sqrtLog_integral_le` | `0 < Оө`, `Оө вүӨ Оҙ` вҶ’ `вҲ« x in Оө..Оҙ, sqrt (log (Оҙ / x)) вүӨ Оҙ` | Dudley entropy-integral evaluation using the AM-GM pointwise bound and the logarithmic-ratio integral estimate. |

#### 12'.e4 `Stat/Concentration/VCCovering.lean`

VC-dimension control of empirical covering numbers for real-valued classes
whose sample evaluations factor through a Boolean class.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `restrictionPattern` | `(рқ’і вҶ’ Bool) вҶ’ (Fin n вҶ’ рқ’і) вҶ’ Finset (Fin n)` | Records the sample coordinates at which a Boolean classifier is true. |
| `growthFamily` | `(О№ вҶ’ рқ’і вҶ’ Bool) вҶ’ (Fin n вҶ’ рқ’і) вҶ’ Finset (Finset (Fin n))` | The finite family of Boolean restriction patterns realized on the sample. |
| `card_growthFamily_le_sum_choose` | `рқ’ң.vcDim вүӨ d вҶ’ рқ’ң.card вүӨ вҲ‘ k вҲҲ Finset.Iic d, n.choose k` | Sauer-Shelah cardinality bound for a finite family on an `n`-point sample. |
| `vc_coveringNumber_le_growth` | Binary factorization of `F` through `ПҖ` on the sample and `Оө > 0` вҶ’ `coveringNumber h' Оө вүӨ (growthFamily ПҖ S).card` | Bounds the empirical covering number by choosing one representative per realized Boolean pattern. |
| `vc_coveringNumber_le_sum_choose` | Same hypotheses plus `(growthFamily ПҖ S).vcDim вүӨ d` вҶ’ `coveringNumber h' Оө вүӨ вҲ‘ k вҲҲ Finset.Iic d, n.choose k` | Combines the finite self-cover with Sauer-Shelah. |
| `sum_choose_le_exp_mul_div_pow` | `0 < d`, `d вүӨ n` вҶ’ `вҲ‘_{kвүӨd} (n choose k) вүӨ (e n / d)^d` | Standard binomial-sum estimate; currently the only intentionally scaffolded arithmetic lemma in this file. |
| `log_coveringNumber_le` | Nonempty class, binary factorization, `Оө > 0`, `0 < d`, `d вүӨ n`, and VC dimension at most `d` вҶ’ logarithmic covering bound | Converts the VC covering bound into the usual entropy display using the standard binomial-sum estimate. |

#### 12'.e4b `Stat/Concentration/HausslerPacking.lean`

Haussler-style finite-VC packing bounds for weighted Boolean Hamming metrics,
isolating the random-coordinate extraction core from the deterministic
Sauer-Shelah cardinality step.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `weightedHammingSq` | Coordinate weights and two Boolean vectors on `Fin n` вҶ’ weighted Hamming pseudo-distance squared | Sums the weights of coordinates where the two Boolean vectors differ. |
| `subsamplePattern` | Coordinate map `Fin m вҶ’ Fin n` and Boolean vector вҶ’ `Finset (Fin m)` | Records the true coordinates after restricting a Boolean vector to a sampled coordinate list. |
| `weightedHammingSq_nonneg` | Nonnegative weights вҶ’ `0 вүӨ weightedHammingSq w a b` | Basic nonnegativity of the weighted Hamming pseudo-distance squared. |
| `card_subsample_family_le_succ_pow` | Restricted trace VC dimension at most `d` вҶ’ restricted trace cardinality at most `(m + 1)^d` | Applies Sauer-Shelah and a binomial-sum bound to a sampled coordinate family. |
| `subsample_image_vcDim_le` | Original true-set family VC dimension at most `d` вҶ’ every coordinate restriction has VC dimension at most `d` | Shows restriction along any coordinate map cannot increase VC dimension. |
| `finite_averaging_exists_separating_subsample` | Total weight at most `rВІ`, `ОөВІ`-separation, `2 вүӨ #P`, and `0 < Оө вүӨ r` вҶ’ a separating restricted trace with `m вүӨ 1 + (2*rВІ/ОөВІ)*log #P` | Isolates the finite product-sum averaging step for the random-coordinate extraction; currently the remaining `sorry` in this module. |
| `exists_separating_subsample` | VC control, total weight at most `rВІ`, `ОөВІ`-separation, `2 вүӨ #P`, and `0 < Оө вүӨ r` вҶ’ a separating restricted trace with `m вүӨ 1 + (2*rВІ/ОөВІ)*log #P` and restricted VC dimension at most `d` | Combines finite averaging with VC-dimension preservation for sampled coordinate families. |
| `self_log_solve` | `L вүӨ d*log(2 + a*L)` with `d вүҘ 1`, `a вүҘ 2`, and `L вүҘ 0` вҶ’ an explicit `1 + 2*d*log(2*a*d)` bound | Scalar self-logarithm inequality used after Sauer-Shelah. |
| `vc_weightedHamming_packing_card_le` | VC control, total weight at most `rВІ`, and `ОөВІ`-separation вҶ’ `log #P вүӨ 1 + 2*d*log(4*d*rВІ/ОөВІ)` | Logarithmic Haussler Оө-packing bound with no dependence on the ambient coordinate count, modulo the isolated extraction lemma. |
| `vc_weightedHamming_log_packing_le` | Same hypotheses вҶ’ `log #P вүӨ 1 + 2*d*log(4*d*rВІ/ОөВІ)` | Alias for the clean logarithmic weighted VC packing bound. |

#### 12'.e4c `Stat/Concentration/VCLocalizedCovering.lean`

Metric bridges connecting binary-factored real classes with the weighted
Boolean Hamming geometry used in localized finite-VC covering arguments.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `empiricalDist_sq_eq_weightedHammingSq` | Binary factorization `F i (S j) = ПҶ j (ПҖ i (S j))` вҶ’ squared empirical LВІ distance equals weighted Hamming squared distance with weights `(ПҶ j true вҲ’ ПҶ j false)ВІ/n` | Converts empirical metric separation for a binary-factored real class into weighted Boolean trace separation on the same sample. |

#### 12'.e5 `Stat/Concentration/VCLocalizedRegime.lean`

Localized finite-VC Rademacher regime used by the sharp localized uniform
deviation layer.  The elementary sub-root and critical-radius algebra is proved
directly.  The remaining Massart finite-realization construction is isolated as
an explicit analytic sublemma rather than packaged as a hypothesis field.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `BinaryFactoredVCClass` | Real-valued class `F` factors through a Boolean class whose sample growth family has VC dimension at most `d` | Packages the binary trace substrate used by VC covering arguments. |
| `BinaryTraceEntropyControl` | Either samplewise VC-dimension control or direct `(m + 1)^d` growth-cardinality control | Common entropy input for the localized star-hull theorem. |
| `LocalizedVCDudleyHypotheses` | Empirical radius and total boundedness fields | Deterministic hypothesis bundle for the sample-path localized Dudley step. |
| `localizedVCDudleyHypotheses_of_empiricalRadius` | Bounded binary-factored finite-VC class plus an explicit samplewise empirical-radius bridge вҶ’ `LocalizedVCDudleyHypotheses` | Constructor for the localized Dudley hypothesis bundle. For population LВІ localization norms, the empirical-radius bridge remains a real extra obligation; boundedness alone does not give deterministic empirical-radius control on every sample. |
| `starHullZeroOut_empirical_rademacher_massart_vc` | Binary trace entropy + localized hypotheses вҶ’ Massart finite-realization bound with rate `r * sqrt((2 d log(n+1)+2)/n)` | Isolates the remaining analytic finite-pattern Massart construction. |
| `absolute_dudley_vc_starHullZeroOut_linear_residual_shared` | Same localized star-hull bound with either VC-dimension or direct growth-cardinality trace control, assuming `K вүҘ 1` | Shared residual used by the downstream VC localized bounds. |
| `vcLocalizedRademacherUpperBound` | Binary-factored finite-VC class + localized hypotheses вҶ’ local Rademacher upper bound and critical-radius rate | Headline localized finite-VC Rademacher envelope. |
| `vcLocalizedEnvelope` | Finite-VC localized assumptions вҶ’ `localized_uniform_deviation_sharp` envelope | Connects the finite-VC Rademacher regime to the localized uniform-deviation theorem. |

#### 12'.f `Stat/Concentration/Hoeffding.lean`

Causalean-facing corollaries of Mathlib's sub-Gaussian framework
(`Mathlib.Probability.Moments.SubGaussian`) specialised to the `IIDSample`
model. All declarations **proved, sorry-free, axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.map_eq` | `lemma` | Each sample point has law `P`: `Ој.map (S.Z i) = P`. |
| `IIDSample.integral_comp_eq` | `lemma` | Population-mean identity `вҲ« f (S.Z i) вҲӮОј = вҲ« f вҲӮP` for measurable `f`. |
| `IIDSample.iIndepFun_comp` | `lemma` | Independence of the composed family `fun i вҶҰ f вҲҳ S.Z i` from `S.indep`. |
| `hoeffding_ge` | `theorem` | One-sided Hoeffding: for `f` valued in `[a,b]` (`a<b`) `P`-a.e., `P(XМ„вӮҷ вҲ’ E[f] вүҘ Оө) вүӨ exp(вҲ’2nОөВІ/(bвҲ’a)ВІ)`. |
| `Concentration.measureReal_abs_dev_le_two_sided` | `lemma` | Generic two-sided assembly: from one-sided bounds `Ој.real{Оө вүӨ TвҲ’m} вүӨ B` and `Ој.real{Оө вүӨ вҲ’T+m} вүӨ B`, concludes `Ој.real{Оө вүӨ |TвҲ’m|} вүӨ 2B`. Shared by `hoeffding_abs_ge` and `bernstein_abs_ge` (the former duplicated union+calc boilerplate). |
| `hoeffding_abs_ge` | `theorem` | Two-sided Hoeffding: `P(|XМ„вӮҷ вҲ’ E[f]| вүҘ Оө) вүӨ 2В·exp(вҲ’2nОөВІ/(bвҲ’a)ВІ)`. Two-sided assembly via `measureReal_abs_dev_le_two_sided`. |

#### 12'.g `Stat/Concentration/SubExponential.lean`

Sub-exponential MGF framework mirroring Mathlib's sub-Gaussian design, in the
`(v, b)` parameterization (validity interval encoded as `bВ·|t| < 1`, so `b = 0`
recovers the sub-Gaussian/all-`t` case). All declarations **proved, sorry-free,
axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasSubexponentialMGF X v b Ој` | `structure` | `exp(tВ·X)` integrable and `mgf X Ој t вүӨ exp(v tВІ/2)` for every `t` with `bВ·|t| < 1`. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.isFiniteMeasure` | `lemma` | A sub-exponential variable lives on a finite measure. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.neg` | `lemma` | Closure under negation (same `(v,b)`). |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.congr` | `lemma` | Transfer along an a.e.-equality of variables. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.const_mul` | `lemma` | Scaling `rВ·X` has parameters `(rВІv, |r|b)`. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.add_of_indepFun` | `lemma` | Independent variables add: `X+Y` has parameters `(vX+vY, max bX bY)`. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.zero` | `lemma` | The constant `0` is sub-exponential with parameters `(0, b)`. |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.measure_ge_le` | `theorem` | Chernoff tail `P(X вүҘ Оө) вүӨ exp(вҲ’ОөВІ/(2(v + bОө)))` (single explicit minimizer `t = Оө/(v+2bОө)`). |
| `HasSubexponentialMGF.sum_range_of_iIndepFun` | `lemma` | A sum of `n` independent variables with common `(v,b)` is sub-exponential with `(nвҖўv, b)`. |

#### 12'.h Mathlib sub-Gaussian re-use index

The sub-Gaussian half of the toolbox is consumed directly from Mathlib's
`ProbabilityTheory` namespace (no Causalean duplication); the Causalean wrappers above
build on it. Key upstream names:

| Mathlib declaration | Description |
|---|---|
| `HasSubgaussianMGF X c Ој` | Sub-Gaussian MGF predicate `mgf X Ој t вүӨ exp(c tВІ/2)` for all `t`. |
| `hasSubgaussianMGF_of_mem_Icc` | Hoeffding's lemma: a `[a,b]`-valued RV is sub-Gaussian with proxy `((bвҲ’a)/2)ВІ` after centering. |
| `HasSubgaussianMGF.measure_ge_le` | Sub-Gaussian Chernoff right tail. |
| `HasSubgaussianMGF.measure_sum_range_ge_le_of_iIndepFun` | Hoeffding's inequality for sums of independent sub-Gaussians (used by `hoeffding_ge`). |
| `HasSubgaussianMGF.add_of_indepFun` / `sum_of_iIndepFun` | Independent sums (parameters add). |

#### 12'.i `Stat/Concentration/Bernstein.lean`

Bernstein's inequality routed through the constant-`v` sub-exponential framework.
**Fully proved and `sorry`-free** (all four declarations are axiom-clean:
`propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound` only). The earlier power-series blocker
is avoided entirely: the sub-exponential packaging only needs the MGF bound on
the validity interval `c|t| < 1`, where `|tX| вүӨ 1` a.e. lets the elementary
pointwise bound `exp u вүӨ 1 + u + uВІ` integrate term-by-term.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `exp_le_one_add_add_sq` | `lemma` | Pointwise `exp u вүӨ 1 + u + uВІ` for `|u| вүӨ 1`; a real corollary of `Real.norm_exp_sub_one_sub_id_le`. **Proved.** |
| `bounded_mgf_le_exp_sq` | `lemma` | For mean-zero `X`, `|X| вүӨ c`, `E[XВІ] вүӨ ПғВІ`: `mgf X Ој t вүӨ exp(ПғВІtВІ)` when `c|t| вүӨ 1`. Integrates the pointwise bound using `E[X]=0`, `E[XВІ]вүӨПғВІ`. **Proved.** |
| `bounded_hasSubexponentialMGF` | `lemma` | A bounded mean-zero variable is sub-exponential with parameters `(2ПғВІ, c)` (note `2ПғВІВ·tВІ/2 = ПғВІtВІ`). **Proved.** |
| `bernstein_ge` | `theorem` | One-sided Bernstein: `P(XМ„вӮҷ вҲ’ E[f] вүҘ Оө) вүӨ exp(вҲ’n ОөВІ / (2(2ПғВІ + cОө)))`. From `bounded_hasSubexponentialMGF` + independent-sum + Chernoff. **Proved.** |
| `bernstein_abs_ge` | `theorem` | Two-sided Bernstein: `P(|XМ„вӮҷ вҲ’ E[f]| вүҘ Оө) вүӨ 2В·exp(вҲ’n ОөВІ / (2(2ПғВІ + cОө)))`. **Proved.** |

> **Constant note.** The variance enters with proxy `2ПғВІ` and the scale is `c`
> (so the tail is `exp(вҲ’nОөВІ/(2(2ПғВІ+cОө)))`). The sharper textbook constant
> `exp(вҲ’nОөВІ/(2(ПғВІ+cОө/3)))` is *not* attainable through the constant-`v`
> sub-exponential structure вҖ” it needs a Bernstein-specific Chernoff optimisation
> on the denominator-form MGF вҖ” and was deliberately traded for this cleaner,
> looser constant. The `c|t| < 1` validity interval forces the scale `b вүҘ c`, so
> `b = c` is optimal for this route.

---

#### 12'.j `Stat/Concentration/ConfidenceInterval.lean`

Inverts the two-sided Hoeffding and Bernstein tails (12'.f, 12'.i) into explicit
*finite-sample* confidence half-widths. For a confidence level `Оҙ вҲҲ (0,1]` and
sample size `n`, each result gives a half-width `w(n,Оҙ)` such that the population
mean `m = вҲ« f вҲӮP` is covered by the random interval `[XМ„вӮҷ вҲ’ w, XМ„вӮҷ + w]` with
probability `вүҘ 1 вҲ’ Оҙ`. Both a miss-probability form (`P(w вүӨ |XМ„вӮҷ вҲ’ m|) вүӨ Оҙ`) and
its coverage complement are provided. Holds at every fixed `n`, unlike the
asymptotic Wald interval. **Fully proved and `sorry`-free.**

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `hoeffdingCIHalfWidth` | `(a b : в„қ) (n : в„•) (Оҙ : в„қ) : в„қ` | Hoeffding half-width `(bвҲ’a)В·вҲҡ(log(2/Оҙ)/(2n))` for an `[a,b]`-valued statistic. | **Proved** (def) |
| `bernsteinCIHalfWidth` | `(c Пғ : в„қ) (n : в„•) (Оҙ : в„қ) : в„қ` | Variance-adaptive Bernstein half-width `2ПғВ·вҲҡ(log(2/Оҙ)/n) + 2cВ·(log(2/Оҙ)/n)`; leading term scales with `Пғ`. | **Proved** (def) |
| `hoeffding_ci_miss` | `вҖҰ : Ој.real {Пү | hoeffdingCIHalfWidth a b n Оҙ вүӨ |XМ„вӮҷ вҲ’ m|} вүӨ Оҙ` | Miss bound: the mean falls outside the Hoeffding interval w.p. `вүӨ Оҙ`. Inverts `hoeffding_abs_ge`. | **Proved** |
| `bernstein_ci_miss` | `вҖҰ : Ој.real {Пү | bernsteinCIHalfWidth c Пғ n Оҙ вүӨ |XМ„вӮҷ вҲ’ m|} вүӨ Оҙ` | Miss bound for the variance-adaptive Bernstein interval; exact quadratic-slack inversion of `bernstein_abs_ge`. | **Proved** |
| `hoeffding_ci_cover` | `вҖҰ : 1 вҲ’ Оҙ вүӨ Ој.real {Пү | |XМ„вӮҷ вҲ’ m| < hoeffdingCIHalfWidth a b n Оҙ}` | Coverage complement of `hoeffding_ci_miss`. | **Proved** |
| `bernstein_ci_cover` | `вҖҰ : 1 вҲ’ Оҙ вүӨ Ој.real {Пү | |XМ„вӮҷ вҲ’ m| < bernsteinCIHalfWidth c Пғ n Оҙ}` | Coverage complement of `bernstein_ci_miss`. | **Proved** |

---

#### 12'.k `Stat/Concentration/EmpiricalBernstein.lean`

Sample-variance infrastructure plus an **elementary, fully rigorous** data-driven
("empirical") Bernstein confidence interval. The full MaurerвҖ“Pontil bound is out
of scope; instead the population variance `ПғВІ = вҲ«(fвҲ’m)ВІ` is bounded above by the
*observed* sample variance `VМӮвӮҷ` plus a deterministic slack `П„` (via two Hoeffding
deviations вҖ” one for `f`, one for `fВІ` вҖ” at level `Оҙ/2` each), and `Пғ вүӨ вҲҡ(VМӮвӮҷ+П„)`
is fed into the Bernstein half-width using monotonicity in `Пғ`. Constants are
looser than MaurerвҖ“Pontil's (the second-moment Hoeffding step is wasteful) but
every step is elementary. **Fully proved and `sorry`-free** (axiom-clean:
`propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound` only).

The explicit slack (with `M := max |a| |b|`, `d := вҲҡ(log(4/Оҙ)/(2n))`) is
`П„ = MВІВ·d + 2 M (bвҲ’a)В·d`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `IIDSample.sampleVariance` | `(S : IIDSample О© X Ој P) (f : X вҶ’ в„қ) (n : в„•) : О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Biased (`1/n`-normalised) empirical variance `(1/n) вҲ‘_{i<n} (f(Zбөў Пү) вҲ’ XМ„вӮҷ(Пү))ВІ` (in namespace `Causalean.Stat` for dot-notation). | **Proved** (def) |
| `IIDSample.sampleVariance_nonneg` | `вҖҰ (Пү) : 0 вүӨ S.sampleVariance f n Пү` | The sample variance is nonnegative (a scaled sum of squares). | **Proved** |
| `IIDSample.measurable_sampleVariance` | `(hf : Measurable f) (n) : Measurable (S.sampleVariance f n)` | The sample variance is measurable in the sample point `Пү`. | **Proved** |
| `IIDSample.sampleVariance_eq` | `вҖҰ (Пү) : S.sampleVariance f n Пү = (n:в„қ)вҒ»В№ * вҲ‘ i вҲҲ range n, (f (Z i Пү))^2 вҲ’ (XМ„вӮҷ Пү)^2` | Computational form: empirical second moment minus squared empirical mean. | **Proved** |
| `empiricalVarianceSlack` | `(a b : в„қ) (n : в„•) (Оҙ : в„қ) : в„қ` | The deterministic slack `П„ = MВІВ·d + 2M(bвҲ’a)В·d` (`M = max|a||b|`, `d = вҲҡ(log(4/Оҙ)/(2n))`). | **Proved** (def) |
| `empirical_variance_concentration` | `(hf) (hab : a<b) (hbound : fвҲҲ[a,b] a.e.) (n) (hn) (hОҙ0) (hОҙ1) : Ој.real {Пү | VМӮвӮҷ + П„ < ПғВІ} вүӨ Оҙ` | Data-driven variance bound: w.p. `вүҘ 1вҲ’Оҙ`, `ПғВІ вүӨ VМӮвӮҷ + empiricalVarianceSlack a b n Оҙ`. Union of two level-`Оҙ/2` Hoeffding events (for `f` and `fВІ`) plus a null set for the a.e. `|XМ„вӮҷ| вүӨ M` bound. | **Proved** |
| `empiricalBernsteinCIHalfWidth` | `(S) (f) (a b c : в„қ) (n) (Оҙ) : О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Data-driven half-width: `bernsteinCIHalfWidth` with oracle `Пғ` replaced by the observed `вҲҡ(VМӮвӮҷ(Пү) + П„)`. | **Proved** (def) |
| `empirical_bernstein_ci_miss` | `(hf) (hab) (hbound_ab) (hc : 0вүӨc) (hbound : |fвҲ’m|вүӨc a.e.) (hposvar : 0<ПғВІ) (n) (hn) (hОҙ0) (hОҙ1) : Ој.real {Пү | empiricalBernsteinCIHalfWidth S f a b c n Оҙ Пү вүӨ |XМ„вӮҷ вҲ’ m|} вүӨ 2Оҙ` | Data-driven Bernstein CI miss bound: the mean falls outside the observed-variance interval w.p. `вүӨ 2Оҙ`. Unions the oracle Bernstein miss (`Пғ=вҲҡПғВІ`) with the variance-bound failure, using monotonicity of the width in `Пғ` on the good event. | **Proved** |

---

## 12g. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/` вҖ” Abstract DML estimation and inference framework

Generic moment-functional layer that consolidates the DML asymptotic-linearity
machinery so any concrete instance (currently AIPW) reduces to instantiating
the abstract structure. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/dml_ate.tex`
generalised away from AIPW-specific algebra.

### 12g.1 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/MomentFunctional.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `GeneralMoment О© Ој Z P_Z H` | structure | Abstract bundle: moment functional `m : H вҶ’ Z вҶ’ в„қ вҶ’ в„қ`, truth `О·вӮҖ : H`, target `ОёвӮҖ : в„қ`, perturbation set `H_Оө вҠҶ H`, bilinear seminorms `(ПҒвӮҒ, ПҒвӮӮ) : H вҶ’ H вҶ’ NNReal`, joint measurability of `m _ В· _`, and `О·вӮҖ вҲҲ H_Оө`. |
| `GeneralMoment.JвӮҖ` | field, `в„қ` | Jacobian `вҲӮ_Оё вҲ« m(О·вӮҖ, z, Оё) dP_Z |_{Оё=ОёвӮҖ}` of the population moment. Scalar form (vector-Оё generalisation deferred). For AIPW (linear score `m = ПҲ вҲ’ Оё`), `JвӮҖ = вҲ’1`. |
| `GeneralMoment.JвӮҖ_ne_zero` | field, `Prop` | Non-singularity witness; ensures `JвӮҖ_inv` is well-defined. |
| `GeneralMoment.JвӮҖ_inv` | `noncomputable def` | `JвӮҖвҒ»В№`. **Proved.** |
| `GeneralMoment.JвӮҖ_mul_JвӮҖ_inv` | `lemma`, `@[simp]` | `JвӮҖ * JвӮҖ_inv = 1`. **Proved.** |
| `MeanZero M` | `Prop` | `вҲ« z, M.m M.О·вӮҖ z M.ОёвӮҖ вҲӮP_Z = 0`. |
| `H_Оө_PerturbClosed M` | `Prop` | `M.H_Оө` is closed under convex combinations between `О·вӮҖ` and `О· вҲҲ H_Оө`. |

##### `LinearMoment` (linear-score extension)

Extension of `GeneralMoment` carrying the decomposition `m О· z Оё = m_a О· z * Оё + m_b О· z` (Chernozhukov 2018 Thm 3.1 "linear score" form). AIPW is the canonical instance with `m_a О· z = -1` and `m_b О· z = ПҲ_AIPW(О·, z)`, giving `JвӮҖ = -1`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LinearMoment` | structure (extends `GeneralMoment`) | Adds `m_a`, `m_b`, measurability, decomposition `m_decomp`, and consistency `JвӮҖ_eq : JвӮҖ = вҲ« m_a О·вӮҖ z dP_Z`. |

### 12g.2 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DirectionalDeriv.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `HasDirDeriv M` | structure | Pointwise directional derivative data: function `dM : H вҶ’ Z вҶ’ в„қ`, pointwise `Tendsto` witness for the difference quotient along the segment `О·вӮҖ вҶ’ О·`, and measurability of `dM О·`. |

### 12g.3 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/NeymanOrthogonal.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `NeymanOrthogonal M D` | `Prop` | `вҲҖ О· вҲҲ M.H_Оө, вҲ« z, D.dM О· z вҲӮP_Z = 0`. |
| `DiffQuotientEnvelope M` | `Prop` | Existence of an LВ№(P_Z) envelope dominating the difference quotient near `t = 0`. |
| `integratedMoment_diffQuotient_tendsto_zero` | `theorem` | DCT bridge: `NeymanOrthogonal` + `DiffQuotientEnvelope` вҮ’ integrated difference quotient вҶ’ 0 along `рқ“қ[вү ] 0`. **Proved** (forward-compat; not on AIPW path). |

### 12g.4 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/RemainderBound.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `BilinearRemainder M C` | `Prop` | `вҲҖ О· вҲҲ M.H_Оө, |вҲ« z, M.m О· z M.ОёвӮҖ вҲӮP_Z| вүӨ C В· ПҒвӮҒ(О·, О·вӮҖ) В· ПҒвӮӮ(О·, О·вӮҖ)`. |
| `bilinear_remainder_of_smoothness` | `theorem` | NeymanOrthogonal + uniform second-order envelope on the linearization residual вҮ’ `вҲғ C, BilinearRemainder M C`. **Proved** (after signature strengthening: uniform `K, g`). |

### 12g.5. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DMLChernozhukov.lean`

Classical Chernozhukov-form DML theorem; conclusion uses IF `вҲ’JвӮҖвҒ»В№ В· m(О·вӮҖ, В·, ОёвӮҖ)` and asymptotic variance `JвӮҖвҒ»В№ ОЈ JвӮҖвҒ»бөҖ`. Matches Chernozhukov et al. 2018 Thm 3.1 / 3.2. The earlier zero-centered `dml_asymptoticLinear` (`Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DML.lean`) was deleted on 2026-05-08 since AIPW migrated and no other instance used it.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dmlChernozhukovEstimator M sample split О·_hat` | `noncomputable def` | One-step Chernozhukov estimator: `ОёМӮ_n := ОёвӮҖ вҲ’ JвӮҖвҒ»В№ В· PвӮҷ_B m(О·МӮ, В·, ОёвӮҖ)`. Reduces to `(1/|B|) ОЈ ПҲ(О·МӮ, Z_i)` for AIPW (JвӮҖ = вҲ’1). |
| `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear` | `theorem` | Asymptotic linearity of `dmlChernozhukovEstimator` at `M.ОёвӮҖ` with IF `вҲ’JвӮҖвҒ»В№ В· m(О·вӮҖ, В·, ОёвӮҖ)`, indexed over `split.foldB`. **Proved by Codex** (G+B decomposition with the Chernozhukov-form score `m(О·МӮ, В·, ОёвӮҖ)` and a `вҲ’JвӮҖвҒ»В№` constant-multiplication wrapper; bundled an inline `const_mul_isLittleOp_one` helper). **API refactored 2026-05-06:** dropped the `D : HasDirDeriv M`, `_hNO : NeymanOrthogonal M D`, and `_h_in_HОө : вҲҖ n Пү, О·_hat n Пү вҲҲ M.H_Оө` parameters; replaced the вҲҖ-quantified `_hBR : BilinearRemainder M Crem` with the per-О·МӮ_n bound `_hBR_at : вҲҖ n Пү, |вҲ« z, M.m (О·_hat n Пү) z M.ОёвӮҖ вҲӮP_Z| вүӨ Crem В· ПҒвӮҒ(О·МӮ_n, О·вӮҖ) В· ПҒвӮӮ(О·МӮ_n, О·вӮҖ)`. Net effect: instances supply only what they actually need (orthogonality is implicit in the per-О·МӮ_n bound), removing the artificial вҲҖ-over-`H_Оө` hypothesis. **Further trimmed 2026-05-09:** dropped the unused `_hc_lt : c < 1`, `_h_indiv_rate_ПҒвӮҒ`, `_h_indiv_rate_ПҒвӮӮ` parameters (all discarded in the body вҖ” the abstract proof needs only the product rate); callers (`AIPWInstance`, `AutoDebias.DML`) keep them at their own level when needed locally. |

### 12g.5'. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/DMLCrossFit.lean`

K-fold cross-fitted version of `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear`. Same Chernozhukov form, K folds, score evaluated independently of nuisance training fold per Chernozhukov et al. 2018 В§3.2. Concludes asymptotic linearity at the **full-sample** index `Finset.range n` (the K fold-k sub-aggregations are reweighted into a `(1/вҲҡn)`-normalised sum modulo `o_p(1)`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `dmlCrossFitEstimator M sample split О·_hat` | `noncomputable def` | K-fold averaged Chernozhukov estimator: `ОёМӮ := M.ОёвӮҖ вҲ’ JвӮҖвҒ»В№ В· KвҒ»В№ В· ОЈ_k \|fold n k\|вҒ»В№ В· ОЈ_{i вҲҲ fold n k} m(О·МӮ^{(-k)}, Z_i, ОёвӮҖ)`. |
| `foldNormalizedSum_isBigOp` | private lemma | `(1/вҲҡ\|fold n k\|) ОЈ_{i вҲҲ fold n k} ПҲ(Z_i) = O_p(1)` whenever `ПҲ` is mean-zero and `LВІ(P_Z)`. Markov + the abstract `iid_centered_sum_sq_lintegral_le` with trivial training Пғ-algebra `m_A = вҠҘ` (independence is automatic via `ProbabilityTheory.indep_bot_left`); mean-zero collapses centered = uncentered. **Proved.** |
| `foldNormalizedSumOverN_isBigOp` | private lemma | `(1/вҲҡn) ОЈ_{i вҲҲ fold n k} ПҲ(Z_i) = O_p(1)`. Bound by `foldNormalizedSum_isBigOp` Г— `вҲҡ(\|fold n k\|/n)` (the latter is bounded eventually via `split.cover` + `split.partition` giving `(fold n k).card вүӨ n`). **Proved.** |
| `dml_crossFit_asymptoticLinear` | `theorem` | Asymptotic linearity of `dmlCrossFitEstimator` at `M.ОёвӮҖ` with IF `вҲ’JвӮҖвҒ»В№ В· m(О·вӮҖ, В·, ОёвӮҖ)`, indexed over `fun n => Finset.range n`. Hypotheses: per-fold measurability/integrability (`_h_m_meas`, `_h_m_train`, `_h_m_train_uncurry`, `_h_m_int`, `_h_m_sq_int`), per-fold score-difference rate (`_h_score_diff_rate k`), per-(n,k,Пү) bilinear remainder bound `_hBR_at`, individual ПҒ-rates, product rate, and `_hK_pos : 1 < K` (tightened from `0 < K` because the K-fold bias step needs `(K:в„қ)вҒ»В№ вҲҲ (0, 1)`; `K = 1` reduces to the one-shot estimator and is out of scope). Proof structure: `mean_zero` and `finite_var` by constant-rescaling of `MeanZero M` / `_hFV`; the `remainder` decomposes into `h_fold_centered` + `h_fold_bias` (per-fold, lifted from the OneShot proof in `DMLChernozhukov.lean` via `KFoldSplit.fold_centered_sum_isLittleOp_one` and an inline bias derivation analogous to OneShot `hB`) and `h_full_reweighting` (the cover/partition + ratio rescaling). The reweighting step uses `IsBigOp.const_mul_tendsto_zero` for the weight-discrepancy "ratio piece" and `IsBigOp.mul_isLittleOp_one_isLittleOp` for the "score piece"; finite K-fold sum closed via a small inline `IsLittleOp` finite-Finset additivity helper. **Proved by Codex (A5).** **API refactored 2026-05-09:** mirrors the OneShot 2026-05-06 refactor вҖ” dropped the `D : HasDirDeriv M`, `_hNO : NeymanOrthogonal M D`, `_h_in_HОө`, and вҲҖ-quantified `_hBR : BilinearRemainder M Crem` parameters; replaced by the per-(n,k,Пү) bound `_hBR_at`. |

### 12g.6 `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AIPWInstance.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipwGeneralMoment S h_e_pointwise` | `noncomputable def` | AIPW realization of `GeneralMoment` with `ПҒвӮҒ О· О·' = вҖ–О”Ој_TвҖ–_{LВІ(P_X)} + вҖ–О”Ој_FвҖ–_{LВІ(P_X)}`, `ПҒвӮӮ О· О·' = вҖ–О”eвҖ–_{LВІ(P_X)}`, `H_Оө = ATE.H_Оө Оө`. |
| `aipwGeneralMoment.JвӮҖ`, `JвӮҖ_ne_zero` | fields | AIPW Jacobian `JвӮҖ = вҲ’1` (linear score `m = ПҲ вҲ’ Оё`). |
| `aipw_meanZero` | `theorem` | Wraps `aipw_mean_zero`. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_bilinearRem` | `theorem` | `aipw_remainder_bound` reshaped into `BilinearRemainder` via `Fintype.sum_bool` distribution. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_dml_isAsymLinear` | `theorem` | Headline AIPW DML AL, retargeted to use `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear`. Conclusion: `IsAsymLinear (dmlChernozhukovEstimator (aipwGeneralMoment S _) _ _ _) S.ОёвӮҖ (fun z => вҲ’JвӮҖ_inv В· aipwMomentFunctional S.О·вӮҖ z S.ОёвӮҖ)`. **Refactored 2026-05-06:** dropped the вҲҖ-quantified `h_L2 : вҲҖ О· вҲҲ H_Оө Оө, вҖҰ` hypothesis (and the `hОө_pos` / `hОө_le` extracts that only fed the now-removed `aipw_neymanOrthog` / `aipwHasDirDeriv` calls). Now takes per-О·МӮ_n LВІ: `h_mu_diff_memLp : вҲҖ n Пү a, MemLp (ОјМӮ(n,Пү,a) вҲ’ Ој_val a) 2 P_X` and `h_e_diff_memLp : вҲҖ n Пү, MemLp (ГӘ(n,Пү) вҲ’ e_val) 2 P_X`. Body builds the per-О·МӮ_n bilinear remainder bound directly via `aipw_remainder_bound` (followed by `Fintype.sum_bool` to align with the `ПҒвӮҒ В· ПҒвӮӮ = (вҖ–О”Ој_TвҖ– + вҖ–О”Ој_FвҖ–) В· вҖ–О”eвҖ–` shape) and feeds it as `_hBR_at` to the refactored abstract theorem. The GГўteaux-derivative `aipwHasDirDeriv` / `aipw_neymanOrthog` wrappers (and the underlying `Neyman.lean` / `NeymanAux/` machinery) are no longer on the AL critical path and were archived to `Estimation/ATE/Archived/` on 2026-05-06. **Proved.** |

### 12g.7. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/Riesz.lean`

Riesz representer pattern for linear-in-Оі functionals (ATE, policy value, average derivative). Light skeleton; concrete instances live next to their estimators.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `RieszRepresentation H_Оі Оі_target L P_X` | structure | A Riesz representer `ОұвӮҖ : X вҶ’ в„қ` with `representation : вҲҖ Оі, L Оі = вҲ« x, ОұвӮҖ x В· Оі x вҲӮP_X`, plus measurability and LВ№ witnesses. |
| `rieszScore Оі_target L proj_X Y_obs Оі Оұ Оё z` | `noncomputable def` | The Riesz orthogonal score `m(z; Оё, Оі, Оұ) := L Оі + Оұ(proj_X z) В· (Y_obs z вҲ’ Оі_target Оі (proj_X z)) вҲ’ Оё`. |
| `rieszScore_meanZero` | `theorem` | Under the regression-orthogonality assumption `вҲ« z, ОұвӮҖ(proj_X z)В·(Y_obs z вҲ’ Оі_target ОівӮҖ (proj_X z)) вҲӮP_Z = 0`, the population score at the truth `(ОівӮҖ, ОұвӮҖ, L ОівӮҖ)` integrates to zero. **Proved.** |
| `rieszScore_bilinearRem` | `theorem` | Bilinear remainder identity: `вҲ« z, m(z; L ОівӮҖ, Оі, Оұ) вҲӮP_Z вҲ’ вҲ« z, m(z; L ОівӮҖ, ОівӮҖ, ОұвӮҖ) вҲӮP_Z = вҲ’ вҲ« x, (Оұ x вҲ’ ОұвӮҖ x)В·(Оі_target Оі x вҲ’ Оі_target ОівӮҖ x) вҲӮP_X` under pushforward `P_X = P_Z.map proj_X`, dual regression-orthogonality, integrability of the perturbed residual, and integrability of the four product terms. **Proved.** |

### 12g.8. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/Parametric.lean`

Parametric (no-nuisance) specialisation of `GeneralMoment` (`H = Unit`). With `H = Unit`, the abstract `dmlChernozhukovEstimator` collapses to the classical parametric one-step estimator and the rescaled remainder is identically zero. OLS, IV-2SLS, MLE instantiate this pattern.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `parametricMoment m_par ОёвӮҖ JвӮҖ hJ m_meas` | `noncomputable def` | Constructs `GeneralMoment О© Ој Z P_Z Unit` from a user-supplied moment `m_par : в„қ вҶ’ Z вҶ’ в„қ`, target `ОёвӮҖ`, and Jacobian `JвӮҖ вү  0`. Bilinear seminorms are zero (no nuisance to perturb). |
| `parametric_asymptoticLinear` | `theorem` | `IsAsymLinear (dmlChernozhukovEstimator (parametricMoment вҖҰ) sample split (fun _ _ => ())) ОёвӮҖ (fun z => вҲ’JвӮҖвҒ»В№ В· m_par ОёвӮҖ z) sample split.foldB` from population mean-zero `вҲ« m_par(ОёвӮҖ, В·) dP_Z = 0` and LВІ-finite `Integrable ((m_par ОёвӮҖ)ВІ) P_Z`. The remainder collapses identically: the parametric one-step estimator equals the influence-function partial sum exactly. **Proved.** |

### 12g.9. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/SecondStageOperator.lean`

Abstract second-stage regression operator and stability framework for the DR-Learner CATE estimator (Kennedy 2023). Operator-level / target-agnostic content: takes a real-valued pseudo-outcome `f : Оі Г— Bool Г— в„қ вҶ’ в„қ` and returns a real-valued estimate at a query point `x : Оі`. No AIPW algebra, no causal content. Mirrors `def:est-cate-second-stage` and `def:est-cate-stability` in `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/dr_learner_cate.tex`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SecondStageOperator О© Ој Оі` | structure | Abstract bundle: `evalAt : в„• вҶ’ О© вҶ’ (Оі Г— Bool Г— в„қ вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Оі вҶ’ в„қ` taking sample size, randomness scope, pseudo-outcome, and query point; plus a minimal joint-measurability stub `meas_evalAt_const`. Linearity in input is left as a separate predicate `IsLinearInInput`. |
| `SecondStageOperator.IsLinearInInput op` | `Prop` | `evalAt n Пү (f + g) x = evalAt n Пү f x + evalAt n Пү g x`. Linear smoothers satisfy this; tree mean estimators with random splits typically do not. |
| `SecondStageOperator.oracleEstimator op f` | `def` | Operator applied to a fixed "true" pseudo-outcome `f` (Kennedy's `\tilde\tau_n`). |
| `SecondStageOperator.oracleRiskScale op f target x n` | `noncomputable def`, `в„қ` | `R^*_n(x) := вҲҡ(вҲ« Пү, (op.evalAt n Пү f x вҲ’ target x)ВІ вҲӮОј)`. Pointwise oracle risk scale. |
| `IsConditionalBias` | removed | The old placeholder `Prop` alias (`True`) was removed. Conditional-bias identification now enters through a caller-supplied `BiasIdent` predicate parameter on `Stable` and `oracle_expansion`. |
| `Stable op target d_n x BiasIdent` | `Prop` | Def 4 stability: for any `(fHat_n, f, bHat_n)` with `BiasIdent fHat_n f bHat_n`, `d_n вҶ’_p 0` implies the operator-level discrepancy `op.evalAt(fHat_n) вҲ’ op.evalAt(f) вҲ’ op.evalAt(bHat_n)` is `o_p(R^*_n(x))`. |
| `oracle_expansion` | `theorem` | Headline: under `Stable op target d_n x BiasIdent`, `Tendsto_inProb d_n 0`, and a `BiasIdent fHat_n f bHat_n` witness, the operator-level oracle expansion `op.evalAt(fHat_n) вҲ’ op.evalAt(f) вҲ’ op.evalAt(bHat_n) = o_p(R^*_n(x))` holds. **Proved by Codex** (one-line application of `Stable`). |

**Refactored 2026-05-09:** the placeholder `IsConditionalBias := True` was replaced by an explicit Prop predicate parameter `BiasIdent` that callers supply, restoring semantic content.

### 12g.10. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/LinearSmoother.lean`

Abstract weighted-HГ¶lder bound for linear-smoother second-stage operators. Mirrors `prop:est-cate-linear-smoother-bound` in `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/dr_learner_cate.tex`.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LinearSmootherOp О© Ој Оі` | structure (extends `SecondStageOperator`) | Adds `weights : в„• вҶ’ О© вҶ’ Оі вҶ’ (Оі Г— Bool Г— в„қ) вҶ’ в„қ`. The actual weighted-sum identity `evalAt n Пү f x = ОЈ w В· f(z_i)` is supplied as a separate predicate `IsLinearSmoother` rather than baked in. |
| `LinearSmootherOp.IsLinearSmoother op n Пү x B w xs` | `Prop` | Predicate witnessing the weighted-sum identity `вҲҖ f, op.evalAt n Пү f x = ОЈ_{i вҲҲ B} w i В· f (xs i)` for explicit weights `w : О№ вҶ’ в„қ` and data points `xs : О№ вҶ’ Оі Г— Bool Г— в„қ`. |
| `WeightedNorm B w g p` | `noncomputable def`, `в„қ` | Weighted Lбө– norm `(ОЈ_i вҲҲ B, |w i| / (ОЈ_j |w j|) В· |g i|^p)^(1/p)`. Index type `О№` abstract; uses `Real.rpow`. |
| `WeightedNorm_nonneg` | `lemma` | Non-negativity of `WeightedNorm`. **Proved.** |
| `smoother_bias_holder` | `theorem` | `|op.evalAt n Пү (fun z => g z.1) x| вүӨ c_n В· WeightedNorm B w (g вҲҳ xs.1) 1` under `IsLinearSmoother op n Пү x B w xs` and `ОЈ |w| вүӨ c_n`. **Proved by Codex** via triangle inequality + factor-out-`ОЈ|w|` rearrangement of the weighted-LВ№ norm. |
| `smoother_bias_product_holder` | `theorem` | HГ¶lder for `g = gвӮҒВ·gвӮӮ`: `|op.evalAt n Пү (fun z => gвӮҒ(z.1) В· gвӮӮ(z.1)) x| вүӨ c_n В· вҖ–gвӮҒ вҲҳ xs.1вҖ–_{w,p} В· вҖ–gвӮӮ вҲҳ xs.1вҖ–_{w,q}` under `IsLinearSmoother`, `ОЈ |w| вүӨ c_n`, and `Real.HolderConjugate p q`. **Proved by Codex** via `Real.inner_le_Lp_mul_Lq_of_nonneg` applied to `f i := |w i|^{1/p} В· |gвӮҒ (xs i).1|`, `g i := |w i|^{1/q} В· |gвӮӮ (xs i).1|` with `1/p + 1/q = 1`. |

### 12g.11. `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AutoDebias/` вҖ” Automatic debiasing module

Reusable construction that converts any **linear-in-regression target** (or, more generally, any **regression-nuisance moment**) into a Neyman-orthogonal score by adding the Riesz-representer correction `Оұ(X)В·(Y вҲ’ g(X))`. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/automatic_debiasing.tex`. The module contains an algebraic core (linear functional case), a general-moment layer, and a one-shot Auto-DML estimator with its asymptotic-linearity theorem.

#### 12g.11.a `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AutoDebias/Linear.lean` вҖ” Linear functional core

Defs 1, 3, 5 and Props 4, 6 of the tex.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `LinRegFnSys` | structure | Linear regression-functional system: types `Z, X, H_Оі` with measure/AddCommGroup/Module instances, `proj_X, Y_obs, Оі_target` (linear in `H_Оі`), observation-level linear-in-Оі functional `m_lin : Z вҶ’ H_Оі вҶ’ в„қ`, regression truth `gвӮҖ`, pushforward `P_X = P_Z.map proj_X`, and the integrated dual-orthogonality `regression_resid_orthog`. |
| `L_of_m S Оі` | `noncomputable def` | Population linear functional `Оі вҶҰ вҲ« m_lin(z, Оі) dP_Z`. |
| `L_of_m_add`, `L_of_m_smul` | `theorem`, `theorem` | Linearity of `L_of_m` in `Оі` (under integrability). **Proved.** |
| `linRieszScore S Оі Оұ Оё z` | `noncomputable def` | Alias of `rieszScore` from `Estimation.OrthogonalMoments.Riesz` specialised to a `LinRegFnSys`. |
| `linRieszScore_meanZero` | `theorem` | Population score at the truth `(gвӮҖ, ОұвӮҖ, L gвӮҖ)` integrates to zero (Prop 6, mean-zero). Wraps `rieszScore_meanZero` via `regression_resid_orthog`. **Proved.** |
| `linRieszScore_directional_g_zero` | `theorem` | Directional zero in the regression direction: `(вҲ« m_lin z ОҪ_g dP_Z) вҲ’ вҲ« rep.ОұвӮҖ В· Оі_target ОҪ_g dP_X = 0` (Prop 6, part b). Direct from `rep.representation`. **Proved.** |
| `linRieszScore_directional_Оұ_zero` | `theorem` | Directional zero in the representer direction: residual orthogonality of `ОҪ_Оұ` against `Y_obs вҲ’ Оі_target gвӮҖ` (Prop 6, part c). Direct call to `regression_resid_orthog`. **Proved.** |
| `linRieszLoss S Оұ` | `noncomputable def` | Linear Riesz loss `вҲ« (Оі_target Оұ x)ВІ dP_X вҲ’ 2 В· L_of_m S Оұ` (Def 3). |
| `linRieszLoss_excess_eq_l2dist` | `theorem` | Prop 4, second half: `linRieszLoss Оұ вҲ’ linRieszLoss ОұвӮҖ_idx = вҲ« (Оі_target Оұ вҲ’ Оі_target ОұвӮҖ_idx)ВІ dP_X` when `rep.ОұвӮҖ = Оі_target ОұвӮҖ_idx` pointwise. Complete-the-square via `rep.representation`. **Proved.** |
| `linRieszLoss_FOC_iff_representer` | `theorem` | Prop 4, first half: `ОұвӮҖ_idx` is a directional minimizer of `linRieszLoss` along every line `ОұвӮҖ_idx + t вҖў ОҪ` iff `L(ОҪ) = вҲ« Оі_target ОұвӮҖ_idx В· Оі_target ОҪ dP_X` for every `ОҪ`. Parabolic-identity FOC argument with quadratic-coefficient case split on `a = вҲ« (Оі_target ОҪ)ВІ dP_X`. **Proved.** |

#### 12g.11.b `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AutoDebias/General.lean` вҖ” General-moment layer

Defs 8, 9, 10, 12, 13 and Prop 11 of the tex.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `RegNuisanceMomentSys` | structure | Regression-nuisance moment system. Same data layer as `LinRegFnSys`; replaces the linear functional `m_lin` with a general moment `M : в„қ вҶ’ H вҶ’ в„қ` (kernel `m : H вҶ’ Z вҶ’ в„қ вҶ’ в„қ`, `M_truth : M ОёвӮҖ gвӮҖ = 0`, `m_population : M Оё g = вҲ« m g z Оё dP_Z`) and a precomputed Gateaux derivative `D_g_M : H вҶ’ в„қ` with linearity laws. |
| `AutoDebiasRepresenter S` | structure | The representer `ОұвӮҖ : X вҶ’ в„қ` characterised by `D_g_M ОҪ = вҲ« ОұвӮҖ В· Оі_target ОҪ dP_X` (Def 9). |
| `autoDebiasedScore S g Оұ Оё z` | `noncomputable def` | Observation-level form: `m g z Оё + Оұ(proj_X z) В· (Y_obs z вҲ’ Оі_target g (proj_X z))`. |
| `autoDebiasedMoment S g Оұ Оё` | `noncomputable def` | `вҲ« z, autoDebiasedScore S g Оұ Оё z вҲӮP_Z` (Def 10). |
| `autoDebiasedMoment_meanZero_at_truth` | `theorem` | Prop 11, part a: `autoDebiasedMoment S gвӮҖ rep.ОұвӮҖ ОёвӮҖ = 0` from `M_truth` + `regression_resid_orthog` + `m_population`. **Proved.** |
| `autoDebiasedMoment_directional_g_zero` | `theorem` | Prop 11, part b: `D_g_M ОҪ_g вҲ’ вҲ« rep.ОұвӮҖ В· Оі_target ОҪ_g dP_X = 0`. Direct from `rep.representation`. **Proved.** |
| `autoDebiasedMoment_directional_Оұ_zero` | `theorem` | Prop 11, part c: residual orthogonality of arbitrary measurable `ОҪ_Оұ`. Direct call to `regression_resid_orthog`. **Proved.** |
| `genRieszLoss S Оұ` | `noncomputable def` | General Riesz loss `вҲ« (Оі_target Оұ x)ВІ dP_X вҲ’ 2 В· D_g_M Оұ` (Def 12). FOC вҶ” representer characterisation is structurally the same as `linRieszLoss_FOC_iff_representer`; not re-proved here. |
| `finDiffRieszLoss S Z_data Оё_hat g_hat Оө Оұ C n Пү` | `noncomputable def` | Finite-difference representer loss (Def 13): empirical second moment minus a centred finite-difference approximation of `D_g M(ОёМӮ_n, Дқ_n)[Оұ]` with perturbation scale `Оө n`. Pure definition; intended target of future RieszNet-style analysis. |

#### 12g.11.c `Estimation/OrthogonalMoments/AutoDebias/DML.lean` вҖ” Auto-DML estimator and AL theorem

Def 6 and Thm 7 of the tex.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `linAutoNuisance S` | `def` | Joint nuisance type `S.H_Оі Г— (S.X вҶ’ в„қ)` with componentwise `AddCommGroup` (`Prod.instAddCommGroup`) and `Module в„қ` (`Prod.instModule`). |
| `linAutoGeneralMoment Ој S rep Оө hОө_nn h_score_meas` | `noncomputable def` | `GeneralMoment` instance over `linAutoNuisance S`. `m (Оі, Оұ) z Оё := linRieszScore S Оі Оұ Оё z`; `О·вӮҖ := (S.gвӮҖ, rep.ОұвӮҖ)`; `ОёвӮҖ := L_of_m S S.gвӮҖ`; `H_Оө := { (Оі, Оұ) | вҖ–Оі_target Оі вҲ’ Оі_target gвӮҖвҖ–_{LВІ(P_X)} вүӨ Оө вҲ§ вҖ–Оұ вҲ’ ОұвӮҖвҖ–_{LВІ(P_X)} вүӨ Оө }`; `ПҒвӮҒ`/`ПҒвӮӮ` are the corresponding LВІ norms; `JвӮҖ = вҲ’1`. The measurability witness `h_score_meas : вҲҖ О· Оё, Measurable (fun z => linRieszScore S О·.1 О·.2 Оё z)` and non-negativity `hОө_nn : 0 вүӨ Оө` are passed as parameters because `linAutoNuisance` does not internally constrain `Оұ : X вҶ’ в„қ` to be measurable. |
| `linAuto_meanZero` | `theorem` | `MeanZero` of the linear Auto-DML moment at the truth. Direct call to `linRieszScore_meanZero`. **Proved.** |
| `linAuto_int_pred S rep О·` | `private def` | Per-О· integrability bundle (10 components) exposing the exact API shape required by `rieszScore_bilinearRem`. Used as a hypothesis in `linAuto_bilinearRem`. |
| `linAuto_bilinearRem` | `theorem` | Bilinear remainder bound: `\|вҲ« z, score О· z ОёвӮҖ вҲӮP_Z\| вүӨ C В· ПҒвӮҒ(О·, О·вӮҖ) В· ПҒвӮӮ(О·, О·вӮҖ)` for `О· вҲҲ H_Оө` with `linAuto_int_pred S rep О·` and explicit `MemLp` witnesses for the two LВІ(P_X) differences. Proof factors via `rieszScore_bilinearRem` then applies HГ¶lder-2 (`integral_mul_norm_le_Lp_mul_Lq`). **Proved.** |
| `linAutoDMLEstimator S rep Оө hОө_nn h_score_meas sample split О·_hat` | `noncomputable def` | One-shot linear Auto-DML estimator (Def 6), built as `dmlChernozhukovEstimator (linAutoGeneralMoment Ој S rep Оө hОө_nn h_score_meas) sample split О·_hat`. Equivalently: `(1/\|B(n)\|) ОЈ_{i вҲҲ B(n)} [m_lin(Z_i; Дқ_n) + ОұМӮ_n(X_i)В·(Y_i вҲ’ Дқ_n(X_i))]`. |
| `linAutoDML_asymptoticLinear` | `theorem` | Thm 7 headline: `IsAsymLinear (linAutoDMLEstimator вҖҰ) (L_of_m S S.gвӮҖ) (fun z => вҲ’JвӮҖ_inv В· linRieszScore S S.gвӮҖ rep.ОұвӮҖ ОёвӮҖ z) sample split.foldB` under `MeanZero`, finite LВІ of the oracle score, per-О· bilinear remainder, the standard fold-A measurability/integrability hypotheses, and the score-difference / individual / product rate hypotheses inherited from `dml_chernozhukov_asymptoticLinear`. Proof: direct composition. **Proved.** |

The module skips the ATE-equivalence remark from the tex (Remark 7) per design.

## 13'''. `Estimation/CATE/` вҖ” DR-Learner CATE estimation (Kennedy 2023)

Specific-system layer instantiating `BackdoorEstimationSystem` for the function-valued target `П„вӮҖ(x) = Ој_val(true, x) вҲ’ Ој_val(false, x)`. Mirrors `doc/basic_concepts/po/estimation/dr_learner_cate.tex`. The DR-Learner is built on top of the same AIPW pseudo-outcome already used for ATE; the new content is (1) the function-valued target with `condDistrib`-style pointwise interpretation, (2) the DR conditional-bias identity (Prop 2 / `prop:est-cate-dr-bias-identity`, the milestone), and (3) the second-stage regression operator with stability hypothesis driving the oracle expansion (Theorem 5).

### 13'''.a `Estimation/CATE/Setup.lean`

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `CATEEstimationSystem P Оі` | structure (extends `BackdoorEstimationSystem`) | Empty extension вҖ” the CATE target `П„_val` is purely derived. All back-door substrate (consistency, exchangeability, overlap, integrability, `Ој_val` / `e_val`, Пғ(X)-compatibility) is inherited. |
| `CATEEstimationSystem.П„_val S x` | `def`, `в„қ` | Value-space CATE: `П„_val x := Ој_val true x вҲ’ Ој_val false x`. Definition `def:est-cate-system`. |
| `CATEEstimationSystem.measurable_П„_val` | `lemma` | Measurability of `П„_val`. **Proved.** |
| `CATEEstimationSystem.tau_val_eq_CATE` | `theorem` | `(fun Пү => П„_val (factualX Пү)) =бөҗ Ој[Y(true) вҲ’ Y(false) | Пғ(X)]` under the back-door assumptions. **Proved by Codex** (combine `Ој_compat true/false` with `condExp_sub`). |

| `phiвӮҖ S z` | `noncomputable def` | True pseudo-outcome `ПҶ_0 := ПҶ_{О·_0}`. |
