| `О“`, `chiSqOverlapG`, `chiSqOverlap_eqG` | ChiSqOverlap | `О“ = mвӮҖОұВІ/gвӮҒ + mвӮҖ(Оұ+ОІ/gвӮҒ)ВІ/(1вҲ’gвӮҒ) + mвӮҖВІОІВІ/(gвӮҒВІ(1вҲ’mвӮҖ))`; `chiSqOverlapG О» О»' = 1 + (О“/K)В·ОЈ_j s_j s'_j` | **Exact single-obs ПҮВІ-overlap closed form** (linear-`О”` terms cancel by construction; `О“=2(ОұВІ+2ОұОІ+3ОІВІ)` at `mвӮҖ=gвӮҒ=1/2`). |
| `QfalseG`, `QpertG`, `QtrueG`, `О“_nonneg` | LowerBound | `PМӮ^вҠ—n`; `QО»^вҠ—n`; `(1/2^K)ОЈ_О» QО»^вҠ—n`; `0вүӨО“` | The two `n`-sample laws and nonnegativity of the overlap coefficient. |
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_QtrueG_QfalseG`, `chiSqDiv_QtrueG_QfalseG_le_one`, `tvDist_QfalseG_QtrueG_le_half` | LowerBound | mixture 2nd-moment identity; `О“вүӨ1`, `2nВІ(О“/2)ВІвүӨKВ·log 2 вҮ’ ПҮВІвүӨ1`; вҮ’ `tvDistвүӨ1/2` | ПҮВІ indistinguishability via `ingster_bound` at `Оі=О“/2`. |
| `minimax_lower_bound_gen` | LowerBound | budgets + `О“вүӨ1` + `2nВІ(О“/2)ВІвүӨKВ·log 2` + `Measurable est` вҮ’ `1/4 вүӨ minimaxMiss mМӮ Дқ Оөg Оөm n est (gвӮҒОІ(Оұ+ОІ)/(2(gвӮҒВІвҲ’ОІВІ)))` | **Headline (general constant center, unconditional).** No estimator is within `s вүҚ вҲҡ(ОөgВ·Оөm)` of the true ATE w.p. `>3/4` over the class, for any constant bounded-away `(mвӮҖ,gвӮҖ,gвӮҒ)`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `minimax_lower_bound_mse_gen` | MSE | same hyps вҮ’ `вҲғ p : InClassDGP mМӮ Дқ Оөg Оөm, (gвӮҒОІ(Оұ+ОІ)/(2(gвӮҒВІвҲ’ОІВІ)))ВІ/4 вүӨ nMSE p.2.valid n est` | **Expected-risk form (general center).** MSE `вүҘ sВІ/4` on some in-class DGP; the `(1вҲ’Оі)`-factored consequence. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.9 Cell-varying center вҖ” `VaryingCenterCase1/` (`Construction/Gap/Membership/ChiSqOverlap/LowerBound.lean`, `namespace VarConstr`) + `Reduction/WitnessMixture.lean` + `VaryingCenterCase1/Ingster.lean` (non-uniform Ingster)

Generalizes В§13sa.8 from a *constant* center to a **cell-varying center**: the
nuisance estimates are arbitrary *functions* of the pair index `j : Fin K` (bounded
away from `{0,1}`), genuinely non-constant over the covariate space вҖ” the finite-model
analogue of the paper's functional center, for centers constant *within* each
Rademacher pair (the residual within-pair variation is gap (i'') of the В§13sa scope
note). Because the center is shared by a pair's two positions, the per-pair ATE gap is
still **exactly `О»`-independent** (no Taylor remainder), but the ПҮВІ-overlap coefficient
`О“вұј` now **varies per pair**, so the bound needs the non-uniform Ingster inequality.
Two reusable pieces of infrastructure were added; everything **sorry-free + axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | File | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `mixture_two_point_lower_bound` | Reduction/WitnessMixture | null in-class DGP + finite weighted family of in-class alternatives with **per-component** separation `2s вүӨ \|ate gвӮҖ вҲ’ ate(gО» i)\|` + `tvDist(QвӮҖ, mixture) вүӨ c` вҮ’ `(1вҲ’c)/2 вүӨ minimaxMiss` | **One-sided mixture two-point lemma.** Sharper than `TwoPointWitness`: the alternatives need not share one ATE вҖ” a per-component *lower* bound suffices (the asymmetric testing argument measures each component at its own ATE). The abstract key enabling fully-functional centers. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `ingster_bound_general` | Ingster | `(вҲҖj,0вүӨd j)`, `ОЈвұј d j вүӨ 1`, `(nВІ/2)ОЈвұј(d j)ВІ вүӨ log 2` вҮ’ `ОЈ_{О»,О»'}(2^K)вҒ»ВІ(1+ОЈвұј d jВ·s_j s'_j)^n вүӨ 2` | **Non-uniform Ingster ПҮВІ bound.** Per-pair coefficients `d j` (vs the constant `2Оі/K` of `ingster_bound`); factorizes to `вҲҸвұј cosh(nВ·d j)` via `Finset.prod_univ_sum`. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |
| `VarConstr` | Construction | `structure` (fields `Оұ ОІ : в„қ`, `mвӮҖ gвӮҖ gвӮҒ : Fin K вҶ’ в„қ` + per-pair bounded-away/smallness inequalities) | Bundles the bump scalars and the **cell-varying** center (one bounded-away value per pair). |
| `mhatV`, `ghatV`, `mPertV`, `gPertV`, `validDGP_hatV`, `validDGP_pertV` | Construction | `mМӮ x=mвӮҖ x.1`; `Дқ(1,x)=gвӮҒ x.1, Дқ(0,x)=gвӮҖ x.1`; `mО»=mвӮҖ x.1(1вҲ’(ОІ/gвӮҒ x.1)О”)`; `gО»(1,x)=(gвӮҒ x.1+ОұО”)/(1вҲ’(ОІ/gвӮҒ x.1)О”)` ; both `ValidDGP` | The cell-varying perturbed DGP, indexed through the pair index `x.1`. |
| `ate_ghatV`, `ate_gPertV`, `ate_gapV`, `ate_gap_nonneg` | Gap | `ate gО»` is a `О»`-independent sum over pairs; `ate gО» вҲ’ ate Дқ = (card)вҒ»В№В·2ОІ(Оұ+ОІ)В·ОЈвұј gвӮҒвұј/(gвӮҒвұјВІвҲ’ОІВІ) вүҘ 0` | **Exact per-pair ATE gap** (sign-invariant within each pair), nonnegative. |
| `l2sq_mPertV_le`, `l2sq_gPertV_true_le`, `inClassV` | Membership | per-pair budgets `(mвӮҖвұјВ·ОІ/gвӮҒвұј)ВІвүӨОөm`, `gвӮҒвұјВІ(Оұ+ОІ)ВІ/(gвӮҒвұјвҲ’ОІ)ВІвүӨОөg` (all `j`) вҮ’ `InClass mМӮ Дқ Оөg Оөm mО» gО»` | Class membership via average-of-bounded-terms (per-pair sup bounds are *sufficient*). |
| `О“V`, `О“V_nonneg`, `chiSqOverlapV`, `chiSqOverlap_eqV` | ChiSqOverlap | `О“вұј = mвӮҖвұјОұВІ/gвӮҒвұј + mвӮҖвұј(Оұ+ОІ/gвӮҒвұј)ВІ/(1вҲ’gвӮҒвұј) + mвӮҖвұјВІОІВІ/(gвӮҒвұјВІ(1вҲ’mвӮҖвұј))`; `chiSqOverlapV О» О»' = 1 + ОЈвұј (О“вұј/K)В·s_j s'_j` | **Per-pair ПҮВІ-overlap closed form** (linear-`О”` terms cancel; coefficient varies per pair вҶ’ feeds `ingster_bound_general` with `d j = О“вұј/K`). |
| `chiSqDiv_QtrueV_QfalseV_le_one`, `tvDist_QfalseV_QtrueV_le_half` | LowerBound | `ОЈвұј О“вұј/K вүӨ 1`, `(nВІ/2)ОЈвұј(О“вұј/K)ВІ вүӨ log 2` вҮ’ `ПҮВІвүӨ1` вҮ’ `tvDistвүӨ1/2` | ПҮВІ indistinguishability via the non-uniform Ingster bound. |
| `minimax_lower_bound_var` | LowerBound | per-pair budgets + `ОЈвұј О“вұј/K вүӨ 1` + `(nВІ/2)ОЈвұј(О“вұј/K)ВІ вүӨ log 2` + `Measurable est` вҮ’ `1/4 вүӨ minimaxMiss mМӮ Дқ Оөg Оөm n est (ВҪ(ate gО» вҲ’ ate Дқ))` | **Headline (cell-varying center, Case 1 `Оөg вүі Оөm`).** No estimator is within `s` of the true ATE w.p. `>3/4`, for a nuisance center varying arbitrarily across pairs. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.9b Cell-varying center, Case 2 (`Оөm > Оөg`) вҖ” `VaryingCenterCase2/Construction/Gap/Membership/ChiSqOverlap/LowerBound.lean`

The **symmetric second construction** (JinвҖ“Syrgkanis 2024 eq. (18), `f_n > e_n'`).
`VaryingCenterCase1`'s Case-1 construction attains the product rate `s вүҚ вҲҡ(ОөgВ·Оөm)` only when the
outcome budget dominates (`Оөg вүі Оөm`); this file covers the opposite regime by
**swapping the two nuisances' roles**: the propensity deviation is the large one
(`O(Оұ+ОІ)`, carrying `вҲҡОөm`) and the outcome deviation the small one (`O(ОІ)`, carrying
`вҲҡОөg`). In the finite-cell model the construction is `gО»(0,В·)=gвӮҖ`,
`gО»(1,x)=gвӮҒвұј/D`, `mО»=mвӮҖвұј(1+ОұgвӮҒвұјО”)D` with `D=1+(ОІ/gвӮҒвұј)О”вҲ’ОұОІ`. Although non-linear in
`О»`, the observed masses are polynomial in `О”` (the `D` cancels in `mО»В·gО»(1)`; the
propensity collapses to `mО»=mвӮҖвұј(1+ОәвұјО”)`, `Оәвұј=ОІ/gвӮҒвұј+ОұgвӮҒвұјвҲ’ОұВІОІgвӮҒвұј`). Reuses
`VaryingCenterCase1/Ingster`'s `ingster_bound_general`. **All proved, axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | File | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `VarConstr2` | VaryingCenterCase2/Construction | `structure` (fields `Оұ ОІ : в„қ`, `mвӮҖ gвӮҖ gвӮҒ : Fin K вҶ’ в„қ` + per-pair `hgU`/`hОә`/`hmU` inequalities) | Bundles the bump scalars and cell-varying center for the propensity-dominant construction; `hgU` forces `D>0` and `gО»(1)вүӨ1`, `hОә` keeps `mО»вүҘ0`, `hmU` keeps `mО»вүӨ1`. |
| `Оә`, `D2`, `mhat2`, `ghat2`, `mPert2`, `gPert2`, `mPert2_eq`, `validDGP_hat2`, `validDGP_pert2` | VaryingCenterCase2/Construction | `Оәвұј=ОІ/gвӮҒвұј+ОұgвӮҒвұјвҲ’ОұВІОІgвӮҒвұј`; `D=1+(ОІ/gвӮҒ)О”вҲ’ОұОІ`; `mО»=mвӮҖ(1+ОұgвӮҒО”)D`, `gО»(1)=gвӮҒ/D`; `mPert2_eq : mО»=mвӮҖ(1+ОәО”)`; both `ValidDGP` | The Case-2 perturbed DGP and its affine `О”`-collapse (via `О”ВІ=1`), proved valid. |
| `ate_ghat2`, `ate_gPert2`, `ate_gap2`, `ate_gap2_nonneg`, `gPert2_true_eq2`, `clearedDenom_pos`, `denomE_pos` | VaryingCenterCase2/Gap | `ate gО»` is `О»`-independent; `ate gО» вҲ’ ate Дқ = (card)вҒ»В№В·2ОІВ·ОЈвұј gвӮҒвұј(ОұgвӮҒвұјВІ(1вҲ’ОұОІ)+ОІ)/Eвұј вүҘ 0`, `Eвұј=gвӮҒвұјВІ(1вҲ’ОұОІ)ВІвҲ’ОІВІ` | **Exact per-pair ATE gap** (cleared treated arm `gвӮҒВІ/(gвӮҒ(1вҲ’ОұОІ)+ОІО”)`; the two-position sum collapses `ПғВІ`), nonnegative. |
| `l2sq_mPert2_le`, `l2sq_gPert2_true_le`, `gPert2_true_sub`, `inClass2` | VaryingCenterCase2/Membership | per-pair budgets `(mвӮҖвұјОәвұј)ВІвүӨОөm` (exact, propensity), `ОІВІ(ОұgвӮҒвұј+1)ВІ/(1вҲ’ОІ/gвӮҒвұјвҲ’ОұОІ)ВІвүӨОөg` (worst-case, outcome) вҮ’ `InClass mМӮ Дқ Оөg Оөm mО» gО»` | Class membership in the swapped regime: `mО»вҲ’mМӮ=mвӮҖОәО”` is **exact** (`O(Оұ+ОІ)`вҶ’`вҲҡОөm`), `gО»(1)вҲ’Дқ(1)=ОІ(ОұgвӮҒвҲ’О”)/D` is `O(ОІ)`вҶ’`вҲҡОөg`. |
| `О“V2`, `О“V2_nonneg`, `chiSqOverlapV2`, `obsReal_pert2_eq`, `chiSqOverlap_eq2` | VaryingCenterCase2/ChiSqOverlap | `О“вұј = mвӮҖвұјОұВІgвӮҒвұјВі + mвӮҖвұј(ОІ/gвӮҒвұј+ОұgвӮҒвұј(1вҲ’gвӮҒвұј)вҲ’ОұВІОІgвӮҒвұј)ВІ/(1вҲ’gвӮҒвұј) + mвӮҖвұјВІОәвұјВІ/(1вҲ’mвӮҖвұј)`; `chiSqOverlapV2 О» О»' = 1 + ОЈвұј(О“вұј/K)В·s_j s'_j` | **Per-pair ПҮВІ-overlap closed form** (constant part `=1`, the finite analogue of `E_О» qО»=pМӮ`; only the `О»В·О»'` cross term survives вҶ’ feeds `ingster_bound_general` with `d j=О“вұј/K`). |
| `chiSqDiv_Qtrue2_Qfalse2_le_one`, `tvDist_Qfalse2_Qtrue2_le_half` | VaryingCenterCase2/LowerBound | `ОЈвұј О“вұј/K вүӨ 1`, `(nВІ/2)ОЈвұј(О“вұј/K)ВІ вүӨ log 2` вҮ’ `ПҮВІвүӨ1` вҮ’ `tvDistвүӨ1/2` | ПҮВІ indistinguishability via the non-uniform Ingster bound. |
| `minimax_lower_bound_var2` | VaryingCenterCase2/LowerBound | propensity-dominant per-pair budgets + `ОЈвұј О“вұј/K вүӨ 1` + `(nВІ/2)ОЈвұј(О“вұј/K)ВІ вүӨ log 2` + `Measurable est` вҮ’ `1/4 вүӨ minimaxMiss mМӮ Дқ Оөg Оөm n est (ВҪ(ate gО» вҲ’ ate Дқ))` | **Headline (cell-varying center, Case 2 `Оөm > Оөg`).** The symmetric construction covering the regime `minimax_lower_bound_var` cannot; together they give the full product rate `вҲҡ(ОөgВ·Оөm)` in both regimes. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.10 Parametric `1/n` term вҖ” `Parametric.lean`

The additive `1/n` (parametric-variance) term of the paper's `О©(Оөg Оөm + 1/n)` bound,
via a **separate** two-point construction independent of the Rademacher/Ingster bias
machinery. Both hypotheses are single product laws (no mixture): a constant center
`(mвӮҖ,gвӮҖ,gвӮҒ)` and a constant **outcome shift** `Оҙ` on the treated arm only. The single-
observation ПҮВІ has the exact closed form `ПҮВІвӮҒ = mвӮҖОҙВІ/(gвӮҒ(1вҲ’gвӮҒ))`, so tensorizing
(`one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid`) and bounding `(1+ПҮВІвӮҒ)^n вүӨ e^{nПҮВІвӮҒ} вүӨ 2` keeps `tvDist вүӨ ВҪ`
once `nВ·ПҮВІвӮҒ вүӨ log 2` вҖ” i.e. `Оҙ вүҚ 1/вҲҡn`. The ATE gap is exactly `Оҙ`. **Axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `one_add_chiSqDiv_obsPert_obsNull` | `1 + chiSqDiv (obsLaw pert) (obsLaw null) = 1 + mвӮҖОҙВІ/(gвӮҒ(1вҲ’gвӮҒ))` | **Exact single-obs ПҮВІ** for a constant treated-arm shift `Оҙ` (the `d=0` block cancels; `d=1` block gives `mвӮҖ(1+ОҙВІ/(gвӮҒ(1вҲ’gвӮҒ)))`). |
| `parametric_lower_bound` | `0вүӨОҙ`, `gвӮҒ+ОҙвүӨ1`, `ОҙВІвүӨОөg`, `nВ·mвӮҖОҙВІ/(gвӮҒ(1вҲ’gвӮҒ)) вүӨ log 2`, `Measurable est` вҮ’ `1/4 вүӨ minimaxMiss (fun _=>mвӮҖ) (fun d _=>if d then gвӮҒ else gвӮҖ) Оөg Оөm n est (Оҙ/2)` | **Headline (parametric term).** With `Оҙ вүҚ 1/вҲҡn` (so `s = Оҙ/2 вүҚ 1/вҲҡn`, `sВІ вүҚ 1/n`), no estimator is within `s` of the ATE w.p. `>3/4` вҖ” the `1/n` minimax floor even with a perfectly-known nuisance class. **Proved, axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.11 Achievability вҖ” `AIPWEstimator.lean`

The **matching upper bound**: the fixed-center AIPW estimator attains the `вҲҡ(ОөgВ·Оөm)` rate.
Because the structure-agnostic class is centered at *fixed* nuisances `(mhat, ghat)` (not
data-estimated), the natural estimator is the plug-in AIPW **sample average**
`ОёМӮ(sample) = (1/n) ОЈ_i ПҲ_AIPW(Z_i; mhat, ghat)`. Everything stays in the finite `Obs C` /
`productLaw` world (no potential-outcome lift, no asymptotics), so the pairing with the
`minimaxMiss` converse is direct. This is the finite specialization of the general AIPW DML
asymptotic-normality theorem `Estimation/ATE/DML.lean` (`dml_ATE_tendstoNormal`). **All proved,
axiom-clean.**

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipwScoreFin mhat ghat z` | `def`, `в„қ` | Fixed-center AIPW score `(ДқвӮҒ x вҲ’ ДқвӮҖ x) + DВ·(YвҲ’ДқвӮҒ x)/mМӮ x вҲ’ (1вҲ’D)В·(YвҲ’ДқвӮҖ x)/(1вҲ’mМӮ x)` at one observation `z=(x,D,Y)` (`yReal` reads `Y:Bool` as `{0,1}`). |
| `estAIPW mhat ghat n` | `def`, `(Fin n вҶ’ Obs C) вҶ’ в„қ` | The estimator: sample average `(n)вҒ»В№ ОЈ_i aipwScoreFin mhat ghat (sample i)`. |
| `aipw_pop_mean` | `вҲ« aipwScoreFin вҖҰ вҲӮ(obsLaw hv) = ОЈ_z obsReal m g z В· aipwScoreFin вҖҰ` | Population mean of the score is the finite PMF-weighted sum. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_bias_identity` | `(ОЈ_z obsRealВ·score) вҲ’ ate g = (1/\|C\|) ОЈ_x [(1вҲ’m/mМӮ)(ДқвӮҒвҲ’gвӮҒ) вҲ’ (1вҲ’(1вҲ’m)/(1вҲ’mМӮ))(ДқвӮҖвҲ’gвӮҖ)]` | **Doubly-robust remainder identity** (the analytic heart): the plug-in bias factors into products of nuisance errors. **Proved.** |
| `exists_center_overlap` | `(вҲҖx, 0<mМӮ x<1) вҮ’ вҲғ Оө>0, вҲҖx, Оө вүӨ mМӮ x вҲ§ Оө вүӨ 1вҲ’mМӮ x` | Uniform center overlap from the finite `min` (so the score weights `1/mМӮ`, `1/(1вҲ’mМӮ)` are bounded). **Proved.** |
| `aipw_bias_bound` | `\|bias\| вүӨ ОөвҒ»В№В·((вҲҡl2sq(gвӮҒ,ДқвӮҒ)+вҲҡl2sq(gвӮҖ,ДқвӮҖ))В·вҲҡl2sq(m,mМӮ))` | Finite CauchyвҖ“Schwarz over uniform `P_X`: the bias is bounded by the product of `LВІ` nuisance errors. **Proved.** |
| `aipwScore_bound` | `\|aipwScoreFin mhat ghat z\| вүӨ 1 + 2/Оө` | The score is bounded (center valid + `mМӮ` `Оө`-off `{0,1}`). **Proved.** |
| `aipw_var_bound` | `variance (estAIPW mhat ghat n) (productLaw hv n) вүӨ (1+2/Оө)ВІ/n` | Variance of the i.i.d. average (via `variance_sum_pi` + bounded score). **Proved.** |

### 13sa.12 Capstone вҖ” `Optimality.lean` (minimax rate is `Оҳ(вҲҡ(ОөgВ·Оөm))`, attained by AIPW)

Pairs the converse (`minimax_lower_bound_var`) with the AIPW achievability into the
optimality statement. **All proved, axiom-clean** (`aipw_attains_minimax_rate` uses
only `propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound`).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `aipw_mean_eq` | `0<n вҮ’ вҲ« estAIPW вҖҰ вҲӮ(productLaw hv n) = ОЈ_z obsRealВ·score` | Mean of the i.i.d. average equals the single-obs population mean (via `measurePreserving_eval` + `aipw_pop_mean`). **Proved.** |
| `aipw_nMiss_le` | bias `вүӨ b`, `variance вүӨ V`, `b<s` вҮ’ `nMiss hv n (estAIPW вҖҰ) s вүӨ V/(sвҲ’b)ВІ` | **Bias-centered Chebyshev** miss bound (`meas_ge_le_variance_div_sq`; `MemLp` free on the finite domain via `eLpNorm_lt_top_of_finite`). **Proved.** |
| `aipw_inclass_bias_bound` | `InClass mhat ghat Оөg Оөm m g вҮ’ \|bias\| вүӨ ОөвҒ»В№В·(2вҲҡОөgВ·вҲҡОөm)` | Uniform-over-class bias bound: `aipw_bias_bound` + `l2sq вүӨ Оөg/Оөm` via `Real.sqrt` monotonicity. **Proved.** |
| `aipw_minimaxMiss_le` | `s > ОөвҒ»В№В·2вҲҡОөgвҲҡОөm вҮ’ minimaxMiss mhat ghat Оөg Оөm n (estAIPW вҖҰ) s вүӨ ((1+2/Оө)ВІ/n)/(s вҲ’ ОөвҒ»В№В·2вҲҡОөgвҲҡОөm)ВІ` | **Worst-case AIPW miss bound**, uniform over the class (`ciSup_le` + the three pieces), `вҶ’ 0` as `nВ·ОөgВ·Оөm вҶ’ вҲһ`. **Proved.** |
| `MinimaxRateThreshold mhat ghat Оөg Оөm n` | `structure` | Reusable "rate attained + unbeatable" vocabulary: a single `estimator` with a converse miss-lower-bound (`probLower` at `sepLower`) and an achievability miss-upper-bound (`missUpper` at `sepUpper`). |
| `aipw_attains_minimax_rate` | `noncomputable def`, `MinimaxRateThreshold P.mhatV P.ghatV Оөg Оөm n` | **Capstone.** For the cell-varying construction `P` with the lower-bound budgets/regularity: every estimator misses by `s_P = gap/2 вүҚ вҲҡ(ОөgВ·Оөm)` w.p. `вүҘ 1/4` (`converse` вҶҗ `minimax_lower_bound_var`), while the AIPW estimator's worst-case miss at any `sepUpper > ОөвҒ»В№В·2вҲҡОөgвҲҡОөm` (also `Оҳ(вҲҡ(ОөgВ·Оөm))`) is `вүӨ ((1+2/Оө)ВІ/n)/(sepUpperвҲ’вҖҰ)ВІ`. Hence the minimax ATE rate is `Оҳ(вҲҡ(ОөgВ·Оөm))` and DR/AIPW attains it (constant-factor, not constant-sharp). **Axiom-clean.** |

### 13sa.C Causal grounding вҖ” `Causal/Construction.lean`, `Causal/Bridge.lean`, `Causal/Minimax.lean`

Grounds the observed-data contrast `ate g` in the potential-outcome framework: the minimax lower bound is a bound on the genuine causal ATE `E[Y(1) вҲ’ Y(0)]`, identified by backdoor adjustment, not merely a regression contrast. `Construction.lean` builds вҖ” from a finite DGP `(m, g)` on a finite covariate `C` вҖ” the first concrete `POBackdoorSystem` in the library: a backdoor SCM `Un вҶ’ Xc вҶ’ A вҶ’ Y`, `Xc вҶ’ Y` with latent noise roots `Ea вҶ’ A`, `Ey вҶ’ Y` (`A = 1{Ea вүӨ m Xc}`, `Y = 1{Ey вүӨ g A Xc}`), lifted through `POSystem.ofSCM`. `Bridge.lean` defines the causal estimand and proves it equals the contrast.

| Declaration | Statement | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `dgpSCM` / `dgpPO` / `dgpBackdoor` | the witness gSCM, its `ofSCM` PO system, and the extracted `POBackdoorSystem (dgpPO m g) C` | The concrete finite backdoor construction (treatment `A`, outcome `Y`, covariate `Xc`); `structFun_measurable`/`isProbability_latent` discharged | **Defined** |
| `causalATE m g` | `(dgpBackdoor m g).ATE` | The causal estimand `E[Y(1) вҲ’ Y(0)]` of the constructed system | **Defined** |
| `dgp_consistency` / `dgp_unconfoundedness` / `dgp_propScore_eq_m` / `dgp_adjustedCE_eq_g` / `dgp_overlap` | the backdoor `Assumptions` fields | Consistency (free from `ofSCM_consistency`); unconfoundedness `A вҹӮ (Y(1),Y(0))вҲЈX` (via `ofSCM_condIndepCF_of_dSep` + `condIndepCF_congr_cond`); `P[A=1вҲЈX]=m(X)` and `E[YвҲЈA=d,X]=g(d,X)` (uniform-coordinate conditional expectations); two-sided overlap from strict `m вҲҲ (0,1)` | **Proved** |
| `causalATE_eq_ate` | `(hv : ValidDGP m g) (hso : вҲҖ x, m x вҲҲ Ioo 0 1) вҶ’ causalATE m g = ate g` | **Estimand bridge.** The causal ATE equals the observed-data contrast `ate g`, via `BackdoorEstimationSystem.ОёвӮҖ_eq_ATE` + uniform `P_X`. So every minimax lower bound transfers to `E[Y(1) вҲ’ Y(0)]`. Axiom-clean (standard axioms + `native_decide` for the graph checks) | **Proved** |
| `minimaxMissCausal mhat ghat Оөg Оөm n est s` | `вЁҶ p : InClassDGP mhat ghat Оөg Оөm, (productLaw p.valid n).real {x | s вүӨ \|est x вҲ’ causalATE p.m p.g\|}` | Causal-centered analogue of `minimaxMiss`, with each miss event centered at the concrete backdoor causal ATE instead of the observed-data contrast. | **Defined** |
| `minimax_lower_bound_var_causal` | `hm`, `hg`, strict `вҲҖj, P.mвӮҖ j * (1 + P.ОІ / P.gвӮҒ j) < 1`, budgets/regularity вҮ’ `1/4 вүӨ minimaxMissCausal P.mhatV P.ghatV вҖҰ gap/2` | Causal version of the cell-varying minimax lower bound. The additional strict-overlap hypothesis keeps the perturbed Var witnesses inside the overlap region, allowing `causalATE_eq_ate` to transfer the observed-data lower bound to `E[Y(1) вҲ’ Y(0)]`. | **Proved** |

---

## 13eff. `Estimation/Efficiency/` вҖ” Hahn (1998) semiparametric efficiency bound

The **third vertex** of the ATE efficiency triangle, joining achievability
(`dml_ATE_isAsymLinear`, В§13c) and the minimax lower-bound tower (В§13sa): the
*semiparametric efficiency bound* `= Var(efficient influence function)`, the
theorem that the AIPW influence function `ПҲ_AIPW` (В§13a.2) **is** Hahn's efficient
influence function, and the "role of the propensity score" (the bound does not
depend on whether the propensity score is known). Reference: Hahn (1998),
*On the Role of the Propensity Score in Efficient Semiparametric Estimation of
Average Treatment Effects*, Econometrica 66(2):315вҖ“331. Plan: `doc/hahn_efficiency_plan.md`.

### 13eff.1 `Estimation/Efficiency/TangentProjection.lean` вҖ” abstract Hilbert efficiency machine

Pure Hilbert-space geometry over a real inner-product space `H` with a tangent
subspace `T` (`[T.HasOrthogonalProjection]`). The reusable abstraction every
future estimator-efficiency theorem cites. All **sorry-free, axiom-clean**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `IsGradient T g ПҲ` | `Prop := вҲҖ s вҲҲ T, вҹӘПҲ, sвҹ«_в„қ = вҹӘg, sвҹ«_в„қ` | `ПҲ` is an influence function / gradient agreeing with reference `g` on every tangent direction. |
| `efficientIF T g` | `noncomputable def := вҶ‘(orthogonalProjection T g)` | The efficient influence function = orthogonal projection of any gradient onto the tangent space. |
| `effBound T g` | `noncomputable def := вҖ–efficientIF T gвҖ–^2` | The semiparametric efficiency bound. |
| `efficientIF_mem` | `efficientIF T g вҲҲ T` | The EIF lies in the tangent space. |
| `isGradient_iff_sub_mem_orthogonal` | `IsGradient T g ПҲ вҶ” ПҲ вҲ’ g вҲҲ Tб—®` | Gradients form the affine coset `g + Tб—®`. |
| `efficientIF_isGradient` | `IsGradient T g (efficientIF T g)` | The EIF is itself a gradient. |
| `orthogonalProjection_eq_of_isGradient` | `IsGradient T g ПҲ вҮ’ orthogonalProjection T ПҲ = efficientIF T g` | The projection onto `T` is the same for every gradient. |
| `normSq_gradient_decomp` | `IsGradient T g ПҲ вҮ’ вҖ–ПҲвҖ–ВІ = вҖ–efficientIF T gвҖ–ВІ + вҖ–ПҲ вҲ’ efficientIF T gвҖ–ВІ` | Pythagoras decomposition along the tangent space. |
| `effBound_le_normSq` | `IsGradient T g ПҲ вҮ’ effBound T g вүӨ вҖ–ПҲвҖ–ВІ` | **The efficiency lower bound**: no gradient beats the EIF's norm. |
| `norm_eq_iff_eq_efficientIF` | `IsGradient T g ПҲ вҮ’ (вҖ–ПҲвҖ–ВІ = effBound T g вҶ” ПҲ = efficientIF T g)` | Sharpness: equality only at the EIF. |
| `efficientIF_unique` | `IsGradient T g ПҲ вҶ’ ПҲ вҲҲ T вҶ’ ПҲ = efficientIF T g` | A gradient lying in `T` is the EIF. |
| `efficientIF_eq_self_of_mem` | `g вҲҲ T вҮ’ efficientIF T g = g` | A tangent reference is its own EIF. |
| `effBound_eq_of_mem_sub` | `T' вүӨ T вҶ’ g вҲҲ T' вҮ’ efficientIF T' g = g вҲ§ efficientIF T g = g вҲ§ effBound T' g = effBound T g` | **Tangent-shrinking** corollary: removing tangent directions orthogonal to `g` leaves the bound unchanged. |

### 13eff.2 `Estimation/Efficiency/Submodel.lean` вҖ” regular parametric submodels via exponential tilting

The analytic engine for pathwise derivatives. For a base law `P` and a bounded
mean-zero score `s`, the exponential tilt `P_t = (e^{tВ·s}/c(t))В·P` realizes `s`
as a regular submodel. All **sorry-free**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `tiltNorm P s t` | `noncomputable def := вҲ« e^{tВ·s} dP` | Normalizing constant `c(t)`. |
| `tiltExp P s ПҶ t` | `noncomputable def := (вҲ« ПҶВ·e^{tВ·s} dP)/tiltNorm` | Tilted expectation `E_{P_t}[ПҶ]`. |
| `tiltMeasure P s t` | `noncomputable def := (ofReal c(t))вҒ»В№ вҖў P.withDensity (ofReal e^{tВ·s})` | The tilted probability measure `P_t`. |
| `tiltNorm_zero` | `tiltNorm P s 0 = 1` | At `t=0` the tilt is `P`. |
| `hasDerivAt_tilt_numerator` | `HasDerivAt (fun t => вҲ« ПҶВ·e^{tВ·s} dP) (вҲ« ПҶВ·s dP) 0` | Differentiation under the integral sign (Mathlib `hasDerivAt_integral_of_dominated_loc_of_deriv_le`), bounded score + integrable `ПҶ`. |
| `hasDerivAt_tiltExp` | bounded mean-zero `s`, integrable `ПҶ` вҮ’ `HasDerivAt (tiltExp P s ПҶ) (вҲ« ПҶВ·s dP) 0` | **The tilt-derivative lemma**: `d/dt E_{P_t}[ПҶ]|вӮҖ = Cov_P(ПҶ,s) = вҲ« ПҶВ·s dP`. The analytic core of every pathwise-derivative argument. |
| `tilt_exp_integrable` | `Integrable (e^{tВ·s}) P` | Bounded score вҮ’ density integrable (comparison with constant). |
| `tiltNorm_pos` | `0 < tiltNorm P s t` | Positivity of the normalizer (lower-bounded by `e^{вҲ’|t|M}`). |
| `isProbabilityMeasure_tiltMeasure` | `IsProbabilityMeasure (tiltMeasure P s t)` | The tilt has total mass 1 (`ofReal_integral_eq_lintegral_ofReal` + `ENNReal.inv_mul_cancel`). |
| `integral_tiltMeasure` | `вҲ« h вҲӮ(tiltMeasure P s t) = tiltExp P s h t` | Bridge: expectations under `P_t` are tilted expectations (`integral_withDensity_eq_integral_smulвӮҖ`). |

### 13eff.3 `Estimation/Efficiency/ATEVariance.lean` вҖ” the Hahn variance decomposition

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `hahn_weight_true_Linf`, `hahn_weight_false_Linf` | `MemLp (a/e)`/`((1вҲ’a)/(1вҲ’e)) вҠӨ P.Ој` | Overlap вҮ’ the IPW weights are bounded (private helpers). |
| `hahn_mu_L2` | `MemLp (Ој_val d вҲҳ X) 2 P.Ој` | Conditional-Jensen `LВІ` bound on the regression (private helper). |
| `aipw_variance_hahn_decomposition` | `вҲ« ПҲ_AIPWВІ dP_Z = вҲ« (ОјвӮҒвҲ’ОјвӮҖвҲ’П„)ВІ dP_X + вҲ« (a/eВІ)(YвҲ’ОјвӮҒ)ВІ dP_Z + вҲ« ((1вҲ’a)/(1вҲ’e)ВІ)(YвҲ’ОјвӮҖ)ВІ dP_Z` | **The Hahn efficiency-bound formula.** `ПҲ = A+BвҲ’C`, `BC=0` (`aвҲҲ{0,1}`), the two cross-terms vanish via `weighted_residual_integral_zero`. The last two terms are `E[ПғВІвӮҒ/e]`, `E[ПғВІвӮҖ/(1вҲ’e)]`. **Proved.** |
| `dml_ATE_attains_hahn_bound` | `вҲҡ\|B(n)\|В·(ОёМӮвҒҝ вҲ’ ОёвӮҖ) вҮ’ N(0, V_H)` | AIPW/DML attains the Hahn bound: `dml_ATE_tendstoNormal` with the limit variance rewritten as the 3-term `V_H`. **Proved.** |

### 13eff.4 `Estimation/Efficiency/ATETangent.lean` вҖ” ПҲ_AIPW is the efficient influence function

Instantiates the abstract machine (В§13eff.1) in `H = Lp в„қ 2 P_Z`, where the
mean-zero tangent space is `Tfull = (в„қвҲҷ1)б—®`. All **sorry-free**.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `propensity_score_residual_integral_zero` | `вҲ« h(X)В·(1_{D=1} вҲ’ e_val(X)) dОј = 0` | The propensity residual is mean-zero given `Пғ(X)` (private helper). |
| `aipw_orthogonal_propensity_score` | `вҲ« ПҲ_AIPWВ·(Оұ(X)В·(indA вҲ’ e_val(X))) dP_Z = 0` | **Role of the propensity score**: `ПҲ_AIPW` is orthogonal to every propensity-score direction (B/C terms via `weighted_residual_integral_zero`, A term via the residual helper). **Proved.** |
| `instIsProbabilityMeasure_P_Z` | `IsProbabilityMeasure S.P_Z` | The data law is a probability measure. |
| `aipw_memLp` | `MemLp ПҲ_AIPW 2 P_Z` | `ПҲ_AIPW вҲҲ LВІ(P_Z)` (from `aipw_finite_var`). |
| `aipwLp`, `oneLp`, `Tfull` | `Lp в„қ 2 P_Z` elements / `(в„қвҲҷoneLp)б—®` | `ПҲ_AIPW` and constant `1` as `LВІ` classes; the mean-zero tangent space. |
| `inner_oneLp` | `вҹӘf, oneLpвҹ« = вҲ« f dP_Z` | The `LВІ` inner product against `1` is the mean. |
| `aipwLp_mem_tangent` | `aipwLp вҲҲ Tfull` | `ПҲ_AIPW` is mean-zero (`aipw_mean_zero`), so lies in the tangent space. |
| `aipw_is_efficientIF` | `efficientIF Tfull aipwLp = aipwLp` | **ПҲ_AIPW is its own efficient influence function** (it lies in the tangent space). |
| `effBound_eq_variance`, `effBound_eq_hahn` | `effBound Tfull aipwLp = вҲ« ПҲ_AIPWВІ dP_Z = V_H` | The efficiency bound equals `Var(ПҲ_AIPW)` equals the Hahn formula. |
| `efficiency_bound_optimal` | `IsGradient Tfull aipwLp ПҲ вҮ’ effBound Tfull aipwLp вүӨ вҖ–ПҲвҖ–ВІ` | **The semiparametric efficiency bound**: every influence function of the ATE has variance `вүҘ V_H`; `ПҲ_AIPW` attains it. |
| `effBound_eq_of_known_propensity` | `T' вүӨ Tfull вҶ’ aipwLp вҲҲ T' вҮ’ effBound T' aipwLp = effBound Tfull aipwLp` | **Propensity-invariance** (Hahn's headline): the bound is unchanged when the tangent space shrinks to the propensity-known model (`ПҲ_AIPW` is still in it by `aipw_orthogonal_propensity_score`), so knowing the propensity score does not improve efficiency. |

### 13eff.5 `Estimation/Efficiency/ATEEfficientIF.lean` вҖ” pathwise ATE efficient influence function

This capstone module connects Hahn's pathwise derivative calculation to the
abstract pathwise-gradient and projection machinery. It records the bridge from
the AIPW covariance formula to the `LВІ(P_Z)` inner product, identifies the full
mean-zero space as the nonparametric tangent space under the supplied density
hypothesis, and states the conditional Hahn theorem that `ПҲ_AIPW` is the
canonical efficient influence function for the backdoor ATE.

<!-- GEN:Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.ATEEfficientIF -->
| Decl | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.inner_aipwLp_eq_integral` | `inner в„қ (S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2) f = вҲ« (z : Оі Г— Bool Г— в„қ), S.ПҲ_AIPW z * вҶ‘вҶ‘f z вҲӮS.P_Z` | The covariance pairing between the AIPW influence function and any square-integrable score equals the Hilbert-space inner product against the AIPW element in the observed-data square-integrable space. |
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.score_mem_Tfull` | `m.score вҲҲ S.Tfull` | Every regular submodel score is mean-zero, so every such score belongs to the full nonparametric tangent space for the observed-data law. |
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.isTangentSpace_Tfull` | `S.Tfull вүӨ Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.tangentSpace S.oneLp S.P_Z вҶ’ Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.IsTangentSpace S.oneLp S.P_Z S.Tfull` | The full mean-zero space is a genuine tangent space once the supplied nonparametric-model hypothesis says it is contained in the closed span of regular-submodel scores. |
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.aipw_isPathwiseGradient_ATE` | `(вҲҖ (m : Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.RegularSubmodel S.oneLp S.P_Z), HasDerivAt (fun t => ПҲ (m.path t)) (вҲ« (z : Оі Г— Bool Г— в„қ), S.ПҲ_AIPW z * вҶ‘вҶ‘m.score z вҲӮS.P_Z) 0) вҶ’ Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.IsPathwiseGradient S.oneLp S.P_Z ПҲ (S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2)` | Hahn's pathwise-derivative identity makes the AIPW score a pathwise gradient of the backdoor ATE functional along every regular submodel. |
| `Estimation.ATE.BackdoorEstimationSystem.aipw_is_efficientInfluenceFunction` | `(вҲҖ (m : Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.RegularSubmodel S.oneLp S.P_Z), HasDerivAt (fun t => ПҲ (m.path t)) (вҲ« (z : Оі Г— Bool Г— в„қ), S.ПҲ_AIPW z * вҶ‘вҶ‘m.score z вҲӮS.P_Z) 0) вҶ’ S.Tfull вүӨ Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.tangentSpace S.oneLp S.P_Z вҶ’ Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.IsPathwiseGradient S.oneLp S.P_Z ПҲ (S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2) вҲ§ S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2 вҲҲ S.Tfull вҲ§ вҲҖ (g : вҶҘ(MeasureTheory.Lp в„қ 2 S.P_Z)), Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.IsPathwiseGradient S.oneLp S.P_Z ПҲ g вҶ’ Causalean.Estimation.Efficiency.efficientIF S.Tfull g = S.aipwLp h_overlap hA h_y2 h_yd2` | The AIPW influence function is the efficient influence function for the backdoor average treatment effect in the nonparametric observed-data model. |
<!-- /GEN -->

---

## 14. `Panel/` вҖ” Panel regression algebra

> **PARTIALLY ARCHIVED 2026-06-12.** The dead CausalSmith research *question grammar* and its
> population machinery (`Regression/`, `PathLimit/`, `Probability/`, `DGP/`,
> `Baseline/`, `Assignment/`, `Target/`, `Question.lean`, `Bridge.lean`) were moved
> to `archive/Causalean/Panel/` and their API descriptions removed from this file
> (CausalSmith research now proposes free-form questions; no live consumer; `CausalSmith`
> imports nothing from `Causalean.Panel`). See `archive/README.md` and
> `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-12-panel-substrate-unification-design.md`.

The cell / inner-product / projection / FWL machinery lives in
`Causalean/Panel/Weighted/` (see В§13W). The live `Panel/` layer provides the
`R = I Г— T` instantiation (`Cells I T := WeightedSupport (I Г— T)`) plus the
panel-specific apparatus: balanced panels (`Cells.balanced`), the two-way
fixed-effect subspace (`H_twfe` via `Weighted.twoAxisAdditiveSpan I T`), the
cell-level potential-outcome layer (`PO/`), the share-weighted two-way panel
(`WeightedTwoWayPanel`, В§14c-WTWP), and the TWFE indicator closed forms
(`FixedEffect/IndicatorClosedForms`, В§14d-IndicatorClosedForms). It is
**algebraic, not estimation-theoretic**: finite-sample statements are identities
conditional on the treatment path.

Live subsections: В§14a (`Cells` + `InnerProduct`), В§14b (`Subspace` +
`FixedEffect`), В§14c (`PO/`), В§14c-WTWP (`WeightedTwoWayPanel`),
В§14d-IndicatorClosedForms (`FixedEffect/IndicatorClosedForms`).

### 14a. `Panel/Cells.lean` + `Panel/InnerProduct.lean`

#### 14a.1 `Panel/Cells.lean`

Observed unit-period cells `R вҠҶ I Г— T` with strictly positive weights summing
to 1.  Mirrors Definition 2.1 of the projection note.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Cells I T` | `structure` (over `[Fintype I] [Fintype T] [DecidableEq I] [DecidableEq T]`) | Bundles `observed : Finset (I Г— T)` (nonempty), `weight : I Г— T вҶ’ в„қ`, `weight_pos`, `weight_zero_off`, `weight_sum_one`. |
| `Cells.weight_nonneg` | `0 вүӨ c.weight r` for all `r` | Pointwise nonnegativity (positive on `observed`, zero off it). **Proved.** |
| `Cells.sum_weight` | `вҲ‘ r вҲҲ c.observed, c.weight r = 1` | Restated normalization. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.sum_weight_univ` | `вҲ‘ r, c.weight r = вҲ‘ r вҲҲ c.observed, c.weight r` | Sum over `Finset.univ` agrees with sum over `observed`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.sum_weight_univ_eq_one` | `вҲ‘ r, c.weight r = 1` | Normalization restated over `Finset.univ`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.balanced` | `Cells I T` (under `[Nonempty I] [Nonempty T]`) | Balanced panel: `observed = univ`, `weight = 1/(\|I\|В·\|T\|)`. `noncomputable`. |
| `Cells.balanced_observed`, `balanced_weight` | `@[simp]` | Definitional unfoldings of `balanced`. |

#### 14a.2 `Panel/InnerProduct.lean`

Weighted inner product on scalar arrays and its matrix-valued lift to vector
arrays modeled as `Fin K вҶ’ ((I Г— T) вҶ’ в„қ)`.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Cells.ip` | `(c : Cells I T) вҶ’ (I Г— T вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (I Г— T вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ в„қ` | `c.ip A B = вҲ‘ r вҲҲ c.observed, c.weight r В· A r В· B r`. |
| `Cells.ip_def` | `@[simp]` unfolding | Direct unfolding of `c.ip A B`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_symm` | `c.ip A B = c.ip B A` | Symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_add_left` / `ip_add_right` | bilinearity | Additivity in left / right argument. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_smul_left` / `ip_smul_right` | `c.ip (s вҖў A) B = s В· c.ip A B` (and symmetrically) | Scalar homogeneity. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_self_summand_nonneg` | `вҲҖ r вҲҲ c.observed, 0 вүӨ c.weight r * A r * A r` | Per-summand nonnegativity, used by both positivity and definiteness. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_self_nonneg` | `0 вүӨ c.ip A A` | Positivity. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ip_self_eq_zero_iff` | `c.ip A A = 0 вҶ” вҲҖ r вҲҲ c.observed, A r = 0` | Definiteness on the support `R`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ipMat` | `(Fin K вҶ’ I Г— T вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ (Fin K вҶ’ I Г— T вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Matrix (Fin K) (Fin K) в„қ` | Entrywise inner product `(c.ipMat A B) j k = c.ip (A j) (B k)`. |
| `Cells.ipMat_apply` | `@[simp]` unfolding | Direct unfolding. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ipMat_transpose` | `(c.ipMat A B).transpose = c.ipMat B A` | Transposed symmetry. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ipMat_add_left` / `ipMat_add_right` | bilinearity | Additivity in each tuple argument (entrywise). **Proved.** |
| `Cells.ipMat_smul_left` / `ipMat_smul_right` | `c.ipMat (s вҖў A) B = s вҖў c.ipMat A B` (and symmetrically) | Scalar homogeneity entrywise. **Proved.** |

### 14b. `Panel/Subspace.lean` + `Panel/FixedEffect.lean`

Nuisance spaces, weighted residualization, and the two-way fixed-effect
subspace from Definition 2.2.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Cells.V` | `(I T : Type*) вҶ’ Type _ := (I Г— T) вҶ’ в„қ` | Scalar cell-array space used throughout `Panel/`. |
| `Cells.weighted_orthogonal_projection_exists` | `вҲғ P : V вҶ’вӮ—[в„қ] V, ...` | Existence of a weighted orthogonal projection onto a subspace. Proved by packaging `c.ip` as a positive-semidefinite bilinear form and applying the generic semidefinite-projection helper. |
| `Cells.proj` | `Cells I T вҶ’ Submodule в„қ (V I T) вҶ’ V I T вҶ’вӮ—[в„қ] V I T` | Chosen weighted projection onto a nuisance subspace. |
| `Cells.proj_mem` / `proj_orthogonal` | membership and orthogonality lemmas | Defining properties of `proj`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.proj_apply_of_mem` / `proj_apply_eq_of_mem_orthogonal` / `proj_idem_apply` | supportwise projection identities | Projection agrees with members of `H` on observed cells; any orthogonal candidate nuisance component agrees with the chosen projection on observed cells; projection is idempotent there. |
| `Cells.residualize` | `V I T вҶ’вӮ—[в„қ] V I T` | Residual maker `M_H = id - P_H`. |
| `Cells.tildeX` / `tildeXVec` | scalar and column-wise residualization | Residualized scalar array and `Fin K` tuple of arrays. |
| `Cells.residualize_in_orthogonal` | `c.ip (c.tildeX H X) h = 0` for `h вҲҲ H` | Key residual orthogonality. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.residualize_self_of_mem` / `residualize_idem_apply` | supportwise residual identities | Residuals of nuisance-space arrays vanish on observed cells; `M_H` is idempotent on observed cells. |
| `Cells.H_twfe` | `Submodule в„қ (V I T)` | Two-way fixed-effect subspace `{h : h(i,t)=a_i+b_t}`. |
| `Cells.const_mem_H_twfe` | constants are in `H_twfe` | Fixed-effect space contains constant arrays. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.H_twfe_finiteDimensional` | `Module.Finite в„қ (c.H_twfe)` | Finite-dimensionality inherited from the finite ambient cell-array space. |

### 14c. `Panel/PO/` вҖ” cell-level potential outcomes

Statement-level scaffolding for the panel potential-outcomes layer.
Mirrors Definitions 2.3вҖ“2.4 and Assumption 2.1 of
`CausalSmith/doc/general_projection_carryover_note.tex`.  Treatment paths,
exposure mappings, the bundled `PanelPOSystem`, and the (definitional)
consistency lemma.  Conditional means `m_r^0(D)` and exposure responses
`П„_r(e | D)` are defined via `MeasureTheory.condExp` against the
Пғ-algebra `Пғ(D)`.

#### 14c.1 `Panel/PO/TreatmentPath.lean`

Realized treatment paths and finite-memory histories.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `TreatmentPath` | `(I T A : Type*) вҶ’ Type _ := I вҶ’ T вҶ’ A` | A unit's treatment trajectory. Mirrors `D = (D_{it})` of Def. 2.3. |
| `TreatmentPath.History` | `[Inhabited A] (p : в„•) (D : TreatmentPath I (Fin TвӮҖ) A) (i : I) (t : Fin TвӮҖ) : Fin (p+1) вҶ’ A` | Length-`p+1` history `(D_{it}, вҖҰ, D_{i,t-p})` clamped to `default` for out-of-range lags. Implements the `D_{is} = 0` boundary convention. |
| `TreatmentPath.History_zero` | `History p D i t 0 = D i t` | Lag-0 unfolding (contemporaneous treatment). `@[simp]`. **Proved.** |

#### 14c.2 `Panel/PO/Exposure.lean`

Exposure mappings and baseline exposures.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `Exposure` | `(I T A : Type*) (E : I Г— T вҶ’ Type*) вҶ’ Type _ := (r : I Г— T) вҶ’ TreatmentPath I T A вҶ’ E r` | Cell-indexed known function `E_r = e_r(D)` of the realized treatment paths. Mirrors Def. 2.3. |
| `BaselineExposure` | `{I T : Type*} (E : I Г— T вҶ’ Type*) вҶ’ Type _ := (r : I Г— T) вҶ’ E r` | Baseline `e_r^0 вҲҲ E_r` for each cell. Mirrors `e_r^0` of Def. 2.4. |

A binary finite-memory builder is intentionally not shipped (awkward dependent-type instantiation); downstream files build the `Exposure` inline against `TreatmentPath.History`.

#### 14c.3 `Panel/PO/CellPO.lean`

Bundled panel potential-outcomes system and its derived objects.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PanelPOSystem` | `structure` | Bundles `I, T, A, E, e, e0, О©, Ој, D, Y` together with the typeclass instances (`Fintype`, `DecidableEq`, `MeasurableSpace`, `IsProbabilityMeasure`) and measurability/integrability hypotheses (`D_meas`, `Y_meas`, `Y_integrable`). Mirrors Defs. 2.3вҖ“2.4. |
| `PanelPOSystem.observedExposure` | `(r : I Г— T) вҶ’ О© вҶ’ E r := e r (D Пү)` | Realized exposure at cell `r`. |
| `PanelPOSystem.observedY` | `(r : I Г— T) вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ := Y r (observedExposure r Пү) Пү` | Realized outcome at cell `r`. |
| `PanelPOSystem.DSigmaAlg` | `MeasurableSpace О©` | Пғ-algebra `Пғ(D)` generated by the treatment-path random variable, defined as the supremum over `(i, t)` of the comap of `(fun Пү => D Пү i t)`. |
| `PanelPOSystem.DSigmaAlg_le` | `DSigmaAlg вүӨ ambient Пғ-alg` | `Пғ(D)` is a sub-Пғ-algebra, proved from the cellwise measurability hypotheses `D_meas` via `measurable_iff_comap_le` and `iSup_le`. |
| `PanelPOSystem.m0` | `(r : I Г— T) вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Baseline conditional mean `m_r^0(D) := E[Y_r(e_r^0) | Пғ(D)]`. `noncomputable`. Mirrors `m_r^0` of Def. 2.4. |
| `PanelPOSystem.tau` | `(r : I Г— T) вҶ’ E r вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ` | Cell-level exposure response `П„_r(e | D) := E[Y_r(e) вҲ’ Y_r(e_r^0) | Пғ(D)]`. `noncomputable`. Mirrors `П„_r(e | D)` of Def. 2.4. |
| `PanelPOSystem.realizedTau` | `(r : I Г— T) вҶ’ О© вҶ’ в„қ := tau r (observedExposure r Пү) Пү` | Realized exposure response `П„_r(E_r | D)`. `noncomputable`. |

#### 14c.4 `Panel/PO/Assumptions/Consistency.lean`

Panel consistency (Assumption 2.1).  Definitional under the structure setup.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PanelPOSystem.observedY_eq_potentialOutcome` | `observedY r Пү = Y r (observedExposure r Пү) Пү` | Pointwise consistency, by `rfl`. **Proved.** |
| `PanelConsistency` | `(P : PanelPOSystem) вҶ’ Prop` | Universally-quantified consistency statement: `вҲҖ r Пү, observedY r Пү = Y r (observedExposure r Пү) Пү`. Named alias for Assumption 2.1. |
| `panelConsistency_holds` | `PanelConsistency P` | Consistency holds by `rfl` under the structure setup. **Proved.** |

#### 14c.5 `Panel/PO/Mobius.lean`

Boolean-cube inclusion-exclusion for Lemma 3.1.

| Declaration | Signature | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `PO.Mobius.indicator` | `Finset (Fin (p+1)) вҶ’ Fin (p+1) вҶ’ Fin 2` | Binary history with ones on the selected subset. |
| `PO.Mobius.indicator_empty` | `indicator вҲ… = fun _ => 0` | Empty-subset indicator is the zero history. **Proved.** |
| `PO.Mobius.delta` | `((Fin (p+1) вҶ’ Fin 2) вҶ’ в„қ) вҶ’ Finset (Fin (p+1)) вҶ’ в„қ` | Boolean-cube interaction coefficient `Оҙ_S` by alternating sums. |
| `PO.Mobius.prod_indicator_eq` | product over `S` equals `1` iff `S` is contained in active history support | Converts binary products to subset tests. **Proved.** |
| `PO.Mobius.coeff_sum` | alternating coefficient sum is `1` on `A=B`, else `0` | Inclusion-exclusion coefficient identity. **Proved.** |
| `PO.Mobius.mobius_expansion` | `П„ h = вҲ‘_{nonempty S} Оҙ П„ S * вҲҸ_{kвҲҲS} h k` when `П„ 0 = 0` | Lemma 3.1 Boolean-cube expansion. **Proved.** |

### 14c-WTWP. `Panel/WeightedTwoWayPanel.lean` вҖ” Share-weighted two-way panel (L1 unification target)

Share-weighted generalization of `Panel/UniformTwoWayPanel`
(audit `shared_core.md` item **M5**): two-way demeaning under a general unit pmf
`p` (Goodman-Bacon cohort shares; `p вүЎ 1/|Unit|` recovers the uniform panel) with
a uniform period measure. Intended as the single L1 demeaning/FWL layer the
EstimandCharacterization papers instantiate (migration is a separate, review-gated
step).

A **bridge** section connects these concrete closed forms to the generic
`WeightedSupport` FWL substrate (`Panel/Weighted/`, В§13W): the panel is the cell
support `R = Unit Г— Time` with weight `Пү_{(i,t)} = p_i/|Time|`, the `p`-weighted
inner product is `|Time|` times `WeightedSupport.ip`, and `ddot` is the generic
residual maker against the two-axis additive span. The abstract FWL coefficient
lemma `Weighted.WeightedSupport.scalar_fwl_of_normalEqs` is thus reusable through
the bridge, so the former bespoke (and consumer-less)
`finite_residualized_coefficient_eq_of_normalEqs` and its helper
`finiteResidualizedCoefficient` were removed. (The same-named live helpers in
`UniformTwoWayPanel`, В§9i0a-UniformTwoWayPanel, are unaffected.)

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `WeightedTwoWayPanel.UnitWeights` | `structure { p, pos, sum_one }` | Unit weights as a probability vector (`0 < p i`, `вҲ‘ p = 1`) | **Defined** |
| `unitMean` / `timeMean` / `grandMean` | `вҖҰ вҶ’ в„қ` | Uniform-time unit mean, `p`-weighted time mean `вҲ‘_i p_i V_{it}`, `p`-weighted grand mean | **Defined** |
| `ddot` | `(w) (V) (i) (t) вҶ’ в„қ` | Double-demeaned residual `V вҲ’ unitMean вҲ’ timeMean + grandMean` | **Defined** |
| `inner` | `(w) (V W) вҶ’ в„қ` | `p`-weighted finite inner product `вҲ‘_i вҲ‘_t p_i В· V_{it} W_{it}` | **Defined** |
| `IsUnitTimeAdditive` | `abbrev` | Shared additive-class predicate `h_it = a_i + b_t` | **Defined** |
| `ddot_reconstruct` / `sub_ddot_eq_unitTimeProjection` / `unitTimeProjection_additive` | identities | Reconstruction + residual/projection identities | **Proved** |
| `ddot_orthogonal_unit` | `(hT : 0 < card Time) вҶ’ вҲ‘_i вҲ‘_t p_i (ddotВ·a_i) = 0` | `ddot вҹӮ` unit-only functions (period sum of `ddot` vanishes per unit) | **Proved** |
| `ddot_orthogonal_time` | `вҲ‘_i вҲ‘_t p_i (ddotВ·b_t) = 0` | `ddot вҹӮ` time-only functions (`p`-weighted unit sum vanishes per period) | **Proved** |
| `ddot_orthogonal_unit_time` | `IsUnitTimeAdditive h вҶ’ inner w (ddot V) h = 0` | `ddot вҹӮ` the whole additive class | **Proved** |
| `cellSupport` *(bridge)* | `(w) вҶ’ WeightedSupport (Unit Г— Time)` | The panel as a cell-indexed weighted support, weight `Пү_{(i,t)} = p_i/|Time|` (every cell observed); needs `[Nonempty Unit] [Nonempty Time]` | **Defined** |
| `mem_twoAxisAdditiveSpan_iff` *(bridge)* | `h вҲҲ twoAxisAdditiveSpan вҶ” IsUnitTimeAdditive (uncurry h)` | Two-axis additive span membership = the panel additive-class predicate | **Proved** |
| `inner_eq_card_smul_ip` *(bridge)* | `inner w V W = |Time| В· (cellSupport w).ip V W` | The `p`-weighted panel inner product is `|Time|` times the generic `WeightedSupport.ip` | **Proved** |
| `ddot_eq_residualize` *(bridge)* | `(cellSupport w).residualize (twoAxisAdditiveSpan) V (i,t) = ddot w V i t` | ConcreteвҶ”abstract: `ddot` is the generic residual maker against the two-axis additive span (pointwise, every cell observed) | **Proved** |

### 14d-IndicatorClosedForms. `Panel/FixedEffect/IndicatorClosedForms.lean` вҖ” TWFE residual closed forms

Reusable substrate for `Cells.tildeX c.H_twfe A` when `A` is a structured
indicator (cohort-period, switch-on diagonal, triangular). Bridges the gap
identified in `Q1_StaggeredMinimalBasis/Helpers.lean` (L3, L4) and is the
cleanest place to put any future "closed-form residual under TWFE" lemma.
Includes a self-contained `cohortPeriodCells` construction (mirrors
`positiveCohortPeriodCells` from the staggered Q1 attempt; no Q-instance
import).

| Declaration | Signature | Description | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Cells.tildeX_const` | `c.tildeX c.H_twfe (fun _ => k) r = 0` on observed cells | Constants are absorbed by `H_twfe` (one-line via `residualize_self_of_mem`). **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_unit_indicator` | unit-only indicator вҶ’ 0 | `ind(i = iвӮҖ)` is a member of `H_twfe` via `a(i) := ind(i = iвӮҖ)`, `b(t) := 0`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_period_indicator` | period-only indicator вҶ’ 0 | Symmetric: `ind(t = tвӮҖ)` вҲҲ `H_twfe`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_product_indicator_balanced` | `tildeX (1_{S_I}(i)В·1_{S_T}(t)) = (ind вҲ’ |S_I|/|I|)В·(ind вҲ’ |S_T|/|T|)` on observed | Centered-product residual under balanced weights. Proof: candidate `g вҲҲ H_twfe` plus `H_twfe_orthogonal_iff` plus balanced row/column centered-sum lemmas, then `proj_apply_eq_of_mem_orthogonal`. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.cohortPeriodCells` | `(law : CohortLaw C) вҶ’ Cells (Fin C) (Fin S)` with weights `ПҖ(g)/S` | Generic positive-cohort cells construction. **Defined.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_cell_indicator_cohortPeriod` | cell `ind(g = gвӮҖ вҲ§ t = tвӮҖ)` вҶ’ four-term factorization | Centered-cell residual under cohort-period weights. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_diagonal_indicator_cohortPeriod` | staggered diagonal `ind(g.val = t.val)` | Closed form with the explicit grand-mean correction `(1/S)В·вҲ‘_{g.val<S} ПҖ(g)`. Proof uses row/column normal equations plus the diagonal-hit Finset reindexing. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.tildeX_triangular_indicator_cohortPeriod` | staggered triangular `ind(g.val < t.val)` | Closed form with the explicit grand-mean correction. Proof uses row/column normal equations plus finite-sum Fubini for the triangular predicate. **Proved.** |
| `Cells.H_twfe_orthogonal_iff` (private) | unit/period basis test suffices for `H_twfe`-orthogonality | Helper used by the substantive proofs. **Proved**: forward by specialization to unit/period indicators; backward by decomposing arbitrary `h(p) = a(p.1) + b(p.2)` into the `e_{iвӮҖ}`/`f_{tвӮҖ}` basis via Fintype sum manipulation. |

## 15. `ML/` вҖ” Standalone machine-learning spine

A causal-free supervised-learning library: basic regression methods as first-class
objects with their defining-optimization / closed-form-or-structure / population-target
properties. Does **not** import OSL/SCM/PO/Estimation. Design + plan:
`docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-15-causalean-ml-module-design.md`,
`docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-15-causalean-ml-module.md`.

### 15a. `ML/Core/` вҖ” the spine (Phase 0, complete)

Dual-view: a parametric `Predictor` (optimization lives here) and an extensional
`HypothesisClass` (population targets live here), joined by one `Bridge`.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `squaredLoss`, `bool01`, `sigmoid`, `softplus`, `logisticScoreLoss` | `Core/Losses.lean` | Pointwise losses + logistic link; with `sigmoid_pos/lt_one`, `softplus_pos`, `squaredLoss_nonneg`. |
| `Predictor` | `Core/Hypothesis.lean` | Parametrized predictor family: admissible `paramSet` + `predict : Оҳ вҶ’ X вҶ’ Y`. |
| `HypothesisClass` | `Core/Hypothesis.lean` | Extensional class: a set of measurable functions `X вҶ’ Y`. |
| `FeatureMap` | `Core/Hypothesis.lean` | Feature transform `X вҶ’ (K вҶ’ в„қ)`; identity gives linear regression, other choices give polynomial/spline/Fourier (sieve) regression. |
| `Loss`, `empiricalRisk(P)`, `populationRisk(P)`, `iidEmpiricalRisk`, `HasFinitePopulationRisk` | `Core/Risk.lean` | Risk functionals in both views; `iidEmpiricalRisk` via `Stat.IIDSample.sampleMean`. |
| `IsERMP`, `IsApproxERMP`, `RegularizedERM`, `IsERM`, `IsPopulationRiskMinimizer` | `Core/ERM.lean` | Candidate-wise minimizer predicates via `IsMinOn` (parametric + extensional). |
| `imageClass`, `isERMP_to_extensional`, `populationTarget_pushforward` | `Core/Bridge.lean` | The realized class of a predictor; parametric minimizers push forward to extensional minimizers. |
| `empiricalRiskP_convexOn_of_loss_convex`, `convexOn_comp_inner`, `exists_isMinOn_of_isCompact`, `exists_isMinOn_univ_of_coercive`, `fderiv_eq_zero_of_isMinOn_univ`, `SubgradientAt`, `subgradientAt_zero_iff_isMinOn` | `Core/Convex.lean` | Convex-analysis substrate: convexity of empirical risk, linear-composition convexity, compact/coercive existence, Fermat's rule, subgradientвҶ”minimizer. |
| `populationRisk_eq_of_both_minimizers`, `populationRisk_minimizer_eq_target`, `IsL2Projection`, `square_loss_population_target_of_isL2Projection` | `Core/PopulationTarget.lean` | P3 scaffold + squared-loss keystone: a residual orthogonal to all functions of the covariate (the LВІ-projection / regression function) minimizes squared population risk. |

### 15b. `ML/Linear/`, `ML/Ridge/` вҖ” linear least squares and ridge (Phase 1, complete)

Linear-in-features regression: predictor `x вҶҰ вҲ‘вӮ– ОІвӮ– (ПҶ x)вӮ–`; the `FeatureMap` makes
polynomial/spline/Fourier (sieve) regression instances of the same theorems.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `linearPredict`, `olsObjective`, `ols_is_squaredLoss_ERM_of_normalEq`, `empiricalRisk_squaredLoss_linear` | `Linear/Finite.lean` | OLS objective; P1: a normal-equation solution `XбөҖX ОІМӮ = XбөҖy` minimizes SSE; bridge to the spine's `empiricalRisk`. |
| `olsCoef`, `ols_normalEq_of_minimizer`, `olsCoef_normalEq`, `olsCoef_unique` | `Linear/ClosedForm.lean` | P2: minimizer вҮ’ normal equations (converse); closed form `(XбөҖX)вҒ»В№XбөҖy` and uniqueness under `IsUnit (XбөҖX).det`. |
| `IsPopulationOLS`, `bestLinearPredictor_minimizes_populationRisk` | `Linear/Population.lean` | P3: a coefficient with residual uncorrelated to every feature minimizes squared population risk over the linear class (best linear predictor). |
| `ridgeObjective`, `ridge_is_regularized_squaredLoss_ERM_of_normalEq` | `Ridge/Finite.lean` | Ridge objective `SSE + О»вҖ–ОІвҖ–ВІ`; P1: a ridge normal-equation solution `(XбөҖX+О»I)ОІМӮ = XбөҖy` minimizes it (О» вүҘ 0). |
| `ridgeCoef`, `ridgeGram_posDef`, `ridgeCoef_normalEq`, `ridgeCoef_unique` | `Ridge/ClosedForm.lean` | P2: for О»>0 the ridge Gram is positive definite вҮ’ closed form `(XбөҖX+О»I)вҒ»В№XбөҖy`, unique without a full-rank assumption. |
| `populationRidgeObjective`, `IsPopulationRidge`, `populationRidge_minimizes` | `Ridge/Population.lean` | P3: the regularized population normal equations `E[(YвҲ’вҹӘОІ,ПҶвҹ«)ПҶвӮ–] = О»ОІвӮ–` characterize the (shrunk) population ridge minimizer. |

### 15b'. `ML/Ridge/Rate.lean` вҖ” ridge root-n estimation rate (learning theory)

The causal-free learning-theory result `ML/Causal/RateAssembly` consumes to discharge the
DML `o_p(n^{-1/4})` nuisance condition: the sample ridge predictor converges to the population
ridge minimizer at the LВІ(P_X) root-n rate. Built from the i.i.d.-sample second-moment engine
`Stat.IIDSample.sampleMean_sub_isBigOp` and the in-probability continuous-mapping /
stochastic-order substrate in `Stat/Limit/ContinuousMapping.lean` and
`Stat/EmpiricalProcess/CrossFitRate.lean`.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `empiricalGram`, `empiricalCross`, `sampleRidgeCoef`, `sampleRidgePredictor`, `populationGram` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | The sample ridge estimator over the first `n` i.i.d. points: empirical feature Gram `ДңвӮҷ`, cross moment `ДҲвӮҷ`, coefficient `(ДңвӮҷ+О»I)вҒ»В№ДҲвӮҷ`, the induced predictor, and the entrywise population Gram `GвӮ–вӮ— = вҲ« ПҶвӮ–ПҶвӮ—`. |
| `empiricalGram_posSemidef`, `sampleRidgeCoef_sub_eq` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | `ДңвӮҷ` is PSD (average of rank-one `ПҶПҶбөҖ`); the algebraic crux factoring the coefficient error `ОІМӮвӮҷвҲ’ОІвӢҶ = (ДңвӮҷ+О»I)вҒ»В№(ДҲвӮҷвҲ’ДңвӮҷОІвӢҶвҲ’О»ОІвӢҶ)`. |
| `centered_score_mean_isBigOp` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | The centered empirical score mean `ДҲвӮҷвҲ’ДңвӮҷОІвӢҶвҲ’О»ОІвӢҶ` is `O_p(n^{-1/2})` (centered i.i.d. vector mean with square-integrable score coordinates; population mean `О»ОІвӢҶ` from `IsPopulationRidge`). |
| `sampleRidgeCoef_isBigOp` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | The ridge coefficient error `вҖ–ОІМӮвӮҷвҲ’ОІвӢҶвҖ–` is `O_p(n^{-1/2})`: the regularized empirical-Gram inverse is `O_p(1)` (entrywise matrix LLN + inverse continuity at the PosDef limit) times the centered score mean. |
| `continuousAt_matrixInv_entry` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | Matrix-topology helper: at any nonsingular finite matrix, each inverse entry `M вҶҰ (MвҒ»В№)вӮ–вӮ—` is continuous. |
| `eLpNorm_predictor_sub_le` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | Deterministic: the prediction LВІ(P_X) error is bounded by a finite constant times the coefficient error, `вҖ–вҲ‘вӮ– ОҙвӮ–ПҶВ·вӮ–вҖ–_{LВІ} вүӨ CвҖ–ОҙвҖ–`. |
| `ridge_achievesL2Rate` | `Ridge/Rate.lean` | **Headline.** Under the population ridge normal equation, `О»>0`, the LвҒҙ feature moment and square-integrable score, the sample ridge predictor attains the root-n LВІ rate toward the population ridge minimizer. |

### 15c. `ML/Lasso/` вҖ” L1-regularized least squares (Phase 2)

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `l1penalty`, `lassoObjective`, `softThreshold`, `l1penalty_nonneg`, `convexOn_l1penalty`, `convexOn_olsObjective`, `convexOn_lassoObjective` | `Lasso/Finite.lean` | L1 penalty and lasso objective; convexity of the (non-smooth) objective. |
| `softThreshold_isMinOn` | `Lasso/Optimality.lean` | P2: the soft-threshold `S_О»(a)` is the exact minimizer of the scalar prox objective `(uвҲ’a)ВІ + 2О»|u|`. |
| `rademacherComplexity_l1_ball_le` | `Lasso/Rate.lean` | **Rademacher rate (high-dim).** Over `LвҲһ`-bounded features, the LВ№-ball linear class has `rademacherComplexity вүӨ (XвҲһВ·W/вҲҡn)В·вҲҡ(2 log 2d)` вҖ” the `вҲҡ(log d / n)` lasso rate. |
| `lasso_erm_excess_rate` | `Lasso/Rate.lean` | **Lasso ERM excess-risk rate (linear functional).** Instantiates the separable ERM oracle inequality for the LВ№-ball linear class on the LвҲһ-bounded feature domain, giving a tail bound at threshold `4В·(XвҲһВ·W/вҲҡn)В·вҲҡ(2 log 2d) + 2Оө`. Loss is the bare linear functional. |
| `LassoFeat`, `LassoWeight`, `lasso_erm_squaredLoss_excess_rate` | `Lasso/SquaredLoss.lean` | **Lasso ERM squared-loss excess-risk rate.** Genuine regression loss `(вҲ‘вұј wвұјxвұјвҲ’y)ВІ` (LвҲһ-bounded features, responses in `[вҲ’Yb,Yb]`). Same centered-loss decomposition as the kernel version (quadratic part via the infinite-index contraction + clamped square, cross part `2yВ·x` as a rescaled lasso linear class), carrying the `вҲҡ(2 log 2d)` factor: tail bound at threshold `4В·((4(XвҲһW)ВІ+2В·YbВ·XвҲһВ·W)/вҲҡn)В·вҲҡ(2 log 2d) + 2Оө`. |
| `margin_erm_surrogate_excess_rate` | `Margin/Rate.lean` | **Margin classification surrogate excess-risk rate.** For ANY `L`-Lipschitz surrogate `ПҶ` (hinge, logistic, smoothed 0вҖ“1), the margin loss `ПҶ(yВ·вҹӘw,xвҹ©)` over the `W`-ball of linear classifiers (features in `Xb`-ball, labels in `[вҲ’Yb,Yb]`). Single contraction: the margin `yВ·вҹӘw,xвҹ©=вҹӘw,yВ·xвҹ©` is a linear class over rescaled features and `ПҶМғ=ПҶвҲ’ПҶ(0)` is `LipschitzAt0`, so `RC вүӨ 2LВ·YbВ·XbВ·W/вҲҡn`; tail bound at threshold `8В·LВ·YbВ·XbВ·W/вҲҡn + 2Оө`. |
| `effectiveDimension`, `effectiveDimension_nonneg`, `summable_effectiveDimension_term`, `effectiveDimension_le_trace_div`, `effectiveDimension_le_card`, `effectiveDimension_antitone` | `Kernel/EffectiveDimension.lean` | **Effective dimension** `N(О»)=вҲ‘бөў Ојбөў/(Ојбөў+О»)=tr(T(T+О»)вҒ»В№)` of a trace-class operator (summable nonneg eigenvalues `Ој`), the quantity the CaponnettoвҖ“De Vito kernel-ridge optimal rate is stated in terms of. Definitional foundation + structural properties: nonneg, summand-summability, the dimension-free bound `N(О»)вүӨ(tr T)/О»`, the finite bound `N(О»)вүӨ#О№`, and antitonicity in `О»`. The downstream optimal-rate theorem additionally needs Hilbert-space operator-concentration (operator Bernstein), a separate substrate вҖ” see the file docstring. |

Full subgradient/KKT optimality for general designs and the population L1 target are deferred.

### 15d. `ML/Binary/`, `ML/Surrogate/` вҖ” logistic regression and convex ERM (Phase 2)

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `logisticEmpRisk`, `convexOn_softplus`, `convexOn_logisticScoreLoss`, `convexOn_logisticEmpRisk`, `continuous_logisticEmpRisk`, `logistic_exists_minimizer_on_compact` | `Binary/Logistic.lean` | P1/P2: logistic log-loss is convex (softplus convex via monotone sigmoid derivative); a minimizer exists on any compact parameter set. |
| `bernoulliCE`, `bernoulliCE_isMinOn` | `Binary/FisherConsistency.lean` | P3: cross-entropy is minimized at the true probability on the interior `(0,1)` (Gibbs inequality). The interior is essential вҖ” the closed-interval version is false under `Real.log 0 = 0`. |
| `ProperBinaryLoss`, `properLoss_population_minimizer_recovers_eta` | `Surrogate/GenericERM.lean` | A proper loss is Fisher consistent: the population-risk minimizer recovers the conditional probability `О·` (via `integral_mono`). |

### 15d'. `ML/Binary/Rate.lean` вҖ” LВІ-regularized logistic estimation rate (learning theory)

Binary-response analogue of the ridge rate: the `О»вҖ–ОІвҖ–ВІ` penalty makes the logistic objective
globally `2О»`-strongly convex, so the coefficient error is controlled by a strong-convexity basic
inequality (the role ridge's `О»I` played) rather than a closed-form inverse. Reuses the same
centered-score `O_p(n^{-1/2})` machinery and the shared linear-predictor LВІ bound.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `regLogisticGrad`, `IsPopulationRegLogistic`, `logisticPredictor` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | The empirical penalized-logistic gradient `nвҒ»В№ОЈ(ПғвҹЁОІ,ПҶбөўвҹ©вҲ’yбөў)ПҶбөў + 2О»ОІ`, the penalized population first-order condition `E[(ПғвҹЁОІвӢҶ,ПҶвҹ©вҲ’Y)ПҶвӮ–] + 2О»ОІвӢҶвӮ– = 0`, and the fitted-probability predictor `x вҶҰ Пғ(вҹЁОІ,ПҶ(x)вҹ©)`. |
| `regLogistic_basic_inequality` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | Deterministic strong-convexity crux: a sample point solving the empirical FOC `вҲҮвӮҷ(ОІМӮ)=0` satisfies `2О»вҖ–ОІМӮвҲ’ОІвӢҶвҖ– вүӨ вҲ‘вӮ–\|вҲҮвӮҷ(ОІвӢҶ)вӮ–\|` (gradient strong monotonicity: Пғ monotone вҮ’ logistic part вүҘ 0, penalty gives `2О»В·id`). |
| `regLogisticGrad_coord_isBigOp` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | Each coordinate of the empirical gradient at `ОІвӢҶ` is `O_p(n^{-1/2})` вҖ” a centered i.i.d. mean (population mean `0` by `IsPopulationRegLogistic`), via `sampleMean_sub_isBigOp`. |
| `regLogisticCoef_isBigOp` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | The coefficient error `вҖ–ОІМӮвӮҷвҲ’ОІвӢҶвҖ–` is `O_p(n^{-1/2})`: the basic inequality times the centered gradient. |
| `regLogistic_achievesL2Rate` | `Binary/Rate.lean` | **Headline.** The fitted probability `Пғ(вҹЁОІМӮвӮҷ,ПҶвҹ©)` attains the root-n LВІ rate toward `Пғ(вҹЁОІвӢҶ,ПҶвҹ©)`, via the `1/4`-Lipschitz `Пғ` and `eLpNorm_predictor_sub_le`. |

### 15e. `ML/Tree/`, `ML/NeuralNet/`, `ML/Kernel/` вҖ” trees, networks, kernels (Phase 3)

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `FinitePartitionPredictor`, `eval`, `eval_eqOn_cell` | `Tree/FinitePartitionPredictor.lean` | Piecewise-constant tree predictor; constant on each cell. |
| `RandomForest`, `eval`, `eval_mem_Icc` | `Tree/Forest.lean` | Forest = average of trees; averaging preserves pointwise `[a,b]` bounds. |
| `DenseLayer`, `Activation`, `layerMap`, `evalLayers`, `evalLayers_append`, `evalLayers_lipschitz` | `NeuralNet/Layer.lean`, `NeuralNet/FeedForward.lean` | Feedforward composition class; evaluation respects concatenation; Lipschitz with the product of per-layer constants. (No universal approximation / training.) |
| `IsRKHS` | `Kernel/RKHS.lean` | Abstract reproducing-kernel Hilbert space interface (`f x = вҹӘf, kвӮ“вҹ«`); Mathlib has no RKHS hierarchy. |
| `empiricalRademacherComplexity_innerBall_le`, `rkhs_ball_empiricalRademacher_le` | `Kernel/RKHSRademacher.lean` | **Intrinsic RKHS-ball Rademacher rate.** The empirical complexity of a Hilbert norm-ball linear class, and hence of an RKHS ball with bounded representers, is bounded by `ОәВ·r/вҲҡn`. |
| `gram`, `IsPSDkernel`, `gram_add_lambda_posDef` | `Kernel/Gram.lean` | Kernel Gram matrix; `K + О»I вү» 0` for `О» > 0`. |
| `krrRisk`, `representer_theorem` | `Kernel/Ridge.lean` | P2: any kernel-ridge minimizer lies in the span of the sample representers (orthogonal-decomposition proof). |
| `rademacherComplexity_l2_ball_le` | `Kernel/Rate.lean` | **Rademacher rate.** Over `LВІ`-bounded features, the `W`-ball linear class (dual/RKHS-ball view of kernel ridge) has `rademacherComplexity вүӨ XbВ·W/вҲҡn`. |
| `kernel_erm_excess_rate` | `Kernel/Rate.lean` | **Kernel ERM excess-risk rate (linear functional).** Instantiates the separable ERM oracle inequality for the closed `W`-ball of linear predictors on the closed `Xb`-ball feature domain, giving a tail bound at threshold `4В·XbВ·W/вҲҡn + 2Оө`. Loss is the bare linear functional `вҹӘw,xвҹ©`. |
| `clampedSq`, `clampedSq_eq_sq`, `clampedSq_le_sq`, `lipschitzAt0_clampedSq`, `continuous_clampedSq` | `Surrogate/ClampedSquare.lean` | Globally `2c`-Lipschitz surrogate `(clamp_{[-c,c]} t)ВІ` for `t вҶҰ tВІ`, agreeing with the square on `[-c,c]`; the Lipschitz function fed to the contraction principle so the (non-Lipschitz) squared loss can be pulled out of the Rademacher complexity on a bounded prediction range. |
| `KFeat`, `KWeight`, `kernel_erm_squaredLoss_excess_rate` | `Kernel/SquaredLoss.lean` | **Kernel ERM squared-loss excess-risk rate.** Genuine regression loss `(вҹӘw,xвҹ©вҲ’y)ВІ` (responses in `[вҲ’Yb,Yb]`). RC of the centered squared-loss class is bounded by decomposing `вҹӘw,xвҹ©ВІвҲ’2yвҹӘw,xвҹ©`: quadratic part via the infinite-index LedouxвҖ“Talagrand contraction with the clamped square, cross part `2yвҹӘw,xвҹ©=вҹӘw,2yВ·xвҹ©` as a linear class over rescaled features. Tail bound at threshold `4В·(4(XbW)ВІ+2В·YbВ·XbВ·W)/вҲҡn + 2Оө`. |

### 15f. `ML/Causal/` вҖ” ML learners as causal nuisance estimators (causal-coupling corner)

The **only** ML files permitted to import the causal layers (`PO`/`Estimation`); the
`Causalean.ML` core stays causal-free. Connects the ML population targets to the
AIPW/DML nuisances via Mathlib `condExp`.

| Declaration | File | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `covarSigma`, `covarSigma_le`, `isL2Projection_of_condExp`, `condExp_of_isL2Projection` | `Causal/RegressionBridge.lean` | The conditional mean `E[YвҲЈX]` (Mathlib `condExp`) **is** an `IsL2Projection`, and conversely вҖ” full equivalence linking the ML squared-loss keystone to the regression-function nuisance. |
| `mlOutcomeRegression_ae_eq`, `mlPropensity_ae_eq`, `mlNuisanceVec` | `Causal/Nuisance.lean` | The squared-loss ML target on arm `d` is the outcome regression `E[YвҲЈX,D=d]`; on the treatment indicator it is the propensity `P(D=1вҲЈX)`; `mlNuisanceVec` packages two ML targets into `Estimation.ATE.NuisanceVec`. |
| `aipw_mlNuisance_meanZero_of_wellSpecified` | `Causal/AIPWExample.lean` | End-to-end: well-specified ML nuisances make the AIPW estimating equation mean-zero at the ATE `ОёвӮҖ` (composes `mlNuisanceVec` with `Estimation.ATE.aipw_mean_zero`). |
| `AchievesL2Rate`, `isLittleOp_one_of_le_one`, `isLittleOp_mul_quarter`, `isLittleOp_quarter_of_isBigOp_sqrt` | `Core/Rate.lean` | Learning-rate abstraction (estimator attains LВІ-rate toward its population target) + generic rate algebra: per-nuisance `o_p(n^{-1/4})` rates give DML's individual `o_p(1)` and product `o_p(n^{-1/2})` conditions; a root-n `O_p(n^{-1/2})` rate is `o_p(n^{-1/4})`. (Moved here from `Causal/DMLRate`; causal-free common currency between method rate-proofs and the `Causal` assembly.) |

Note: `ML/Core/PopulationTarget.IsL2Projection` quantifies over **measurable** test functions
(required for the `condExp` bridge); the keystone threads this measurability.

Deferred follow-ups: complexity/generalization bounds, sieve approximation theory, raw-kernelвҶ’RKHS
construction, NN universal approximation, RF consistency; and (causal-ML trajectory) an end-to-end
AIPW-ATE example with ML nuisances, then the fast-rate layer discharging DML's `o(n^{-1/4})`
nuisance-rate condition.

## 16. `Mathlib/Probability/DarmoisSkitovich.lean` вҖ” Bernstein's theorem (finite-variance DarmoisвҖ“Skitovich)

Mathlib-staging probability result: the analytic engine behind linear non-Gaussian
causal discovery (LiNGAM, Shimizu et al. 2006). Proves that non-Gaussianity of
disturbances breaks the rotational symmetry the Gaussian case suffers from. Built
entirely on Mathlib's `charFun` infrastructure (`contDiff_charFun`,
`iIndepFun.charFun_map_sum_eq_prod`, `iteratedDeriv_charFun_zero`,
`Measure.ext_of_charFun`, `charFun_gaussianReal`); none of this result is in Mathlib.
Sorry-free; axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` only.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `bernstein_charFun_funeq` | `f(u+v)В·g(uвҲ’v) = f uВ·g uВ·(f vВ·g(вҲ’v))` for `f = charFun (P.map X)`, `g = charFun (P.map Y)` | Functional equation: joint characteristic function of `(X,Y)` computed two ways, using `X вҹӮ Y` and `(X+Y) вҹӮ (XвҲ’Y)`. Plumbing. **Proved** |
| `charFun_eventually_ne_zero` | `вҲҖб¶  t in рқ“қ 0, charFun Ој t вү  0` (probability `Ој` on `в„қ`) | `charFun` is continuous and `1` at `0`, so nonzero near `0`; this is what makes the logarithmic derivative `(charFun Ој)'/charFun Ој` well defined. **Proved** |
| `charFun_contDiff_two` | `ContDiff в„қ 2 (charFun (P.map Z))` given `MemLp Z 2 P` | `CВІ`-regularity of the characteristic function under a finite second moment, via `contDiff_charFun`. **Proved** |
| `gaussianForm_of_funeq` | functional equation + `CВІ` + value `1` at `0` вҹ№ `вҲғ c`, locally `f t = exp(f'(0)В·t + cВ·tВІ/2)` and `g t = exp(g'(0)В·t + cВ·tВІ/2)` (shared `c`) | The hard pure-analysis core (no probability). Log-derivative argument with no `Complex.log`: differentiate the functional equation once in each variable to force the second logarithmic derivative constant, then solve the resulting ODE. **Proved** |
| `bernstein_charFun_gaussian_nhds_zero` | `вҲғ mf mg ПғВІ вүҘ 0`, locally `charFun (P.map X) t = exp(mfВ·tВ·I вҲ’ ПғВІВ·tВІ/2)` and likewise `Y` with shared `ПғВІ` | Wires the analytic core to characteristic-function facts: identifies the constants as moments (`mf = E[X]`, `ПғВІ = Var X`) via `iteratedDeriv_charFun_zero`; the shared `ПғВІ` (= `Var X = Var Y`) feeds the doubling bootstrap. **Proved** |
| `bernstein` | `X вҹӮ Y`, `MemLp В· 2`, `(X+Y) вҹӮ (XвҲ’Y)` вҹ№ `IsGaussian (P.map X) вҲ§ IsGaussian (P.map Y)` | **Bernstein's theorem** (smallest non-degenerate DarmoisвҖ“Skitovich instance; the 2-variable LiNGAM identification core). Doubling bootstrap `f(2t) = f(t)ВІВ·g(t)В·g(вҲ’t)` propagates the local Gaussian form to all of `в„қ`, then `Measure.ext_of_charFun` against `gaussianReal`. **Proved** |

## 17. `Discovery/LiNGAM/` вҖ” Linear Non-Gaussian Acyclic Model (causal discovery)

Formalization of Shimizu, Hoyer, HyvГӨrinen & Kerminen (JMLR 2006). The non-Gaussianity
engine used here lives in `Mathlib/Probability/DarmoisSkitovich.lean` (В§16), and the
proved headline theorem uses the kurtosis route rather than the abandoned general
Darmois-Skitovich attempt.

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `perm_uniqueness` (`PermutationUniqueness.lean`) | for lower-triangular `M` with non-zero diagonal and permutations `Пғ, П„`, `(вҲҖ i, M (Пғ i) (П„ i) вү  0) вҶ” Пғ = П„` | **Appendix A, Lemma 1.** Uniqueness of the zero-free-diagonal row permutation; this is what pins the disturbanceвҶ”variable correspondence (resolving ICA's column-permutation indeterminacy). Slick proof: nonzero diagonal + lower-triangularity вҹ№ `П„ i вүӨ Пғ i` вҲҖi, and equal permutation index-sums force equality. **Proved** |
| `lingam_reduction` (`Identifiability.lean`) | for unit-diagonal `C, C'` with `C` lower-triangular in order `Пғ`, if `C' i j = d i В· C (П„ i) j` then `C = C'` | **Structural identifiability core (ICA вҶ’ matrix).** Given the ICA gen-permutation relation between the `I вҲ’ B` matrices, acyclicity + unit-diagonal normalization force equality. Reuses the Appendix-A sum argument. **Proved** |
| `lingam_identifiable` (`Identifiability.lean`) | for `B, B'` with zero diagonal, `B` acyclic in order `Пғ`, if `(1 вҲ’ B') i j = d i В· (1 вҲ’ B) (П„ i) j` then `B = B'` | **LiNGAM coefficient identifiability (modulo ICA).** `B`-level packaging of `lingam_reduction` for `C = I вҲ’ B`. **Proved** |
| `affine_of_continuous_of_second_diff_zero` (`Mathlib/Analysis/FrechetFunctionalEquation.lean`) | continuous `f : в„қ вҶ’ E` with `f (x+s+s) + f x = f (x+s) + f (x+s)` вҹ№ `вҲғ a b, f x = a + x вҖў b` | **FrГ©chet functional equation, degree one.** A standalone finite-difference helper: midpoint-affine вҹ№ additive вҹ№ `в„қ`-linear (`AddMonoidHom.toRealLinearMap`). **Proved** |
| `genPerm_of_invertible_of_colSupport` (`GenPerm.lean`) | invertible `W` with вүӨ1 non-zero entry per column вҹ№ `вҲғ П„ d, (d i вү  0) вҲ§ W i j = if j = П„ i then d i else 0` | Generalized-permutation extraction: invertibility upgrades "вүӨ1 per column" to "exactly one per row". **Proved** |
| `kurt` / `cross_fourth_cumulant_eq_sum` / `colSupport_of_kurtosis` (`Kurtosis.lean`) | excess kurtosis `ОәвӮ„`; `cum(yбөў,yбөў,yвӮ–,yвӮ–) = ОЈвұј WбөўвұјВІWвӮ–вұјВІОәвӮ„(eвұј)`; and: independent centered finite-4th-moment sources with non-zero same-sign kurtosis + `yбөў вҹӮ yвӮ–` вҹ№ `WбөўвұјВ·WвӮ–вұј = 0` | **Marcinkiewicz-free column support.** The 4th-cumulant route to the `genPerm` input, avoiding DarmoisвҖ“Skitovich entirely. Identity proved by the full Isserlis 4-index expansion under independence; column support by sum-of-same-sign-is-zero. Sorry-free, axiom-clean. **Proved** |
| `ica_genPerm_relation` / `lingam_identifiability_kurtosis` (`LiNGAMKurtosis.lean`) | column support + invertibility вҹ№ gen-permutation relation; and the headline: invertible acyclic unit-diagonal mixing + independent centered finite-4th-moment same-sign-kurtosis disturbances + equal observed law вҹ№ `AвҒ»В№ = A'вҒ»В№` (`B = B'`) | **Complete, fully sorry-free LiNGAM identification theorem (kurtosis route).** Chains `colSupport_of_kurtosis` вҶ’ `ica_genPerm_relation` вҶ’ `lingam_reduction`; no DarmoisвҖ“Skitovich, no Marcinkiewicz, no FrГ©chet ladder. `#print axioms` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. **Proved** |

## 17b. `Discovery/LinearDisentanglement/` вҖ” Linear causal disentanglement via interventions (SquiresвҖ“SeigalвҖ“BhateвҖ“Uhler 2023)

Formalization of Squires, Seigal, Bhate & Uhler, *Linear Causal Disentanglement via
Interventions* (ICML 2023, `arXiv:2211.16467`). Latent variables `Z` follow a linear SEM
`BвӮ–Z = Оө` per context, observed only through a full-rank mixing `X = GZ`; a single perfect
intervention per latent node identifies the latent DAG and parameters **up to `S(рқ’ў)`**
(order-preserving relabelings). This is the interventional/second-moment counterpart of
LiNGAM: it **reuses** LiNGAM's structural pinning (`lingam_reduction` / `perm_uniqueness`)
but replaces the non-Gaussianity engine with interventions. Taken at the **algebraic
(matrix-level)** view of the paper's Theorem 2: a model is the tuple `(H, {BвӮ–}, {iвӮ–}, {О»вӮ–})`
and identifiability compares two tuples producing the same precision matrices `{ОҳвӮ–}` (no
MooreвҖ“Penrose pseudoinverse needed). Currently a **complete compiling scaffold** with the
algebraic backbone proven and the deep linear-algebra steps staged (see status column).

| Declaration | Statement | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Solution` / `permMat` / `Theta0` / `Theta` / `InSG` (`Model.lean`) | model tuple (full-row-rank `H`, edge relation with `jвҶ’i вҹ№ i<j`, upper-triangular positive-diagonal `BвӮҖ` with support = edges, perfect single-node interventions `BвӮ–=BвӮҖ+e_{iвӮ–}cвӮ–бөҖ`, `cвӮ–=О»вӮ–e_{iвӮ–}вҲ’BвӮҖбөҖe_{iвӮ–}`); `ОҳвӮ–=HбөҖBвӮ–бөҖBвӮ–H`; `S(рқ’ў)` order-preserving perms; permutation matrix `(P_Пғ)_{ij}=вҹҰi=Пғjвҹ§` | Model layer + the two `permMat` orthogonality lemmas (`permMat ПғВ·(permMat Пғ)бөҖ=1` and transpose). **Proved (defs + orthogonality, sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `fact_transpose_mul` / `key_identity` (`KeyIdentity.lean`) | `BбөҖB=ОЈбөў(BбөҖeбөў)вҠ—ВІ`; and `ОҳвӮ–вҲ’ОҳвӮҖ=(HбөҖBвӮ–бөҖe_{iвӮ–})вҠ—ВІвҲ’(HбөҖBвӮҖбөҖe_{iвӮ–})вҠ—ВІ` | **Algebraic backbone (their key Proposition).** A single-node intervention changes only one row of `B`, so the rank-one decompositions cancel except in the target row; the precision difference is a difference of two outer products (rank вүӨ 2). **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `sigma_solutions` (`SigmaSolutions.lean`) | conjugating `BвӮҖ,BвӮ–` by `permMat Пғ` and permuting rows of `H` leaves every `ОҳвӮҖ,ОҳвӮ–` unchanged | **(вҠҮ) direction of Theorem 2** (`prop:sigma_solutions`): each `ПғвҲҲS(рқ’ў)` gives a solution. Pure orthogonality of `permMat Пғ`. **Proved (sorry-free)** |
| `pa`/`Pa`/`anc`/`An`/`prec`/`preceq`, `IsPORQ`, `prec_lt`, `inner_toLp_eq_dotProduct`, `dotProduct_self_pos`, `eq_of_mul_eq_mul_row_indep`, `row_indep_of_mul_row_indep` (`PartialOrderRQ.lean`) | parents/ancestors and the partial order `iвүәj вҶ” jвҲҲan(i)`; **Definition 1** (partial order RQ decomposition `H=RQ`, `R` `вӘҜ`-supported with `RбөўбөўвүҘ0`, rows of `Q` unit-norm and orthogonal along `вүә`); + the `в¬қбөҘ`вҶ”`EuclideanSpace` inner-product bridge, positive-definiteness, and full-row-rank cancellation helpers | Graph order + Def 1 + reusable helpers. The helpers (bridge, cancellation, rank propagation) are **proved, axiom-clean**. **Proved (helpers)** |
| `porq_exists` / `porq_unique` (`PartialOrderRQ.lean`) | existence and uniqueness of the partial order RQ decomposition of a full-row-rank `H` | **Existence** by a bespoke partial-order GramвҖ“Schmidt recursion (`porqResidual`, projecting onto the ancestor-row span along `вүә`, normalized via the `в¬қбөҘ`вҶ”`EuclideanSpace` bridge `inner_toLp_eq_dotProduct`); **uniqueness** by downward induction pinning each `qбөў` (`Q=Q'`) then full-row-rank cancellation (`eq_of_mul_eq_mul_row_indep`) for `R=R'`. **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `rowspan_inclusion_a` / `rowspan_inclusion_b` / `rowspan_inclusion_c` (`Rowspan.lean`) | (a) `rowspan(ОҳвӮ–вҲ’ОҳвӮҖ)вҠҶвҹЁhбөў:iвҲҲрқ“ҳвҹ© вҶ” Pa(iвӮ–)вҠҶрқ“ҳ`; (b) `вҠҶвҹЁqбөў:iвҲҲAn(iвӮ–)вҹ©`; (c) `Pa(iвӮ–)вҠ„рқ“ҳ вҹ№ вҠ„вҹЁqбөў:iвҲҲрқ“ҳвҹ©` (all given `ОҳвӮ–вү ОҳвӮҖ`) | **Lemma 1** linking observable precision differences to the latent graph. (a),(b),(c) **all proved sorry-free, axiom-clean.** Two faithfulness fixes the formalization forced: (a)/(c) need the paper's non-degeneracy `ОҳвӮ–вү ОҳвӮҖ` (Assumption 1(b) genericity вҖ” absent, a degenerate source intervention `О»вӮ–=(BвӮҖ)_{iвӮ–iвӮ–}` makes them false); (c) additionally needs `рқ“ҳ` **ancestor-closed** (the algorithm's actual usage вҖ” a "diamond" graph with non-closed `рқ“ҳ` is a genuine counterexample to (c) as the paper states it). Key helpers: `vec_mem_rowSpan_vecMulVec_sub` (Gram extraction), `hSpan_An_eq_qSpan_An` (inverse-PORQ support, by dimension count), `Q_row_dotProduct_H_row_eq_zero` (PORQ orthogonality). **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `disentanglement_uniqueness` (`Uniqueness.lean`) | one intervention per node (`target` **bijective**) + non-degenerate (`ОҳвӮ–вү ОҳвӮҖ`) + equal `{ОҳвӮ–}` вҹ№ the two solutions differ by a single `ПғвҲҲS(рқ’ў)` **up to nonzero signed diagonal scaling**: `H'=diag(Ој)В·PПғВ·H` (Ој nonzero, any sign), `B'вӮ–В·(diag ОјВ·PПғ)=diag(ОҪ)В·PПғВ·BвӮ–` (ОҪ=Вұ1), targets `i'вӮ–=Пғ iвӮ–` | **(вҠҶ) direction of Theorem 2.** The clean orthogonal-matrix route: change-of-basis recovery `H'=MH` (L1), Gram congruence (L2), `gram_to_orthogonal` (L3), the **sum trick** `MбөҖВ·diag(d')В·M=diag(d)` вҶ’ orthogonal `O`, the row-collapse induction вҶ’ `M` signed monomial, signed-Cholesky read-off вҶ’ per-context relations, `InSG Пғ` and target read-off. Reuses `cholesky_unique`. **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `orthogonal_upperTri_signed_diag` / `signed_cholesky` / `permMat_transpose_eq` / `permMat_conj_apply'` / `permMat_mul_diagonal` (`Uniqueness.lean`, private) | orthogonal upper-triangular matrix is a `Вұ1` diagonal; `PбөҖP=BбөҖB` with `B` upper-tri pos-diag вҹ№ `P=diag(s)В·B` with `s i=Вұ1` (signed Cholesky); `(permMat Пғ)бөҖ=permMat ПғвҒ»В№`; `(permMat Пғ)бөҖВ·XВ·permMat Пғ` relabels by `Пғ`; `permMat ПғВ·diag s=diag(sвҲҳПғвҒ»В№)В·permMat Пғ` | Reusable signed-Cholesky / permutation-conjugation helpers feeding the read-off step (F). The signed Cholesky generalizes `cholesky_unique` to a single sign-fixed factor. **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `monomial_relations_from_orthogonal_core` (`Uniqueness.lean`, private) | from the orthogonal `O = diag(вҲҡd')В·MВ·diag(вҲҡdвҒ»В№)` of the sum trick: `M=diag ОјВ·permMat Пғ` (nonzero `Ој`) and the per-context signed relations `B'вӮ–В·M=diag ОҪВ·permMat ПғВ·BвӮ–` with shared `ОҪ=signвҲҳОј` | The geometric core (`prop:orthogonal-correctness`). **(D)** decreasing parent-elimination induction on `O`'s rows (via `central_rank2_eq`, the `e_c`-support argument + orthogonality) вҹ№ row `Пғn` of `O` is `ВұeвӮҷ`; **(E)** monomial form of `M`; **(F)** signed-Cholesky read-off (`signed_cholesky`/`bRel`) with shared sign `signвҲҳОј`, whose Пғ-triangularity input `S'.BвӮҖ(Пғa)(Пғb)=0` for `b<a` is read off directly from the `(b,a)` entry of `central_rank2_eq` (no extra induction). **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |
| `disentanglement_identifiability` (`Identifiability.lean`) | **flagship Theorem 2 (algebraic, up to S(рқ’ў) and signed scaling):** one intervention per node + non-degenerate + equal `{ОҳвӮ–}` вҹ№ solutions related by a single `ПғвҲҲS(рқ’ў)` and a nonzero signed diagonal scaling of latent directions; with `sigma_solutions` (вҠҮ) this characterizes the solution set | **Headline, fully proved.** Reduces to `disentanglement_uniqueness`. `#print axioms` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. **Proved (sorry-free, axiom-clean)** |

## 17c. `Discovery/InvariantPrediction/` вҖ” Invariant Causal Prediction (PetersвҖ“BГјhlmannвҖ“Meinshausen 2016)

Formalization of the setup and soundness of Peters, BГјhlmann & Meinshausen, *Causal inference
using invariant prediction* (JRSS-B 2016, `arXiv:1501.01332`). An **environment family**
